  cliff, while hidden/output rounding add extra F2F and scalar work on top.

Decision:

- Do not pursue BF16-rounded GELU inside the current FFN12 Tile spelling on
  A10G unless a new formulation preserves `HMMA=256`.
- Do not promote hidden-bias BF16 rounding; it keeps Tensor Cores but pays too
  much scalar/permutation overhead.
- `roundout` is a possible source opt-in if the broader all-BF16 experiment
  needs one more output-side conversion removed, but it is not a speed win.
- Future FFN12 work should keep the fast FP32-polynomial GELU spelling and
  focus on reducing non-HMMA scalar/index work while preserving `HMMA=256`.

### 2026-06-05 - Source opt-in: FFN12 output-bias BF16 round

Goal:

- Move the least harmful FFN12 BF16 epilogue ablation into source as an
  explicit experiment knob.
- Preserve the current fast `poly9 + !FullBF16 + pair-H32/TK64 + W1/W2
  latency=2 + no output accumulator round` path as the default.

Source change:

- Added `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_ROUND_OUTPUT_BIAS_BF16=1`.
- The env var only affects the current non-residual, `FullBF16=false`,
  pair-H32/TK64, `RoundOutputAcc=false`, latency=2 FFN12 source path.
- The opt-in rounds the final output-bias result to BF16 before BF16 store. It
  does not enable the slower BF16-rounded GELU polynomial.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_roundout_bias_default_clean_serial \
  --fp16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_ROUND_OUTPUT_BIAS_BF16=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_roundout_bias_opt_clean_serial \
  --fp16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt | \
  rg -n -A4 -B2 "ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel<6, false, 64, false, false, 2"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt > /tmp/cudasep_infer.sass
```

Build:

```text
ninja: Entering directory `build-min'
ninja: no work to do.
```

Serial `test_clean.wav` timing:

```text
default: 918.0 ms, RTF=43.127546
opt-in:  912.0 ms, RTF=43.409684
```

This narrow serial run is timing-noise level. Do not treat the opt-in as a
speed win.

Output diff, opt-in versus default:

```text
Vocals.wav:
  max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=9.9379856e-09
  rel_rms=5.40542012e-08 snr=145.343 dB

Instrumental.wav:
  max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.30947051e-08
  rel_rms=5.43980928e-08 snr=145.288 dB
```

Source binary resource usage for the active `GeluMode=6` pair-H32/TK64 path:

```text
default:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  <6, false, 64, false, false, 2, true, true, true, false>
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

round output-bias BF16 opt-in:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  <6, false, 64, false, false, 2, true, true, true, true>
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count from current `build-min/cudasep_infer`:

```text
default:
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDG=208 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 STS=44 STG=1
  F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160 BAR=35 DEPBAR=29
  IMAD=192 IADD3=147 PRMT=16 SHF=182 LOP3=142 CS2R=32

round output-bias BF16 opt-in:
  instr=2664 HMMA=256 LDG=208 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 STS=44 STG=1
  F2F=96 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160 BAR=35 DEPBAR=29
  IMAD=257 IADD3=143 PRMT=52 SHF=196 LOP3=142 CS2R=32
```

Interpretation:

- The source opt-in preserves the Tensor Core schedule (`HMMA=256`), unlike the
  full-BF16 GELU path.
- The bottom cause of the remaining slowdown risk is scalar/conversion
  overhead, not Tensor Core fallback: `F2F 64 -> 96`, `IMAD 192 -> 257`, and
  `PRMT 16 -> 52`.
- Because the output difference is only around `145 dB` SNR on `test_clean.wav`,
  this is acceptable as a BF16-purity experiment knob.

Decision:

- Keep the opt-in in source.
- Do not enable it by default as a speed optimization.
- For the long-term all-BF16 experiment, this knob is useful because it removes
  one more output-side FP32-lived value while preserving `HMMA=256`.

### 2026-06-05 - Freq QKV fused rotary producer BF16 trig

Goal:

- Continue removing BF16 -> FP32 conversion boundaries in experimental fused
  CUDA Tile producers.
- The default rotary cache is already BF16 in `--bf16`; the older freq QKV
  fused producer still converted `cos/sin` back to FP32 before launch. This
  experiment lets the fused producer consume BF16 trig directly.

Source change:

- Templated these CUDA Tile kernels on `TrigT`:
  - `qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_cutile_static_full_kernel`
  - `qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_cutile_tail_kernel`
  - `qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_bkn_cutile_static_full_kernel`
  - `qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_bkn_cutile_tail_kernel`
- Updated `try_linear_qkv_freq_rotary_cutile_fused(...)` and
  `try_linear_qkv_freq_rotary_bkn_cutile_fused(...)` so BF16 rotary freqs
  dispatch BF16 trig kernel instances and F32 freqs keep the existing F32
  fallback.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/freq_fused_trig_default_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_QKV_FREQ_ROTARY_BKN_CUTILE_FUSED=1 \
CUDASEP_QKV_FREQ_ROTARY_BKN_CUTILE_TILE_N=256 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/freq_fused_trig_bkn256_bf16_clean_serial \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt | \
  rg -n -A3 -B1 "qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64(_bkn)?_cutile_(static_full|tail)_kernel<"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt \
  > /tmp/cudasep_infer_after_freq_trig.sass
```

Build:

```text
[1/2] Building CUDA object CMakeFiles/cudasep_infer.dir/src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu.o
[2/2] Linking CXX executable cudasep_infer
```

Smoke timing:

```text
parallel default BF16 path, test_clean.wav:
  1907.8 ms

parallel BKN fused producer BF16 trig opt-in, test_clean.wav:
  2175.7 ms

serial BKN fused producer BF16 trig opt-in, test_clean.wav:
  1115.5 ms
```

The parallel timings are only smoke evidence because two inference processes
were running concurrently. The serial opt-in timing confirms this older BKN
fused producer is still slower than the current safe default path; do not
promote it.

Output diff, BKN fused producer BF16 trig opt-in versus default BF16 path:

```text
Vocals.wav:
  max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.0041113e-08
  rel_rms=5.46151265e-08 snr=145.254 dB

Instrumental.wav:
  max_abs=2.98023224e-07 rms=1.31716666e-08
  rel_rms=5.47178064e-08 snr=145.237 dB
```

Resource usage evidence:

```text
qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_bkn_cutile_static_full_kernel<TN=256,float>:
  REG=240 STACK=0 SHARED=98320

qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_bkn_cutile_static_full_kernel<TN=256,__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=250 STACK=0 SHARED=98320

qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_cutile_static_full_kernel<16,32,32,...,float>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=49176

qkv_freq60_rotary_pad64_cutile_static_full_kernel<16,32,32,...,__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=49176
```

Static SASS count for BKN fused producer `TN=256`:

```text
F32 trig:
  instr=3072 HMMA=256 LDG=176 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=96 STG=2
  F2F=160 FFMA=64 FMUL=64 BAR=27 DEPBAR=30
  IMAD=534 IADD3=287 PRMT=257 SHF=249 LOP3=236

BF16 trig:
  instr=3144 HMMA=256 LDG=176 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=96 STG=2
  F2F=160 FFMA=64 FMUL=64 BAR=27 DEPBAR=30
  IMAD=598 IADD3=287 PRMT=266 SHF=249 LOP3=236
```

Interpretation:

- BF16 trig preserves the Tensor Core body (`HMMA=256`) and output quality is
  effectively identical to the current BF16 default path.
- The bottom cause of the remaining slowdown risk is scalar/index/packing
  codegen, not Tensor Core fallback: `IMAD 534 -> 598`, `PRMT 257 -> 266`, and
  `REG 240 -> 250` for the `TN=256` BKN fused producer.
- This fused producer already lost to the safe split-contig path before this
  change. BF16 trig removes a conversion boundary for the all-BF16 hypothesis,
  but it does not solve the producer's deeper scalar/codegen overhead.

Decision:

- Keep BF16 trig dispatch in source for the fused freq producer experiments.
- Do not promote `CUDASEP_ENABLE_QKV_FREQ_ROTARY_BKN_CUTILE_FUSED=1`.
- Next work on freq QKV should target the scalar/index overhead in the fused
  BKN producer or keep using the safe split-contig path while optimizing the
  actual attention/FFN hotspots.

## 2026-06-05 - Time Attention Split-Contig Seg2 Row-State Probe

Goal:

- Test whether the remaining exact `Q64/K32` split-contiguous time-attention
  gap is mainly the 40-step online-softmax row-state dependency chain.
- Keep one CUDA Tile kernel, exact online softmax, BF16 Q/K/V, FP32 row state
  and output accumulator, and the same `QRows=64,KTile=32` Tensor Core work.
- Add a bench-only `seg2` variant that forms a local online-softmax state over
  two adjacent K tiles, then merges that local state into the global row state.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_seg2 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input_no_keytail \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_seg2_no_keytail \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -n -A4 -B1 \
  "time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input|seg2)_kernel<64, 32"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/time_attn_seg2.sass
```

Results:

```text
exact split-contig:
  best=3.387 ms median=3.423 ms
  real_math=60.42 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.41 TF/s roof70=86.3%

seg2 split-contig:
  best=3.714 ms median=3.722 ms
  real_math=55.09 TF/s logical_tile_math=56.91 TF/s roof70=78.7%
  compare_vs_main1280_q64k32_exp2: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

exact split-contig no-keytail:
  best=3.298 ms median=3.327 ms
  real_math=61.04 TF/s roof70=87.2%

seg2 split-contig no-keytail:
  best=3.325 ms median=3.333 ms
  real_math=60.55 TF/s roof70=86.5%
```

Resource evidence:

```text
exact no-keytail: REG=217 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
seg2 no-keytail:  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=32768

exact keytail:    REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960
seg2 keytail:     REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=40960
```

Static SASS count:

```text
exact no-keytail:
  instr=2144 HMMA=128 MUFU.EX2=72 LDG=28 LDSM=64 STS=48 STG=4
  F2F=48 IMAD=17 IADD3=105 PRMT=0 SHF=93 LOP3=81

seg2 no-keytail:
  instr=2192 HMMA=128 MUFU.EX2=76 LDG=28 LDSM=64 STS=48 STG=4
  F2F=48 IMAD=17 IADD3=103 PRMT=0 SHF=109 LOP3=91

exact keytail:
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 MUFU.EX2=90 LDG=32 LDSM=64 STS=60 STG=4
  F2F=56 IMAD=62 IADD3=138 PRMT=80 SHF=123 LOP3=141

seg2 keytail:
  instr=2856 HMMA=160 MUFU.EX2=96 LDG=32 LDSM=64 STS=60 STG=4
  F2F=56 IMAD=20 IADD3=160 PRMT=81 SHF=143 LOP3=145
```

Interpretation:

- `seg2` is exact for this synthetic benchmark: output comparison versus the
  current `main1280_q64k32_exp2` reference is bit-identical.
- The no-keytail result is only slightly slower than the exact no-keytail lower
  bound. It adds local state but preserves `STACK=0`, the same `HMMA=128`, and
  only modestly increases static instruction count.
- The full keytail path is a clear negative result. It preserves Tensor Core
  work (`HMMA=160`) but pushes register allocation from `REG=233` to `REG=255`
  and introduces `STACK=8`; runtime drops from `60.42` to `55.09 TF/s`.
- This answers the row-state dependency hypothesis: simply reducing the number
  of global row-state merges is not enough. The extra local accumulator/state
  raises register pressure, and with the keytail present the A10G schedule hits
  the register ceiling before the shorter dependency chain can help.

Decision:

- Keep `seg2` as a bench-only negative diagnostic.
- Do not port `seg2` into the source time-attention path.
- Next exact attention work should avoid holding a second full `OutTile`
  accumulator across the loop. Better candidates are lower-register tail
  handling, split output-D scheduling only if it avoids stack spills, or a
  producer/consumer fusion that removes global memory traffic without forcing
  attention to recompute K rotary or duplicate accumulators.

## 2026-06-05 - Time Attention Split-Contig K-Transposed Consumer Probe

Goal:

- Test whether the split-contiguous time-attention consumer is slowed by reading
  K through a strided `[B,N,H,D]` view.
- Keep exact `Q64/K32` online softmax, BF16 Q/K/V, one CUDA Tile attention
  kernel, and the same V/output layout.
- Build a bench-only K-transposed buffer with layout `[B,H,D,N]`, then compare
  the existing split-contiguous consumer against a variant whose K view is a
  direct contiguous `ct::shape<D,N>` tensor.

Implementation:

- Added `pack_time_split_contig_k_transposed_kernel` for benchmark setup.
- Added `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_kt_input_kernel`.
- Added focused variants:
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_kt_input`
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_kt_input_no_keytail`

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_kt_input \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input_no_keytail \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_kt_input_no_keytail \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -n -A3 -B1 \
  "time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig(_kt)?_input_kernel<64, 32"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/time_attn_kt.sass
```

Results:

```text
exact split-contig:
  best=3.393 ms median=3.418 ms
  real_math=60.31 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.31 TF/s roof70=86.2%

K-transposed split-contig:
  best=3.908 ms median=3.945 ms
  real_math=52.37 TF/s logical_tile_math=54.10 TF/s roof70=74.8%
  compare_vs_main1280_q64k32_exp2: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

exact split-contig no-keytail:
  best=3.320 ms median=3.328 ms
  real_math=60.63 TF/s roof70=86.6%

K-transposed split-contig no-keytail:
  best=3.630 ms median=3.662 ms
  real_math=55.47 TF/s roof70=79.2%
```

Resource evidence:

```text
exact no-keytail: REG=217 STACK=0  SHARED=32768
KT no-keytail:    REG=216 STACK=0  SHARED=28672

exact keytail:    REG=233 STACK=0  SHARED=40960
KT keytail:       REG=255 STACK=64 SHARED=36864
```

Static SASS count:

```text
exact no-keytail:
  instr=2144 HMMA=128 MUFU.EX2=72 LDG=28 LDSM=64 STS=48 STG=4
  F2F=48 FFMA=308 IMAD=17 IADD3=105 PRMT=0 SHF=93 LOP3=81 BAR=29

KT no-keytail:
  instr=2304 HMMA=128 MUFU.EX2=72 LDG=80 LDSM=32 STS=48 STG=4
  F2F=48 FFMA=308 IMAD=50 IADD3=87 PRMT=176 SHF=89 LOP3=83 BAR=17

exact keytail:
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 MUFU.EX2=90 LDG=32 LDSM=64 STS=60 STG=4
  F2F=56 FFMA=358 IMAD=62 IADD3=138 PRMT=80 SHF=123 LOP3=141 BAR=31

KT keytail:
  instr=3064 HMMA=160 MUFU.EX2=90 LDG=98 LDSM=32 STS=58 STG=4
  F2F=56 FFMA=358 IMAD=149 IADD3=168 PRMT=272 SHF=122 LOP3=139 BAR=19
```

Interpretation:

- The K-transposed consumer is exact: output versus the current
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2` reference is bit-identical.
- The layout intuition is wrong for this CUDA Tile compiler shape. Making K
  physically contiguous in `[D,N]` lowers shared memory a little, but it
  destroys the good generated staging pattern.
- The no-keytail KT kernel keeps the same `HMMA=128`, but `LDSM` drops from
  `64 -> 32`, while global-load and permutation work explode
  (`LDG 28 -> 80`, `PRMT 0 -> 176`). It is therefore slower despite similar
  register count and lower shared memory.
- The full KT kernel is worse: it preserves `HMMA=160`, but pushes registers to
  `REG=255`, introduces `STACK=64`, and further raises address/permutation work
  (`LDG 32 -> 98`, `PRMT 80 -> 272`).
- Bottom cause: the existing strided split-contiguous K view lets CUDA Tile
  generate a better `LDSM`/shared-memory MMA schedule than the seemingly more
  direct contiguous `[D,N]` K-transposed layout.

Decision:

- Do not change the time QKV producer to emit K-transposed layout for the
  current attention consumer.
- Keep the existing split-contiguous `[B,N,H,D]` K layout for time attention.
- Future producer/consumer layout changes must be judged by generated SASS, not
  by memory-contiguity intuition. A useful layout must preserve or improve the
  current `LDSM=64`, avoid `PRMT` growth, and keep keytail below the
  `REG=255`/stack cliff.

### Negative/Neutral Probe: Freq Attention CUDA Tile exp2 Softmax

Goal:

- Test the CUDA Tile source-like frequency attention body directly, not the old
  hand-written CUDA `__expf` path.
- Replace `ct::exp(scores - row_max)` with
  `ct::exp2((scores - row_max) * log2(e))` in the q16 source-like benchmark.
- Keep the same fixed shape:

```text
BH=10408, N=60, NPad=64, D=64, QRows=16
freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16>
```

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant q16s --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant q16s_exp2 --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant q16sc --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant q16sc_exp2 --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant q16s_exp2 --warmup 3 --iters 20 --validate
./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant q16sc_exp2 --warmup 3 --iters 20 --validate
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt > /tmp/freq_attn_exp2.sass
```

Single serial timing pass:

```text
q16s:        best=0.809 ms median=0.852 ms 11.857 TF/s roof70=16.9%
q16s_exp2:   best=0.789 ms median=0.851 ms 12.153 TF/s roof70=17.4%
q16sc:       best=0.801 ms median=0.861 ms 11.968 TF/s roof70=17.1%
q16sc_exp2:  best=0.789 ms median=0.850 ms 12.154 TF/s roof70=17.4%
```

Interleaved serial repeats show the best value is noisy at this scale:

```text
q16s:        best=0.795, 0.815, 0.793 ms
q16s_exp2:   best=0.825, 0.788, 0.811 ms
q16sc:       best=0.797, 0.826, 0.799 ms
q16sc_exp2:  best=0.809, 0.789, 0.820 ms
```

Correctness:

```text
q16s_exp2 compare vs q16s:
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

q16sc_exp2 compare vs q16s:
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

BH0 CPU reference validate:
  q16s_exp2:  max_abs=0 rms=0
  q16sc_exp2: max_abs=0 rms=0
```

Resource usage:

```text
q16s:        REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
q16s_exp2:   REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
q16sc:       REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
q16sc_exp2:  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
```

Static SASS count:

```text
q16s:
  instr=2020 HMMA=16 MUFU.EX2=8 MUFU=18 F2F=8 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28
  STG=8 IMAD=185 IADD3=42 PRMT=72 SHF=71 LOP3=102

q16s_exp2:
  instr=1972 HMMA=16 MUFU.EX2=8 MUFU=18 F2F=8 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28
  STG=8 IMAD=175 IADD3=42 PRMT=72 SHF=71 LOP3=102

q16sc:
  instr=2020 HMMA=16 MUFU.EX2=8 MUFU=18 F2F=8 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28
  STG=8 IMAD=185 IADD3=44 PRMT=72 SHF=71 LOP3=102

q16sc_exp2:
  instr=1972 HMMA=16 MUFU.EX2=8 MUFU=18 F2F=8 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28
  STG=8 IMAD=175 IADD3=44 PRMT=72 SHF=71 LOP3=102
```

Interpretation:

- `ct::exp2` is numerically identical after BF16 probability/output rounding for
  this deterministic bench input.
- It removes about 48 static instructions and lowers integer address/index work
  (`IMAD 185 -> 175`) without changing the real compute or memory shape.
- It does not change the important bottom-level bottlenecks: `HMMA=16`,
  `MUFU.EX2=8`, `F2F=8`, `LDG=9`, `LDS=26`, `STS=28`, `STG=8`,
  `REG=92`, and `SHARED=22528` are unchanged.
- The best-time difference is within repeated-run noise; median time is
  effectively unchanged. This is therefore not enough evidence to promote the
  source freq attention default.
- The low `~17%` of the accepted 70 TF/s dense BF16 reference is not caused by
  choosing `ct::exp` over `ct::exp2`. The deeper issue remains the tiny
  `N=60/D=64` attention shape: each CTA only performs 16 HMMA instructions and
  carries softmax/reduction/index/store overhead that dense GEMM does not have.

Decision:

- Keep `q16s_exp2` and `q16sc_exp2` as benchmark-only probes.
- Do not change `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu` freq attention to exp2 yet.
- Next useful freq work should remove global-memory boundaries or fuse producer
  work into the attention body, not micro-optimize the exponent call alone.

### Negative Probe: Freq Packed-QKV Direct Attention Consumer

Goal:

- Test the lower bound for removing the freq split/rotary/pad64 global-memory
  boundary.
- Use a deterministic identity-rotary packed QKV input:

```text
packed qkv: [B=1301, N=60, 3*H*D=1536] BF16
heads=8, D=64, BH=10408
```

- Compare a two-stage CUDA Tile pipeline against a direct packed-QKV attention
  consumer:

```text
q16qkv_pipe:
  split_packed_qkv_identity_freq60_pad64_kernel
  -> freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16>

q{16,32,64}qkv_direct:
  freq_attention60_packed_qkv_identity_kernel<QRows>
```

This intentionally omits real rotary math so the experiment is a lower bound
for consumer-side fusion. If even this identity case loses, adding cos/sin loads
and rotary arithmetic cannot make the same packed-QKV consumer design viable.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8
for variant in q16qkv_pipe q16qkv_direct q32qkv_direct q64qkv_direct; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$variant" \
    --warmup 30 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
done
./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant q16s --warmup 30 --iters 200
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt > /tmp/freq_qkv_direct.sass
```

Timing:

```text
q16s, pre-split padded q/k/v attention only:
  best=0.791 ms median=0.859 ms 12.133 TF/s roof70=17.3%

q16qkv_pipe, packed QKV split/pad then attention:
  best=1.574 ms median=1.580 ms 6.094 TF/s roof70=8.7%

q16qkv_direct:
  best=2.304 ms median=2.307 ms 4.164 TF/s roof70=5.9%

q32qkv_direct:
  best=2.126 ms median=2.136 ms 4.512 TF/s roof70=6.4%

q64qkv_direct:
  best=1.833 ms median=1.851 ms 5.232 TF/s roof70=7.5%
```

Correctness:

```text
q16qkv_direct vs q16qkv_pipe: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
q32qkv_direct vs q16qkv_pipe: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
q64qkv_direct vs q16qkv_pipe: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource usage:

```text
padded q16 attention: REG=92  STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
identity split/pad:   REG=32  STACK=0 SHARED=0     LOCAL=0
direct q16:           REG=193 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
direct q32:           REG=236 STACK=0 SHARED=28672 LOCAL=0
direct q64:           REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
```

Static SASS count:

```text
padded q16 attention:
  instr=2020 HMMA=16 MUFU.EX2=8 LDG=9  LDS=26 STS=28 STG=8
  IMAD=185 IADD3=42  PRMT=72  SHF=71  LOP3=102

identity split/pad:
  instr=276  HMMA=0  MUFU.EX2=0 LDG=12 LDS=0  STS=0  STG=12
  IMAD=25  IADD3=13  PRMT=11  SHF=12  LOP3=15

direct q16:
  instr=3492 HMMA=16 MUFU.EX2=8  LDG=70 LDS=26 STS=41 STG=8
  IMAD=309 IADD3=187 PRMT=132 SHF=86  LOP3=158

direct q32:
  instr=4932 HMMA=32 MUFU.EX2=16 LDG=78 LDS=42 STS=48 STG=16
  IMAD=373 IADD3=237 PRMT=208 SHF=116 LOP3=201

direct q64:
  instr=7220 HMMA=64 MUFU.EX2=32 LDG=94 LDS=40 STS=16 STG=32
  IMAD=618 IADD3=293 PRMT=288 SHF=104 LOP3=249
```

Interpretation:

- The direct packed-QKV consumer is exact for the identity-rotary lower bound,
  so the slowdown is purely codegen/layout, not math drift.
- Removing the intermediate q/k/v global-memory tensors is not enough. The
  packed-QKV consumer destroys the compact attention load/staging shape.
- Direct q16 keeps the same `HMMA=16` as padded q16 attention, but raises
  registers from `92 -> 193`, global loads from `9 -> 70`, static instructions
  from `2020 -> 3492`, and integer/permutation work from
  `IMAD/PRMT 185/72 -> 309/132`.
- Increasing QRows reduces repeated K/V reads conceptually, but the generated
  kernel gets heavier faster than reuse pays back. Direct q64 reaches
  `REG=255`, `instr=7220`, `LDG=94`, `IMAD=618`, and is still slower than the
  simple two-stage split/pad plus padded attention pipeline.
- This is the same lesson as the time packed-QKV direct rotary consumer:
  consumer-side fusion only works if Q/K/V remain compact and tile-contiguous
  at the point where attention stages them for MMA.

Decision:

- Keep `q16qkv_direct`, `q32qkv_direct`, and `q64qkv_direct` as benchmark-only
  negative lower bounds.
- Do not port packed-QKV direct indexing into `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`.
- The next freq fusion direction must preserve compact/tile-contiguous Q/K/V
  staging, for example by changing the producer output layout or fusing a
  coalesced split-contiguous producer with an attention consumer that does not
  repeatedly gather packed QKV inside the hot loop.

### Positive Lower Bound: Freq Packed-QKV Split/Pad u32 Pair Copy

Goal:

- Keep the good two-stage freq path:

```text
packed QKV -> compact padded q/k/v -> padded q16 attention
```

- Optimize only the identity split/pad lower-bound kernel. The previous
  `split_packed_qkv_identity_freq60_pad64_kernel` moved each rotary pair as two
  BF16 scalar values. The new
  `split_packed_qkv_identity_freq60_pad64_u32_kernel` reinterprets each even/odd
  BF16 pair as one 32-bit value, cutting pair load/store count in half.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8
for variant in q16qkv_pipe q16qkv_pipe_u32 q16qkv_pipe q16qkv_pipe_u32; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$variant" \
    --warmup 30 --iters 200
done
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt > /tmp/freq_qkv_u32_split.sass
```

Timing:

```text
q16qkv_pipe:
  best=1.572 ms median=1.580 ms 6.101 TF/s roof70=8.7%
  best=1.573 ms median=1.580 ms 6.096 TF/s roof70=8.7%

q16qkv_pipe_u32:
  best=1.553 ms median=1.562 ms 6.177 TF/s roof70=8.8%
  best=1.557 ms median=1.563 ms 6.159 TF/s roof70=8.8%
```

Correctness smoke:

```text
checksum q16qkv_pipe:     0.1885
checksum q16qkv_pipe_u32: 0.1885

q16qkv_direct compare against the original pipeline reference still gives:
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource usage:

```text
split BF16 pair-scalar: REG=32 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0
split u32 pair-copy:    REG=26 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0
padded q16 attention:   REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
```

Static SASS count:

```text
split BF16 pair-scalar:
  instr=276 LDG=12 STG=12 STG_U16=12 IMAD=25 IADD3=13 PRMT=11 SHF=12 LOP3=15 BAR=3

split u32 pair-copy:
  instr=244 LDG=6  STG=6  STG_U16=0  IMAD=27 IADD3=14 PRMT=0  SHF=16 LOP3=14 BAR=0
```

Interpretation:

- The u32 pair-copy lower bound is a small but real positive result. It reduces
  register count, halves global load/store instruction count, removes `PRMT`,
  and improves the full split+attention pipeline by about `0.015-0.020 ms`.
- The gain is small because the attention kernel still dominates the pipeline
  (`~0.79 ms` attention only versus `~1.55 ms` split+attention), but this proves
  the split/pad boundary still has low-level instruction waste that can be
  trimmed without hurting the attention staging layout.
- This identity lower bound does not directly replace the source
  `split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64_kernel`, because real Q/K still
  need FP32 rotary math per BF16 pair. It does, however, point to source-side
  improvements:
  - keep V as pair-wide copy/store when possible;
  - avoid BF16 scalar pair stores for pad/identity paths;
  - look for a pair-wise rotary kernel shape that writes q/k as a 32-bit pair
    after computing both rotated BF16 values.

Decision:

- Keep `q16qkv_pipe_u32` as a positive benchmark lower bound.
- Do not port it directly to `src` yet because the real path includes Q/K
  rotary.
- Next source-facing experiment should apply the same pair-wide output idea to
  the actual freq split/rotary/pad64 kernel, starting with V/pad writes or a
  q/k rotated-pair pack after rotary.

### Reverted Source Attempt: Freq Split V-only u32 Pair Store

Goal:

- Move the previous identity `u32` pair-copy lower bound one step toward the real
  source path without changing Q/K rotary math.
- Add an opt-in specialization for the real source kernel:

```text
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_V_U32=1
split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64_kernel<TrigT, VU32=true>
```

- Keep Q/K loads, rotary, and stores unchanged; only move V even/odd BF16 pairs
  through a `reinterpret_cast<unsigned int*>` load/store.

Outcome:

```text
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8
```

The source attempt was stopped and reverted before timing because the main
`mbr_cuda_tile.cu` CUDA compilation did not progress to a completed build in the
available verification window after adding the new VU32 source specialization.
After the VU32 source edits were removed, no VU32 source symbols or env gates
remain in `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`.

Interpretation:

- The bench-only `q16qkv_pipe_u32` result remains valid as a lower bound.
- Directly adding another source specialization to the already large
  `mbr_cuda_tile.cu` is not a good next step unless the experiment is moved into
  a smaller bench/source file or replaces an existing specialization instead of
  multiplying template instances.
- The source path was intentionally not left half-integrated. The next attempt
  should first make a standalone real-rotary split bench that compares scalar V
  stores and V-u32 stores with the same trig dtype and then only promote if
  `cudasep_infer` build time and runtime evidence are both acceptable.

Decision:

- Keep the source VU32 attempt reverted.
- Continue with bench-first real-rotary split experiments before touching the
  default inference path again.

### CUDA Tile Freq Real-Rotary Split: V u32 Pair Store

Goal:

- Move the identity packed-QKV `u32` pair-copy lower bound onto a source-facing
  real-rotary split shape without touching `src`.
- Keep Q/K rotary math and Q/K BF16 scalar stores unchanged.
- Compare scalar V even/odd BF16 stores against a V-only `unsigned int` pair
  load/store in the same CUDA Tile kernel.

Implementation:

- Added bench-only variants in `bench/bf16_freq_attention_cutile.cu`:
  - `qkv_rot_split`
  - `qkv_rot_split_vu32`
  - `q16qkv_rot_pipe`
  - `q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32`
- The split kernel uses `__tile_global__`,
  `cuda::tiles`, `ct::load_masked`, `ct::store_masked`, and fixed
  `freq60 -> pad64` MelBandRoformer shapes.
- The VU32 specialization only changes V pair movement. Q/K still load BF16,
  convert to FP32 for rotary, and write rotated BF16 pairs.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8
for v in qkv_rot_split qkv_rot_split_vu32 q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 5 --iters 20 --compare-baseline
done
for v in qkv_rot_split qkv_rot_split_vu32 q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
done
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt > /tmp/freq_qkv_rot_split.sass
```

Correctness:

```text
qkv_rot_split_vu32 vs qkv_rot_split:
  Q max_abs=0 rms=0
  K max_abs=0 rms=0
  V max_abs=0 rms=0

q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32 vs q16qkv_rot_pipe:
  output max_abs=0 rms=0
```

Timing:

```text
short 5 warmup / 20 iters:
qkv_rot_split          best=0.772 ms median=0.776 ms 597.5 GiB/s
qkv_rot_split_vu32     best=0.772 ms median=0.776 ms 597.6 GiB/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe        best=1.557 ms median=1.562 ms 6.160 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32   best=1.555 ms median=1.563 ms 6.168 TF/s

stable 30 warmup / 200 iters:
qkv_rot_split          best=0.772 ms median=0.775 ms 598.1 GiB/s
qkv_rot_split_vu32     best=0.772 ms median=0.777 ms 597.8 GiB/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe        best=1.576 ms median=1.584 ms 6.088 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32   best=1.570 ms median=1.576 ms 6.111 TF/s
```

Resource:

```text
split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_pad64_kernel<false>:
  REG=38 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_pad64_kernel<true>:
  REG=36 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false,false,false>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
```

SASS instruction counts:

```text
rot_scalar:
  instr=208 HMMA=0 MUFU=0 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=5
  FFMA=8 FMUL=8 IMAD=42 IADD3=25 PRMT=19 SHF=15 LOP3=15 BAR=3

rot_vu32:
  instr=200 HMMA=0 MUFU=0 LDG=14 STG=10 STG_U16=8 F2F=4
  FFMA=8 FMUL=8 IMAD=45 IADD3=25 PRMT=17 SHF=15 LOP3=15 BAR=2

attn_q16:
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=8 FFMA=92 FADD=32 FMUL=24 IMAD=185 IADD3=42 PRMT=72
  SHF=71 LOP3=102 BAR=16
```

Interpretation:

- The VU32 specialization is functionally safe in this deterministic bench:
  Q/K/V split outputs and final attention outputs are bit-identical.
- The codegen improvement is real but small: rotary split loses 8 SASS
  instructions, 2 global loads, 2 global stores, 4 `STG.U16`, 1 `F2F`, 2
  `PRMT`, and 1 barrier-like instruction.
- The timing impact is too small to promote directly. Standalone split is flat,
  and full split+attention improves only about `0.006 ms` best /
  `0.008 ms` median in the stable run.
- The reason is visible in SASS: real Q/K rotary still keeps FP32 rotary
  arithmetic (`FFMA=8`, `FMUL=8`), BF16 conversion, and address math in the
  split kernel, while the downstream q16 attention kernel remains much larger
  (`HMMA=16`, `MUFU=18`, `PRMT=72`, `instr=1008`).

Decision:

- Keep the VU32 real-rotary experiment in `bench` as evidence.
- Do not promote V-only pair-copy to `src` as a standalone change.
- The next CUDA Tile direction should be deeper source-facing fusion:
  `packed QKV + rotary + QK/softmax/AV`, or at least a fused rotary-attention
  consumer that removes the padded Q/K/V global write/read boundary. That is
  more aligned with the project goal than shaving the remaining V store path.

### CUDA Tile Freq Packed-QKV + Rotary Direct Attention

Goal:

- Test the next obvious deep-fusion idea from the previous result:
  directly consume packed QKV, apply Q/K rotary inside the attention kernel, and
  run QK/softmax/AV without writing padded Q/K/V to global memory.
- Keep the implementation bench-only until evidence shows it is worth moving
  into `src`.
- Compare against the source-facing reference:
  `rotary split freq60->pad64 + q16 padded attention`.

Implementation:

- Added CUDA Tile variants in `bench/bf16_freq_attention_cutile.cu`:
  - `q16qkv_rot_direct`
  - `q32qkv_rot_direct`
  - `q64qkv_rot_direct`
- Each direct variant:
  - loads Q/K/V from packed `[B, N=60, 3 * heads * D]`;
  - applies BF16 trig, FP32 rotary math, then writes Q/K tiles only into tile
    registers/fragments;
  - runs `ct::mma` for QK and AV;
  - stores only the final `[BH, 60, 64]` BF16 output.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8
for v in q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_direct q32qkv_rot_direct q64qkv_rot_direct; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 5 --iters 20 --compare-baseline
done
for v in q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32 q16qkv_rot_direct q32qkv_rot_direct q64qkv_rot_direct; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
done
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt > /tmp/freq_qkv_rot_direct.sass
```

Correctness:

```text
q16qkv_rot_direct vs q16qkv_rot_pipe:
  output max_abs=0 rms=0
q32qkv_rot_direct vs q16qkv_rot_pipe:
  output max_abs=0 rms=0
q64qkv_rot_direct vs q16qkv_rot_pipe:
  output max_abs=0 rms=0
```

Timing:

```text
short 5 warmup / 20 iters:
q16qkv_rot_pipe       best=1.558 ms median=1.562 ms 6.158 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_direct     best=2.688 ms median=2.689 ms 3.569 TF/s
q32qkv_rot_direct     best=2.752 ms median=2.761 ms 3.486 TF/s
q64qkv_rot_direct     best=2.681 ms median=2.693 ms 3.577 TF/s

stable 30 warmup / 200 iters:
q16qkv_rot_pipe       best=1.577 ms median=1.582 ms 6.082 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe_vu32  best=1.576 ms median=1.584 ms 6.087 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_direct     best=2.687 ms median=2.689 ms 3.570 TF/s
q32qkv_rot_direct     best=2.745 ms median=2.759 ms 3.495 TF/s
q64qkv_rot_direct     best=2.671 ms median=2.695 ms 3.591 TF/s
```

Resource:

```text
split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_pad64_kernel<false>:
  REG=38 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false,false,false>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_packed_qkv_rotary_kernel<16,false>:
  REG=235 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_packed_qkv_rotary_kernel<32,false>:
  REG=236 STACK=0 SHARED=28672 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_packed_qkv_rotary_kernel<64,false>:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
```

SASS instruction counts:

```text
rot_split:
  instr=208 HMMA=0 MUFU=0 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=5
  FFMA=8 FMUL=8 IMAD=42 IADD3=25 PRMT=19 SHF=15 LOP3=15 BAR=3

attn_q16:
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=8 FFMA=92 FADD=32 FMUL=24 IMAD=185 IADD3=42 PRMT=72
  SHF=71 LOP3=102 BAR=16

rot_direct_q16:
  instr=2496 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=184 LDS=26 STS=41 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=28 FFMA=168 FADD=32 FMUL=100 IMAD=532 IADD3=236 PRMT=152
  SHF=170 LOP3=238 BAR=16

rot_direct_q32:
  instr=3440 HMMA=32 MUFU=34 LDG=216 LDS=42 STS=48 STG=16 STG_U16=16
  F2F=40 FFMA=264 FADD=58 FMUL=140 IMAD=692 IADD3=305 PRMT=256
  SHF=224 LOP3=271 BAR=18

rot_direct_q64:
  instr=4096 HMMA=64 MUFU=48 LDG=280 LDS=36 STS=12 STG=0 STG_U16=0
  F2F=52 FFMA=364 FADD=102 FMUL=220 IMAD=793 IADD3=294 PRMT=352
  SHF=256 LOP3=395 BAR=3
```

Interpretation:

- This direct fusion removes the padded Q/K/V global write/read boundary, and
  the estimated memory traffic drops from about `0.77 GiB` to about `0.30 GiB`.
- It is still much slower. The best direct shape is q64 at `2.671 ms`, versus
  the split+attention reference at `1.576-1.577 ms`.
- The bottom-level reason is not Tensor Core availability. Direct q16 still has
  the same `HMMA=16` as q16 padded attention, but the non-HMMA work explodes:
  `LDG=184`, `IMAD=532`, `PRMT=152`, `F2F=28`, `FFMA=168`, `FMUL=100`, and
  `REG=235`.
- QRows scaling adds more HMMA but also raises register pressure and instruction
  count. q64 gets `HMMA=64`, but `REG=255`, `PRMT=352`, `IMAD=793`, and
  `instr=4096`, so the higher Tensor Core work is buried under staging and
  address/format work.

Decision:

- Keep these direct rotary variants as a negative deep-fusion benchmark.
- Do not promote this naive `packed QKV + rotary + attention` direct consumer to
  `src`.
- The next aligned experiment should not repeat packed address and rotary work
  inside each attention tile. Better candidates:
  - a two-stage tiled layout transform that produces an attention-friendly
    packed/rotated tile layout without full Q/K/V pad64 global tensors;
  - a fused kernel that stages one BH block's Q/K/V into shared memory once,
    then runs QK/softmax/AV from the staged layout;
  - a direct consumer variant that pre-packs Q/K pair loads or vectorizes rotary
    pair handling to reduce `LDG`, `PRMT`, `F2F`, and `IMAD` before trying
    deeper fusion again.

### CUDA Tile Freq Rotary Compact Split + Masked Attention

Goal:

- Test whether removing the `freq60 -> pad64` Q/K/V global write/read boundary
  is useful when the attention consumer stays separate.
- This is the middle ground between:
  - current source-facing path:
    `packed QKV + rotary split to pad64 + padded q16 attention`;
  - failed deep direct path:
    `packed QKV + rotary + attention` inside one high-register kernel.

Implementation:

- Added bench-only variants:
  - `qkv_rot_compact_split`
  - `qkv_rot_compact_split_vu32`
  - `q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe`
  - `q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe_vu32`
- Compact split writes `[BH, 60, 64]` Q/K/V instead of `[BH, 64, 64]`.
- Compact pipeline consumes those tensors with
  `freq_attention60_cutile_masked_kernel<16>`.
- Reference for pipeline correctness remains the pad64 path:
  `rotary split freq60->pad64 + q16 padded attention`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8
for v in qkv_rot_split qkv_rot_compact_split qkv_rot_compact_split_vu32 q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe_vu32; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 5 --iters 20 --compare-baseline
done
for v in qkv_rot_split qkv_rot_compact_split qkv_rot_compact_split_vu32 q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe_vu32; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
done
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt > /tmp/freq_qkv_rot_compact.sass
```

Correctness:

```text
compact split scalar/VU32:
  Q/K/V max_abs=0 rms=0

compact pipeline vs pad64 pipeline:
  output max_abs=0.001953125
  output rms=0.000408297368
  output rel_rms=0.001616854
```

The compact pipeline difference is BF16-sized and comes from using the masked
attention kernel rather than the partition-view padded kernel. It is not a
split correctness issue; compact split itself is bit-identical against its
scalar reference.

Timing:

```text
short 5 warmup / 20 iters:
qkv_rot_split                  best=0.773 ms median=0.776 ms 597.2 GiB/s
qkv_rot_compact_split          best=0.744 ms median=0.747 ms 600.1 GiB/s
qkv_rot_compact_split_vu32     best=0.748 ms median=0.751 ms 597.0 GiB/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe                best=1.559 ms median=1.563 ms 6.152 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe        best=2.733 ms median=2.736 ms 3.509 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe_vu32   best=2.731 ms median=2.736 ms 3.513 TF/s

stable 30 warmup / 200 iters:
qkv_rot_split                  best=0.771 ms median=0.776 ms 598.7 GiB/s
qkv_rot_compact_split          best=0.744 ms median=0.747 ms 600.5 GiB/s
qkv_rot_compact_split_vu32     best=0.745 ms median=0.750 ms 599.5 GiB/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe                best=1.577 ms median=1.584 ms 6.083 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe        best=2.725 ms median=2.730 ms 3.520 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_compact_pipe_vu32   best=2.728 ms median=2.732 ms 3.516 TF/s
```

Resource:

```text
split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_pad64_kernel<false>:
  REG=38 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_compact_kernel<false>:
  REG=45 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_compact_kernel<true>:
  REG=40 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false,false,false>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_cutile_masked_kernel<16>:
  REG=141 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
```

SASS instruction counts:

```text
pad64_split:
  instr=208 HMMA=0 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=5 FFMA=8 FMUL=8
  IMAD=42 IADD3=25 PRMT=19 SHF=15 LOP3=15 BAR=3

compact_scalar:
  instr=256 HMMA=0 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=5 FFMA=8 FMUL=8
  IMAD=73 IADD3=28 PRMT=19 SHF=26 LOP3=17 BAR=3

compact_vu32:
  instr=248 HMMA=0 LDG=14 STG=10 STG_U16=8 F2F=4 FFMA=8 FMUL=8
  IMAD=78 IADD3=29 PRMT=16 SHF=20 LOP3=17 BAR=2

attn_padded_q16:
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=8 FFMA=92 FADD=32 FMUL=24 IMAD=185 IADD3=42 PRMT=72
  SHF=71 LOP3=102 BAR=16

attn_masked_q16:
  instr=1592 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=70 LDS=26 STS=41 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=8 FFMA=92 FADD=36 FMUL=24 IMAD=188 IADD3=115 PRMT=140
  SHF=94 LOP3=188 BAR=15
```

Interpretation:

- Compact split alone is a small positive result: it avoids writing four padded
  rows per BH and improves split time from about `0.771 ms` to `0.744 ms`.
- That gain is completely overwhelmed when the consumer changes from padded
  partition-view attention to masked compact attention.
- The reason is visible in resource/SASS evidence:
  - padded q16 attention: `REG=92`, `instr=1008`, `LDG=9`, `PRMT=72`;
  - masked q16 attention: `REG=141`, `instr=1592`, `LDG=70`, `PRMT=140`.
- Both attention kernels still issue `HMMA=16` and `MUFU=18`; the compact
  consumer is slower because address/mask/load/permute work grows, not because
  Tensor Core disappeared.

Decision:

- Keep compact split/pipeline as evidence.
- Do not replace the pad64 source path with compact split + masked attention.
- Padding to 64 is not just waste. For this CUDA Tile attention, pad64 enables a
  simpler partition-view layout that dramatically lowers non-HMMA overhead.
- The next layout experiment should preserve the attention-friendly 64-wide
  tile shape while reducing global writes elsewhere, for example:
  - write a pad64-compatible staged layout but avoid per-element masked compact
    addressing in the consumer;
  - fuse split with an attention kernel that stages one BH block into an
    attention-friendly 64 layout in shared/tile memory once;
  - specialize a compact attention consumer until its SASS approaches the
    padded path (`REG~92`, `LDG~9`, `PRMT~72`) before considering source
    promotion.

### CUDA Tile Freq Rotary Valid-Only Pad64 Split + Padded Attention

Goal:

- Keep the attention-friendly `[BH, 64, 64]` padded layout from the successful
  q16 padded attention path.
- Avoid doing useless split/rotary work for rows `60..63`.
- Test whether a split kernel that writes only valid `N=60` rows into a pad64
  stride can keep padded attention fast while reducing split cost.

Implementation:

- Added bench-only variants:
  - `qkv_rot_validpad_split`
  - `qkv_rot_validpad_split_vu32`
  - `q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe`
  - `q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe_vu32`
- The validpad split writes `[BH, 64, 64]` stride but its launch total covers
  only `[BH, 60, 64]` elements.
- The pipeline initializes Q/K/V pad64 buffers with `cudaMemset` outside the
  timed region and then times only:
  `validpad rotary split + q16 padded attention`.
- This is a steady-state bench. Source promotion must still prove pad-row
  lifetime is safe in the real inference buffer reuse path.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8
for v in qkv_rot_split qkv_rot_validpad_split qkv_rot_validpad_split_vu32 q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe_vu32; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 5 --iters 20 --compare-baseline
done
for v in qkv_rot_split qkv_rot_validpad_split qkv_rot_validpad_split_vu32 q16qkv_rot_pipe q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe_vu32; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
done
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile | c++filt > /tmp/freq_qkv_rot_validpad.sass
```

Correctness:

```text
validpad split scalar/VU32 vs validpad scalar reference:
  Q/K/V max_abs=0 rms=0

validpad pipeline vs pad64 pipeline:
  output max_abs=0 rms=0
```

Timing:

```text
short 5 warmup / 20 iters:
qkv_rot_split                  best=0.772 ms median=0.775 ms 597.6 GiB/s
qkv_rot_validpad_split         best=0.744 ms median=0.748 ms 600.5 GiB/s
qkv_rot_validpad_split_vu32    best=0.748 ms median=0.752 ms 597.0 GiB/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe                best=1.558 ms median=1.564 ms 6.156 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe       best=1.528 ms median=1.538 ms 6.276 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe_vu32  best=1.533 ms median=1.542 ms 6.257 TF/s

stable 30 warmup / 200 iters:
qkv_rot_split                  best=0.771 ms median=0.775 ms 598.7 GiB/s
qkv_rot_validpad_split         best=0.743 ms median=0.747 ms 600.9 GiB/s
qkv_rot_validpad_split_vu32    best=0.747 ms median=0.751 ms 598.2 GiB/s
q16qkv_rot_pipe                best=1.577 ms median=1.584 ms 6.081 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe       best=1.560 ms median=1.569 ms 6.150 TF/s
q16qkv_rot_validpad_pipe_vu32  best=1.552 ms median=1.565 ms 6.179 TF/s
```

Resource:

```text
split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_pad64_kernel<false>:
  REG=38 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_validpad_kernel<false>:
  REG=44 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_validpad_kernel<true>:
  REG=39 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false,false,false>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
```

SASS instruction counts:

```text
pad64_split:
  instr=208 HMMA=0 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=5 FFMA=8 FMUL=8
  IMAD=42 IADD3=25 PRMT=19 SHF=15 LOP3=15 BAR=3

validpad_scalar:
  instr=248 HMMA=0 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=5 FFMA=8 FMUL=8
  IMAD=72 IADD3=30 PRMT=19 SHF=22 LOP3=15 BAR=3

validpad_vu32:
  instr=240 HMMA=0 LDG=14 STG=10 STG_U16=8 F2F=4 FFMA=8 FMUL=8
  IMAD=79 IADD3=31 PRMT=16 SHF=17 LOP3=15 BAR=2

attn_padded_q16:
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=9 LDS=26 STS=28 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=8 FFMA=92 FADD=32 FMUL=24 IMAD=185 IADD3=42 PRMT=72
  SHF=71 LOP3=102 BAR=16
```

Interpretation:

- This is the first positive result from the layout experiments after the
  direct-fusion failures.
- The validpad split kernel has more static SASS instructions than the original
  pad64 split, because its row indexing is less trivial. However, it launches
  `78060` blocks instead of `83264` and avoids writing the four padded rows per
  BH. Runtime improves from about `0.771 ms` to `0.743 ms`.
- Unlike compact split + masked attention, this keeps the fast padded attention
  consumer unchanged (`REG=92`, `HMMA=16`, `LDG=9`, `PRMT=72`).
- The full pipeline improves from `1.577 ms` to `1.560 ms` for scalar and
  `1.552 ms` for VU32 in the stable run, with bit-identical output.
- VU32 again has better SASS for V movement, but runtime is close enough that it
  should be treated as a minor variant, not the main win. The main win is
  skipping pad-row split work while preserving the 64-wide attention layout.

Decision:

- Keep validpad split/pipeline as a source-facing positive bench.
- This is a candidate for source promotion, but only after checking real
  inference buffer lifetime:
  - pad rows must be initialized or provably irrelevant for all consumers;
  - no later kernel may read Q/K/V rows `60..63` without applying the attention
    validity mask;
  - the source change should avoid adding another large template instance if it
    can replace the current freq split specialization.
- If promoted, prefer the scalar validpad form first; gate VU32 separately only
  if source compile time and real inference timing show a repeatable win.

### Source Check: Existing Freq Split Skip-QK-Pad-Zero Gate

Goal:

- Check whether the bench-positive valid-only pad64 split is already represented
  in the real inference path.
- Validate the existing source opt-in gate:

```text
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO=1
```

Source facts:

- `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu` already has
  `freq_split_skip_qk_pad_zero_enabled()`.
- When enabled in freq attention shape `[B,60,3*H*D]`, it launches:
  - `split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_to_pad64_kernel`
    over only valid 60 rows;
  - `freq60_v_pad_rows_zero_kernel` for V pad rows;
  - `freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,true>`.
- When disabled, it launches:
  - `split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64_kernel`
    over pad64 rows;
  - `freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false>`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8
./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/validpad_default_clean --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/validpad_gate_clean --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test.wav --output outputs/validpad_default_test --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test.wav --output outputs/validpad_gate_test --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt > /tmp/cudasep_validpad_source.sass
```

CLI timing:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  default: 911.5 ms
  gate-on: 910.6 ms

test.wav:
  default: 5360.7 ms
  gate-on: 5374.6 ms
```

Output difference:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  Vocals       max_abs=1.78813934e-07 rms=9.96801161e-09 rel_rms=5.42175172e-08
  Instrumental max_abs=2.98023224e-07 rms=1.30908017e-08 rel_rms=5.43818773e-08

test.wav:
  Vocals       max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.025411e-08   rel_rms=5.42449807e-08
  Instrumental max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.04375719e-08 rel_rms=5.44434599e-08
```

Resource:

```text
split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64_kernel<bf16>:
  REG=40 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_to_pad64_kernel<bf16>:
  REG=38 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

freq60_v_pad_rows_zero_kernel:
  REG=30 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,true>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
```

SASS instruction counts:

```text
pad64_bf16:
  instr=536 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=15 FFMA=8 FMUL=10
  IMAD=176 IADD3=71 PRMT=38 SHF=24 LOP3=16 BAR=3

to_pad64_bf16:
  instr=536 LDG=16 STG=12 STG_U16=12 F2F=14 FFMA=8 FMUL=8
  IMAD=202 IADD3=78 PRMT=35 SHF=22 LOP3=12 BAR=3

v_pad_zero:
  instr=248 LDG=0 STG=2 STG_U16=2 F2F=1 IMAD=101 IADD3=34 PRMT=1
  SHF=5 LOP3=10

attn_false:
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=9 STS=28 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=8 FFMA=92 IMAD=185 PRMT=72

attn_true:
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 LDG=9 STS=28 STG=8 STG_U16=8
  F2F=8 FFMA=84 IMAD=185 PRMT=72
```

Interpretation:

- The source gate is numerically safe on both validation WAVs. Output difference
  is only float WAV roundoff scale.
- Runtime is not a repeatable win in real inference:
  - tiny win on `test_clean.wav` (`0.9 ms`);
  - regression on `test.wav` (`13.9 ms`).
- The source implementation does not exactly match the bench-positive steady
  state. It adds a separate `freq60_v_pad_rows_zero_kernel` launch and store
  path, while the bench validpad pipeline initialized pad rows outside the timed
  region.
- The source split itself has similar static instruction count to the full
  pad64 split (`536` vs `536`) and more address math (`IMAD=202` vs `176`).
  Its only expected gain is fewer launched elements. The extra V zero kernel can
  erase that gain.

Decision:

- Do not enable `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO` by default yet.
- Keep it as a safe opt-in source experiment.
- Next source-facing experiment should target the gap between bench and source:
  prove V pad rows are unnecessary for the padded attention consumer, or
  initialize/reuse pad rows outside the timed path so the separate V zero kernel
  disappears. Only then retest default promotion.

### Source Check: Skip V Pad Zero Under Existing Validpad Gate

Goal:

- Test the missing source-side piece from the validpad bench:
  remove the extra `freq60_v_pad_rows_zero_kernel` launch from the existing
  valid-only split gate.
- Keep it opt-in and off by default.

Implementation:

- Added:

```text
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_V_PAD_ZERO=1
```

- It is only active when the existing gate is also active:

```text
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO=1
```

- Default behavior is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8
./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/skipv_default_clean --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/skipv_qk_clean --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO=1 CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_V_PAD_ZERO=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/skipv_qkv_clean --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_QK_PAD_ZERO=1 CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_V_PAD_ZERO=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm --input test.wav --output outputs/skipv_qkv_test --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Timing:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  default:       912.5 ms
  skipQK:        915.5 ms
  skipQK+skipV:  912.7 ms

test.wav:
  previous default same build family: 5360.7 ms
  previous skipQK:                    5374.6 ms
  skipQK+skipV:                       5375.8 ms
```

Output difference:

```text
test_clean.wav vs default:
  skipQK Vocals       max_abs=1.78813934e-07 rms=9.95630195e-09 rel_rms=5.41538265e-08
  skipQK Instrumental max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.3168086e-08  rel_rms=5.4702932e-08

  skipQK+skipV Vocals       max_abs=1.78813934e-07 rms=9.97440631e-09 rel_rms=5.42522989e-08
  skipQK+skipV Instrumental max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.30929837e-08 rel_rms=5.43909415e-08

test.wav vs default:
  skipQK+skipV Vocals       max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.02556374e-08 rel_rms=5.42530606e-08
  skipQK+skipV Instrumental max_abs=1.78813934e-07 rms=1.04548856e-08 rel_rms=5.45337702e-08
```

Interpretation:

- Skipping V pad zero is numerically safe for both validation WAVs.
- It does not produce a reliable end-to-end speedup:
  - on `test_clean.wav`, it recovers the skipQK regression but only ties the
    default path;
  - on `test.wav`, it remains slower than the previous default run.
- This confirms the freq validpad optimization is too small relative to full
  inference noise and remaining larger kernels. The bench-positive effect is
  real locally, but current source-level end-to-end impact is not worth default
  promotion.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FREQ_SPLIT_SKIP_V_PAD_ZERO=1` as an opt-in experiment.
- Do not default-enable skipQK or skipV.
- Move the main optimization focus away from freq split pad rows and toward
  larger CUDA Tile stages with higher non-HMMA overhead or more direct Tensor
  Core utilization potential.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 source-matching GELU degree sweep

Goal:

- Explain part of the remaining FFN12 gap to the local A10G dense BF16
  reference without changing the Tensor Core dataflow.
- Keep the current source-matching CUDA Tile schedule fixed:
  `M32`, pair-H32, `TK64`, W1/W2 `latency=2`, and output accumulator
  no-round.
- Vary only the GELU formula so the result is not confounded with a different
  scheduling hint or output policy.

Bench implementation:

- Added candidates-only variants:
  - `fused_h32_poly5_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`
  - `fused_h32_poly7_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`
  - `fused_h32_poly9_tinyblend_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`
  - `fused_h32_hard_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`
  - `fused_h32_quick_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`
  - `fused_h32_identity_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`
- Default inference behavior is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_poly5_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_poly7_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_poly9_tinyblend_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_hard_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_quick_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_identity_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300
done
```

Timing on A10G:

```text
poly9 baseline:
  best=1.793 ms, median=1.798 ms
  useful=45.64 TF/s, roof70=65.2%, checksum=4080.0000

poly5:
  best=1.772 ms, median=1.776 ms
  useful=46.18 TF/s, roof70=66.0%, checksum=4080.0000

poly7:
  best=1.687 ms, median=1.700 ms
  useful=48.52 TF/s, roof70=69.3%, checksum=4080.0000

poly9 tinyblend:
  best=1.774 ms, median=1.784 ms
  useful=46.13 TF/s, roof70=65.9%, checksum=4080.0000

hard:
  best=1.839 ms, median=1.865 ms
  useful=44.51 TF/s, roof70=63.6%, checksum=4080.0000

quick:
  best=2.064 ms, median=2.071 ms
  useful=39.65 TF/s, roof70=56.6%, checksum=4080.0000

identity:
  best=1.815 ms, median=1.873 ms
  useful=45.08 TF/s, roof70=64.4%, checksum=4080.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
poly9:     REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
poly5:     REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
poly7:     REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
tinyblend: REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
hard:      REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
quick:     REG=229 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
identity:  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
poly9:
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=96 STS=44 STG=8
  FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=35 DEPBAR=29
  IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 SHF=180 LOP3=142 R2UR=18 BRA=3

poly5:
  instr=2472 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=96 STS=44 STG=8
  FFMA=128 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=35 DEPBAR=30
  IMAD=320 IADD3=172 PRMT=50 SHF=155 LOP3=168 BRA=3

poly7:
  instr=2528 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=96 STS=44 STG=8
  FFMA=192 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=35 DEPBAR=25
  IMAD=282 IADD3=171 PRMT=48 SHF=171 LOP3=165 BRA=3

tinyblend:
  instr=2648 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=96 STS=44 STG=8
  FFMA=320 FADD=192 FMUL=128 BAR=35 DEPBAR=30
  IMAD=220 IADD3=161 PRMT=16 SHF=178 LOP3=145 R2UR=18 BRA=3

hard:
  instr=2248 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=96 STS=44 STG=8
  FFMA=32 FADD=128 FMUL=64 BAR=35 DEPBAR=30
  IMAD=441 IADD3=159 PRMT=60 SHF=114 LOP3=160 R2UR=1 BRA=3

quick:
  instr=2936 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=96 STS=44 STG=8
  FFMA=300 FADD=162 FMUL=96 MUFU=66 BAR=35 DEPBAR=30
  IMAD=359 IADD3=218 PRMT=58 SHF=202 LOP3=217 BRA=51

identity:
  instr=1936 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=96 STS=44 STG=8
  FADD=96 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=416 IADD3=131 PRMT=28 SHF=101 LOP3=146 R2UR=10 BRA=3
```

Source opt-in check:

```bash
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_poly9_serial_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_POLY7_GELU=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_poly7_serial_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Serial CLI timing:

```text
poly9 default: 922.7 ms
poly7 opt-in:  915.0 ms
```

Output difference, poly7 versus poly9 default:

```text
Vocals.wav:
  max_abs=0.0129804611 rms=0.000947595632
  rel_rms=0.00515411523 snr=45.757 dB

Instrumental.wav:
  max_abs=0.01195544 rms=0.00106645946
  rel_rms=0.00443029151 snr=47.071 dB
```

Serial Nsight kernel isolation:

```text
poly9 FFN12 main:
  total=177.431 ms / 96 calls, avg=1848.242 us

poly7 FFN12 main:
  total=175.140 ms / 96 calls, avg=1824.373 us
```

Interpretation:

- Poly7 is the best same-schedule candidate in the microbench. It keeps the
  full `HMMA=256` Tensor Core body and reduces GELU polynomial work
  (`FFMA=256 -> 192`) without falling into the codegen cliffs seen with
  full-BF16 GELU.
- "Simpler formula" is not automatically faster. `quick` has lower register
  use (`REG=229`) but introduces `MUFU=66` and `BRA=51`, and drops to
  `39.65 TF/s`. `identity` removes most floating epilogue work but increases
  scalar/index work (`IMAD=416`) and is also slower than poly9.
- Source-level FFN12 isolation shows only a small improvement
  (`177.431 -> 175.140 ms`, about `1.3%` for the FFN12 main kernel). End-to-end
  timing is near noise level because other large CUDA Tile kernels still
  dominate.
- The output difference is much larger than prior BF16-roundoff-only toggles
  (`rel_rms` about `0.4-0.5%`, `SNR` about `46-47 dB`). This is acceptable as
  an experiment but not enough evidence to change the default approximation.
- The useful FFN12 direction is now clearer: preserve `HMMA=256`, avoid
  `MUFU`/branch-heavy GELU forms, and search approximations/schedules that
  reduce FFMA without increasing scalar address/control pressure.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_POLY7_GELU=1` as an opt-in experiment.
- Do not make poly7 the default from this evidence alone.
- Future FFN12 work should target a new GELU polynomial/schedule family around
  the poly7 shape, or move to the next larger bottleneck (`time_attention`) if
  source-level FFN12 gains remain too small.

### 2026-06-05 - Time QKV/rotary BF16 trig dispatch closure

Goal:

- Continue the CUDA Tile-first BF16 path cleanup by removing remaining forced
  BF16-to-FP32 rotary-frequency conversions in time-QKV helper paths.
- Keep the attention main loop and known-good `QRows=64,KTile=32` schedule
  unchanged.

Source changes:

- `split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_time1301_d64_kernel` now templates its
  `cos/sin` pointers on `TrigT` and dispatches either `float` or
  `__nv_bfloat16`.
- `qkv_time_rotary_pair_split_contig_static_full_kernel` and its tail rotary
  pass now also dispatch on BF16/F32 trig tensors.
- This is a source cleanup for BF16 purity and conversion removal; it is not a
  new attention-shape or Tensor Core scheduling change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/trig_bf16_default_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_PAIR_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/trig_bf16_pairrot_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_PAIR_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1 \
CUDASEP_DISABLE_ROTARY_FREQS_BF16=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/trig_f32_pairrot_serial_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt | \
  rg -n -A5 -B2 "split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_time1301_d64_kernel|qkv_time_rotary_pair_split_contig_static_full_kernel|rotary_time_split_contig_tail_inplace_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt | \
  rg -n "split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_time1301_d64_kernel|qkv_time_rotary_pair_split_contig_static_full_kernel|rotary_time_split_contig_tail_inplace_kernel"

git diff --check
```

Validation:

```text
Build:
  cudasep_infer OK

Default BF16 smoke, test_clean.wav:
  completed, 1892.5 ms

Pair rotary producer BF16 trig smoke, test_clean.wav:
  completed, 2152.8 ms

Pair rotary producer F32 trig rollback smoke, test_clean.wav:
  completed, 2246.0 ms

git diff --check:
  OK

src LOC:
  15936 total
```

Timing note:

- The `1892.5/2152.8/2246.0 ms` timings above were collected while multiple
  inference processes were running concurrently, so they are smoke-test
  timings only, not source-level performance conclusions.

Resource/SASS evidence:

```text
qkv_time_rotary_pair_split_contig_static_full_kernel<float>:
  REG=110 STACK=0 SHARED=65560

qkv_time_rotary_pair_split_contig_static_full_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=108 STACK=0 SHARED=65560

rotary_time_split_contig_tail_inplace_kernel<float>:
  REG=29 STACK=0 SHARED=0

rotary_time_split_contig_tail_inplace_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=29 STACK=0 SHARED=0

split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_time1301_d64_kernel<float>:
  REG=37 STACK=0 SHARED=0

split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_time1301_d64_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=37 STACK=0 SHARED=0
```

SASS symbol check:

```text
qkv_time_rotary_pair_split_contig_static_full_kernel<..., float>
qkv_time_rotary_pair_split_contig_static_full_kernel<..., __nv_bfloat16>
rotary_time_split_contig_tail_inplace_kernel<float>
rotary_time_split_contig_tail_inplace_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>
split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_time1301_d64_kernel<float>
split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_time1301_d64_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>
```

Output difference:

```text
Pair rotary producer BF16 trig vs default BF16 path:
  Vocals.wav:
    max_abs=1.78813934e-07 rms=1.00771e-08
    rel_rms=5.48108655e-08 snr=145.223 dB
  Instrumental.wav:
    max_abs=2.98023224e-07 rms=1.3213581e-08
    rel_rms=5.48919274e-08 snr=145.210 dB

Pair rotary producer F32 trig rollback vs BF16 trig:
  Vocals.wav:
    max_abs=0.00805529952 rms=0.000861156895
    rel_rms=0.00468396212 snr=46.588 dB
  Instrumental.wav:
    max_abs=0.0104619265 rms=0.00100107164
    rel_rms=0.00415865708 snr=47.621 dB
```

Interpretation:

- The new BF16/F32 template instances are present in the binary and visible in
  SASS/resource output, so this is not only a host-side branch change.
- The BF16 trig pair-producer output is effectively identical to the current
  default BF16 path (`rel_rms ~= 5.5e-8`), while forcing F32 rotary freqs gives
  the expected BF16-rotary difference (`rel_rms ~= 0.42%-0.47%`).
- This closes another BF16 conversion gap in the time QKV/rotary helpers
  without changing the hot time-attention MMA schedule.

Decision:

- Keep the BF16/F32 trig dispatch in source.
- Do not infer performance from the concurrent smoke timings.
- Next performance work should still target structural fusion around
  QKV/rotary/attention global-memory round trips or the remaining large
  FFN/time-attention kernels, not this small dispatch cleanup.

### 2026-06-05 - Generic/row rotary BF16 trig fallback closure

Goal:

- Continue closing BF16/FP32 conversion gaps in the CUDA Tile-first path.
- Extend the BF16/F32 rotary-freq template dispatch beyond the fixed time/freq
  producers to the generic BF16 QKV split fallback and the opt-in row-tile
  rotary experiment.

Source changes:

- `split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_kernel` is now templated on `TrigT`, so
  BF16 `cos/sin` can be read directly instead of first materializing FP32
  buffers.
- `rotary_time_split_contig_row_tile_inplace_kernel` is now templated on
  `TrigT`, with host dispatch for BF16 and FP32 trig inputs.
- This is not promoted as a speed path. It closes fallback/experiment coverage
  for full-BF16 arithmetic experiments.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/generic_trig_bf16_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ROTARY_ROW_TILE=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/row_trig_bf16_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_QKV_SPLIT_CONTIG_PATH=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/generic_split_trig_bf16_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/generic_trig_bf16_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Validation:

```text
build: pass
test_clean.wav default BF16: pass, 908.4 ms
test_clean.wav with CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ROTARY_ROW_TILE=1: pass, 918.8 ms
test_clean.wav with CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_QKV_SPLIT_CONTIG_PATH=1: pass, 924.4 ms
test.wav default BF16: pass, 5376.2 ms
forbidden library/raw-WMMA scan: no hits
```

Source LOC:

```text
before this cleanup: 15936 total
after this cleanup:  15971 total
```

Resource evidence:

```text
rotary_time_split_contig_row_tile_inplace_kernel<float>:
  REG=32 STACK=0 SHARED=0

rotary_time_split_contig_row_tile_inplace_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=32 STACK=0 SHARED=0

split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_kernel<float>:
  REG=42 STACK=0 SHARED=0

split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=42 STACK=0 SHARED=0
```

SASS symbol check:

```text
rotary_time_split_contig_row_tile_inplace_kernel<float>
rotary_time_split_contig_row_tile_inplace_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>
split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_kernel<float>
split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>
```

Interpretation:

- The new BF16 trig variants are present in the binary and have identical
  register counts to their FP32 trig variants.
- Default fixed-shape inference still runs.
- The opt-in row-tile experiment and the forced generic split-contig fallback
  both run with BF16 trig dispatch, so future all-BF16 experiments no longer
  silently depend on these rotary trig FP32 buffer conversions.
- This is coverage work for the CUDA Tile/BF16 objective, not a measured
  performance win.

### 2026-06-05 - Mask scatter bands-per-frequency BF16 denominator

Goal:

- Remove another small FP32 model-internal buffer from the default BF16 path.
- `num_bands_per_freq_gpu_` is the denominator used by mask scatter when
  averaging overlapping mel bands. It was always materialized as FP32 even when
  masks/gates/model activations were BF16.

Source changes:

- Added `mbr_tile::bands_per_freq_bf16_enabled()` with rollback flag
  `CUDASEP_DISABLE_BANDS_PER_FREQ_BF16=1`.
- `MelBandRoformer::load()` now converts `num_bands_per_freq_gpu_` to BF16 by
  default under `--bf16`.
- `apply_mask_and_scatter_kernel` and `apply_mask_and_scatter_bf16_kernel` are
  templated on the denominator type and dispatch both FP32 and BF16
  `bands_per_freq` inputs.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/bands_bf16_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_DISABLE_BANDS_PER_FREQ_BF16=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/bands_f32_rollback_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/bands_bf16_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Validation:

```text
build: pass
test_clean.wav default BF16 denominator: pass, 911.0 ms
test_clean.wav FP32 denominator rollback: pass, 910.9 ms
test.wav default BF16 denominator: pass, 5375.6 ms
```

Resource evidence:

```text
apply_mask_and_scatter_kernel<float>:
  REG=68 STACK=0 SHARED=0

apply_mask_and_scatter_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=66 STACK=0 SHARED=0

apply_mask_and_scatter_bf16_kernel<float>:
  REG=66 STACK=0 SHARED=0

apply_mask_and_scatter_bf16_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=66 STACK=0 SHARED=0
```

SASS symbol check:

```text
apply_mask_and_scatter_kernel<float>
apply_mask_and_scatter_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>
apply_mask_and_scatter_bf16_kernel<float>
apply_mask_and_scatter_bf16_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>
```

Interpretation:

- This does not affect Tensor Core utilization because mask scatter is a
  memory/atomic/scatter path and still writes the final STFT-domain result in
  FP32 for iSTFT.
- It does close one more default BF16-path FP32 buffer and keeps a simple
  rollback for A/B testing.
- Timing is neutral at smoke-test scale, as expected for a tiny denominator
  table.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 CUDA Tile GELU scalar-cost probe

Goal:

- Continue the A10G CUDA Tile-first FFN12 investigation without remeasuring the
  dense BF16 roofline.
- Check whether replacing the current source-matching poly9 GELU approximation
  with cheaper approximations explains part of the gap between the fused CUDA
  Tile kernel and the ~70 TFLOP/s A10G dense BF16 reference.

Source change:

- Fixed the bench-only
  `fused_h32_odd5_l175_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround` registration so
  its `RoundOutAcc` template argument is actually `false`, matching the variant
  name and the poly7/poly9 out-no-round baseline.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_poly7_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_odd5_l175_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 3 --iters 12
done
```

Timing:

```text
poly9 out-no-round: best 1.799 ms, useful 45.50 TF/s, roof 65.0%
poly7 out-no-round: best 1.692 ms, useful 48.37 TF/s, roof 69.1%
odd5 out-no-round:  best 1.674 ms, useful 48.90 TF/s, roof 69.9%
```

Resource evidence for the target out-no-round instances:

```text
poly9: REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
poly7: REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
odd5:  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

SASS count for the target instances:

```text
poly9: HMMA=1792 LDSM=896 LDGSTS=560 LDG=1514 STG=72 FFMA=1792
       FADD=1504 FMUL=1120 FSEL=32 F2FP=664
poly7: HMMA=256  LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80  LDG=208  STG=8  FFMA=192
       FADD=192  FMUL=160  F2FP=64
odd5:  HMMA=256  LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80  LDG=208  STG=8  FFMA=64
       FADD=192  FMUL=160  F2FP=64
```

Interpretation:

- CUDA Tile is generating Tensor Core work for these FFN12 candidates; the
  relevant evidence is the `HMMA` SASS, not just the use of `ct::mma` in source.
- The cheaper odd5 GELU remains fastest after fixing the out-no-round template
  argument, but only improves poly7 by about 1.1% and poly9 by about 7.4%.
- The remaining gap to the dense BF16 reference is not explained by missing
  Tensor Core lowering. The stronger evidence points to non-MMA overhead:
  maximum register pressure (`REG=255`), one-block residency pressure from
  96 KiB shared memory, tile-space load/store traffic, index/address arithmetic,
  BF16 conversion stores, and scalar GELU/epilogue instructions.
- Next FFN12 experiments should therefore target resource shape, not another
  dense roofline check: reduce shared footprint below the one-CTA limit if
  possible, lower accumulator/live-tile pressure, try smaller output/hidden
  tiles that keep `HMMA` while improving residency, and only then consider
  promoting odd5 behind an inference opt-in for output-quality measurement.

### 2026-06-05 - Source opt-in: FFN12 odd5 GELU

Goal:

- Move the best bench-only odd5 GELU candidate into the real inference path as
  an opt-in experiment, then check whether the FFN12 microbenchmark win survives
  end-to-end inference and what output drift it causes.

Source changes:

- Added `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1`.
- Added `kGeluErfOdd5L175` to the FFN12 fused CUDA Tile dispatch.
- Added the same fast odd5 GELU spelling used by the benchmark to
  `src/mbr_ffn12_cutile.cu`.
- Default poly9 behavior is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
```
