```bash
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_odd5_default_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_odd5_opt_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_odd5_opt_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Validation:

```text
build: pass
test_clean.wav default poly9: 912.0 ms
test_clean.wav odd5 opt-in:  900.4 ms
test.wav default poly9:      5362.4 ms
test.wav odd5 opt-in:        5293.7 ms
```

Output difference, odd5 opt-in versus default poly9 on `test_clean.wav`:

```text
Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.131668024 rms=0.00782591150 rel_rms=0.0425663119 snr=27.419 dB
Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.143223479 rms=0.00821282886 rel_rms=0.0341177767 snr=29.340 dB
```

Output difference, odd5 opt-in versus default poly9 on `test.wav`:

```text
Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.119069606 rms=0.00464570921 rel_rms=0.0245761365 snr=32.190 dB
Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.138709854 rms=0.00512313498 rel_rms=0.0267228046 snr=31.462 dB
```

Interpretation:

- The bench speedup survives as a small end-to-end win on this run
  (`912.0 -> 900.4 ms` on `test_clean.wav`, `5362.4 -> 5293.7 ms` on
  `test.wav`, both about 1.3%).
- The output drift is much larger than BF16-roundoff-only toggles and also much
  larger than the earlier poly7 source experiment. This is expected: odd5 is a
  very cheap GELU approximation, not a numerically close source match.
- Keep odd5 as an opt-in experiment only. It is useful evidence that GELU scalar
  work is part of the FFN12 roofline gap, but it is not the right default answer
  for quality-preserving acceleration.
- The next FFN12 performance direction should not keep lowering GELU polynomial
  degree. It should change the CUDA Tile dataflow/resource shape while
  preserving a closer GELU approximation: reduce 96 KiB shared pressure, avoid
  `REG=255`, or split the W2/output accumulation so `HMMA` stays high without
  one-CTA residency.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 W2 accumulator BF16-round experiment

Goal:

- Test whether pushing FFN12 closer to a "full BF16" arithmetic path by
  rounding the W2/output accumulator after each W2 MMA can reduce resource
  pressure or expose a useful A10G CUDA Tile direction.

Implementation:

- Added bench-only
  `fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround_w2accbf16`.
- The CUDA Tile API does not support true BF16 accumulator MMA for BF16 inputs
  in this environment: `/usr/local/cuda/include/crt/cuda_tile.h` defines
  `medium_precision_mma_v` as BF16/TF32/float input with `float` accumulator.
  Therefore this variant keeps `ct::mma` on BF16 input / FP32 accumulator and
  immediately applies `bf16_round_if<RoundW2AccBF16>` after each W2 MMA.
- This is a deliberate stress test of the cost and numeric effect of forcing
  W2 accumulation back through BF16 between MMA steps. It is not promoted to
  the inference source path.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround_w2accbf16; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 3 --iters 12
done

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
  --variant fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround_fullbf16 \
  --warmup 3 --iters 12

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -n -A4 -B2 \
  "ffn12_fused_split2_pairh32_bf16_kernel<32, 6, 64, 32, true, false, true, true, true, true, false, long long, false, false, false, false, 2, 2, false, 2, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, (false|true)>"
```

Timing:

```text
baseline out-no-round:
  best 1.801 ms, median 1.804 ms, useful 45.44 TF/s, roof 64.9%, checksum 4080.0000
w2accbf16:
  best 3.324 ms, median 3.326 ms, useful 24.62 TF/s, roof 35.2%, checksum 4096.0000
fullbf16 epilogue reference:
  best 3.536 ms, median 3.538 ms, useful 23.14 TF/s, roof 33.1%, checksum 4080.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline out-no-round:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
w2accbf16:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count for the two target kernel instances:

```text
baseline: HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STG=8 FFMA=256 F2FP=64  PRMT=16 INT_ADDR=516
w2acc:   HMMA=128 LDSM=64  LDGSTS=40 LDG=136 STG=8 FFMA=128 F2FP=112 PRMT=94 INT_ADDR=610
```

Interpretation:

- This is not a useful performance direction. Rounding the W2 accumulator after
  every W2 MMA nearly halves useful throughput (`45.44 -> 24.62 TF/s`) while
  leaving the 96 KiB shared-memory footprint unchanged and reducing registers
  only trivially (`255 -> 254`).
- The SASS still contains `HMMA.16816.F32.BF16`, so the slowdown is not because
  Tensor Core lowering disappeared entirely. The generated code adds much more
  BF16 pack/permutation work (`F2FP`, `PRMT`) and a different static instruction
  shape, while preserving the same one-CTA shared-memory residency problem.
- The checksum changes (`4080 -> 4096`), so this also confirms that forcing W2
  accumulation through BF16 is not a harmless roundoff-only toggle.
- For A10G, the next FFN12 route should keep BF16 Tensor Core input with FP32
  accumulator, then reduce the real bottlenecks: 96 KiB shared memory,
  `REG=255`, scalar GELU/epilogue work, and tile-space load/store/addressing
  overhead. Trying to make BF16-input MMA "more BF16" by repeatedly rounding the
  accumulator is counterproductive with CUDA Tile 13.3 on this GPU.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 THidden=16 current-shape closure

Goal:

- Test whether shrinking the hidden tile from H32 to H16 can reduce the
  `REG=255` / 96 KiB shared-memory pressure while preserving the current
  source-matching policy: `TK64`, W1/W2 `latency=2`, and output accumulator
  no-round.

Implementation:

- Added candidates-only
  `fused_h16_poly9_split2_pairh16_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`.
- This reuses the existing pair-H32 template with `THidden=16`, so the only
  intended shape change is the hidden tile width. The output tile remains
  `M32 x 256`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h16_poly9_split2_pairh16_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 5 --iters 30
done
```

Timing:

```text
H32 current best:
  best 1.795 ms, median 1.800 ms, useful 45.59 TF/s, roof 65.1%
H16 current-shape closure:
  best 2.294 ms, median 2.298 ms, useful 35.68 TF/s, roof 51.0%
```

Resource usage:

```text
H32 current best:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
H16 current-shape closure:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=96256
```

Static SASS count:

```text
H32:
  instr=5076 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 F2FP=64 PRMT=16 IMAD=199
  IADD3=162 LOP3=145 SHF=201 BAR=35 DEPBAR=61 BRA=3

H16:
  instr=4964 HMMA=192 LDSM=120 LDGSTS=72 LDG=208 STS=126 STG=8
  FFMA=192 FADD=176 FMUL=120 F2FP=56 PRMT=97 IMAD=278
  IADD3=178 LOP3=200 SHF=172 BAR=32 DEPBAR=89 BRA=3
```

Interpretation:

- H16 does not solve the resource bottleneck. Register pressure stays at
  `REG=255`, stack stays at 8 bytes, and shared memory only drops by 2 KiB
  (`98304 -> 96256`), still in the same one-CTA residency regime.
- It also worsens the generated schedule: static `HMMA` drops from `256` to
  `192`, while scalar/permutation/synchronization pressure increases
  (`PRMT 16 -> 97`, `IMAD 199 -> 278`, `LOP3 145 -> 200`,
  `DEPBAR 61 -> 89`).
- The throughput loss (`65.1% -> 51.0%` of the 70 TF/s reference) is therefore
  not surprising. Smaller hidden tiles increase loop/scheduling overhead
  without enough occupancy/resource relief.

Decision:

- Close the H16 current-shape direction for FFN12 on A10G.
- The next resource-shape experiment should not simply shrink `THidden`.
  It needs to change the output accumulator/shared-memory footprint enough to
  break the 96 KiB one-CTA residency regime while preserving the H32-style
  compact `HMMA=256` schedule.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 TM16/H32 current-shape closure

Goal:

- Test whether shrinking the M tile from 32 to 16 reduces output accumulator
  pressure enough to help A10G, while keeping `THidden=32`, `TK64`, W1/W2
  `latency=2`, and output accumulator no-round.

Implementation:

- Added candidates-only
  `fused_m16_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround`.
- This reuses the pair-H32 CUDA Tile template with `TM=16`; only the row tile
  size changes.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_m16_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 5 --iters 30
done
```

Timing:

```text
M32/H32 current best:
  best 1.795 ms, median 1.801 ms, useful 45.60 TF/s, roof 65.1%
M16/H32 current-shape closure:
  best 2.906 ms, median 2.916 ms, useful 28.16 TF/s, roof 40.2%
```

Resource usage:

```text
M32/H32:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
M16/H32:
  REG=228 STACK=0 SHARED=100352
```

Static SASS count:

```text
M32/H32:
  instr=5076 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 F2FP=64 PRMT=16 IMAD=199
  IADD3=162 LOP3=145 SHF=201 BAR=35 DEPBAR=61 BRA=3

M16/H32:
  instr=3252 HMMA=128 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=72 LDG=152 STS=108 STG=4
  FFMA=128 FADD=96 FMUL=80 F2FP=32 PRMT=100 IMAD=172
  IADD3=137 LOP3=118 SHF=85 BAR=27 DEPBAR=59 BRA=3
```

Interpretation:

- TM16 lowers reported register pressure (`255 -> 228`) but fails the actual
  objective. Shared memory increases from `98304` to `100352`, so it moves in
  the wrong direction for breaking the one-CTA residency problem.
- It also cuts the static Tensor Core body in half (`HMMA=256 -> 128`) and
  raises permutation work sharply (`PRMT=16 -> 100`).
- The result is much slower (`65.1% -> 40.2%` of the 70 TF/s reference).
  Lower register count alone is not useful when the compiler emits a smaller
  and less efficient tile schedule.

Decision:

- Close the simple TM16/H32 current-shape direction for FFN12 on A10G.
- Viable next FFN12 resource work must preserve the M32/H32 `HMMA=256` shape or
  replace it with a different schedule that recovers equivalent Tensor Core
  issue density. Simply shrinking one tile dimension has now failed for both
  `THidden=16` and `TM=16`.

### 2026-06-05 - Generic QKV BKN A/B/store latency split diagnostic

Goal:

- Follow up the source-default generic QKV BKN producer
  `linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<32,256,16,78048,1536,256,...>`,
  which remains a top end-to-end hotspot at about `64-65 ms / 48 calls`.
- Test whether the already-promoted all-site `latency=2` hint can be reduced
  to a lighter A/B-load-only spelling, mirroring the split-contiguous QKV
  latency split diagnostics.
- Keep the same CUDA Tile shape, BF16 input/output, `ct::mma`, B(K,N) layout,
  and output tensor layout. This is a bench-only diagnostic.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The CUDA Tile guide describes `__tile_global__` kernels as block/tile-level
  programs where the compiler maps tile loads, `ct::mma`, and stores to the
  hardware.
- The CUDA Tile C++ API reference says `cutile::hint(... latency=...)` attaches
  to direct tile load/store call expressions and is an optimization hint, not a
  semantic guarantee. Therefore A-load, B-load, and store hints need separate
  resource/SASS/timing evidence.

Code:

- Generalized bench-only `qkv_bkn_static_tiled_latency_kernel` from one
  `Latency` template argument to independent `LoadLatency`, `BLoadLatency`,
  and `StoreLatency`.
- Added variants:
  - `qkv_t32x256x16bkn_a2_b2_s0`
  - `qkv_t32x256x16bkn_a2_b1_s0`

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm -j8

for v in \
  qkv_t32x256x16bkn \
  qkv_t32x256x16bkn_lat2 \
  qkv_t32x256x16bkn_a2_b2_s0 \
  qkv_t32x256x16bkn_a2_b1_s0; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm \
    --shape infer_qkv --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done

for round in 1 2 3; do
  for v in \
    qkv_t32x256x16bkn_lat2 \
    qkv_t32x256x16bkn_a2_b2_s0 \
    qkv_t32x256x16bkn_a2_b1_s0; do
    ./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm \
      --shape infer_qkv --variant "$v" --warmup 20 --iters 300
  done
done
```

Timing:

```text
500-iter first pass:
baseline:
  best=1.381 ms, median=1.394 ms, 44.44 TF/s, roof70=63.5%
all-site A2/B2/S2:
  best=1.284 ms, median=1.299 ms, 47.79 TF/s, roof70=68.3%
A2/B2/S0:
  best=1.250 ms, median=1.304 ms, 49.11 TF/s, roof70=70.2%
A2/B1/S0:
  best=1.255 ms, median=1.317 ms, 48.90 TF/s, roof70=69.9%

300-iter cross-check:
round 1:
  all-site A2/B2/S2 best=1.241 ms, median=1.293 ms, 49.46 TF/s
  A2/B2/S0          best=1.249 ms, median=1.307 ms, 49.16 TF/s
  A2/B1/S0          best=1.246 ms, median=1.319 ms, 49.25 TF/s
round 2:
  all-site A2/B2/S2 best=1.246 ms, median=1.305 ms, 49.27 TF/s
  A2/B2/S0          best=1.247 ms, median=1.308 ms, 49.22 TF/s
  A2/B1/S0          best=1.245 ms, median=1.317 ms, 49.29 TF/s
round 3:
  all-site A2/B2/S2 best=1.274 ms, median=1.315 ms, 48.17 TF/s
  A2/B2/S0          best=1.247 ms, median=1.316 ms, 49.22 TF/s
  A2/B1/S0          best=1.250 ms, median=1.316 ms, 49.10 TF/s
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline:        REG=170 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
all-site A2/B2/S2: REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
A2/B2/S0:        REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
A2/B1/S0:        REG=162 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline:
  instr=1872 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=61 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
all-site A2/B2/S2:
  instr=1984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
A2/B2/S0:
  instr=1984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
A2/B1/S0:
  instr=1888 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
```

Interpretation:

- Generic BKN behaves differently from the split-contiguous producer at the
  store site: `A2/B2/S2` and `A2/B2/S0` generate identical resources and static
  SASS. Removing the store hint is not a proven structural improvement here.
- `A2/B1/S0` lowers register count by one and reduces static `BAR` count back
  to the baseline level, but does not produce a stable timing win. The median is
  slightly worse than the current all-site `latency=2` family.
- The robust result remains the original low-latency A/B staging family:
  baseline `44.4 TF/s` rises to roughly `49 TF/s`, while all tested variants
  keep `HMMA=256` and the same `96KB` shared footprint.
- The remaining gap is not explained by the output store hint. It is still the
  high-shared-memory, one-CTA-residency BKN schedule plus CUDA Tile codegen
  around A/B staging.

Decision:

- Do not promote `A2/B2/S0` or `A2/B1/S0` into the source default.
- Keep the current source default on the simpler all-site `latency=2` generic
  QKV BKN path.
- Future work should move away from store-hint micro-variants and toward either
  reducing the `96KB` internal staging footprint without losing `TN256`
  Tensor Core density, or eliminating producer output traffic through a
  producer-consumer fusion that does not explode registers.
- Practical workflow note: `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` now takes several
  minutes to recompile for tiny variant changes. Splitting QKV/linear producer
  probes into smaller CUDA translation units would materially improve the
  shape-sweep loop for the long-running A10G CUDA Tile project.

### 2026-06-05 - Small QKV BKN bench target for faster shape sweeps

Goal:

- Remove a practical bottleneck in the CUDA Tile optimization loop: the
  all-in-one `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` translation unit takes several
  minutes to rebuild after small QKV producer edits.
- Add a small, source-relevant QKV BKN bench target that compiles only the
  `M=78060, N=1536, K=256`, `TM32 x TN256 x TK16` baseline and latency-hint
  variants.
- This does not change the inference path. It makes future A10G CUDA Tile shape
  and schedule experiments cheaper to run.

Code:

- Added `bench/bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu`.
- Added CMake target `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile`.
- Variants:
  - `baseline`
  - `lat1`
  - `lat2`
  - `lat4`
  - `a2_b2_s0`
  - `a2_b1_s0`

Build timing:

```bash
time ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8
```

```text
real 0m7.717s
```

This first build included CMake regeneration. The previous all-in-one bench
compile for two QKV variants took multiple minutes, so this target is the new
preferred entry point for QKV BKN producer micro-sweeps.

Bench command:

```bash
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant all --warmup 20 --iters 300
```

Timing:

```text
baseline:
  best=1.381 ms, median=1.394 ms, 44.45 TF/s, roof70=63.5%
lat1:
  best=1.271 ms, median=1.317 ms, 48.28 TF/s, roof70=69.0%
lat2:
  best=1.269 ms, median=1.307 ms, 48.36 TF/s, roof70=69.1%
lat4:
  best=1.425 ms, median=1.430 ms, 43.06 TF/s, roof70=61.5%
A2/B2/S0:
  best=1.251 ms, median=1.303 ms, 49.08 TF/s, roof70=70.1%
A2/B1/S0:
  best=1.287 ms, median=1.318 ms, 47.68 TF/s, roof70=68.1%
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline: REG=170 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
lat1:     REG=164 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
lat2:     REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
lat4:     REG=174 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
A2/B2/S0: REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
A2/B1/S0: REG=162 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline:
  instr=1872 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=61 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
lat1:
  instr=2000 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
lat2:
  instr=1984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
lat4:
  instr=1904 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=30 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
A2/B2/S0:
  instr=1984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
A2/B1/S0:
  instr=1888 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
```

Interpretation:

- The small target reproduces the large bench's key signals: baseline is about
  `44.4 TF/s`, low-latency A/B staging lifts the shape to about `48-49 TF/s`,
  `lat4` regresses, and all variants keep `HMMA=256`.
- The resource and SASS counts match the large bench diagnostics, so this target
  is valid for future QKV BKN micro-sweeps.
- This is an infrastructure improvement for the long-running CUDA Tile project:
  it does not claim any new inference speedup, but it makes finding the next
  source-level speedup materially faster.

Decision:

- Use `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile` for future generic QKV BKN producer
  experiments before promoting anything to `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`.
- Keep `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` for broader cross-shape comparisons only.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV BKN internal 2xTN128 compound probe

Goal:

- Test a structural shared-memory hypothesis for the generic QKV BKN producer:
  can one CTA still cover `TN256` output columns, but internally compute two
  `TN128` sub-tiles, reducing shared memory without doubling CTA count?
- Keep the external launch shape `grid=(2439,6)`, BF16 input/output, `ct::mma`,
  and total `HMMA=256` Tensor Core body.
- Use the small QKV BKN bench target so this kind of CUDA Tile shape probe
  rebuilds quickly.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The CUDA Tile programming guide requires tile kernels to launch with one CUDA
  thread per CTA and uses compile-time tile dimensions. The internal
  `32x128` tile shapes are legal powers of two, and the experiment still uses
  official tile views and `ct::mma`.
- Because CUDA Tile source structure affects generated resources and SASS, the
  decision must use timing plus `cuobjdump` resource/SASS evidence.

Code:

- Added bench-only variants to `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu`:
  - `compound2x128`
  - `compound2x128_lat2`
- Each CTA computes two adjacent `32x128` accumulator tiles and writes them as
  one logical `TN256` output block.

Build:

```bash
time ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8
```

```text
real 0m9.098s
```

Commands:

```bash
for v in baseline lat2 compound2x128 compound2x128_lat2; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 20 --iters 300
done
```

Timing:

```text
baseline:
  best=1.380 ms, median=1.393 ms, 44.47 TF/s, roof70=63.5%
lat2:
  best=1.244 ms, median=1.306 ms, 49.33 TF/s, roof70=70.5%
compound2x128:
  best=1.427 ms, median=1.442 ms, 43.00 TF/s, roof70=61.4%
compound2x128_lat2:
  best=1.299 ms, median=1.338 ms, 47.26 TF/s, roof70=67.5%
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline:              REG=170 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
lat2:                  REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
compound2x128:         REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=97280
compound2x128_lat2:    REG=188 STACK=0 SHARED=97280
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline:
  instr=1872 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=61 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
lat2:
  instr=1984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
compound2x128:
  instr=2064 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=44 DEPBAR=96 IMAD=73 IADD3=77 PRMT=0 SHF=67 LOP3=84 BRA=2
compound2x128_lat2:
  instr=2192 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=45 DEPBAR=96 IMAD=85 IADD3=77 PRMT=0 SHF=68 LOP3=90 BRA=2
```

Interpretation:

- The compound spelling preserves the total `HMMA=256` body and the same CTA
  count, but it does not meaningfully reduce the limiting shared footprint:
  `98304 -> 97280` bytes is only a `1KB` reduction.
- The cost is substantial. The non-latency compound version hits a register
  cliff (`REG=254`) and is slower than even the baseline. The latency-hinted
  compound version avoids the worst cliff but still raises `REG` from `163` to
  `188`, `DEPBAR` from `64` to `96`, `BAR` from `33` to `45`, and instruction
  count from `1984` to `2192`.
- Bottom-level cause: expressing two `32x128` sub-tiles inside one CTA does not
  make the CUDA Tile compiler allocate two lightweight independent schedules.
  It keeps almost the same shared staging and adds live accumulators,
  strided-view address work, and synchronization dependency overhead.

Decision:

- Do not promote `compound2x128` or `compound2x128_lat2`.
- Close the simple "internal split TN256 into two TN128 sub-tiles" direction.
- A useful shared-memory reduction must change the staging strategy itself, not
  just split the accumulator/source view spelling. The next QKV BKN direction
  should test a different CTA ownership model or producer-consumer fusion rather
  than this compound sub-tile form.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV BKN output epilogue lower-bound probes

Goal:

- Determine whether the generic QKV BKN producer is primarily limited by the
  output epilogue: BF16 conversion, full-tile BF16 stores, or output bandwidth.
- Keep the same `M32 x N256 x TK16`, `latency=2`, B(K,N) input layout, and
  Tensor Core body.
- Add two diagnostic-only variants:
  - `store_one_lat2`: computes the full accumulator tile, converts to BF16, but
    uses `ct::store_masked` so only one output element per CTA is written.
  - `store_f32_lat2`: writes the FP32 accumulator tile directly, avoiding BF16
    conversion but doubling output bytes.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The CUDA Tile API reference documents `store_masked` as an official tile view
  store form. An attempted `ct::gather` spelling failed to compile because CUDA
  Tile C++ 13.3 does not expose that API; the final diagnostic uses
  `ct::store_masked` instead.
- This reinforces that low-level source spelling must be checked against the
  official API and then judged by generated resource/SASS evidence.

Code:

- Added bench-only variants to `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu`:
  - `store_one_lat2`
  - `store_f32_lat2`

Build:

```bash
time ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8
```

```text
real 0m10.659s
```

Commands:

```bash
for v in baseline lat2 store_one_lat2 store_f32_lat2; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 20 --iters 300
done
```

Timing:

```text
baseline:
  best=1.381 ms, median=1.393 ms, 44.44 TF/s, roof70=63.5%
lat2:
  best=1.255 ms, median=1.308 ms, 48.90 TF/s, roof70=69.9%
store_one_lat2:
  best=2.391 ms, median=2.394 ms, 25.67 TF/s, roof70=36.7%
store_f32_lat2:
  best=1.554 ms, median=1.588 ms, 39.51 TF/s, roof70=56.4%
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline:        REG=170 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
lat2:            REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
store_one_lat2:  REG=138 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
store_f32_lat2:  REG=174 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline:
  instr=1872 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=61 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
lat2:
  instr=1984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=8
  F2F=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 SHF=61 LOP3=77 BRA=1
store_one_lat2:
  instr=1808 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=1
  F2F=32 BAR=17 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=39 IADD3=71 PRMT=34 SHF=58 LOP3=76 BRA=1
store_f32_lat2:
  instr=2016 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=16 STG=16
  F2F=0 BAR=29 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=168 IADD3=53 PRMT=2 SHF=39 LOP3=55 BRA=1
```

Interpretation:

- Output store count is not the simple limiter. `store_one_lat2` reduces `STG`
  from `8` to `1` and lowers `REG` to `138`, yet runtime collapses to
  `25.67 TF/s`. The masked pointer-tile epilogue changes codegen enough to
  introduce heavy permutation/control behavior (`PRMT=34`) and destroys the
  optimized structured-store schedule.
- BF16 conversion is not the main bottleneck either. `store_f32_lat2` removes
  `F2F=32`, but it doubles store instructions/bytes (`STG=16`, `STS=16`) and
  raises address/control work sharply (`IMAD=168`), dropping to `39.51 TF/s`.
- The current BF16 `partition_view.store(element_cast<bf16>(acc))` epilogue is
  already a good CUDA Tile codegen shape for this producer. The remaining QKV
  gap is dominated by the A/B staging and high shared-memory schedule, not by
  BF16 output conversion or raw output-store bandwidth.

Decision:

- Do not pursue `store_masked`/partial-store epilogues for the QKV producer.
- Do not pursue FP32 output as a performance path. It is slower and violates the
  BF16 dataflow objective anyway.
- The next useful QKV work should move to producer-consumer fusion or a true
  staging-layout change. Epilogue-only changes are now closed.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: Mask final linear + GLU epilogue fusion

Goal:

- Test whether the mask-estimator final `linear(..., N=48, K=1024)` followed by
  `glu_last_dim()` should be fused into one CUDA Tile kernel.
- The intended win is one fewer BF16 global-memory round trip and one fewer
  kernel launch for the `N=48 -> N=24` mask-output bands.

Implementation:

```text
CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_GLU_LAST_DIM_FUSED=1
```

The opt-in path adds:

```text
linear_glu_last_dim_static_masked_mn_bf16_kernel<32,32,64,1301,24,1024>
try_linear_glu_last_dim_bf16_output(...)
```

The kernel uses CUDA Tile `ct::mma` twice: one accumulator for the first half of
the final linear output and one accumulator for the gate half. It then applies
the GLU sigmoid epilogue and writes BF16 `[B,T,N/2]`. No cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN,
raw WMMA, or inline asm path was introduced.

Build:

```text
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8: pass
git diff --check: pass
```

Plain timing on `test_clean.wav`:

```text
default: 1176.5 ms
opt-in:   912.4 ms
```

The plain timing had large run-to-run noise, so the decision uses same-binary
Nsight Systems A/B instead:

```text
default nsys infer: 924.5 ms
opt-in  nsys infer: 931.4 ms
```

Same-binary Nsight target kernel comparison:

```text
default total CUDA kernel time: 888.106 ms, calls=6506
  final N=48 linear:
    112 calls, 2.244 ms, avg=20.038 us, REG=148, SHARED=81920
  glu_last_dim_bf16_to_bf16:
    480 calls, 1.427 ms, avg=2.973 us, REG=26, SHARED=0

opt-in total CUDA kernel time: 888.260 ms, calls=6394
  linear_glu_last_dim:
    112 calls, 2.802 ms, avg=25.019 us, REG=143, SHARED=90112
  remaining glu_last_dim_bf16_to_bf16:
    368 calls, 1.208 ms, avg=3.282 us, REG=26, SHARED=0
```

Target segment:

```text
default replaced segment: 2.244 + 0.333 = 2.577 ms
opt-in fused segment:     2.802 ms
```

The `0.333 ms` default GLU component is the 112 calls removed by the opt-in
path, estimated from the same default profile's per-call average. Therefore the
fusion is about `0.225 ms` slower on the target segment and neutral/slightly
negative end to end.

Output difference, opt-in versus default on `test_clean.wav`:

```text
Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.002640754 rms=0.000312549 rel_rms=0.001700002
Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.005013533 rms=0.000593756 rel_rms=0.002466584
```

Resource usage:

```text
linear_glu_last_dim_static_masked_mn_bf16_kernel:
  REG=143 STACK=0 SHARED=90112
linear_cutile_static_masked_mn_bf16_kernel<N=48,K=1024>:
  REG=148 STACK=0 SHARED=81920
glu_last_dim_bf16_to_bf16_kernel:
  REG=26 STACK=0 SHARED=0
```

Static SASS count:

```text
linear_glu:
  instr_lines=4469 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=128 BAR=131 PRMT=47
  MUFU=18 F2F=28 IMAD=209 LDG=204 STG=1 DEPBAR=128 IADD3=166 LOP3=203

linear_n48:
  instr_lines=3621 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=128 BAR=114 PRMT=55
  MUFU=0 F2F=24 IMAD=143 LDG=188 STG=2 DEPBAR=96 IADD3=139 LOP3=152

glu_last_dim:
  instr_lines=1029 HMMA=0 LDSM=0 LDGSTS=0 BAR=0 PRMT=11
  MUFU=11 F2F=5 IMAD=165 LDG=4 STG=2 DEPBAR=0 IADD3=57 LOP3=29
```

Interpretation:

- The fusion does reduce launch count by `112` and removes those standalone GLU
  reads/writes, but it makes the Tensor Core producer heavier.
- HMMA count stays the same as the standalone final linear (`HMMA=256`), while
  the fused kernel increases shared memory (`81920 -> 90112`), barriers
  (`114 -> 131`), dependency barriers (`96 -> 128`), integer/address work
  (`IMAD=143 -> 209`), global loads (`188 -> 204`), and epilogue scalar math
  (`MUFU=0 -> 18`).
- On A10G this is exactly the fusion cliff seen in other high-pressure kernels:
  reducing global-memory boundaries is not enough if the fused producer becomes
  a worse CTA resource shape and HMMA schedule.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_GLU_LAST_DIM_FUSED=1` as an opt-in negative
  experiment only.
- Do not promote this GLU epilogue fusion to default.
- Future mask-output work should use a shape/layout redesign or consumer-side
  fusion that avoids raising the Tensor Core producer to `90KB` shared memory,
  not a simple “do more epilogue work in the same kernel” fusion.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: Mask final linear + GLU TK32 fused variant

Goal:

- Follow up on the negative `linear + GLU` fusion by testing whether a smaller
  `TK=32` fused kernel avoids the A10G shared-memory cliff.
- The previous `TK=64` fused kernel reduced launches but raised shared memory
  from `81920` to `90112` bytes and was slower than the default `linear + GLU`
  segment.

Implementation:

```text
CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_GLU_LAST_DIM_FUSED=1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_GLU_LAST_DIM_FUSED_TK32=1
```

Build and audits:

```text
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8: pass
git diff --check: pass
forbidden library/raw WMMA/asm scan: no matches
```

Resource usage:

```text
linear_glu TK64: REG=143 SHARED=90112
linear_glu TK32: REG=88  SHARED=26624
default N=48 linear: REG=148 SHARED=81920
```

Nsight Systems on `test_clean.wav`:

```text
default total CUDA kernel time: 888.106 ms, calls=6506
  final N=48 linear: 112 calls, 2.244 ms, avg=20.038 us
  glu_last_dim:      480 calls, 1.427 ms, avg=2.973 us

TK64 fused total CUDA kernel time: 888.260 ms, calls=6394
  linear_glu:        112 calls, 2.802 ms, avg=25.019 us
  remaining GLU:     368 calls, 1.208 ms, avg=3.282 us

TK32 fused total CUDA kernel time: 887.605 ms, calls=6394
  linear_glu:        112 calls, 2.810 ms, avg=25.087 us
  remaining GLU:     368 calls, 1.208 ms, avg=3.281 us
```

Plain profiled inference:

```text
TK32 fused nsys infer: 928.9 ms
```

Output difference, TK32 fused versus default on `test_clean.wav`:

```text
Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.002640724 rms=0.000312549 rel_rms=0.001700002
Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.005013525 rms=0.000593756 rel_rms=0.002466584
```

Static SASS count:

```text
linear_glu TK64:
  instr_lines=4469 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=128 BAR=131 PRMT=47
  MUFU=18 F2F=28 IMAD=209 LDG=204 STG=1 DEPBAR=128 IADD3=166 LOP3=203

linear_glu TK32:
  instr_lines=1701 HMMA=16 LDSM=12 LDGSTS=8 BAR=19 PRMT=50
  MUFU=18 F2F=28 IMAD=174 LDG=28 STG=1 DEPBAR=16 IADD3=52 LOP3=78

default N=48 linear:
  instr_lines=3621 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=128 BAR=114 PRMT=55
  MUFU=0 F2F=24 IMAD=143 LDG=188 STG=2 DEPBAR=96 IADD3=139 LOP3=152
```

Interpretation:

- The `TK32` fused variant succeeds at reducing static resource pressure:
  `SHARED=90112 -> 26624` and `REG=143 -> 88`.
- It does not become faster. The fused kernel remains about `25 us/call`,
  essentially tied with `TK64` and still slower than the default target segment.
- Bottom-level cause: the low-shared `TK32` shape loses Tensor Core density in
  CUDA Tile codegen (`HMMA=256 -> 16`). It avoids the shared-memory cliff but
  gives up the useful MMA schedule. The remaining runtime is then dominated by
  small-kernel launch/epilogue/sigmoid/addressing work rather than dense BF16
  Tensor Core throughput.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_GLU_LAST_DIM_FUSED_TK32=1` as an opt-in negative
  experiment only.
- Do not promote either `TK64` or `TK32` final-linear+GLU fusion.
- This closes the simple producer-epilogue fusion direction for mask final GLU.
  Further mask-estimator work should target broader dataflow changes, not this
  local epilogue fusion family.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV split-contig component/zcomponent retest

Goal:

- Continue the QKV BKN producer investigation using existing CUDA Tile
  structural variants before porting anything to the inference path.
- Test whether splitting Q/K/V by component, either as three launches or as a
  `z` grid dimension, reduces the current `M32 x N256 x TK16` split-contiguous
  producer bottleneck.

Commands:

```text
./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm --shape infer_qkv \
  --variant qkv_t32x256x16bkn_split_contig_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500
./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm --shape infer_qkv \
  --variant qkv_t32x256x16bkn_split_contig_zcomponents_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500
./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm --shape infer_qkv \
  --variant qkv_t32x256x16bkn_split_contig_components_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500
cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm | c++filt | \
  rg -A8 "qkv_bkn_static_tiled_split_contig"
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm | c++filt > /tmp/qkv_big.sass
```

Timing:

```text
infer_qkv M=78060 N=1536 K=256

split_contig_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6) fullM=78048
  best=1.250 ms median=1.268 ms 49.12 TF/s roof=70.2%

zcomponents_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,2,3) fullM=78048
  best=1.259 ms median=1.347 ms 48.75 TF/s roof=69.6%

components_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 component_grid=(2439,2) fullM=78048 launches=3
  best=1.320 ms median=1.353 ms 46.51 TF/s roof=66.4%
```

Resource usage:

```text
split_contig_lat2: REG=165 SHARED=98304
zcomponents_lat2:  REG=163 SHARED=98304
components_lat2:   REG=164 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=143 LDG=112 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=1 IADD3=82 LOP3=79

zcomponents_lat2:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=146 LDG=112 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=1 IADD3=80 LOP3=79

components_lat2:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=70 LDG=112 STG=8 F2F=32 PRMT=2 IADD3=77 LOP3=77
```

Interpretation:

- The three variants keep the same Tensor Core body: `HMMA=256`, `LDSM=96`,
  `LDGSTS=80`, `BAR=97`, and `DEPBAR=64`.
- `zcomponents_lat2` is not a structural improvement. It keeps the same
  `98304` byte shared-memory footprint and nearly identical instruction mix,
  then loses on the longer median run.
- `components_lat2` reduces per-kernel address/store work because each launch
  writes only one component, but the three-launch structure loses more than it
  saves. It also does not address the `98KB` shared-memory cliff.

Decision:

- Do not port either component split to `src/`.
- Keep the current `qkv_t32x256x16bkn_split_contig_lat2` path as the QKV BKN
  producer default/lower-bound for now.
- Next QKV work should target a real resource-shape change that reduces
  shared-memory pressure or CTA residency while preserving the useful
  `HMMA=256` CUDA Tile schedule. Grid-only or launch-only reshaping is closed.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV split-contig TM16/TM64 resource-shape probe

Goal:

- Test whether the current `TM32 x TN256 x TK16` QKV BKN producer is leaving
  A10G performance on the table through the M tile dimension.
- This is a CUDA Tile resource-shape probe, not a grid-only rewrite:
  `TM16` should lower per-CTA row work, while `TM64` should increase per-CTA
  Tensor Core work and cut `grid.x` roughly in half.
- The desired positive signal would be lower shared/register pressure or fewer
  fixed scheduling costs without losing useful `HMMA` density.

Code:

- Added bench-only variants:
  - `qkv_t16x256x16bkn_split_contig_lat2`
  - `qkv_t64x256x16bkn_split_contig_lat2`
- Both reuse the existing CUDA Tile split-contiguous Q/K/V producer template.

Commands:

```text
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm --shape infer_qkv \
  --variant qkv_t16x256x16bkn_split_contig_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500
./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm --shape infer_qkv \
  --variant qkv_t32x256x16bkn_split_contig_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500
./build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm --shape infer_qkv \
  --variant qkv_t64x256x16bkn_split_contig_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm | c++filt | \
  rg -A6 "qkv_bkn_static_tiled_split_contig_kernel<(16, 256, 16|32, 256, 16|64, 256, 16), 780"
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm | c++filt > /tmp/qkv_tm.sass
```

Timing:

```text
infer_qkv M=78060 N=1536 K=256

TM16:
  tile=16x256x16 grid=(4878,6) fullM=78048
  best=2.376 ms median=4.756 ms 25.83 TF/s roof=36.9%

TM32:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6) fullM=78048
  best=1.236 ms median=1.245 ms 49.64 TF/s roof=70.9%

TM64:
  tile=64x256x16 grid=(1219,6) fullM=78016
  best=2.263 ms median=2.815 ms 27.11 TF/s roof=38.7%
```

Resource usage:

```text
TM16: REG=120 SHARED=97792
TM32: REG=165 SHARED=98304
TM64: REG=220 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
TM16:
  HMMA=128 LDSM=80 LDGSTS=80 BAR=82 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=51 LDG=112 STG=12 F2F=16 PRMT=33 IADD3=83 LOP3=79 SHF=63 STS=88

TM32:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=143 LDG=112 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=1 IADD3=82 LOP3=79 SHF=63 STS=88

TM64:
  HMMA=512 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 BAR=85 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=54 LDG=112 STG=48 F2F=64 PRMT=129 IADD3=52 LOP3=49 SHF=33 STS=96
```

Interpretation:

- `TM16` does not solve the shared-memory limiter. Shared memory only drops
  from `98304` to `97792` bytes, while `grid.x` doubles and `HMMA/CTA` halves.
  The fixed CUDA Tile schedule costs are paid twice as often for the same total
  QKV math.
- `TM64` cuts `grid.x` in half and doubles `HMMA/CTA`, but raises registers to
  `220` and expands epilogue/packing work (`F2F=64`, `STG=48`, `PRMT=129`).
  The larger M tile is therefore a register/codegen cliff, not a throughput win.
- `TM32` is still the best current A10G balance for this fixed QKV shape:
  enough Tensor Core work per CTA to amortize barriers and scheduling, without
  the `REG=220+` large-tile cliff.

Decision:

- Do not port `TM16` or `TM64` to the inference path.
- Keep `TM32 x TN256 x TK16` as the current QKV split-contig default.
- The next QKV improvement should not be a simple M/N tile resize. The open
  problem is to reduce the `~98KB` CUDA Tile staging footprint or one-CTA
  residency while preserving the `TM32/TN256`-like `HMMA` density.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV small bench source-relevant split-contig baseline

Goal:

- Move future QKV BKN producer iteration away from the large
  `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` target when possible.
- Add the source-relevant split-contiguous Q/K/V output path to the small
  `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile` target so new CUDA Tile dataflow probes can build
  faster before being promoted to the larger bench or `src/`.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The CUDA Tile programming guide and API reference define tile kernels as
  compile-time tile-shaped block programs. Because new tile shapes instantiate
  new device code, keeping a small QKV-only target materially reduces iteration
  cost while preserving the same `ct::mma` lowering evidence.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Code:

- Added `split_contig_lat2` to `bench/bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu`.
- The new small-bench kernel mirrors the source-relevant
  `TM32 x TN256 x TK16`, BF16 input/output, `ct::mma`, split-contiguous Q/K/V
  producer with load/store latency hints.

Build:

```text
time ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8
real 0m11.527s
```

Timing:

```text
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant lat2 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant split_contig_lat2 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500

lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6) fullM=78048
  best=1.282 ms median=1.296 ms 47.89 TF/s roof=68.4%

split_contig_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6) fullM=78048
  best=1.290 ms median=1.300 ms 47.60 TF/s roof=68.0%
```

Resource usage:

```text
contiguous lat2:    REG=163 SHARED=98304
split_contig_lat2:  REG=165 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
contiguous lat2:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=72 LDG=112 STG=8 F2F=32 PRMT=2 IADD3=75 LOP3=77 SHF=61 STS=88

split_contig_lat2:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=143 LDG=112 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=1 IADD3=82 LOP3=79 SHF=63 STS=88
```

Interpretation:

- The small bench now reproduces the source-relevant QKV split-contig resource
  shape: `REG=165`, `SHARED=98304`, and the same `HMMA=256` Tensor Core body.
- Split-contiguous Q/K/V output is a real but secondary cost: it keeps the same
  `HMMA/LDSM/LDGSTS/BAR/DEPBAR` body as contiguous output, while increasing
  address/store work (`IMAD=72 -> 143`, `STG=8 -> 24`).
- The main unsolved limiter remains CUDA Tile staging/shared-memory shape, not
  the split output epilogue alone.

Decision:

- Use `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant split_contig_lat2` as the fast
  source-relevant QKV producer baseline for future dataflow probes.
- Only move a QKV experiment back to `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` or `src/`
  after the small bench shows a clear positive signal.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV small bench split-contig TK sweep

Goal:

- Test whether the source-relevant QKV split-contiguous producer can reduce the
  `~98KB` CUDA Tile staging footprint by changing the K tile dimension.
- This uses the small QKV bench added above, so the experiment keeps the same
  split Q/K/V output epilogue as the source path but avoids the large
  all-in-one bench compile cost.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- CUDA Tile C++ tile dimensions are compile-time types and must be powers of
  two. `TK8`, `TK16`, `TK32`, and `TK64` are therefore legal tile shapes for
  this fixed `K=256` producer.
- Because the compiler owns the block/thread mapping and staging schedule, the
  decision is based on generated resources/SASS plus timing, not on source shape
  intuition alone.

Code:

- Added small-bench variants:
  - `split_contig_tk8_lat2`
  - `split_contig_tk32_lat2`
  - `split_contig_tk64_lat2`
- Existing `split_contig_lat2` remains the `TK16` control.

Build:

```text
time ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8
real 0m14.579s
```

Timing:

```text
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant split_contig_tk8_lat2 \
  --warmup 20 --iters 120
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant split_contig_lat2 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant split_contig_tk32_lat2 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant split_contig_tk64_lat2 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500

TK8:
  tile=32x256x8 grid=(2439,6)
  best=3.275 ms median=6.800 ms 18.74 TF/s roof=26.8%

TK16:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6)
  best=1.236 ms median=1.247 ms 49.65 TF/s roof=70.9%

TK32:
  tile=32x256x32 grid=(2439,6)
  best=1.353 ms median=1.360 ms 45.35 TF/s roof=64.8%

TK64:
  tile=32x256x64 grid=(2439,6)
  best=1.778 ms median=1.783 ms 34.52 TF/s roof=49.3%
```

Resource usage:

```text
TK8:  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=97792
TK16: REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
TK32: REG=178 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
TK64: REG=172 STACK=0 SHARED=81920
```

Static SASS count:

```text
TK8:
  HMMA=512 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=96 BAR=161 DEPBAR=128
  IMAD=170 LDG=160 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=0 IADD3=79 LOP3=61 SHF=44 STS=104

TK16:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=143 LDG=112 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=1 IADD3=82 LOP3=79 SHF=63 STS=88

TK32:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=72 BAR=42 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=147 LDG=88 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=1 IADD3=68 LOP3=63 SHF=48 STS=80

TK64:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=72 BAR=21 DEPBAR=16
  IMAD=141 LDG=80 STG=24 F2F=32 PRMT=2 IADD3=53 LOP3=50 SHF=35 STS=80
```

Interpretation:

- `TK8` is a hard negative. It hits `REG=255`, spills stack, doubles static
  `HMMA` to `512`, and roughly doubles synchronization work. The low K tile
  spelling loses the useful CUDA Tile MMA schedule.
- `TK64` is the important diagnostic: it does reduce shared memory from
  `98304` to `81920` bytes and cuts `BAR/DEPBAR`, but performance still falls
  from `49.65` to `34.52 TF/s`. Therefore the QKV gap is not solved by lowering
  shared memory in isolation. The larger K tile hurts the generated schedule and
  latency hiding enough to dominate the occupancy benefit.
- `TK32` sits between the two, but still loses to `TK16`.
- Current best remains `TM32 x TN256 x TK16`, even though it uses `98KB`
  shared. For this A10G CUDA Tile lowering, `TK16` is the best tested balance
  between staging footprint, fixed schedule overhead, and Tensor Core issue
  density.

Decision:

- Do not port `TK8`, `TK32`, or `TK64` split-contig variants to source.
- Keep source QKV split-contig at `TM32 x TN256 x TK16 + latency=2`.
- Close the simple K-tile sweep direction. Future QKV work needs a staging or
  producer-consumer dataflow change that preserves the `TK16`-like schedule
  quality while reducing global-memory boundaries or shared-memory residency.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV split-contig Pair-K loop grouping closure

Goal:

- Test whether grouping two adjacent `K16` tiles per source loop changes CUDA
  Tile codegen for the source-relevant split-contiguous Q/K/V producer.
- Keep the current source shape fixed: `TM32 x TN256 x TK16`,
  `M=78048,N=1536,K=256`, BF16 input/output, `ct::mma`, and split-contiguous
  Q/K/V stores.
- This is not a new end-to-end path; it is a small-bench codegen probe before
  considering any source promotion.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The CUDA Tile programming guide describes tile kernels as block-level programs
  where the compiler maps tile operations to hardware threads, so source loop
  spelling can change generated scheduling even with the same mathematical
  `ct::mma` count.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Code:

- Added small-bench variants to `bench/bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu`:
  - `split_contig_pairk`
  - `split_contig_pairk_lat2`
- The new kernel loads two adjacent `K16` A/B tiles in one loop body and issues
  two `ct::mma` calls before moving to the next pair. The output epilogue is the
  same split Q/K/V store used by `split_contig_lat2`.

Build:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8
```

Timing:

```bash
for v in split_contig_lat2 split_contig_pairk split_contig_pairk_lat2; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done
```

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  best=1.255 ms, median=1.269 ms, 48.92 TF/s, roof70=69.9%
split_contig_pairk:
  best=2.540 ms, median=2.543 ms, 24.17 TF/s, roof70=34.5%
split_contig_pairk_lat2:
  best=1.439 ms, median=1.469 ms, 42.66 TF/s, roof70=60.9%
```

Resource usage:

```text
split_contig_lat2:       REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
split_contig_pairk:      REG=150 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
split_contig_pairk_lat2: REG=239 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  instr=2288 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=88 STG=24 F2F=32
  LDGDEPBAR=32 DEPBAR.LE=32 BAR.SYNC=33 IMAD=143 IADD3=82
  PRMT=1 SHF=63 LOP3=79 BRA=3

split_contig_pairk:
  instr=2176 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=88 STG=24 F2F=32
  LDGDEPBAR=32 DEPBAR.LE=32 BAR.SYNC=13 IMAD=143 IADD3=80
  PRMT=1 SHF=63 LOP3=79 BRA=3

split_contig_pairk_lat2:
  instr=2240 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=88 STG=24 F2F=32
  LDGDEPBAR=32 DEPBAR.LE=32 BAR.SYNC=29 IMAD=143 IADD3=80
  PRMT=1 SHF=63 LOP3=79 BRA=3
```

Interpretation:

- Pair-K does not lose Tensor Core lowering: all three variants still emit
  `HMMA=256`, with the same `LDSM`, `LDGSTS`, shared-memory footprint, and
  split-output store count.
- The unhinted pair-K spelling lowers registers and static instruction count,
  but collapses to `24.17 TF/s`. This means the source loop grouping breaks the
  useful CUDA Tile staging/issue schedule even though the static instruction
  count looks smaller.
- Adding the existing `latency=2` hints recovers part of the schedule, but
  raises register pressure to `REG=239` and still loses to the current default
  by about `12.8%` on best time.
- Bottom-level cause for this shape: the compiler-generated pipeline is
  sensitive to the one-K-tile loop structure. Pairing two K tiles does not
  reduce the `98KB` staging footprint or global-memory traffic, and it either
  damages latency hiding or inflates register pressure.

Decision:

- Do not promote pair-K into the source QKV split-contig producer.
- Close the simple pair-K grouping direction for this path.
- The next useful QKV work should target a real producer-consumer fusion or a
  different staging layout that reduces global-memory boundaries or shared
  residency while preserving the current `HMMA=256`/`TK16` schedule quality.

### 2026-06-05 - Current producer-consumer fusion boundary recheck

Goal:

- Recheck the current time-QKV producer-consumer boundary after the recent QKV
  shape sweeps closed simple `TM/TN/TK` changes.
- Compare the current default split-contiguous QKV producer plus standalone
  rotary against the existing opt-in QKV+rotary fused producer and the opt-in
  Q-rotary-in-attention path.
- Decide whether any producer-consumer fusion path should be revisited or
  promoted before moving to another hotspot.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- CUDA Tile kernels express tile-level block programs. Fusion is only useful if
  the generated tile program actually reduces global-memory boundaries without
  creating a worse register/shared-memory/SASS schedule.
- The checks below therefore include timing, resource usage, and SASS counts.

Important measurement note:

- An initial fused/qrot run was launched in parallel on the same A10G and
  produced ~2s end-to-end times. That data is discarded as GPU contention.
- The timing below uses sequential runs only.

Commands:

```text
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

CUDASEP_PROFILE_TIME_QKV_PRODUCER=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/prod_cons_default_current_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_FUSED_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1 \
CUDASEP_PROFILE_TIME_QKV_PRODUCER=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/prod_cons_fused_current_seq_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_Q_ROTARY_IN_ATTENTION=1 \
CUDASEP_PROFILE_TIME_QKV_PRODUCER=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav --output outputs/prod_cons_qrot_current_seq_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt | rg ...
cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/cudasep_infer | c++filt > /tmp/prod_cons_current.sass
```

Timing:

```text
default split producer + standalone rotary:
  inference=916.9 ms
  split_producer: 48 calls, 64.3667 ms total, 1.34097 ms avg
  split_rotary:   48 calls, 24.0267 ms total, 0.500555 ms avg
  producer+rotary total: 88.3934 ms

QKV+rotary fused producer:
  inference=969.4 ms
  bkn_fused_producer: 48 calls, 141.453 ms total, 2.94693 ms avg

Q-rotary inside attention:
  inference=951.5 ms
  split_producer: 48 calls, 64.0777 ms total, 1.33495 ms avg
  K-only split_rotary: 48 calls, 12.1396 ms total, 0.252907 ms avg
```

Resource usage:

```text
qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<32,256,16,...,2>:
  REG=165 SHARED=98304

qkv_bkn_rotary_split_contig_static_full_kernel<32,256,16,...,__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=174 SHARED=98304

rotary_time_split_contig_inplace_kernel<256,__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=29 SHARED=0

rotary_time_split_contig_k_only_inplace_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=22 SHARED=0

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64,32,true,true>:
  REG=237 SHARED=40960

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_qrot_input_kernel<64,32,true,true,__nv_bfloat16>:
  REG=225 SHARED=65536
```

Static SASS count:

```text
QKV split producer:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=97 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=143 LDG=112 STG=24 STS=88 F2F=32 FFMA=0 PRMT=1
  IADD3=82 LOP3=79 SHF=63

QKV+rotary fused producer:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 BAR=86 DEPBAR=64
  IMAD=379 LDG=176 STG=24 STS=136 F2F=160 FFMA=64 PRMT=256
  IADD3=98 LOP3=99 SHF=75

default time attention main:
  HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 BAR=31 DEPBAR=16
  IMAD=69 LDG=32 STG=4 STS=60 F2F=56 FFMA=358 MUFU=124 PRMT=81
  IADD3=184 LOP3=157 SHF=125

qrot time attention main:
  HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 BAR=33 DEPBAR=16
  IMAD=405 LDG=64 STG=4 STS=64 F2F=120 FFMA=390 MUFU=124 PRMT=234
  IADD3=216 LOP3=180 SHF=217
```

Interpretation:

- QKV+rotary fused producer still preserves Tensor Core lowering
  (`HMMA=256`), but it makes the already high-pressure QKV producer much
  heavier: `REG=165 -> 174`, `LDG=112 -> 176`, `STS=88 -> 136`,
  `F2F=32 -> 160`, `IMAD=143 -> 379`, and `PRMT=1 -> 256`.
- The fused producer removes a standalone streaming rotary pass, but replaces
  the default `88.39 ms` producer+rotary segment with `141.45 ms` of fused
  producer work. This is a fusion cliff, not a missed promotion.
- Q-rotary-in-attention successfully halves the standalone rotary pass
  (`24.03 ms -> 12.14 ms`), but it pushes the main attention kernel from the
  default `40KB` shared-memory class to `65KB` and sharply increases scalar
  address/permute work (`IMAD=69 -> 405`, `PRMT=81 -> 234`,
  `F2F=56 -> 120`). End-to-end remains slower.
- These results match the earlier negative conclusions in the current binary:
  the current producer-consumer fusion spellings are not limited by missing
  HMMA. They lose because the added rotary work increases register/shared
  pressure and scalar/control work more than the removed global-memory pass
  saves.

Decision:

- Keep the default path as split-contiguous QKV producer plus standalone rotary.
- Do not promote `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_FUSED_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1`.
- Do not promote `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_Q_ROTARY_IN_ATTENTION=1`.
- Close this current-spelling producer-consumer fusion boundary for now. The
  next productive A10G step should either redesign attention-load fusion so it
  keeps the default `40KB` attention footprint, or move to another hotspot such
  as FFN12 where the remaining Tensor Core time is larger.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 residual fast-path source expansion compile probe

Goal:

- Check whether the existing residual fused FFN12 path can simply reuse the
  current non-residual best CUDA Tile spelling:
  `GeluMode=poly9/odd5`, `TK64`, `RoundOutputAcc=false`, `MemoryLatency=2`.
- This matters because the current source binary still contains only the old
  residual main instance:
  `ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel<6, false, 64, true, true, 0,
  false, false, false, false>`.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The proposed change stayed within official CUDA Tile constructs:
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::mma`, and load/store
  `latency` hints.
- No raw WMMA, inline PTX, cuBLAS, cuDNN, or cuFFT path was introduced.

Attempted source change:

- First tried routing residual through the same typed dispatcher used by the
  non-residual path. This expanded too many FFN12 template combinations.
- Then narrowed it to explicit current residual instances only:
  `poly9`/`odd5`, `!FullBF16`, `AddResidual=true`, `TK64`, with the
  `latency=2` and output-no-round policy.

Result:

- Both attempts entered `/usr/local/cuda/bin/nvcc --enable-tile` and then
  stalled in `tileiras` while compiling `src/mbr_ffn12_cutile.cu`.
- Process samples showed `tileiras` resident with nearly zero CPU progress
  after repeated waits; memory and disk were not limiting.
- The source experiment was reverted to avoid leaving the main build in a
  non-verifiable state.

Existing binary evidence:

```text
non-residual current default fast instance:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  <6, false, 64, false, false, 2, true, true, true, false>
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

non-residual current output-bias-BF16 opt-in:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  <6, false, 64, false, false, 2, true, true, true, true>
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

old residual fused instance:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  <6, false, 64, true, true, 0, false, false, false, false>
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Interpretation:

- There is a real optimization opportunity: residual fused FFN12 is not using
  the current non-residual `latency=2 + output-no-round` spelling.
- The immediate blocker is compile/codegen cost in the large source file, not a
  runtime failure. Adding more FFN12 Tile instances directly to
  `src/mbr_ffn12_cutile.cu` is now a poor iteration loop.
- The next residual FFN12 step should be an isolated candidates-only bench or a
  split translation unit that instantiates exactly one residual fast-path
  kernel. Only after that compiles and shows resource/SASS/timing benefit
  should it be promoted into source.

Decision:

- Do not promote residual FFN12 fast-path source expansion in this form.
- Keep the existing residual source path unchanged for now.
- Treat `tileiras` compile time as part of the CUDA Tile-first engineering
  evidence: on this project, code size and template instantiation strategy are
  now performance workflow constraints, not just build-system details.

### 2026-06-05 - Isolated FFN12 residual fast-path CUDA Tile bench

Goal:

- Retest the residual FFN12 fast-path idea outside the large source translation
  unit.
- Compile only two residual CUDA Tile instances:
  - old source-like residual: `RoundOutputAcc=true`, `MemoryLatency=0`
  - proposed residual fast-path: `RoundOutputAcc=false`, `MemoryLatency=2`

Source change:

- Added `bench/bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile.cu`.
- Added CMake target `bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile`.
- The bench uses only CUDA Tile API constructs: `cuda_tile.h`,
  `ct::partition_view`, `ct::mma`, `ct::load/store`, and `latency` hints.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant all --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant old --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant fast_lat2_outnoround --warmup 30 --iters 300

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -n -A3 -B1 "ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/ffn12_residual.sass
```

Build:

```text
[1/2] Building CUDA object CMakeFiles/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile.dir/bench/bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile.cu.o
[2/2] Linking CUDA executable bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile
```

Timing on A10G:

```text
500-iteration A/B run:
old_round_lat0:        best=4.335 ms, median=4.345 ms, useful=18.88 TF/s, roof70=27.0%
fast_lat2_outnoround:  best=4.572 ms, median=4.583 ms, useful=17.90 TF/s, roof70=25.6%

300-iteration serial rerun:
old_round_lat0:        best=4.327 ms, median=4.336 ms, useful=18.91 TF/s, roof70=27.0%
fast_lat2_outnoround:  best=4.567 ms, median=4.580 ms, useful=17.92 TF/s, roof70=25.6%
```

Resource usage:

```text
old residual:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<true, 0, false, false, false>
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

fast residual:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<false, 2, true, true, true>
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
old residual:
  instr_lines=4228 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=80 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=411 IADD3=142 PRMT=40 SHF=161 LOP3=134 BRA=5

fast residual:
  instr_lines=3620 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=48 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=265 IADD3=124 PRMT=12 SHF=121 LOP3=130 BRA=5
```

Interpretation:

- Isolating the bench fixes the compile-loop problem. The earlier source
  `tileiras` stall was caused by the large source translation unit/template
  instantiation strategy, not by this kernel being impossible to compile.
- The proposed residual fast-path is still a negative runtime result:
  `4.327 -> 4.567 ms` in the clean serial rerun.
- `latency=2 + output-no-round` reduces static scalar/conversion work
  (`F2F 80 -> 48`, `IMAD 411 -> 265`, `PRMT 40 -> 12`) but does not improve the
  generated Tensor Core body or residency: both variants remain `HMMA=128`,
  `SHARED=98304`, one-CTA residency class.
- This explains why copying the non-residual best policy is not enough.
  Non-residual source-best FFN12 reached much higher throughput with a
  different generated schedule (`HMMA=256` in prior source/bench evidence).
  The residual version is stuck in a lower Tensor Core schedule and pays the
  residual load/add/store work on top.

Decision:

- Do not promote `latency=2 + output-no-round` residual FFN12 as-is.
- Keep the new isolated bench because it gives a fast compile loop for future
  residual FFN12 experiments.
- Next residual FFN12 work should target the `HMMA=128` codegen shape first,
  not more epilogue/latency toggles. A useful next probe is to remove or delay
  the residual add in the same isolated bench to see whether the `AddResidual`
  epilogue is what changes the Tile compiler schedule.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 residual add codegen causality probe

Goal:

- Explain why isolated residual FFN12 stays around `18 TF/s` while the
  non-residual FFN12 spelling reaches `45 TF/s`.
- Test whether the residual add itself changes CUDA Tile codegen, or whether
  the earlier slowdown was caused by output rounding, `latency=2`, or store
  hints.

Bench changes:

- Extended `bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile` with control variants:
  - `outnoround_lat0`
  - `fast_lat2_outnoround_nostore`
  - `nores_old`
  - `nores_outnoround_lat0`
  - `nores_fast_lat2_outnoround`
- The no-residual variants use the same source body but compile with
  `AddResidual=false`, so this isolates residual epilogue effects.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- `ct::mma` with explicit accumulator remains the official Tile MMA spelling.
- `latency` is an official load/store scheduling hint, not a correctness or
  guaranteed-performance contract. The experiment treats it as codegen input
  and validates the generated SASS.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant all --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant old --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant nores_fast_lat2_outnoround --warmup 30 --iters 300

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -n -A2 -B1 "ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/ffn12_residual2.sass
```

Timing on A10G:

```text
old_round_lat0:                best=4.332 ms, useful=18.89 TF/s, roof70=27.0%
outnoround_lat0:               best=4.641 ms, useful=17.63 TF/s, roof70=25.2%
fast_lat2_outnoround:          best=4.562 ms, useful=17.94 TF/s, roof70=25.6%
fast_lat2_outnoround_nostore:  best=4.562 ms, useful=17.94 TF/s, roof70=25.6%

nores_round_lat0:              best=1.871 ms, useful=43.74 TF/s, roof70=62.5%
nores_outnoround_lat0:         best=1.858 ms, useful=44.04 TF/s, roof70=62.9%
nores_fast_lat2_outnoround:    best=1.794 ms, useful=45.61 TF/s, roof70=65.2%
```

Serial confirmation:

```text
old_round_lat0:              best=4.350 ms, useful=18.81 TF/s, roof70=26.9%
nores_fast_lat2_outnoround:  best=1.795 ms, useful=45.60 TF/s, roof70=65.1%
```

Resource usage:

```text
nores_fast_lat2_outnoround:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<false, false, 2, true, true, true>
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

nores_outnoround_lat0:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<false, false, 0, false, false, false>
  REG=242 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

nores_round_lat0:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<false, true, 0, false, false, false>
  REG=242 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

fast_lat2_outnoround_nostore:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<true, false, 2, true, true, false>
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

fast_lat2_outnoround:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<true, false, 2, true, true, true>
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

outnoround_lat0:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<true, false, 0, false, false, false>
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

old_round_lat0:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<true, true, 0, false, false, false>
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
nores_fast_lat2_outnoround:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=96 DEPBAR=61
  IMAD=197 IADD3=163 PRMT=16 SHF=203 LOP3=145 BRA=3

nores_outnoround_lat0:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=86 DEPBAR=59
  IMAD=284 IADD3=171 PRMT=55 SHF=182 LOP3=175 BRA=3

nores_round_lat0:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2F=96 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=86 DEPBAR=59
  IMAD=356 IADD3=172 PRMT=116 SHF=189 LOP3=175 BRA=3

fast_lat2_outnoround_nostore:
  HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=48 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=265 IADD3=124 PRMT=12 SHF=121 LOP3=130 BRA=5

fast_lat2_outnoround:
  HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=48 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=265 IADD3=124 PRMT=12 SHF=121 LOP3=130 BRA=5

outnoround_lat0:
  HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=48 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=329 IADD3=143 PRMT=12 SHF=134 LOP3=149 BRA=5

old_round_lat0:
  HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=80 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=411 IADD3=142 PRMT=40 SHF=161 LOP3=134 BRA=5
```

Interpretation:

- The root cause is now isolated: compiling the same FFN12 body with
  `AddResidual=true` changes the Tile compiler schedule from `HMMA=256` to
  `HMMA=128`.
- Output rounding and `latency=2` are second-order effects. Without residual,
  the same `latency=2 + out-no-round` policy reaches `45.61 TF/s` and keeps
  `HMMA=256`. With residual, store hint on/off is identical and both remain
  `HMMA=128`.
- The slowdown is therefore not simply the cost of one extra residual BF16
  load/add. The residual epilogue changes live ranges/codegen enough that the
  generated Tensor Core body is halved, which dominates runtime.
- The path to recover performance is to keep the main FFN12 kernel in the
  `AddResidual=false`/`HMMA=256` shape and handle residual add outside that
  codegen cliff, or find a CUDA Tile epilogue spelling that does not perturb
  the MMA schedule.

Decision:

- Do not fuse residual add into the current FFN12 Tile kernel spelling.
- Next residual experiment should benchmark a two-kernel path:
  `nores_fast_lat2_outnoround` followed by a small BF16 residual-add Tile
  kernel. This adds one launch and one global-memory pass, but preserves
  `HMMA=256` in the expensive FFN12 kernel; based on current numbers, it has a
  realistic chance to beat the fused residual `4.33 ms` path.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 residual two-kernel CUDA Tile recovery

Goal:

- Test the proposed fix for the residual codegen cliff:
  keep the expensive FFN12 kernel in the `AddResidual=false`/`HMMA=256` shape,
  then apply residual add in a separate small BF16 CUDA Tile kernel.
- Compare against the current fused residual path, where `AddResidual=true`
  forces the FFN12 body to `HMMA=128`.

Bench change:

- Added `residual_add_bf16_kernel` to
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile.cu`.
- Added `two_kernel_fast` variant:
  1. `ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<false, false, 2, true, true, true>`
  2. `residual_add_bf16_kernel`

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant old --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant two_kernel_fast --warmup 30 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile \
  --variant nores_fast_lat2_outnoround --warmup 30 --iters 300

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -n -A2 -B1 \
  "ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel|residual_add_bf16_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_residual_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/ffn12_residual3.sass
```

Timing on A10G:

```text
fused residual old:
  old_round_lat0
  best=4.328 ms, median=4.336 ms
  useful=18.91 TF/s, roof70=27.0%

two-kernel residual:
  two_kernel_fast
  best=1.983 ms, median=1.996 ms
  useful=41.27 TF/s, roof70=59.0%

no-residual fast reference:
  nores_fast_lat2_outnoround
  best=1.795 ms, median=1.804 ms
  useful=45.59 TF/s, roof70=65.1%
```

Resource usage:

```text
main no-residual fast FFN12 kernel:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<false, false, 2, true, true, true>
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

current fused residual old:
  ffn12_residual_pairh32_poly9_kernel<true, true, 0, false, false, false>
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

separate residual add kernel:
  residual_add_bf16_kernel
  REG=70 STACK=0 SHARED=0
```

Static SASS count:

```text
main no-residual fast FFN12 kernel:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=96 DEPBAR=61
  IMAD=197 IADD3=163 PRMT=16 SHF=203 LOP3=145 BRA=3

current fused residual old:
  HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=80 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=411 IADD3=142 PRMT=40 SHF=161 LOP3=134 BRA=5

separate residual add kernel:
  HMMA=0 LDG=16 STG=8
  IMAD=99 IADD3=39 SHF=16 LOP3=16 BRA=32
```

Interpretation:

- This validates the recovery strategy. Splitting residual add out preserves the
  expensive FFN12 kernel's `HMMA=256` schedule and beats fused residual by more
  than 2x (`4.328 ms -> 1.983 ms`).
- The extra launch and global-memory pass cost about `0.188 ms` over the
  no-residual fast reference (`1.795 ms -> 1.983 ms`), which is far smaller
  than the cost of letting the fused residual epilogue drop the main FFN12 body
  to `HMMA=128`.
- This is a concrete example where "deeper fusion" is worse for CUDA Tile on
  A10G. The right performance answer is to preserve Tensor Core issue density
  in the dominant kernel and accept a small follow-up memory kernel.

Decision:

- Promote this direction for source evaluation: residual FFN12 should use the
  fast no-residual FFN12 Tile kernel plus a separate BF16 residual-add Tile
  kernel, instead of the current fused residual kernel.
- Before source promotion, verify end-to-end quality and timing because the
  two-kernel path changes output rounding order relative to fused residual.

### 2026-06-05 - Source opt-in: FFN12 residual two-kernel path

Goal:

- Move the successful residual two-kernel FFN12 recovery into source as an
  explicit opt-in experiment.
- Preserve default behavior. The new path is enabled only with:
  `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_RESIDUAL=1` and
  `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1`.

Source changes:

- Added `src/mbr_residual_cutile.cu` with:
  - `residual_add_bf16_main_kernel`
  - `residual_add_bf16_tail_kernel`
  - `try_residual_add_bf16_cutile(...)`
- Added the source file to `CMakeLists.txt`.
- Updated `try_feedforward_fused_impl(...)` so the opt-in residual path does:
  1. existing non-residual `launch_ffn12_fused256_cutile(...)`
  2. `try_residual_add_bf16_cutile(...)`
- Kept the existing fused residual source path unchanged for default/rollback.

Implementation note:

- An initial source attempt passed an empty `Tensor ff_out_flat` into the
  non-residual FFN12 launcher and produced an illegal memory access. This was
  fixed by explicitly allocating:
  `Tensor::empty({total_batch, 256}, DType::BFloat16)`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_RESIDUAL=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_residual_source_fused_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_RESIDUAL=1 \
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_residual_source_twokernel_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_RESIDUAL=1 \
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_residual_source_twokernel_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

End-to-end timing:

```text
test_clean.wav, fused residual source path:
  1124.5 ms

test_clean.wav, residual two-kernel opt-in:
  897.3 ms

test.wav, residual two-kernel opt-in:
  5276.6 ms
```

Output difference, two-kernel opt-in versus fused residual source path on
`test_clean.wav`:

```text
Vocals.wav:
  max_abs=0.0170755386 rms=0.000958355884
  rel_rms=0.00521275779 snr=45.659 dB

Instrumental.wav:
  max_abs=0.0136511065 rms=0.00108169978
  rel_rms=0.00449493152 snr=46.946 dB
```

Resource usage from `build-min/cudasep_infer`:

```text
non-residual FFN12 fast kernel used by two-kernel path:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  <6, false, 64, false, false, 2, true, true, true, false>
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

old fused residual FFN12 kernel:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  <6, false, 64, true, true, 0, false, false, false, false>
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

residual add main:
  residual_add_bf16_main_kernel
  REG=70 STACK=0 SHARED=0

residual add tail:
  residual_add_bf16_tail_kernel
  REG=64 STACK=0 SHARED=0
```

Static SASS count from `build-min/cudasep_infer`:

```text
non-residual FFN12 fast kernel:
  HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160 BAR=96 DEPBAR=61
  IMAD=197 IADD3=163 PRMT=16 SHF=203 LOP3=145 BRA=3

old fused residual FFN12 kernel:
  HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=144 STS=82 STG=8
  F2F=80 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80 BAR=59 DEPBAR=32
  IMAD=411 IADD3=142 PRMT=40 SHF=161 LOP3=134 BRA=5

residual add main kernel:
  HMMA=0 LDG=16 STG=8
  IMAD=99 IADD3=39 SHF=16 LOP3=16 BRA=32

residual add tail kernel:
  HMMA=0 LDG=128 STG=64
  IMAD=66 IADD3=508 PRMT=226 SHF=63 LOP3=129 BRA=1
```

Interpretation:

- The source opt-in reproduces the bench conclusion: preserving `HMMA=256` in
  the dominant FFN12 kernel is worth more than saving one launch by fusing the
  residual add.
- `test_clean.wav` improves from `1124.5 ms` to `897.3 ms` versus the existing
  fused residual source path.
- The output difference is moderate (`~0.45-0.52% rel_rms`) because the
  two-kernel path changes residual/add rounding order. This is much smaller
  than the odd5 GELU experiment and acceptable as an opt-in performance
  experiment, but it should not be silently promoted without a quality decision.
- The residual add tail kernel has a large static SASS count for only 12 rows;
  it is not the dominant cost, but a future cleanup could merge tail handling
  into a generic masked add kernel or specialize it more tightly.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1` as a source opt-in.
- Do not replace the fused residual default yet; first compare against the
  current default non-residual/residual-rms flow and decide whether the
  `~0.5%` rel_rms change is acceptable.
- Future source organization work should avoid putting small experimental
  helpers in common headers: touching `mbr_cuda_tile.h` forced
  `mbr_ffn12_cutile.cu` to recompile and exposed the known `tileiras` cost of
  the large FFN12 translation unit.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 residual two-kernel versus current default path

Goal:

- Compare the new source opt-in against the actual current default BF16
  inference path, not only against the older fused-residual source path.
- This is required because the default path already uses a separate
  residual/RMS flow in some cases, so the fused-residual microbench is not the
  whole end-to-end story.

Commands:

```bash
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_default_current_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_RESIDUAL=1 \
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_residual_source_twokernel_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_default_current_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_RESIDUAL=1 \
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_residual_source_twokernel_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Timing:

```text
test_clean.wav current default BF16:
  903.9 ms

test_clean.wav residual two-kernel opt-in:
  897.3 ms

test.wav current default BF16:
  5306.7 ms
```
