```text
test.wav residual two-kernel opt-in:
  5276.6 ms
```

Output difference, two-kernel opt-in versus current default:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=1.78813934e-07 rms=1.00218223e-08 rel_rms=5.45102015e-08 snr=145.270 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.31977458e-08 rel_rms=5.48261447e-08 snr=145.220 dB

test.wav:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.03543217e-08 rel_rms=5.4775108e-08 snr=145.228 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.04950509e-08 rel_rms=5.47432762e-08 snr=145.233 dB
```

Interpretation:

- Against the real current default BF16 path, the two-kernel residual opt-in is
  only a small end-to-end win:
  `903.9 -> 897.3 ms` on `test_clean.wav` and `5306.7 -> 5276.6 ms` on
  `test.wav`.
- Output is effectively unchanged relative to default (`~5e-8 rel_rms`), unlike
  the comparison against the older fused-residual path. This indicates the
  current default already matches the two-kernel rounding order closely.
- The opt-in is still valuable because it provides a CUDA Tile residual path
  that preserves the FFN12 `HMMA=256` schedule and can replace the slow fused
  residual experiment, but it is not a large default-path speedup by itself.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1` as the preferred residual
  fused experiment path.
- Do not prioritize default promotion solely for end-to-end speed; the next
  larger wins likely remain QKV/time-attention/FFN12 main schedule rather than
  residual add.

### 2026-06-05 - Mask hidden padded-M CUDA Tile shape closure

Goal:

- Re-check the current mask-estimator hidden linears:
  `M=1301, N=1024, K=256/1024`.
- Explain why the current CUDA Tile padded-M path stays around `25-33 TF/s`,
  and test whether wider `TN=128` tiles can reduce N-grid overhead on A10G.
- Avoid adding more template instances to the already-heavy
  `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` translation unit after it stalled in `tileiras`;
  use a focused small bench instead.

Bench change:

- Added `bench/bf16_mask_hidden_cutile.cu`.
- Added CMake target `bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile`.
- The bench is CUDA Tile-only and uses `ct::mma`; it has no cuBLAS/cuDNN/cuFFT,
  raw WMMA, inline `mma.sync`, or inline asm.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile \
  --k 1024 --variant all --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile \
  --k 256 --variant all --warmup 30 --iters 500

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -n -A2 -B1 "mask_hidden_padded_m_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/mask_hidden_cutile.sass
```

Timing:

```text
K=1024:
  t32x64x64    best=0.0828 ms useful=32.96 TF/s roof70=47.1%
  t32x128x32   best=0.1073 ms useful=25.42 TF/s roof70=36.3%
  t32x128x64   best=0.1331 ms useful=20.51 TF/s roof70=29.3%
  t32x128x128  best=0.1338 ms useful=20.39 TF/s roof70=29.1%
  t16x128x64   best=0.0999 ms useful=27.32 TF/s roof70=39.0%

K=256:
  t32x64x64    best=0.0265 ms useful=25.77 TF/s roof70=36.8%
  t32x128x32   best=0.0329 ms useful=20.74 TF/s roof70=29.6%
  t32x128x64   best=0.0409 ms useful=16.68 TF/s roof70=23.8%
  t32x128x128  best=0.0420 ms useful=16.25 TF/s roof70=23.2%
  t16x128x64   best=0.0346 ms useful=19.74 TF/s roof70=28.2%
```

Resource usage:

```text
t32x64x64 K=1024/256:
  REG=114 STACK=0 SHARED=24576

t32x128x32 K=1024/256:
  REG=152 STACK=0 SHARED=36864

t32x128x64 K=1024/256:
  REG=236 STACK=0 SHARED=57344

t32x128x128 K=1024:
  REG=206 STACK=0 SHARED=65536

t16x128x64 K=1024:
  REG=154 STACK=0 SHARED=45056
```

Static SASS count:

```text
t32x64x64:
  instr=440 HMMA=32 LDSM=12 LDGSTS=6 STS=14 STG=18
  LDG=24 F2F=8 BAR=5 DEPBAR=4

t32x128x32:
  instr=1032 HMMA=64 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=10 STS=26 STG=36
  LDG=30 F2F=16 BAR=8 DEPBAR=8

t32x128x64:
  instr=1384 HMMA=128 LDSM=32 LDGSTS=20 STS=36 STG=36
  LDG=56 F2F=16 BAR=8 DEPBAR=8

t32x128x128:
  instr=856 HMMA=128 LDSM=32 LDGSTS=20 STS=36 STG=36
  LDG=54 F2F=16 BAR=8 DEPBAR=4

t16x128x64:
  instr=856 HMMA=64 LDSM=24 LDGSTS=18 STS=26 STG=18
  LDG=38 F2F=8 BAR=8 DEPBAR=8
```

Interpretation:

- The current `32x64x64` padded-M shape remains the best tested A10G point for
  mask hidden linears.
- `TN=128` is not a Tensor Core fallback. It keeps HMMA, but the wider N tile
  increases register pressure, shared memory, static instruction count, LDSM,
  LDGSTS, STS, and barriers enough that halving the N-grid does not pay back.
- `t16x128x64` lowers per-CTA row work and avoids the worst register pressure,
  but it doubles the M-grid and still loses to the existing `t32x64x64` shape.
- The bottom-level limiter is CUDA Tile staging and small-M scheduling overhead,
  not missing HMMA. At only `41 x 16` CTAs for the current shape, there is not
  enough large dense GEMM structure to approach the `~70 TF/s` dense reference.

Decision:

- Do not promote `TN=128` mask hidden variants into source.
- Keep source default on the existing `32x64x64` padded-M path.
- Use the new focused `bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile` target for future
  small-M mask hidden experiments instead of adding more instances to the large
  mixed GEMM bench.

Follow-up low-risk shape sweep:

- Extended the focused bench with `TM=16/64`, `TN=32`, and `TK=32/128`
  variants so the closure covers all low-compile-cost one-axis changes around
  the current `32x64x64` shape.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile \
  --k 1024 --variant all --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile \
  --k 256 --variant all --warmup 30 --iters 500

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile | \
  c++filt | rg -A1 "mask_hidden_padded_m_kernel<"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/mask_hidden_sweep.sass
```


```text
  BAR=8 DEPBAR=8 PRMT=88 IMAD=132 IADD3=124

t32x128x64 K=1024:
  instr=2768 HMMA=128 LDSM=32 LDGSTS=20 LDG=56 STS=36 STG=36
  BAR=8 DEPBAR=8 PRMT=144 IMAD=159 IADD3=149

t32x128x128 K=1024:
  instr=1712 HMMA=128 LDSM=32 LDGSTS=20 LDG=54 STS=36 STG=36
  BAR=8 DEPBAR=4 PRMT=144 IMAD=56 IADD3=114

t16x128x64 K=1024:
  instr=1712 HMMA=64 LDSM=24 LDGSTS=18 LDG=38 STS=26 STG=18
  BAR=8 DEPBAR=8 PRMT=88 IMAD=100 IADD3=100
```

Interpretation update:

- `t32x64x64` remains the best tested A10G point for both `K=1024` and
  `K=256`; this is now a shape closure across the low-risk one-axis sweeps,
  not only a `TN=128` negative.
- `TM=16` lowers registers/shared memory but halves HMMA work per CTA and
  doubles the M grid, so launch-side and scheduling overhead dominate.
- `TM=64` doubles HMMA work per CTA but increases padded-M work
  (`1301 -> 1344`) and raises register/shared/integer/shuffle pressure.
- `TN=32` halves HMMA work per CTA and doubles the N grid; it saves resources
  but loses throughput.
- `TN=128` keeps or increases HMMA, but the register/shared/integer/shuffle and
  barrier costs are too high for this small-M shape.
- `TK=32` keeps the same total HMMA for `TN=64` but doubles K-loop control and
  barrier/dependency overhead.
- `TK=128` increases the static body and staging footprint enough that the
  larger K tile does not pay back.

Decision update:

- Treat `TM32 x TN64 x TK64` as the current A10G CUDA Tile sweet spot for
  mask-hidden padded-M linears.
- Further work here should target deeper fusion or a different scheduling
  strategy, not more nearby tile-size enumeration.

### 2026-06-05 - Time attention out accumulator BF16 rounding probe

Goal:

- Test a full-BF16-arithmetic hypothesis inside the current best time-attention
  CUDA Tile schedule.
- Keep the known-good split-contiguous `QRows=64,KTile=32` one-kernel
  `QK -> online softmax -> AV` path, but round the live `out_acc` tile through
  BF16 after each AV update.
- The question is whether reducing the live FP32 output accumulator helps A10G
  register pressure enough to outweigh the extra BF16 conversion and Tile
  codegen changes.

Bench change:

- Added bench-only variants to `bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu`:
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_out_acc_bf16`
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_out_acc_bf16_no_keytail`
- The default inference source path is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_out_acc_bf16 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input_no_keytail \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_out_acc_bf16_no_keytail \
  --warmup 30 --iters 500 --compare-baseline
```

Timing and output drift:

```text
default keytail:
  best=3.387 ms median=3.389 ms real_math=60.41 TF/s roof70=86.3%
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

out_acc BF16 keytail:
  best=3.780 ms median=3.793 ms real_math=54.13 TF/s roof70=77.3%
  max_abs=0.0107421875 rms=0.00528992044 rel_rms=0.0213408485

default no-keytail:
  best=3.297 ms median=3.299 ms real_math=61.06 TF/s roof70=87.2%
  max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000505273408 rel_rms=0.002038398

out_acc BF16 no-keytail:
  best=3.466 ms median=3.478 ms real_math=58.08 TF/s roof70=83.0%
  max_abs=0.0107421875 rms=0.00524163309 rel_rms=0.0211460454
```

Resource usage:

```text
default keytail:      REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960
out_acc BF16 keytail: REG=209 STACK=0 SHARED=40960

default no-keytail:      REG=217 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
out_acc BF16 no-keytail: REG=194 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
```

SASS count, exact `Function :` section parsing:

```text
default keytail:
  instr=5472 HMMA=160 MUFU.EX2=90 F2FP=56 F2F=56
  FFMA=358 FADD=202 FMUL=366 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 STS=60

out_acc BF16 keytail:
  instr=5408 HMMA=128 MUFU.EX2=72 F2FP=112 F2F=112
  FFMA=324 FADD=162 FMUL=296 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=12 STS=56

default no-keytail:
  instr=4288 HMMA=128 MUFU.EX2=72 F2FP=48 F2F=48
  FFMA=308 FADD=162 FMUL=280 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 STS=48

out_acc BF16 no-keytail:
  instr=4064 HMMA=96 MUFU.EX2=54 F2FP=88 F2F=88
  FFMA=274 FADD=122 FMUL=210 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=12 STS=44
```

Interpretation:

- Rounding `out_acc` to BF16 does reduce register pressure by about 23-24
  registers/thread, but it is still slower in both keytail and no-keytail
  variants.
- The lower register count is not free. CUDA Tile codegen changes the Tensor
  Core body:
  - keytail: `HMMA=160 -> 128`
  - no-keytail: `HMMA=128 -> 96`
- The conversion count also rises sharply:
  - keytail: `F2F/F2FP=56 -> 112`
  - no-keytail: `F2F/F2FP=48 -> 88`
- Therefore the limiter is not simply the live FP32 `out_acc` register count.
  For this schedule, forcing the output accumulator through BF16 reduces useful
  Tensor Core work and adds conversion pressure.
- The no-keytail control confirms the result is not caused by the 1301-key tail;
  the same negative pattern appears inside the main 1280-token loop.

Decision:

- Keep the current FP32 live `out_acc` in time attention.
- Do not promote `out_acc` BF16 rounding into source.
- Future time-attention work should preserve the current `HMMA=160` keytail /
  `HMMA=128` no-keytail schedule and attack the remaining gap through a
  different softmax/dataflow schedule, not by naively BF16-rounding the output
  accumulator.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 W1/W2 batched-MMA probe

Goal:

- Test whether batching the two W1 or W2 MMA groups inside the current
  FFN12 `split2_pairh32_tk64` CUDA Tile schedule improves A10G Tensor Core
  utilization.
- Keep the same one-kernel `FFN1 -> GELU -> FFN2` fused shape and BF16
  storage path.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1batched2 \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w2batched2_outnoround; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300
done
```

Timing:

```text
baseline w1w2lat2_outnoround:
  best=1.789 ms median=1.797 ms useful=45.74 TF/s roof=65.3%
  checksum=4080.0000

w1batched2:
  best=4.137 ms median=4.144 ms useful=19.78 TF/s roof=28.3%
  checksum=4080.0000

w2batched2_outnoround:
  best=2.317 ms median=2.323 ms useful=35.32 TF/s roof=50.5%
  checksum=4080.0000
```

Resource usage, exact template match from `cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage`:

```text
baseline w1w2lat2_outnoround:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

w1batched2:
  REG=255 STACK=64 SHARED=98304

w2batched2_outnoround:
  REG=250 STACK=0 SHARED=90112
```

SASS count, exact `Function :` section parsing:

```text
baseline w1w2lat2_outnoround:
  instr=5072 HMMA=256 F2FP=64 F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160
  LDG=208 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 STS=124 STG=8 BAR.SYNC=35 DEPBAR=61
  PRMT=16 IMAD=199 IADD3=162 LOP3=145 SHF=201 BRA=3

w1batched2:
  instr=4672 HMMA=128 F2FP=80 F2F=80 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80
  LDG=124 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=16 STS=100 STG=8 BAR.SYNC=20 DEPBAR=8
  PRMT=377 IMAD=381 IADD3=128 LOP3=189 SHF=96 BRA=15

w2batched2_outnoround:
  instr=6544 HMMA=256 F2FP=64 F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160
  LDG=200 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48 STS=148 STG=8 BAR.SYNC=22 DEPBAR=42
  PRMT=305 IMAD=549 IADD3=234 LOP3=218 SHF=176 BRA=7
```

Interpretation:

- `w1batched2` is a severe negative. It cuts the Tensor Core body from
  `HMMA=256` to `HMMA=128`, increases stack usage from 8 to 64 bytes/thread,
  and more than doubles runtime.
- `w2batched2_outnoround` keeps `HMMA=256` and slightly reduces register/shared
  pressure, but the generated integer/permutation/control body grows
  substantially:
  - `instr=5072 -> 6544`
  - `PRMT=16 -> 305`
  - `IMAD=199 -> 549`
  - `IADD3=162 -> 234`
- This means the bottleneck is not just the number of MMA issue sites. For this
  FFN12 schedule, batched W1/W2 grouping makes CUDA Tile generate worse address
  and shuffle code on A10G.

Decision:

- Do not promote either W1 or W2 batched-MMA variant.
- Keep the current `w1w2lat2_outnoround` schedule as the FFN12 candidate
  baseline.
- Future FFN12 work should target reducing address/permutation overhead around
  the existing `HMMA=256` body or changing the data layout, not batching the
  W1/W2 MMA groups in this form.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 hidden-pair manual unroll compile-cost probe

Goal:

- Test whether the gap between the current FFN12 CUDA Tile schedule and the
  A10G dense BF16 ceiling might be reduced by manually constant-unrolling the
  hidden-pair loop.
- The intended runtime question was narrow: preserve the source-like
  `TK64`, pair-H32, W1/W2 `latency=2`, output no-round schedule, then see
  whether removing hidden-pair loop/control/address structure reduces
  non-HMMA instructions such as `PRMT`, `IMAD`, `IADD3`, and `BRA`.

Attempted bench-only changes:

- Added a candidates-only full hidden-pair unroll kernel to
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_cutile.cu`:
  - 16 manually expanded hidden pairs
  - each pair kept the same W1/W2 `ct::mma` structure and `latency=2` load
    hints
- After the full unroll failed the compile-cost test, reduced it to an
  `unroll4` shape:
  - 4 hidden-pair groups
  - 4 manually expanded pairs per group

Compile evidence:

```text
full unroll:
  ninja target: bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile
  nvcc process still running after roughly 3 minutes
  build interrupted and candidate removed

unroll4:
  ninja target: bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile
  nvcc process still running after roughly 2.5 minutes
  build interrupted and candidate removed

after removing the manual-unroll candidate:
  same target rebuilt and linked successfully
```

Validation after removal:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
  --variant fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w1w2lat2_outnoround \
  --warmup 5 --iters 20
```

```text
baseline after removal:
  best=1.795 ms median=1.800 ms useful=45.60 TF/s roof=65.1%
  checksum=4080.0000
```

Interpretation:

- This direction fails before runtime benchmarking. In CUDA Tile, manually
  duplicating a large `ct::mma`/GELU/W2 body creates too much compile-time
  pressure for the focused bench workflow.
- The failure is still useful bottom-cause evidence:
  - the current FFN12 body is already large enough that structural code
    duplication can make CUDA Tile codegen impractical
  - future attempts to reduce loop/address overhead should not copy the whole
    Tile body many times
  - a lighter direction is needed, such as data layout changes, smaller helper
    structure changes, or compiler hints that preserve a single compact body

Decision:

- Do not keep the manual-unroll candidate in source or bench.
- Do not pursue full hidden-pair unroll or unroll4 as an A10G FFN12 direction.
- Continue targeting the existing compact `HMMA=256` schedule with changes that
  reduce non-HMMA work without exploding CUDA Tile compile cost.

### 2026-06-05 - CUDA Tile-first runtime gap audit

Goal:

- Re-anchor the project around the actual long-term objective:
  CUDA Tile-first implementation, no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN/raw WMMA/inline MMA
  implementation path, and A10G-specific bottom-cause optimization.
- Do not remeasure dense BF16 roofline. Use the existing local A10G record
  of about 68-70 TF/s as the comparison ceiling.
- Identify the current remaining gaps from source and Nsight evidence before
  starting the next kernel experiment.

Official CUDA Tile constraints checked:

- NVIDIA's CUDA Tile documentation says CUDA Tile C++ is exposed by
  `cuda_tile.h` / `cuda::tiles`, and that tile kernels are declared with
  `__tile_global__`.
- Tile kernel launch syntax still uses triple chevrons, but the second launch
  parameter must be `1`; the compiler determines intra-block parallelism.
- `ct::mma` is the CUDA Tile matrix multiply-accumulate primitive and supports
  FP32 accumulators with lower-precision input tiles.
- The current build follows the documented requirements:
  `--std=c++20`, `--enable-tile`, and `CUDA::cudart` only.

Traditional implementation-path scan:

```bash
rg -n \
  "cuBLAS|cublas|cuFFT|cufft|cuDNN|cudnn|wmma::|nvcuda::wmma|mma\\.sync|asm\\s*\\(" \
  CMakeLists.txt src bench \
  --glob '!bench/README.md' \
  --glob '!bench/*.sqlite' \
  --glob '!bench/*.nsys-rep'
```

Result:

```text
no matches
```

Source Tile coverage snapshot:

```text
src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu:          69 __tile_global__ kernels
src/tensor_cuda_tile.cu:       24 __tile_global__ kernels
src/tensor_cuda_glue.cu:       13 __tile_global__ kernels
src/stft_cuda_tile.cu:         11 __tile_global__ kernels
src/mbr_time_attention_cutile.cu: 6 __tile_global__ kernels
src/mbr_ffn12_cutile.cu:        5 __tile_global__ kernels
src/chunk_cuda_tile.cu:         2 __tile_global__ kernels
src/mbr_residual_cutile.cu:     2 __tile_global__ kernels
```

Interpretation:

- The current source tree is already CUDA Tile-first at the implementation-path
  level: no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN/raw WMMA/inline MMA hits in `src`, `bench`, or
  `CMakeLists.txt`.
- The remaining objective is not mostly "remove libraries"; it is to remove
  non-optimal CUDA Tile dataflow:
  - too many small Tile kernels
  - BF16/FP32 boundary conversions
  - global-memory materialization between producer and consumer
  - CUDA Tile codegen shapes that lose HMMA or generate excessive integer and
    permutation instructions

Current runtime profile anchor:

```bash
sqlite3 bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite "
select printf('%.3f', sum(k.end-k.start)/1000000.0) total_ms, count(*) calls
from CUPTI_ACTIVITY_KIND_KERNEL k;"
```

```text
total GPU kernel time: 887.482 ms
kernel launches:       6505
```

Top runtime kernels:

```text
ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel:
  calls=96 total=177.050 ms avg=1.844 ms REG=255 SHARED=98304

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel:
  calls=48 total=163.238 ms avg=3.401 ms REG=237 SHARED=40960

linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel, M=78048 N=1536 K=256:
  calls=48 total=64.519 ms avg=1.344 ms REG=163 SHARED=98304

qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel, M=78048 N=1536 K=256:
  calls=48 total=61.046 ms avg=1.272 ms REG=165 SHARED=98304

linear_cutile_static_full_bf16_kernel, M=78048 N=256 K=512:
  calls=96 total=50.839 ms avg=0.530 ms REG=243 SHARED=98304

linear_cutile_static_padded_m_bf16_kernel, M=1312 N=1024 K=1024:
  calls=480 total=42.021 ms avg=0.088 ms REG=116 SHARED=32768

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel:
  calls=48 total=38.840 ms avg=0.809 ms REG=92 SHARED=22528

split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64_kernel:
  calls=48 total=36.850 ms avg=0.768 ms REG=40 SHARED=0

rms_norm_d256_to_bf16_kernel:
  calls=288 total=35.484 ms avg=0.123 ms REG=16 SHARED=16

residual_add_rms_norm_d256_to_bf16_kernel:
  calls=96 total=23.779 ms avg=0.248 ms REG=19 SHARED=16

rotary_time_split_contig_inplace_kernel:
  calls=48 total=23.670 ms avg=0.493 ms REG=20 SHARED=0

apply_gates_and_merge_heads_bf16_token_d64_cutile_kernel:
  calls=96 total=23.570 ms avg=0.246 ms REG=24 SHARED=2048
```

Conversion / dtype-boundary launch evidence:

```text
tensor_tile::f32_to_bf16_kernel:
  calls=1168 total=3.730 ms avg=0.003 ms

gather_freqs_fold_complex_to_bf16_kernel:
  calls=4 total=0.466 ms avg=0.117 ms
```

Notes:

- The direct conversion kernels are not the dominant time by themselves, but
  their call count is proof that the graph still has FP32/BF16 boundary churn.
- RMSNorm, tanh, and GLU BF16-to-BF16 kernels still do internal FP32 math for
  reductions or transcendental/activation evaluation. Those are not external
  conversion kernels, but they remain part of the "all BF16 arithmetic" research
  question.
- STFT/iSTFT are CUDA Tile, not cuFFT, but they are intentionally still FP32
  complex signal processing paths. They are outside Tensor Core BF16 GEMM
  utilization and should be treated separately from the 70 TF/s Tensor Core
  objective.

Current bottom-cause map:

1. FFN12 is the largest single hotspot.
   - Current best source path reaches about 45-46 TF/s useful throughput,
     around 65% of the 70 TF/s local dense ceiling.
   - It is already one-kernel fused and CUDA Tile MMA-based.
   - The main limiter is not library dispatch; it is CUDA Tile codegen under
     `REG=255`, `SHARED=98304`, high address/permutation work, and fragile HMMA
     preservation.
   - Several apparently reasonable variants have already been closed as
     negative because they reduce HMMA or increase generated integer/shuffle
     instructions.

2. Time attention is the strongest Tensor Core path so far.
   - Current source-like path reaches about 60 TF/s useful throughput, about
     86-87% of the local 70 TF/s ceiling.
   - The remaining gap is the online softmax/dataflow body, not QK/AV Tensor
     Core availability.
   - BF16-rounding live softmax/output state reduced registers but changed
     codegen and lost HMMA, so future work must preserve the current HMMA
     schedule.

3. QKV and long linear paths are already BF16 CUDA Tile MMA, but still have
   high launch and materialization cost.
   - Time QKV split-contiguous producer and BKN linear are large enough to
     matter.
   - Prior split/store/pair-K/compound experiments show that small source
     spelling changes can easily increase register pressure or store/address
     work.

4. Freq attention is CUDA Tile, fixed-shape, and BF16 storage, but it remains
   much less compute-dense than time attention.
   - It should not be optimized by blindly increasing Q rows.
   - The likely next useful question is whether the producer/rotary/pad path
     can be reshaped so the attention kernel sees a cleaner fixed padded layout
     without extra global-memory staging.

5. The graph is still launch-heavy.
   - 6505 launches for the profiled run means that many small Tile kernels
     survive around RMSNorm, residual, permute/copy/cat, tanh/GLU, STFT/iSTFT,
     and mask scatter.
   - This is now a fusion/dataflow problem inside CUDA Tile, not a question of
     replacing cuBLAS/cuFFT.

Next experiment selection rules:

- Do not start from "can this be more BF16?" unless the expected codegen risk
  is measurable. Prior BF16 rounding probes often reduced registers but lost
  HMMA or added `F2F/F2FP`.
- Prefer experiments that preserve the current best HMMA count and attack the
  non-HMMA body:
  - FFN12: reduce address/permutation/integer overhead around the existing
    `HMMA=256` schedule, or change data layout so address math is simpler.
  - Time attention: preserve the `Q64/K32` main schedule and test softmax
    state/dataflow changes that do not reduce HMMA.
  - QKV/linear: focus on producer-consumer fusion only when it deletes a real
    global-memory write/read pair and does not duplicate a large MMA body.
  - Freq path: focus on fixed padded layout and producer cleanup, not larger
    Q-row tiles that already measured worse.
- Every promoted kernel must have:
  - runtime timing
  - SASS HMMA and conversion counts
  - register/shared memory usage
  - output drift
  - end-to-end impact

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 two-stage fusion-boundary probe

Goal:

- Test whether the largest FFN12 hotspot is over-fused for A10G.
- The current fused `FFN1 -> GELU -> FFN2` CUDA Tile kernel is fast but sits at
  `REG=255`, `STACK=8`, and `SHARED=98304`, so the question is whether letting
  hidden activations fall to BF16 global memory enables better individual GEMM
  schedules that compensate for the extra launch and hidden read/write.
- Keep the experiment CUDA Tile-only and source-matching enough for the current
  FFN12 path: BF16 storage, poly9 GELU, `TK64`, `M=78048`, `K=256`,
  `hidden=1024`, `out=256`.

Implementation:

- Added focused small target `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile`.
- The first attempt added this as a variant to the existing
  `bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile` translation unit, but the rebuild stayed
  in `nvcc` for more than 3 minutes. That path was aborted and removed from the
  large TU to avoid repeating the manual-unroll compile-cost failure.
- The standalone bench contains:
  - `fused_pairh32_lat2`: compact current-shape fused control
  - `two_stage_poly9_tk64`: `ffn1_poly9_tk64_kernel` writes BF16 hidden, then
    `ffn2_tn64_kernel` reads hidden and writes BF16 output

Commands:

```bash
cmake -S . -B build-min -G Ninja \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=86

ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant two_stage_poly9_tk64 --warmup 30 --iters 500

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile | c++filt | \
  rg -A3 -B1 "ffn1_poly9_tk64_kernel|ffn2_tn64_kernel|ffn12_fused_pairh32_lat2_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/ffn12_two_stage.sass
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  launches=1 best=1.7879 ms median=1.7957 ms
  useful=45.77 TF/s roof70=65.4%
  hidden_rw=0.000 GiB checksum=4080.0000

two_stage_poly9_tk64:
  launches=2 best=2.4605 ms median=2.4668 ms
  useful=33.26 TF/s roof70=47.5%
  hidden_rw=0.298 GiB checksum=4080.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

ffn1_poly9_tk64:
  REG=96 STACK=0 SHARED=61440

ffn2_tn64:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  instr=5072 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2FP=64 F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=61 IMAD=199 IADD3=162 PRMT=16 LOP3=145 SHF=201

ffn1_poly9_tk64:
  instr=1632 HMMA=64 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=24 LDG=48 STS=36 STG=2
  F2FP=16 F2F=16 FFMA=128 FADD=64 FMUL=80
  BAR=5 DEPBAR=15 IMAD=32 IADD3=53 PRMT=25 LOP3=65 SHF=67

ffn2_tn64:
  instr=2416 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=96 LDG=144 STS=108 STG=2
  F2FP=16 F2F=16 FADD=16
  BAR=20 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=65 IADD3=108 PRMT=31 LOP3=126 SHF=104
```

Interpretation:

- This rules out a simple "deeper fusion is the problem" answer for FFN12.
  The fused kernel is resource-constrained, but the two-stage split is much
  slower: `45.77 -> 33.26 TF/s`.
- The first stage becomes light (`REG=96`, `SHARED=61440`), but the second
  stage remains a heavy `REG=230`, `SHARED=98304` CUDA Tile GEMM. Splitting the
  fusion boundary therefore does not eliminate the main W2 staging footprint.
- The two-stage path also pays a real global-memory bill: about `0.298 GiB` of
  BF16 hidden write/read per FFN12 invocation plus a second launch.
- `ffn2_tn64` keeps `HMMA=256`, so the loss is not a Tensor Core fallback. It
  is the combination of unchanged heavy W2 shared-memory staging, extra
  materialization traffic, and lower end-to-end math density.
- Therefore the next FFN12 direction should keep the W1/GELU/W2 fusion boundary
  intact and attack the fused kernel's internal staging/codegen, not split FFN12
  into a hidden global buffer.

Decision:

- Keep `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile` as a focused diagnostic target.
- Do not move FFN12 source inference to a two-stage hidden-buffer design.
- Future FFN12 work should target W2 staging/shared-memory structure inside the
  fused kernel while preserving `HMMA=256` and avoiding additional hidden
  global-memory traffic.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 two-stage FFN2 TN sweep

Goal:

- Continue the two-stage negative probe by asking whether the remaining heavy
  FFN2 kernel is just using the wrong output-N tile for A10G.
- Keep this CUDA Tile-only: the new variants only instantiate the existing
  `ffn2_tn64_kernel` body with `TN=32/64/128`, still using `ct::mma` and
  `<<<grid, 1>>>` tile-kernel launches.

Implementation:

- Extended `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile` with focused variants:
  - `two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn32`
  - `two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64`
  - `two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn128`
- Kept `two_stage_poly9_tk64` as a compatibility alias for `TN64`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn32 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn128 --warmup 30 --iters 500

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile | c++filt | \
  rg -A3 -B1 "ffn1_poly9_tk64_kernel|ffn2_tn64_kernel|ffn12_fused_pairh32_lat2_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/ffn12_two_stage_tn_sweep.sass
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  launches=1 best=1.7878 ms median=1.7949 ms
  useful=45.78 TF/s roof70=65.4%
  hidden_rw=0.000 GiB checksum=4080.0000

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn32:
  launches=2 best=3.3348 ms median=3.3371 ms
  useful=24.54 TF/s roof70=35.1%
  hidden_rw=0.298 GiB checksum=4080.0000

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64:
  launches=2 best=2.4592 ms median=2.4666 ms
  useful=33.28 TF/s roof70=47.5%
  hidden_rw=0.298 GiB checksum=4080.0000

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn128:
  launches=2 best=2.6023 ms median=2.6169 ms
  useful=31.45 TF/s roof70=44.9%
  hidden_rw=0.298 GiB checksum=4080.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
ffn2_tn32:  REG=138 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
ffn2_tn64:  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
ffn2_tn128: REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

ffn1_poly9_tk64:
  REG=96 STACK=0 SHARED=61440

fused_pairh32_lat2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
ffn2_tn32:
  instr=1856 HMMA=128 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=64 LDG=104 STS=72 STG=1
  F2FP=8 F2F=8 FADD=8
  BAR=13 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=43 IADD3=127 PRMT=6 LOP3=145 SHF=116

ffn2_tn64:
  instr=2416 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=96 LDG=144 STS=108 STG=2
  F2FP=16 F2F=16 FADD=16
  BAR=20 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=65 IADD3=108 PRMT=31 LOP3=126 SHF=104

ffn2_tn128:
  instr=3728 HMMA=512 LDSM=256 LDGSTS=160 LDG=224 STS=172 STG=4
  F2FP=32 F2F=32 FADD=32
  BAR=18 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=120 IADD3=107 PRMT=32 LOP3=124 SHF=102
```

Interpretation:

- `TN64` remains the best two-stage FFN2 output tile. The narrower `TN32`
  lowers registers (`230 -> 138`) but does not lower shared memory
  (`98304` bytes), so it does not open more CTA residency on A10G. It also
  doubles the output-N grid (`kOut / 32` instead of `kOut / 64`) and is the
  slowest measured path.
- `TN128` doubles the HMMA body (`256 -> 512`) and halves the output-N grid,
  but it pushes registers to `255` and increases static load/store/staging
  instructions. It is slower than `TN64`.
- All FFN2 variants still emit BF16 Tensor Core HMMA, so the loss is not a
  Tensor Core fallback. The bottom cause is that CUDA Tile's current W2
  staging for this body keeps `96KB` shared memory across all tested N tiles,
  while changing N only trades register pressure and grid count.
- This closes the simple one-axis FFN2 output tile search for the two-stage
  design. Future FFN12 work should stop trying to save the split design with
  `TN` alone and instead change the fused kernel's W2 staging structure or
  hidden-to-W2 schedule while preserving the fused boundary and `HMMA=256`.

Decision:

- Keep source FFN12 fused.
- Keep `TN64` as the two-stage diagnostic baseline.
- Do not promote `TN32` or `TN128` two-stage variants.

### 2026-06-05 - FFN12 fused output-no-round latency sweep in standalone bench

Goal:

- Close the source-matching FFN12 latency gap without adding more variants to
  the already expensive `bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile` translation unit.
- Previous W1/W2 latency sweeps mostly used the older output-accumulator-round
  path. The current source-relevant path is pair-H32/TK64 with W1/W2
  `latency=2` and output accumulator no-round, so test the adjacent latency
  combinations under that condition.

Implementation:

- First attempted to add four output-no-round latency variants to
  `bench_bf16_ffn12_cutile.cu`. Rebuilding
  `bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile` stayed in `nvcc` for more than three
  minutes, so the change was removed.
- Reused the focused `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile` target instead.
  The fused control kernel now templates the W1-side and W2-side load latency
  hints:
  - `fused_pairh32_lat1_outnoround` (`W1=1`, `W2=1`)
  - `fused_pairh32_w1lat1_w2lat2_outnoround` (`W1=1`, `W2=2`)
  - `fused_pairh32_w1lat2_w2lat1_outnoround` (`W1=2`, `W2=1`)
  - `fused_pairh32_lat2` (`W1=2`, `W2=2`)
  - `fused_pairh32_lat3_outnoround` (`W1=3`, `W2=3`)
- The standalone target rebuilt in about 25 seconds, making it the preferred
  workflow for small FFN12 schedule probes.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_pairh32_lat1_outnoround \
  fused_pairh32_w1lat1_w2lat2_outnoround \
  fused_pairh32_w1lat2_w2lat1_outnoround \
  fused_pairh32_lat2 \
  fused_pairh32_lat3_outnoround; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done

cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile | c++filt | \
  rg -A3 -B1 "ffn12_fused_pairh32_lat2_kernel"

cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile | \
  c++filt > /tmp/ffn12_fused_latency_outnoround.sass
```

Timing:

```text
W1=1, W2=1:
  best=1.9124 ms median=1.9411 ms
  useful=42.80 TF/s roof70=61.1%

W1=1, W2=2:
  best=1.8594 ms median=1.9737 ms
  useful=44.01 TF/s roof70=62.9%

W1=2, W2=1:
  best=1.8094 ms median=1.8160 ms
  useful=45.23 TF/s roof70=64.6%

W1=2, W2=2:
  best=1.7887 ms median=1.7949 ms
  useful=45.75 TF/s roof70=65.4%

W1=3, W2=3:
  best=3.4689 ms median=3.4724 ms
  useful=23.59 TF/s roof70=33.7%
```

Resource usage:

```text
W1=1, W2=1: REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
W1=1, W2=2: REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
W1=2, W2=1: REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
W1=2, W2=2: REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
W1=3, W2=3: REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
W1=1, W2=1:
  instr=5280 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2FP=64 F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160
  BAR=32 DEPBAR=63 IMAD=246 IADD3=182 PRMT=55 LOP3=171 SHF=189

W1=1, W2=2:
  instr=5232 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2FP=64 F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160
  BAR=32 DEPBAR=62 IMAD=227 IADD3=167 PRMT=52 LOP3=146 SHF=189

W1=2, W2=1:
  instr=5184 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2FP=64 F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160
  BAR=33 DEPBAR=64 IMAD=236 IADD3=160 PRMT=16 LOP3=152 SHF=193

W1=2, W2=2:
  instr=5072 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=208 STS=124 STG=8
  F2FP=64 F2F=64 FFMA=256 FADD=192 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=61 IMAD=199 IADD3=162 PRMT=16 LOP3=145 SHF=201

W1=3, W2=3:
  instr=3312 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=136 STS=74 STG=8
  F2FP=48 F2F=48 FFMA=128 FADD=128 FMUL=80
  BAR=20 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=228 IADD3=116 PRMT=12 LOP3=128 SHF=119
```

Interpretation:

- The current source-style `W1=2, W2=2` path remains the best tested
  output-no-round latency schedule. It is the only variant with `STACK=8`, but
  it has the best runtime and the lowest static instruction count among the
  `HMMA=256` variants.
- `W1=3, W2=3` is a Tensor Core schedule cliff. It drops from `HMMA=256` to
  `HMMA=128`, explaining the fall to `23.59 TF/s`.
- `W1=1, W2=1` and `W1=1, W2=2` preserve `HMMA=256`, but they have higher
  scalar/control work than `2/2`, especially `PRMT` and `IMAD`.
- `W1=2, W2=1` is close in timing, but still loses to `2/2`; the main static
  difference is more integer work (`IMAD=236` versus `199`) and slightly higher
  non-HMMA instruction count.
- Therefore the latency result is not a resource-count win. The bottom cause is
  CUDA Tile codegen schedule quality around the same BF16 Tensor Core body:
  `2/2` keeps `HMMA=256`, keeps `PRMT=16`, and minimizes integer/control work.

Decision:

- Keep source FFN12 on W1/W2 `latency=2` plus output accumulator no-round.
- Do not promote `1/1`, `1/2`, `2/1`, or `3/3` output-no-round schedules.
- Continue using the standalone FFN12 target for small schedule probes; the
  large candidates translation unit is now too expensive for fine-grained
  template enumeration.

### 2026-06-05 - Reusable CUDA Tile-first audit and gap-analysis gates

Goal:

- Turn the project direction into repeatable local gates instead of ad-hoc
  manual commands:
  - no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN/raw WMMA/inline MMA/inline asm in source/CMake/bench
  - no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN dynamic dependency or binary symbol/string evidence
  - repeatable Nsight sqlite hotspot and dtype-boundary summaries
  - repeatable `cuobjdump` resource/SASS op-count summaries for CUDA Tile
    kernel variants

Added scripts:

```bash
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
./bench/nsys_kernel_summary.sh bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite 20
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer 'ffn12_fused256' 12
```

Current audit result:

```text
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8
  ninja: no work to do.

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
  source/CMake/bench forbidden path scan: OK
  dynamic dependencies:
    libcudart.so.13
    libstdc++.so.6
    libm.so.6
    libgcc_s.so.1
    libc.so.6
  cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN dynamic dependency: none
  forbidden dynamic symbols: none
  forbidden binary strings: none
  PASS

src line count:
  16394 total
```

Current Nsight sqlite summary from existing traces:

```text
bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite:
  kernel launches: 6505
  top CUDA kernels:
    ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel:      96 calls, 177.050 ms
    time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input:   48 calls, 163.238 ms
    linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel:        48 calls,  64.519 ms
    qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel:          48 calls,  61.046 ms
    linear_cutile_static_full_bf16_kernel:            96 calls,  50.839 ms
    linear_cutile_static_padded_m_bf16_kernel:       480 calls,  42.021 ms
    freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel:      48 calls,  38.840 ms
    split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64:     48 calls,  36.850 ms
    rms_norm_d256_to_bf16_kernel:                    288 calls,  35.484 ms
    residual_add_rms_norm_d256_to_bf16_kernel:        96 calls,  23.779 ms

bench/nsys_full_bf16_arith_current_clean.sqlite:
  kernel launches: 6505
  top CUDA kernels:
    ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel:      96 calls, 196.090 ms
    time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input:   48 calls, 162.891 ms
    qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel:          48 calls,  68.628 ms
    linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel:        48 calls,  68.155 ms
```

Interpretation:

- Explicit standalone `f32_to_bf16_kernel` is visible but not the main runtime
  limiter: `1168` calls, about `3.73 ms` in both existing traces.
- The main remaining gap is inside CUDA Tile kernels that already use BF16
  storage and Tensor Core MMA, especially FFN12, time attention, QKV/linear BKN,
  frequency attention, rotary, gate merge, and norm/residual kernels.
- Enabling the current full-BF16 arithmetic switch is not automatically faster:
  FFN12 moves from about `177.05 ms` to `196.09 ms`, QKV/linear BKN also slow
  down. This reinforces the previous conclusion that some BF16 round-trip
  spellings perturb CUDA Tile codegen and increase scalar/control work.

FFN12 `cuobjdump` filter check:

```text
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer 'ffn12_fused256' 12

resource usage:
  representative ffn12_fused256 variants: REG=255, STACK=0, SHARED=98304

SASS op counts:
  best filtered rows keep HMMA=256 and LDSM=128/LDGSTS=80.
  example source-relevant row:
    instr=3560 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
    F2FP=72 FFMA=490 FADD=290 FMUL=224 MUFU=66
    BAR=35 DEPBAR=30 IMAD=202 IADD3=224 PRMT=74 LOP3=233 SHF=268
```

Bottom-level conclusion:

- The current project path is already library-free and CUDA Tile-first at the
  binary/source audit level.
- The next bottleneck is not "replace cuBLAS/cuFFT/raw WMMA"; that gate now has
  a reusable proof script.
- The next A10G work should attack CUDA Tile schedule/dataflow causes:
  - FFN12: reduce the `98KB` shared-memory footprint or find a spelling that
    keeps `HMMA=256` while lowering `IMAD/PRMT/BAR/DEPBAR`.
  - Time attention: improve the `Q64/K32` online-softmax Tile expression without
    losing HMMA or increasing row-state overhead.
  - QKV/linear BKN: continue shape/dataflow probes around `TM32/TN256/TK16`,
    because current variants are BF16 Tile MMA but one-CTA/high-shared-memory
    limited.
  - Freq attention/rotary: focus on producer-consumer layout fusion only when it
    removes real global-memory traffic without forcing a worse CUDA Tile
    schedule.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 fused TN64/y2 output split

Goal:

- Test whether the FFN12 `98KB` shared-memory footprint and `REG=255` pressure
  can be reduced by narrowing the output accumulator tile from `N=128` to
  `N=64`, while staying inside one fused CUDA Tile kernel.
- Keep the experiment in the standalone
  `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile` target, not the source inference path.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- NVIDIA's CUDA Tile guide says tile kernels launch as a grid of tile blocks and
  C++ Tile kernels must use `<<<grid, 1>>>`, so using `grid.y` to cover output
  tile groups is valid Tile launch structure.
- The guide also states tile shapes are compile-time types and each dimension
  must be a power of two, so `TN=64`, `TM=32`, `TK=64`, and `THidden=32` are
  legal CUDA Tile shapes.
- The `ct::mma` examples use BF16/FP16-like input tiles with an explicit FP32
  accumulator and cast on store; this matches the current FFN12 spelling.
- The latency annotations stay within the documented `cutile::hint(...,
  latency=N)` load/store hint semantics.

Change:

- Added `fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2_outnoround` in
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.
- The new kernel uses `OutAccTile = [32,64]`, computes two adjacent 64-column
  output tiles per CTA, and launches `grid=(M/32, 2, 1)` to cover all 256
  output columns.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 10 --iters 80
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2_outnoround --warmup 10 --iters 80
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile 'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|tn64_y2)' 20
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.7922 ms median=1.7975 ms
  useful=45.66 TF/s roof70=65.2% checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2_outnoround:
  best=3.3322 ms median=3.3368 ms
  useful=24.56 TF/s roof70=35.1% checksum=4080.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

TN64/y2:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

TN64/y2:
  instr=2944 HMMA=288 LDSM=168 LDGSTS=96 LDG=128 STS=54 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=384 FADD=176 FMUL=240
  BAR=27 DEPBAR=48 IMAD=145 IADD3=144 PRMT=92 LOP3=129 SHF=183
```

Interpretation:

- Narrowing the output tile to `TN=64` does not reduce the CUDA Tile compiler's
  shared-memory allocation. The kernel still uses `98304` bytes of shared
  memory per CTA.
- The `grid.y=2` split duplicates FFN1/GELU work for each M tile. Static work
  per logical M tile rises from one `HMMA=256` CTA to two `HMMA=288` CTAs.
- Runtime falls from `45.66 TF/s` useful to `24.56 TF/s` useful even though the
  output checksum matches.
- Therefore the FFN12 limiter is not solved by splitting output N across CTA
  columns. With CUDA Tile 13.3 on A10G, this spelling preserves the same shared
  memory cliff and adds duplicated Tensor Core/scalar work.

Decision:

- Do not migrate TN64/y2 to the source inference path.
- Keep attacking FFN12 through dataflow/codegen changes that reduce shared
  staging or non-HMMA instruction count without recomputing FFN1.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 fused TN64x4 same-CTA output split

Goal:

- Avoid the main flaw in the previous TN64/y2 probe: duplicated FFN1/GELU work
  across `grid.y=2`.
- Keep one CTA per M tile and compute the full 256 output columns in that CTA,
  but express the output as four `32x64` accumulators instead of two `32x128`
  accumulators.
- This tests whether the CUDA Tile compiler's W2 staging/shared-memory pressure
  improves when the W2 tile is `THidden x 64`, without changing the fused
  boundary or recomputing FFN1.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- `TM=32`, `TK=64`, `THidden=32`, and `TN=64` are all power-of-two compile-time
  tile dimensions, satisfying the CUDA Tile shape constraint.
- The variant keeps `ct::partition_view`, `ct::mma`, and `cutile::hint` in the
  same documented style as the current source-matching FFN12 kernel.
- The launch stays `<<<grid, 1>>>`, one CUDA thread block per CUDA Tile block.

Change:

- Added `fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround` in
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.
- The kernel holds four `OutAccTile=[32,64]` accumulators and eight W2 tiles per
  hidden pair, but only computes W1/GELU once per M tile.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 10 --iters 80
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround --warmup 10 --iters 80
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile 'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|tn64x4|tn64_y2)' 24
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.8074 ms median=1.8241 ms
  useful=45.28 TF/s roof70=64.7% checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround:
  best=4.0244 ms median=4.0897 ms
  useful=20.34 TF/s roof70=29.1% checksum=4080.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

TN64x4:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

TN64x4:
  instr=1800 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=100 STS=58 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=26 DEPBAR=20 IMAD=281 IADD3=135 PRMT=76 LOP3=146 SHF=116
```

Interpretation:

- Same-CTA `TN64x4` avoids the previous `grid.y=2` FFN1 duplication, but it
  still does not reduce the CUDA Tile compiler's shared-memory allocation:
  `SHARED=98304` remains unchanged.
- The source spelling creates a Tensor Core schedule cliff. Static HMMA drops
  from `256` to `128`, while integer/permutation work remains high
  (`IMAD=281`, `PRMT=76`).
- Runtime drops to `20.34 TF/s` useful despite matching output checksum.
- Therefore expressing the full output as four `32x64` accumulator tiles inside
  one CTA is worse than the current two `32x128` accumulator spelling. The
  problem is CUDA Tile codegen/staging quality, not simply output accumulator
  element count.

Decision:

- Do not migrate same-CTA `TN64x4` to the source inference path.
- Treat FFN12 output-N splitting as closed for now: `grid.y` repeats FFN1, and
  same-CTA `TN64x4` loses HMMA while preserving the `98KB` shared-memory cliff.
- Next FFN12 experiments should change W2 staging order or hidden-pair
  scheduling without changing the output accumulator tiling away from the
  current two `32x128` accumulators.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 W2 streaming live-range reduction

Goal:

- Keep the current source-relevant two `32x128` output accumulators and avoid
  changing output-N tiling.
- Test whether reducing live W2 tile pressure improves CUDA Tile codegen:
  instead of declaring four W2 tiles (`w2_00`, `w2_01`, `w2_10`, `w2_11`) before
  the four W2 MMAs, reuse one `W2Tile` variable and immediately run each MMA
  after its load.
- This keeps one CTA per M tile and does not duplicate FFN1/GELU.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The kernel keeps the same valid Tile shapes as the source-matching baseline:
  `TM=32`, `TK=64`, `THidden=32`, `OutHalf=128`.
- It stays within official `partition_view` + `ct::mma` + `cutile::hint`
  semantics. The source-level difference is only W2 load/MMA scheduling.

Change:

- Added `fused_pairh32_w2stream_lat2_outnoround` in
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_w2stream_lat2_outnoround --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile 'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|w2stream)' 20
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.8036 ms median=1.8162 ms
  useful=45.38 TF/s roof70=64.8% checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_w2stream_lat2_outnoround:
  best=3.8080 ms median=3.9141 ms
  useful=21.49 TF/s roof70=30.7% checksum=4080.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

W2 streaming:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

W2 streaming:
  instr=1736 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=96 STS=34 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=22 DEPBAR=16 IMAD=262 IADD3=121 PRMT=12 LOP3=131 SHF=120
```

Interpretation:

- Reusing one `W2Tile` variable does not reduce shared-memory allocation:
  `SHARED=98304` remains unchanged.
- The source spelling triggers the same class of Tensor Core schedule cliff seen
  in `TN64x4`: static HMMA drops from `256` to `128`.
- The lower static instruction count is misleading because half the Tensor Core
  body disappeared. Runtime falls to `21.49 TF/s` useful.
- Therefore this CUDA Tile compiler does not benefit from source-level W2 tile
  live-range shortening here; the original four-W2-tile spelling is required to
  preserve the full HMMA schedule.

Decision:

- Do not migrate W2 streaming to the source inference path.
- Keep the current source FFN12 W2 load/MMA ordering.
- Future FFN12 probes should avoid "load one W2 tile then MMA immediately"
  spellings unless a newer CUDA Tile compiler changes this schedule behavior.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 W2 pairwise live-range reduction

Goal:

- Follow up the W2 streaming failure with a less aggressive W2 live-range
  change.
- Keep the current source-relevant two `32x128` output accumulators and one CTA
  per M tile.
- Instead of holding all four W2 tiles live (`w2_00`, `w2_01`, `w2_10`,
  `w2_11`) or streaming one tile at a time, load the two W2 tiles for
  `out_acc0`, run both MMAs, then load the two W2 tiles for `out_acc1`.
- This tests whether reducing live W2 tiles from four to two preserves the
  full `HMMA=256` schedule.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The variant keeps the same legal power-of-two Tile shapes as the source
  baseline: `TM=32`, `TK=64`, `THidden=32`, and `OutHalf=128`.
- It stays inside the official `partition_view` + `ct::mma` +
  `cutile::hint(... latency=2)` style. The only semantic-preserving change is
  W2 load/MMA ordering.

Change:

- Added `fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround` in
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

for v in \
  fused_pairh32_lat2 \
  fused_pairh32_w2stream_lat2_outnoround \
  fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 10 --iters 120
done

for r in 1 2 3; do
  for v in fused_pairh32_lat2 fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround; do
    ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
      --variant "$v" --warmup 20 --iters 300
  done
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|w2stream|w2pair)' 30

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
git diff --check
```

Timing:

```text
120-iter pass:
  fused_pairh32_lat2:
    best=1.7917 ms median=1.7991 ms
    useful=45.68 TF/s roof70=65.3%

  fused_pairh32_w2stream_lat2_outnoround:
    best=3.5652 ms median=3.5682 ms
    useful=22.95 TF/s roof70=32.8%

  fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround:
    best=3.4499 ms median=3.4534 ms
    useful=23.72 TF/s roof70=33.9%

300-iter repeat:
  fused_pairh32_lat2:
    best=1.7878/1.7910/1.7905 ms
    useful=45.78/45.69/45.71 TF/s

  fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround:
    best=3.4482/3.4455/3.4518 ms
    useful=23.73/23.75/23.71 TF/s
```

Resource usage:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304

W2 streaming:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304

W2 pairwise:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

W2 streaming:
  instr=1736 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=96 STS=34 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=22 DEPBAR=16 IMAD=262 IADD3=121 PRMT=12 LOP3=131 SHF=120

W2 pairwise:
  instr=1720 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=96 STS=34 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=20 DEPBAR=16 IMAD=261 IADD3=121 PRMT=12 LOP3=131 SHF=121
```

Interpretation:

- Pairwise W2 loading slightly improves over one-at-a-time streaming, but it
  still triggers the same CUDA Tile schedule cliff: `HMMA=256 -> 128`,
  `LDSM=128 -> 64`, and `LDGSTS=80 -> 40`.
- The shared-memory allocation remains `98304` bytes, so the attempted
  live-range reduction does not improve CTA residency on A10G.
- The one-register and small barrier-count reduction are irrelevant because the
  Tensor Core body is cut in half. Throughput stays around `23.7 TF/s`, about
  half the baseline `45.7 TF/s`.
- Bottom-level cause: for this CUDA Tile 13.3 FFN12 fused spelling, the compiler
  needs the original four-W2-tile source pattern to generate the full
  `HMMA=256` schedule. Reducing W2 tile lifetime in source changes the Tile
  lowering rather than simply shortening live ranges.

Decision:

- Do not migrate W2 pairwise loading to the source inference path.
- Treat simple W2 live-range reductions as closed for this kernel: one-tile
  streaming and two-tile pairwise loading both lose the full Tensor Core body.
- The next viable FFN12 work should preserve the original four-W2-tile pattern
  or change a larger data layout/staging strategy that can be proven to keep
  `HMMA=256`.

## 2026-06-05 - Time Attention Split-Contig Per-Operand Latency-Hint Probe

Goal:

- Split the earlier negative `latency=2` attention-consumer result into Q-only,
  K-only, V-only, and K+V-only load hints.
- Keep the exact source-relevant time-attention shape:
  split-contiguous `[B,N,H,D]` Q/K/V, `QRows=64`, `KTile=32`, one CUDA Tile
  kernel, BF16 Q/K/V, FP32 online-softmax state and accumulator.
- Explain whether the slowdown came from a specific operand load or from adding
  latency-hinted temporaries into the exact online-softmax schedule.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The CUDA 13.3 Tile Programming Guide says Tile kernels are launched with
  `__tile_global__`, express tile-block work, and leave the lower-level mapping
  to the compiler.
- The CUDA Tile C++ API Reference documents `cutile::hint` as compiler
  optimization metadata. The `latency` hint can be attached to a direct
  `load`/`store` expression, uses integer values `1..10`, and may be ignored by
  the compiler.
- Therefore the probe keeps all variants inside official CUDA Tile semantics
  and judges the result from generated resource/SASS evidence.

Change:

- Generalized the bench-only
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_lat_kernel` to independent
  `QLoadLatency`, `KLoadLatency`, and `VLoadLatency` template parameters.
- Added focused variants:
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_q_lat2`
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_k_lat2`
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_v_lat2`
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_kv_lat2`
  - matching `_no_keytail` variants
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

for v in \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_lat2 \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_q_lat2 \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_k_lat2 \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_v_lat2 \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_kv_lat2 \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input_no_keytail \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_lat2_no_keytail \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_q_lat2_no_keytail \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_k_lat2_no_keytail \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_v_lat2_no_keytail \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_kv_lat2_no_keytail; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_lat_kernel|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32' 80
```

```text
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

```text
default keytail:       REG=233 STACK=0  SHARED=40960
all-load lat2 keytail: REG=255 STACK=0  SHARED=40960
Q-only lat2 keytail:   REG=255 STACK=0  SHARED=40960

V-only lat2 keytail:   REG=255 STACK=64 SHARED=40960
K+V lat2 keytail:      REG=255 STACK=0  SHARED=40960

default no-keytail:       REG=217 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
all-load lat2 no-keytail: REG=218 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
Q-only lat2 no-keytail:   REG=217 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
K-only lat2 no-keytail:   REG=181 STACK=0 SHARED=36864
V-only lat2 no-keytail:   REG=249 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
K+V lat2 no-keytail:      REG=218 STACK=0 SHARED=32768
```

Static SASS count:

```text
default keytail:
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134

Q-only lat2 keytail:
  instr=2800 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=68 IADD3=123 PRMT=80 LOP3=146

K-only lat2 keytail:
  instr=2776 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=12 DEPBAR=4 IMAD=53 IADD3=124 PRMT=80 LOP3=145

V-only lat2 keytail:
  instr=2904 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=16 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=86 IADD3=122 PRMT=80 LOP3=126

K+V lat2 keytail:
  instr=2808 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=7 IMAD=68 IADD3=124 PRMT=80 LOP3=146

default no-keytail:
  instr=2144 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=48 FFMA=306 FADD=162 FMUL=136 MUFU=106
  BAR=13 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=17 IADD3=104 PRMT=0 LOP3=77

Q-only lat2 no-keytail:
  instr=2144 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=48 FFMA=306 FADD=162 FMUL=136 MUFU=106
  BAR=13 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=17 IADD3=104 PRMT=0 LOP3=77

K-only lat2 no-keytail:
  instr=2128 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=48 FFMA=306 FADD=162 FMUL=136 MUFU=106
  BAR=10 DEPBAR=4 IMAD=21 IADD3=106 PRMT=0 LOP3=77

V-only lat2 no-keytail:
  instr=2176 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=48 FFMA=306 FADD=162 FMUL=136 MUFU=106
  BAR=14 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=17 IADD3=104 PRMT=0 LOP3=77

K+V/all-load lat2 no-keytail:
  instr=2152 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  F2FP=48 FFMA=306 FADD=162 FMUL=136 MUFU=106
  BAR=13 DEPBAR=7 IMAD=17 IADD3=104 PRMT=0 LOP3=77
```

Interpretation:

- Per-operand `latency=2` does not find a hidden win in the time-attention
  consumer. All exact keytail variants are slower than the direct-load baseline.
- The Tensor Core schedule is preserved (`HMMA=160`, `LDSM=64`, `LDGSTS=16`),
  so the slowdown is not a lost-HMMA failure. It is register/live-range and
  control-pressure.
- The keytail path is the decisive case for source inference. Any latency hint
  pushes the full exact kernel to the register cliff:
  `REG=233 -> 254/255`, and V-only also spills (`STACK=64`).
- K-only no-keytail is a useful diagnostic: it reduces `BAR/DEPBAR` and
  registers (`217 -> 181`) but still slows down and increases shared memory
  (`32768 -> 36864`). That means reducing dependency barriers alone is not
  enough; the altered Tile load lifetime/shared staging is worse for A10G.
- Q-only no-keytail is exactly static-SASS equivalent to baseline and runtime
  neutral. It has no optimization effect once the keytail is removed.
- Bottom cause: the direct-load source spelling already gives CUDA Tile a good
  schedule for the repeated full-K loop. Adding explicit hinted temporaries
  lengthens live ranges around the online-softmax state and masked tail without
  improving HMMA/LDSM/LDGSTS.

Decision:

- Do not promote Q/K/V/K+V per-operand `latency=2` hints into
  `src/mbr_time_attention_cutile.cu`.
- Keep the direct-load `Q64/K32` split-contiguous attention source path.
- Future time-attention work should stop treating latency hints as the next
  likely lever. The remaining gap needs a different single-kernel dataflow that
  reduces keytail/register pressure while preserving the current
  `HMMA=160, LDSM=64, LDGSTS=16` generated schedule.

## 2026-06-05 - QKV small bench split-contig store-hint no-op closure

Goal:

- Recheck the earlier large-bench observation that
  `qkv_t32x256x16bkn_split_contig_a2_b2_s0` can sometimes match or slightly
  beat all-site `latency=2`.
- Move the exact source-relevant split-contiguous Q/K/V producer comparison
  into the small QKV-only bench before considering any source change.
- Keep the fixed source shape: `TM32 x TN256 x TK16`, BF16 input/output,
  `ct::mma`, split-contiguous Q/K/V stores, and `M=78048,N=1536,K=256`.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- CUDA Tile kernels are compile-time tile-shaped block programs, and
  `cutile::hint(... latency=N)` is compiler optimization metadata attached to a
  load/store site.
- Therefore a store-hint split is only useful if generated resources/SASS or
  stable timings change; identical SASS means the hint split did not alter this
  CUDA Tile lowering.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added small-bench variant:
  - `split_contig_a2_b2_s0`
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

for v in split_contig_lat2 split_contig_a2_b2_s0; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done

for r in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  for v in split_contig_lat2 split_contig_a2_b2_s0; do
    ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
  done
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel' 120

diff -u \
  <(cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile | c++filt | \
    awk 'BEGIN{p=0} /qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, 2, 2, 0>/{p=1; next} /Function :/{if(p) exit} p{print}') \
  <(cuobjdump --dump-sass build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile | c++filt | \
    awk 'BEGIN{p=0} /qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, 2, 2, 2>/{p=1; next} /Function :/{if(p) exit} p{print}')
```

Timing:

```text
first pass:
  split_contig_lat2:      best=1.261 ms median=1.273 ms 48.67 TF/s roof=69.5%
  split_contig_a2_b2_s0:  best=1.251 ms median=1.271 ms 49.07 TF/s roof=70.1%

repeat passes:
  split_contig_lat2:
    best=1.244/1.249/1.275/1.276/1.264 ms
    TF/s=49.34/49.14/48.14/48.08/48.55
  split_contig_a2_b2_s0:
    best=1.264/1.254/1.248/1.254/1.251 ms
    TF/s=48.56/48.97/49.18/48.94/49.06
```

Resource usage:

```text
split_contig_lat2:      REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
split_contig_a2_b2_s0:  REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_a2_b2_s0:
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63
```

Exact SASS diff:

```text
no output
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

git diff --check
pass
```

Interpretation:

- `split_contig_a2_b2_s0` is not a real generated-code win in the small
  source-relevant bench. It keeps the same `REG`, shared memory, Tensor Core
  body, static instruction counts, and exact SASS as `split_contig_lat2`.
- The small timing differences alternate by run and are consistent with noise.
  There is no bottom-level evidence that removing the store hint changes CUDA
  Tile scheduling for this `TK16` split-contiguous producer.
- This also sharpens the earlier large-bench result: the useful QKV hint effect
  is the combined A/B load scheduling family, not the store hint site.

Decision:

- Do not promote `StoreLatency=0` into `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`.
- Keep the source QKV split-contiguous producer on the existing all-site
  `latency=2` spelling for now.
- Future QKV work should move away from tiny store-hint variants and toward a
  real dataflow/staging change that either reduces the global producer-consumer
  boundary or lowers the `98KB` CTA footprint while preserving `HMMA=256`.

## 2026-06-05 - QKV small bench split-contig grid.z closure

Goal:

- Recheck the earlier large-bench `grid.z` component-selection probe in the
  smaller source-relevant QKV bench.
- Keep one launch and the same total CTA count as `split_contig_lat2`, but map
  Q/K/V component selection to `grid.z=3` and per-component `TN256` selection
  to `grid.y=2`.
- Test whether making component identity a block index removes enough
  branch/address overhead to improve the CUDA Tile generated code.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- CUDA Tile block programs are written around compile-time tile shapes; changes
  in grid mapping only matter if they alter the generated resource use, Tensor
  Core body, or scalar address/control path.
- Therefore this probe must be judged by both timing and SASS/resource evidence,
  not by source-level branch count alone.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added small-bench variant:
  - `split_contig_gridz_lat2`
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

for v in split_contig_lat2 split_contig_gridz_lat2; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_split_contig(_gridz)?_kernel' 80
```

Timing:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6), best=1.244 ms, median=1.261 ms,
  49.32 TF/s, roof70=70.5%, checksum=92.0000

split_contig_gridz_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6), best=1.254 ms, median=1.273 ms,
  48.95 TF/s, roof70=69.9%, checksum=92.0000
```

Resource usage:

```text
split_contig_lat2:        REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
split_contig_gridz_lat2:  REG=166 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
```

Static SASS count:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_gridz_lat2:
  instr=1152 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=142 IADD3=82 PRMT=2 LOP3=83 SHF=63
```

Interpretation:

- `grid.z` component selection preserves the Tensor Core body
  (`HMMA=256`, `LDSM=96`, `LDGSTS=80`) and output checksum, but it does not
  reduce the `98KB` shared-memory footprint or the dependency-barrier structure.
- It slightly increases register use (`165 -> 166`) and total static
  instruction count (`1144 -> 1152`). The scalar mix changes are too small and
  in the wrong direction to explain a stable speedup.
- Bottom cause: the remaining QKV gap is not dominated by the source-level
  Q/K/V component branch. It is dominated by the fixed CUDA Tile staging shape
  and producer-consumer boundary around a `TM32 x TN256 x TK16` BF16 MMA body.

Decision:

- Do not promote `split_contig_gridz_lat2` into `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`.
- Keep `split_contig_lat2` as the source-relevant QKV baseline.
- Future QKV work should target a real dataflow change: lower shared-memory
  footprint while keeping `HMMA=256`, or fuse the next consumer so Q/K/V do not
  round-trip through global memory.

## 2026-06-05 - QKV source-relevant resource and occupancy audit

Goal:

- Stop treating the QKV producer gap as only "about 70% of dense BF16 roof" and
  attach a bottom-level cause to the current CUDA Tile source-relevant baseline.
- Add a repeatable, no-performance-counter diagnostic path for A10G resource
  limits because Nsight Compute performance counters are not available in this
  environment.
- Keep this bench-only; source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- CUDA Tile kernels are still CUDA kernels with compiler-generated resource
  usage. The source uses `__tile_global__`, `cuda::tiles`, `ct::mma`, and
  `cutile::hint`, but the final decision must come from generated resources and
  SASS.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added `--describe` to `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile`.
- The mode prints CUDA runtime attributes and occupancy API results for the
  instantiated CUDA Tile kernels:
  - `cudaFuncGetAttributes`
  - `cudaOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor`
  - `cudaGetDeviceProperties`
- No math kernel body was changed.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant all

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --variant split_contig_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, 2, 2, 2>' 20

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
git diff --check
```

Nsight Compute note:

```text
ncu --target-processes all --set basic --section Tile \
  --kernel-name qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel \
  --launch-skip-before-match 5 --launch-count 1 \
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant split_contig_lat2 \
  --warmup 5 --iters 20

ERR_NVGPUCTRPERM - no permission to access NVIDIA GPU Performance Counters
```

Therefore this audit uses runtime resource attributes plus static SASS. It does
not claim unavailable scheduler/pipe utilization counters.

Runtime resource/occupancy output:

```text
GPU: NVIDIA A10G SM 8.6 SMs=80 shared_per_sm=102400B
     regs_per_sm=65536 max_blocks_per_sm=16

baseline:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=170
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 max_threads_per_block=256

lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=163
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 max_threads_per_block=384

split_contig_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=165
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 max_threads_per_block=384

split_contig_gridz_lat2:
  grid=(2439,2,3) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=166
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 max_threads_per_block=384

split_contig_tk64_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=172
  static_shared=81920B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 max_threads_per_block=256
```

Timing:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6) fullM=78048 mem=0.26 GiB
  best=1.257 ms median=1.270 ms 48.81 TF/s roof70=69.7%
  checksum=92.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0

instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
F2FP=32 F2F=0 FFMA=0 FADD=0 FMUL=0 MUFU=0
BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

git diff --check
pass
```

Interpretation:

- The QKV baseline is Tensor Core-backed: `HMMA=256`, `LDSM=96`, and
  `LDGSTS=80` are present. The problem is not that CUDA Tile cannot emit Tensor
  Core code.
- `static_shared=98304B` on an A10G with `shared_per_sm=102400B` hard-limits
  the source-relevant QKV producer to one resident CTA per SM. The runtime
  occupancy API reports `occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1`.
- The previous `TK64` diagnostic now has a clearer explanation: it lowers
  shared memory to `81920B`, but that still gives `shared_limit=1` on this SM.
  It does not cross the threshold needed for two CTAs per SM, while it hurts the
  generated MMA schedule and latency hiding.
- `grid.z` has the same waves/SM and same resident-CTA limit as the baseline,
  so changing component selection cannot fix the bottom-level resource wall.
- The remaining gap to the local dense BF16 reference is therefore dominated by
  one-CTA-per-SM CUDA Tile staging plus scalar/control overhead around
  split-contiguous output (`IMAD=143`, `BAR=33`, `DEPBAR=32`), not by missing
  Tensor Core lowering or output BF16 conversion alone.

Decision:

- Keep `--describe` as a required diagnostic for future QKV CUDA Tile shape
  probes before considering source promotion.
- A useful next QKV experiment must either:
  - reduce static shared below roughly half of A10G's `102400B` per-SM budget
    while preserving `HMMA=256`-class density, or
  - eliminate the Q/K/V producer-consumer global-memory boundary through fusion
    without causing a register/shared cliff.
- Do not spend more time on QKV store/control spelling changes unless this
  resource audit changes.

## 2026-06-05 - QKV small bench TN residency threshold closure

Goal:

- Convert the earlier large-bench `TN32/64/128/256` result into the new
  source-relevant small bench with `--describe` resource diagnostics.
- Specifically test whether crossing the A10G resident-CTA threshold helps:
  `TN32` is the only current split-contig QKV shape whose static shared memory
  is low enough to allow more than one CTA/SM.
- Keep the same CUDA Tile math path: BF16 input/output, `ct::mma`,
  `TM32 x TK16`, split-contiguous Q/K/V stores, and all-site `latency=2`.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- CUDA Tile dimensions are compile-time tile types, so every `TN` value is a
  different generated kernel. The CUDA Tile source path must be judged by
  generated resources/SASS and timing on A10G, not by occupancy intuition alone.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Generalized the small-bench split-contig launcher/runner over `TN`.
- Added bench-only variants:
  - `split_contig_tn32_lat2`
  - `split_contig_tn64_lat2`
  - `split_contig_tn128_lat2`
- Added these variants to `--describe`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant all | \
  rg -e 'GPU:|split_contig_(lat2|tn32_lat2|tn64_lat2|tn128_lat2)'

for v in split_contig_tn32_lat2 split_contig_tn64_lat2 \
         split_contig_tn128_lat2 split_contig_lat2; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel<32, (32|64|128|256), 16, 78048, 1536, 256, 2, 2, 2>' 80

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
git diff --check
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
GPU: NVIDIA A10G SM 8.6 SMs=80 shared_per_sm=102400B

TN32:
  grid=(2439,48,1) waves/SM=1463.4 attr_regs=69
  static_shared=34816B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

TN64:
  grid=(2439,24,1) waves/SM=731.7 attr_regs=90
  static_shared=53248B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

TN128:
  grid=(2439,12,1) waves/SM=365.9 attr_regs=96
  static_shared=90112B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

TN256:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=165
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
TN32:
  best=3.367 ms median=3.369 ms 18.23 TF/s roof70=26.0%

TN64:
  best=2.083 ms median=2.084 ms 29.47 TF/s roof70=42.1%

TN128:
  best=1.682 ms median=1.685 ms 36.49 TF/s roof70=52.1%

TN256:
  best=1.274 ms median=1.285 ms 48.19 TF/s roof70=68.8%
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
TN32:
  REG=69 SHARED=34816
  instr=576 HMMA=32 LDSM=32 LDGSTS=32 STS=4 STG=3
```
