```text
  F2FP=4 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=47 IADD3=91 PRMT=0 LOP3=90 SHF=71

TN64:
  REG=90 SHARED=53248
  instr=672 HMMA=64 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=32 STS=4 STG=3
  F2FP=8 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=46 IADD3=94 PRMT=17 LOP3=83 SHF=71

TN128:
  REG=96 SHARED=90112
  instr=856 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=48 STS=4 STG=3
  F2FP=16 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=100 IADD3=94 PRMT=0 LOP3=90 SHF=73

TN256:
  REG=165 SHARED=98304
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

git diff --check
pass
```

Interpretation:

- `TN32` answers the occupancy-threshold question directly: it reaches
  `occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2`, but still collapses to `18.23 TF/s`.
- The bottom-level reason is work-density loss. Per CTA, `BAR=33` and
  `DEPBAR=32` stay fixed, while `HMMA` drops from `256` at `TN256` to `32` at
  `TN32`. Total grid.y grows from `6` to `48`, so the fixed CUDA Tile
  synchronization/control body is paid 8x more often for the same total GEMM
  work.
- `TN64` does not even cross the resident-CTA threshold on A10G:
  `53248B * 2 > 102400B`. It keeps one CTA/SM and halves the per-CTA MMA body,
  so it is predictably slower.
- `TN128` also stays at one CTA/SM and loses half the per-CTA Tensor Core body.
- Therefore "reduce QKV shared by shrinking `TN`" is closed more strongly than
  before: the only shape that increases resident CTAs still loses badly.

Decision:

- Do not promote `split_contig_tn32_lat2`, `split_contig_tn64_lat2`, or
  `split_contig_tn128_lat2`.
- Keep source QKV at `TM32 x TN256 x TK16 + latency=2`.
- The next QKV work must preserve large per-CTA MMA density while changing
  staging/fusion. Smaller N CTAs are not a path to A10G roofline for this
  CUDA Tile producer.

## 2026-06-05 - Time attention split-contig resource and occupancy audit

Goal:

- Add the same runtime resource/occupancy diagnostic used for QKV to the
  source-relevant time-attention CUDA Tile bench.
- Explain why the current `QRows=64, KTile=32` split-contiguous attention path
  sits around `60 TF/s`, instead of only reporting its percentage of the local
  dense BF16 reference.
- Keep this bench-only; source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The source-relevant path uses official CUDA Tile constructs:
  `__tile_global__`, `cuda::tiles`, `ct::mma`, tile reductions, and tile stores.
- CUDA Tile leaves block/thread mapping and staging to the compiler, so the
  bottom-level evidence must come from generated resources, SASS, and runtime
  occupancy attributes.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added `--describe` to `bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile`.
- The mode prints CUDA runtime attributes and occupancy API results for
  selected source-relevant CUDA Tile attention kernels:
  - `cudaFuncGetAttributes`
  - `cudaOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor`
  - `cudaGetDeviceProperties`
- No math kernel body was changed.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant all

for v in \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input_no_keytail; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, (true|false), false, false, false, false>' 80

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
GPU: NVIDIA A10G SM 8.6 SMs=80
Device resources: shared_per_sm=102400B regs_per_sm=65536 max_blocks_per_sm=16

main1280_q64k32_exp2:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0 attr_regs=237
  static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0 attr_regs=233
  static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input_no_keytail:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0 attr_regs=217
  static_shared=32768B shared_limit=3 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=3

main1280_q32k32_exp2:
  grid=(40,480,1) waves/SM=240.0 attr_regs=168
  static_shared=42112B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

main1280_q64k16_exp2:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0 attr_regs=214
  static_shared=32768B shared_limit=3 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=3

main1280_q64k64_exp2:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=49152B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

main1280_q128k32_exp2:
  grid=(10,480,1) waves/SM=60.0 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=49152B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2
```

Timing:

```text
main1280_split_contig_input_exp2:
  qrows=64 ktile=32 best=3.387 ms median=3.394 ms
  real_math=60.41 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.41 TF/s roof70=86.3%
  checksum=0.242188
  compare_vs_main1280_q64k32_exp2: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

main1280_split_contig_input_exp2_no_keytail:
  qrows=64 ktile=32 best=3.292 ms median=3.294 ms
  real_math=61.16 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.16 TF/s roof70=87.4%
  checksum=0.242188
  compare_vs_main1280_q64k32_exp2:
    max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000505273408 rel_rms=0.002038398
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
keytail:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

no-keytail:
  REG=217 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
  instr=2144 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=9 STS=32 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=306 FADD=162 FMUL=136 MUFU=106
  BAR=13 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=17 IADD3=104 PRMT=0 LOP3=77 SHF=93
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

git diff --check
pass
```

Interpretation:

- Unlike QKV, source-relevant time attention is not stuck at one resident CTA
  per SM. The exact keytail path is already at `2 CTA/SM`; the no-keytail
  diagnostic reaches `3 CTA/SM`.
- The remaining gap to 70 TF/s is therefore not primarily the QKV-style
  shared-memory residency wall. It is the online-softmax/tail-control body
  around the Tensor Core work.
- The keytail path adds substantial static work:
  - `REG 217 -> 233`
  - `SHARED 32768 -> 40960`
  - `HMMA 128 -> 160`
  - `MUFU 106 -> 124`
  - `PRMT 0 -> 80`
  - `IMAD 17 -> 58`
  - `LOP3 77 -> 134`
- The extra `HMMA=32` is expected from the exact 21-column tail tile
  (`1301 - 1280 = 21`) being evaluated through a full `KTile=32` masked CUDA
  Tile. The large `PRMT/IMAD/LOP3` jump is the masked-tail index/control cost.
- This explains why earlier load-latency hints failed: the base path already
  has enough resident CTAs. Hints pushed register pressure toward the cliff but
  did not remove the masked-tail/control work.

Decision:

- Keep source time attention on the current `QRows=64, KTile=32` split-contig
  input path.
- Keep `--describe` as a required diagnostic for future time-attention probes.
- Future time-attention work should target keytail/mask codegen and online
  softmax state cost, not generic latency hints. A useful experiment must reduce
  tail `PRMT/IMAD/LOP3` or preserve no-keytail-like resources while retaining
  exact `N=1301` semantics.

## 2026-06-05 - Time attention tail-broadcast plus BF16 out-acc closure

Goal:

- Combine two previous single-axis diagnostics:
  - `tail_col_broadcast` lowers tail mask integer/logical code but raises
    register pressure and is runtime-neutral/slower.
  - `out_acc_bf16` lowers register pressure but slows down and changes output.
- Test whether the combination can keep the lower register count from
  `out_acc_bf16` while also reducing tail mask `IMAD/LOP3` from
  `tail_col_broadcast`.
- This is bench-only. Source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The experiment stays inside official CUDA Tile semantics:
  tile `ct::mma`, tile `ct::select`, tile reductions, and `__tile_global__`
  launch with one compiler-managed tile block.
- Because both previous axes changed generated code in non-obvious ways, the
  decision must use runtime resource attributes plus SASS, not source-level
  intuition.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added bench-only focused variant:
  - `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast_out_acc_bf16`
- Added it to `--describe`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

for v in \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_out_acc_bf16 \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast_out_acc_bf16; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
    --describe --variant "$v" | rg -e 'Device resources|main1280'
done

for v in \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_out_acc_bf16 \
  main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast_out_acc_bf16; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, (false|true), (false|true)>' 120

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
baseline:
  attr_regs=233 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

tail_col_broadcast:
  attr_regs=239 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

out_acc_bf16:
  attr_regs=209 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

tail_col_broadcast_out_acc_bf16:
  attr_regs=208 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2
```

Timing:

```text
baseline:
  best=3.387 ms median=3.395 ms
  real_math=60.42 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.42 TF/s roof70=86.3%
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

tail_col_broadcast:
  best=3.396 ms median=3.403 ms
  real_math=60.25 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.24 TF/s roof70=86.1%
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

out_acc_bf16:
  best=3.779 ms median=3.793 ms
  real_math=54.15 TF/s logical_tile_math=55.94 TF/s roof70=77.4%
  max_abs=0.0107421875 rms=0.00528992044 rel_rms=0.0213408485

tail_col_broadcast_out_acc_bf16:
  best=3.863 ms median=3.877 ms
  real_math=52.97 TF/s logical_tile_math=54.72 TF/s roof70=75.7%
  max_abs=0.0107421875 rms=0.00528992044 rel_rms=0.0213408485
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
baseline:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 F2FP=56
  FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

tail_col_broadcast:
  REG=239 SHARED=40960
  instr=2592 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 F2FP=56
  FFMA=344 FADD=198 FMUL=170 MUFU=120
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=30 IADD3=122 PRMT=80 LOP3=112 SHF=122

out_acc_bf16:
  REG=209 SHARED=40960
  instr=2704 HMMA=128 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=12 F2FP=112
  FFMA=306 FADD=162 FMUL=136 MUFU=106
  BAR=10 DEPBAR=6 IMAD=241 IADD3=113 PRMT=172 LOP3=151 SHF=151

tail_col_broadcast_out_acc_bf16:
  REG=208 SHARED=40960
  instr=2544 HMMA=128 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=12 F2FP=112
  FFMA=310 FADD=158 FMUL=136 MUFU=102
  BAR=10 DEPBAR=6 IMAD=194 IADD3=99 PRMT=169 LOP3=112 SHF=170
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

git diff --check
pass
```

Interpretation:

- The combination does achieve the narrow register goal:
  `REG=233 -> 208`. However, resident CTA count does not improve:
  all variants remain at `2 CTA/SM`.
- The important failure is Tensor Core schedule degradation inherited from
  `out_acc_bf16`: `HMMA=160 -> 128`, `LDSM=64 -> 48`, `LDGSTS=16 -> 12`.
- The combination also keeps the expensive conversion/control shape:
  `F2FP=56 -> 112`, `PRMT=80 -> 169`, `IMAD=58 -> 194`.
- Therefore the lower register count is not useful on A10G here. It does not
  buy another resident CTA, and it destroys the current good Tensor Core body.
- Output drift is the same as `out_acc_bf16`; this is expected because the
  changed math is the BF16 rounding of the running output accumulator, not the
  tail mask spelling.

Decision:

- Do not promote `tail_col_broadcast_out_acc_bf16` into source.
- Close the simple "combine lower-register BF16 accumulator with lower-integer
  tail mask" direction.
- Future exact time-attention work must preserve `HMMA=160, LDSM=64,
  LDGSTS=16` while reducing tail/control overhead. Lowering registers is not a
  sufficient objective unless it also changes occupancy or preserves the Tensor
  Core schedule.

## 2026-06-05 - FFN12 fused/two-stage resource and occupancy audit

Goal:

- Add the same runtime resource/occupancy diagnostic used for QKV and time
  attention to the FFN12 CUDA Tile bench.
- Explain why current fused FFN12 sits around `45-46 TF/s`, instead of only
  reporting its percentage of the local dense BF16 reference.
- Keep this bench-only; source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The FFN12 kernels use legal power-of-two tile shapes and official CUDA Tile
  constructs: `__tile_global__`, `cuda::tiles`, `partition_view`, `ct::mma`,
  and `cutile::hint`.
- CUDA Tile source spelling strongly affects generated staging and Tensor Core
  schedule, so runtime resource attributes and SASS are required before
  promoting any shape.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added `--describe` to `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile`.
- The mode prints CUDA runtime attributes and occupancy API results for:
  - current fused pair-H32 baseline
  - latency variants
  - W2 stream/pair negative variants
  - two-stage FFN1/FFN2 kernels
- No math kernel body was changed.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile --describe --variant all

for v in fused_pairh32_lat2 fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround \
         two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|w2pair)|ffn1_poly9_tk64_kernel|ffn2_tn64_kernel<32, 64, 64>' 100

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
git diff --check
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
GPU: NVIDIA A10G SM 8.6 SMs=80
shared_per_sm=102400B regs_per_sm=65536 max_blocks_per_sm=16

fused_pairh32_lat2:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
  local=8B

fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=254
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

fused_pairh32_w2stream_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn1:
  grid=(2439,16,1) waves/SM=487.8 attr_regs=96
  static_shared=61440B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64:
  grid=(2439,4,1) waves/SM=122.0 attr_regs=230
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn32:
  grid=(2439,8,1) waves/SM=243.9 attr_regs=138
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn128:
  grid=(2439,2,1) waves/SM=61.0 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  launches=1 best=1.7930 ms median=1.7980 ms
  useful=45.64 TF/s roof70=65.2% hidden_rw=0.000 GiB
  checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround:
  launches=1 best=3.4522 ms median=3.4555 ms
  useful=23.71 TF/s roof70=33.9% hidden_rw=0.000 GiB
  checksum=4080.0000

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64:
  launches=2 best=2.4598 ms median=2.4664 ms
  useful=33.27 TF/s roof70=47.5% hidden_rw=0.298 GiB
  checksum=4080.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

fused_pairh32_w2pair_lat2_outnoround:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=1720 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=96 STS=34 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=20 DEPBAR=16 IMAD=261 IADD3=121 PRMT=12 LOP3=131 SHF=121

two_stage FFN1:
  REG=96 STACK=0 SHARED=61440 LOCAL=0
  instr=816 HMMA=64 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=24 LDG=24 STS=12 STG=1
  F2FP=16 FFMA=128 FADD=48 FMUL=80
  BAR=5 DEPBAR=7 IMAD=32 IADD3=53 PRMT=25 LOP3=65 SHF=67

two_stage FFN2 TN64:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=1208 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=96 LDG=48 STS=12 STG=1
  F2FP=16 FADD=16
  BAR=20 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=65 IADD3=108 PRMT=31 LOP3=126 SHF=104
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

git diff --check
pass
```

Interpretation:

- FFN12 fused is again a one-resident-CTA kernel on A10G:
  `static_shared=98304B` against `shared_per_sm=102400B` gives
  `occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1`.
- It is more constrained than QKV in one important way: fused FFN12 has only
  `grid=(2439,1,1)`, or `30.5 waves/SM`, while QKV has many more N tiles. This
  makes the large single-CTA body and tail of the launch more visible in the
  measured kernel time.
- The fused baseline preserves the desired Tensor Core schedule
  (`HMMA=256`, `LDSM=128`, `LDGSTS=80`), but it does so at the register/shared
  cliff: `REG=255`, `STACK=8`, `SHARED=98304`.
- The W2 pairwise live-range reduction explains why simple source-level live
  range shortening is not enough. It slightly reduces registers/stack
  (`255+stack -> 254+no stack`) but halves the Tensor Core body
  (`HMMA=256 -> 128`), dropping to `23.71 TF/s`.
- The two-stage fallback avoids the fused live-set cliff in FFN1, but FFN2 is
  still a `98KB` one-CTA kernel and the design adds a `0.298 GiB` hidden
  global-memory round trip plus a second launch. It is therefore slower than
  fused despite having simpler individual kernels.

Decision:

- Keep current fused pair-H32 FFN12 as the source-relevant baseline.
- Keep `--describe` as a required diagnostic for future FFN12 probes.
- Future FFN12 work must preserve `HMMA=256` while reducing the `98KB` shared
  footprint or avoiding the `REG=255` cliff. Lowering registers is not useful if
  it triggers `HMMA=128`, and splitting into two stages is not useful if it
  materializes the hidden tensor in global memory.

## 2026-06-05 - Freq attention resource and occupancy audit

Goal:

- Add the same runtime resource/occupancy diagnostic used for QKV, time
  attention, and FFN12 to the source-relevant frequency attention bench.
- Explain why the fixed `N=60,D=64` CUDA Tile frequency attention sits around
  `12 TF/s`, instead of only reporting that it is about `17%` of the local
  A10G dense BF16 reference.
- Keep this bench-only; source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The source-relevant frequency attention body uses documented CUDA Tile
  constructs: `__tile_global__`, `cuda::tiles`, `ct::partition_view`,
  `ct::mma`, tile reductions, and tile stores.
- CUDA Tile maps each tile kernel block internally, so the useful evidence is
  generated resource usage, SASS, runtime occupancy, and measured throughput.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added `--describe` to `bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile`.
- The mode prints CUDA runtime attributes and occupancy API results for:
  - source-like padded `q8s/q16s/q32s/q64s/q128s`
  - `q16s_exp2/q16sb/q16sc/q16sc_exp2`
  - frequency QKV rotary split producers
  - packed-QKV direct attention consumers
- Added `roof70` to the main frequency-attention TF/s bench output.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile --describe --variant all

for v in q16s q32s q64s; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline
done

for v in q16qkv_rot_direct q16qkv_direct; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile \
  'freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16, false, false, false>|freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<32, false, false, false>|freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<64, false, false, false>|freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16, true, false, false>|freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16, false, true, false>|freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16, false, false, true>' \
  120

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_freq_attention_cutile \
  'split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_pad64_kernel|split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_compact_kernel|split_packed_qkv_rotary_freq60_validpad_kernel|freq_attention60_packed_qkv_identity_kernel<16, false>|freq_attention60_packed_qkv_rotary_kernel<16, false>' \
  120
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
GPU: NVIDIA A10G SM 8.6 SMs=80
shared_per_sm=102400B regs_per_sm=65536 max_blocks_per_sm=16

q8s:
  grid=(8,10408,1) waves/SM=1040.8 attr_regs=174
  static_shared=19456B shared_limit=5 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=5

q16s:
  grid=(4,10408,1) waves/SM=520.4 attr_regs=92
  static_shared=22528B shared_limit=4 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=4

q32s:
  grid=(2,10408,1) waves/SM=260.2 attr_regs=96
  static_shared=28672B shared_limit=3 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=3

q64s:
  grid=(1,10408,1) waves/SM=130.1 attr_regs=140
  static_shared=32768B shared_limit=3 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=3

q128s:
  grid=(1,10408,1) waves/SM=130.1 attr_regs=220
  static_shared=57344B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

qkv_rot_split:
  grid=(83264,1,1) waves/SM=1040.8 attr_regs=38
  static_shared=0B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=16

q16qkv_direct:
  grid=(4,10408,1) waves/SM=520.4 attr_regs=193
  static_shared=22528B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=4

q16qkv_rot_direct:
  grid=(4,10408,1) waves/SM=520.4 attr_regs=235
  static_shared=22528B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=4
```

Timing:

```text
q16s:
  best=0.789 ms median=0.836 ms
  12.162 TF/s roof70=17.4%
  compare_vs_qrows_baseline: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

q32s:
  best=1.411 ms median=1.416 ms
  6.796 TF/s roof70=9.7%
  compare_vs_qrows_baseline: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

q64s:
  best=1.346 ms median=1.351 ms
  7.126 TF/s roof70=10.2%
  compare_vs_qrows_baseline: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

q16qkv_rot_direct:
  best=2.686 ms median=4.379 ms
  3.571 TF/s roof70=5.1%
  compare_vs_qrows_baseline: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

q16qkv_direct:
  best=2.304 ms median=2.310 ms
  4.164 TF/s roof70=5.9%
  compare_vs_qrows_baseline: max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

qkv_rot_split:
  best=0.771 ms median=0.776 ms
  598.4 GiB/s
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
q16s:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 LDSM=0 LDGSTS=0 LDG=9 STS=22 STG=1
  F2FP=8 FFMA=86 FADD=32 FMUL=12 MUFU=18
  BAR=16 DEPBAR=0 IMAD=180 IADD3=41 PRMT=72 LOP3=98 SHF=71

q32s:
  REG=96 STACK=0 SHARED=28672 LOCAL=0
  instr=1648 HMMA=32 LDSM=0 LDGSTS=0 LDG=8 STS=26 STG=15
  F2FP=16 FFMA=170 FADD=58 FMUL=16 MUFU=34
  BAR=18 DEPBAR=0 IMAD=199 IADD3=60 PRMT=160 LOP3=122 SHF=105

q64s:
  REG=140 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
  instr=2712 HMMA=64 LDSM=0 LDGSTS=0 LDG=12 STS=32 STG=31
  F2FP=32 FFMA=326 FADD=104 FMUL=36 MUFU=66
  BAR=4 DEPBAR=0 IMAD=312 IADD3=126 PRMT=192 LOP3=239 SHF=156

q16s_exp2:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
  instr=984 HMMA=16 F2FP=8 FFMA=54 FADD=24 FMUL=14 MUFU=18
  BAR=16 IMAD=170 IADD3=41 PRMT=72 LOP3=98 SHF=71

q16qkv_direct:
  REG=193 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
  instr=1744 HMMA=16 LDG=30 STS=35 STG=1 F2FP=8
  FFMA=90 FADD=32 FMUL=8 MUFU=18
  BAR=15 IMAD=304 IADD3=186 PRMT=132 LOP3=154 SHF=86

q16qkv_rot_direct:
  REG=235 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
  instr=2496 HMMA=16 LDG=120 STS=35 STG=1 F2FP=28
  FFMA=135 FADD=32 FMUL=88 MUFU=18
  BAR=16 IMAD=527 IADD3=235 PRMT=152 LOP3=234 SHF=170
```

Interpretation:

- Frequency attention is not failing because CUDA Tile misses Tensor Core.
  The source-like kernel emits `HMMA`, but the fixed `N=60,D=64,QRows=16`
  shape gives only `HMMA=16` per CTA.
- It is also not a QKV/FFN12-style one-resident-CTA problem. The source-like
  `q16s` body has `4 CTA/SM`, while `q32s/q64s` have `3 CTA/SM`.
- The bottom cause is low Tensor Core work density per CTA. For `q16s`, only
  `HMMA=16` is surrounded by substantial fixed work:
  `IMAD=180`, `PRMT=72`, `LOP3=98`, `MUFU=18`, and `BAR=16`.
- Increasing `QRows` does not solve it. `q32s` doubles the CTA HMMA count, but
  the fixed/control body also grows and the grid halves; throughput drops to
  `6.80 TF/s`. `q64s` reaches `HMMA=64` but still only gets `7.13 TF/s`.
- Replacing `ct::exp` with `ct::exp2` lowers static instructions slightly
  (`instr=1008 -> 984`, `IMAD=180 -> 170`) but leaves `HMMA=16`, resource
  usage, and runtime essentially unchanged.
- Directly fusing packed QKV and rotary into the attention consumer is much
  worse. It preserves only `HMMA=16`, but raises live state and address/control
  work sharply:
  - identity direct: `REG=193`, `IMAD=304`, `PRMT=132`
  - rotary direct: `REG=235`, `LDG=120`, `IMAD=527`, `LOP3=234`
- Therefore the next frequency-path work should not blindly make one deeper
  fused attention kernel. For this tiny attention body, producer logic can
  overwhelm the Tensor Core work unless it is staged in a way that keeps the
  consumer near the current `q16s` resource shape.

Decision:

- Keep source frequency attention on the current padded `QRows=16` CUDA Tile
  path.
- Keep `--describe` as a required diagnostic for future frequency-attention
  probes.
- Do not promote packed-QKV direct attention or rotary-direct attention.
- Future frequency work should target a producer cleanup or layout change that
  removes global-memory traffic without increasing the attention consumer beyond
  the current `REG=92`, `SHARED=22528`, `HMMA=16` shape. A viable candidate must
  reduce producer traffic or launch count while preserving the q16 attention
  consumer's low address/control cost.

## 2026-06-05 - Gate merge token shape audit

Goal:

- Explain the remaining `apply_gates_and_merge_heads_bf16_token_d64_cutile`
  hotspot instead of treating it like a Tensor Core TFLOP/s problem.
- Test whether the current one-token-per-CTA CUDA Tile gate merge is limited by
  CTA count and can be improved by processing multiple tokens per CTA.
- Keep this bench-only; source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The source gate-merge path is a CUDA Tile fixed-shape elementwise kernel:
  `__tile_global__`, `cuda::tiles`, tile loads/stores, and BF16 element casts.
- It does not use `ct::mma`, so dense BF16 Tensor Core TFLOP/s is not an
  appropriate performance denominator. The right metric is effective bandwidth,
  generated address/control instructions, and launch/global-memory boundary
  cost.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added focused target `bench_bf16_gate_merge_cutile`.
- Variants:
  - `token1`: source-like one merged `[8,64]` token per CTA
  - `token2`: two merged tokens per CTA
  - `token4`: four merged tokens per CTA
  - `flat256`: generic flat 256-element tile
- Added `--describe` resource/occupancy diagnostics.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_gate_merge_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_gate_merge_cutile --describe --shape time --variant all

for v in token1 token2 token4 flat256; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_gate_merge_cutile \
    --shape time --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500 \
    --compare-baseline
done

for v in token1 token2 token4 flat256; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_gate_merge_cutile \
    --shape freq --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500 \
    --compare-baseline
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_gate_merge_cutile \
  'gate_merge_(token1|tokens|flat256)_kernel' \
  120
```

Runtime resource/occupancy, time shape:

```text
GPU: NVIDIA A10G SM 8.6 SMs=80
shape: tokens=78060 n_tokens=1301 heads=8 dim=64 merged=512

token1:
  grid=(78060,1,1) waves/SM=975.8 attr_regs=24
  static_shared=2048B shared_limit=50 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=16

token2:
  grid=(39030,1,1) waves/SM=487.9 attr_regs=56
  static_shared=0B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=16

token4:
  grid=(19515,1,1) waves/SM=243.9 attr_regs=118
  static_shared=0B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=16

flat256:
  grid=(156120,1,1) waves/SM=1951.5 attr_regs=36
  static_shared=0B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=16
```

Timing:

```text
time shape, tokens=78060, n_tokens=1301:
  token1:  best=0.2517 ms median=0.2529 ms
           unique=596.0 GiB/s tile_logic=887.1 GiB/s
  token2:  best=0.2521 ms median=0.2531 ms
           unique=595.1 GiB/s tile_logic=885.8 GiB/s
  token4:  best=0.2585 ms median=0.2601 ms
           unique=580.4 GiB/s tile_logic=863.9 GiB/s
  flat256: best=0.3356 ms median=0.3444 ms
           unique=447.1 GiB/s tile_logic=665.5 GiB/s

freq shape, tokens=3600, n_tokens=60:
  token1:  best=0.0124 ms median=0.0138 ms
           unique=557.4 GiB/s tile_logic=829.5 GiB/s
  token2:  best=0.0152 ms median=0.0159 ms
           unique=454.3 GiB/s tile_logic=676.2 GiB/s
  token4:  best=0.0175 ms median=0.0183 ms
           unique=395.3 GiB/s tile_logic=588.4 GiB/s
  flat256: best=0.0202 ms median=0.0211 ms
           unique=342.7 GiB/s tile_logic=510.1 GiB/s
```

Correctness:

```text
all variants compare_vs_token1:
  max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
token1:
  REG=24 STACK=0 SHARED=2048 LOCAL=0
  instr=120 HMMA=0 LDG=0 STS=4 STG=1 F2FP=2 FMUL=4 MUFU=1
  BAR=1 IMAD=31 IADD3=6 PRMT=4 LOP3=6 SHF=4

token2:
  REG=56 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0
  instr=488 HMMA=0 STG=1 F2FP=4 FMUL=8 MUFU=1
  BAR=0 IMAD=153 IADD3=48 PRMT=20 LOP3=56 SHF=45

token4:
  REG=118 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0
  instr=976 HMMA=0 STG=1 F2FP=8 FMUL=16 MUFU=1
  BAR=0 IMAD=296 IADD3=79 PRMT=40 LOP3=126 SHF=103

flat256:
  REG=36 STACK=0 SHARED=0 LOCAL=0
  instr=440 HMMA=0 STG=1 F2FP=1 FMUL=2 MUFU=8
  BAR=0 IMAD=162 IADD3=39 PRMT=5 LOP3=13 SHF=8
```

Interpretation:

- The current source-like `token1` shape is already the best standalone
  gate-merge Tile shape among the tested variants.
- Reducing CTA count by packing more tokens per CTA does not help. `token2`
  halves grid size but increases registers (`24 -> 56`) and static integer
  work (`IMAD 31 -> 153`), so runtime is only equal. `token4` raises registers
  to `118` and becomes slower.
- The flat 256-element variant is worse because it doubles the grid and
  reintroduces generic index decomposition. It loses the fixed one-token
  `[8,64] -> [512]` shape that made the current CUDA Tile replacement faster
  than the old ordinary CUDA kernel.
- This kernel is memory/index/elementwise bound. The useful time-shape bandwidth
  is about `596 GiB/s` by unique bytes, or `887 GiB/s` by tile-logic bytes where
  gate values are effectively broadcast over D. That is the right scale for this
  non-MMA kernel; it should not be expected to approach `70 TF/s`.
- The remaining end-to-end `~23.6 ms / 96 calls` is mainly a global-memory
  boundary issue. The next useful optimization is cross-op fusion, for example
  applying gates directly in the attention output write or consuming gated
  merged output in the next projection without materializing a separate tensor.

Decision:

- Do not promote `token2`, `token4`, or `flat256` into source.
- Keep source gate merge on the current one-token D64 CUDA Tile path.
- Future gate work should be cross-op fusion, not standalone CTA packing.

## 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: Time attention gated-store fusion

Goal:

- Test the cross-op fusion suggested by the gate-merge audit without repeating
  the already-negative gate+projection fusion.
- Keep the following projection's A operand contiguous. The probe only moves
  gate application into the time-attention output write:

```text
current main path:
  attention main -> [B*H,N,64]
  gate merge     -> [B,N,512]

probe:
  attention main + gate multiply + merged-store -> [B,NMain,512]
```

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The experiment uses documented CUDA Tile C++ constructs:
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::mma`,
  tile reductions, and tile pointer stores.
- The failed first attempt to store through a 4D `partition_view` was corrected
  because CUDA Tile requires the stored tile shape to match the view tile shape.
  The final probe uses `ct::store` with pointer tiles for the `[QRows,D]`
  merged store.
- Reference docs:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`
  and
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-tile-cpp-api-reference/index.html`.

Change:

- Added bench-only variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_gated_store`.
- Added a source-like synthetic baseline pair inside the same bench:
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel` followed by
  `gate_merge_time_main1280_token_d64_kernel`.
- No source inference path was changed.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_gated_store

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_gated_store \
  --warmup 20 --iters 200 --compare-baseline

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_gated_store_kernel|gate_merge_time_main1280_token_d64_kernel|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false>' \
  140
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_gated_store:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0
  attr_regs=239 static_shared=40960B
  shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2
```

Timing:

```text
baseline_pair:
  attention main + gate merge
  best=3.633 ms median=3.641 ms
  56.33 TF/s

fused gated-store:
  best=4.114 ms median=4.137 ms
  49.74 TF/s roof70=71.1%
  speedup=0.883x
  saved_attn_boundary=0.146 GiB
```

Correctness:

```text
compare_vs_attention_plus_gate_merge:
  elems=39321600 max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
gated-store attention:
  REG=239 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=3152 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=13 STS=32 STG=32
  F2FP=72 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=202 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=234 IADD3=104 PRMT=134 LOP3=275 SHF=197

source-like attention main:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

synthetic gate merge:
  REG=24 STACK=0 SHARED=2048 LOCAL=0
  instr=88 HMMA=0 LDG=5 STS=4 STG=1 F2FP=2 FMUL=4
  BAR=1 IMAD=22 IADD3=7 PRMT=2 LOP3=8 SHF=8
```

Interpretation:

- The fused gated-store kernel preserves the Tensor Core body:
  `HMMA=160`, `LDSM=64`, and `LDGSTS=16` match the source-like attention main.
- It is slower because the extra gate read, multiply, BF16 rounding, and
  merged-layout pointer store substantially increase non-HMMA work:
  `instr 2736 -> 3152`, `IMAD 58 -> 234`, `PRMT 80 -> 134`,
  `LOP3 134 -> 275`, and `STG 1 -> 32`.
- The saved attention boundary is only about `0.146 GiB` for the main time path.
  On A10G that memory saving is smaller than the added scalar/index/control
  work inside the already large attention kernel.
- This closes the simple "apply gates directly in attention output write"
  direction. The bottom cause is not missing Tensor Cores; it is that changing
  the store layout and adding gate indexing pollutes the CUDA Tile generated
  schedule around an otherwise strong attention kernel.

Decision:

- Do not promote time-attention gated-store fusion.
- Keep source on separate attention output plus token-D64 gate merge.
- Future gate work should avoid perturbing the strong time-attention kernel.
  A viable next direction would need to reduce gate/global-memory traffic while
  preserving the source attention kernel's low index/control profile.

## 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 hidden-pair x2 scheduling

Goal:

- Continue the FFN12 fused-kernel investigation without changing source
  inference.
- Preserve the current output accumulator shape: two `32x128` FP32 output
  accumulators and BF16 output storage.
- Unlike the earlier W2 streaming/pairwise probes, keep enough W2 tiles live to
  try to preserve the full Tensor Core schedule, but process two hidden pairs
  per loop body:

```text
baseline:
  one loop body = 2 hidden tiles -> 4 W2 MMAs

probe:
  one loop body = 4 hidden tiles -> 8 W2 MMAs
```

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The probe stays inside CUDA Tile C++ semantics: `__tile_global__`,
  `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::irange`, `ct::mma`,
  `ct::assume_aligned`, and `cutile::hint(... latency=2)`.
- Tile shapes remain compile-time powers of two:
  `TM=32`, `TK=64`, `THidden=32`, `OutHalf=128`.
- No cuBLAS/cuDNN/cuFFT/raw WMMA/inline MMA path was added.

Change:

- Added bench-only variant
  `fused_pairh32_pair2_lat2_outnoround` to
  `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_pair2_lat2_outnoround

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_pair2_lat2_outnoround --warmup 20 --iters 300

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|pair2)' 60
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5
  attr_regs=255 static_shared=98304B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=8B

fused_pairh32_pair2_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5
  attr_regs=255 static_shared=98304B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=0B
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.8035 ms median=1.8237 ms
  useful=45.38 TF/s roof70=64.8%

fused_pairh32_pair2_lat2_outnoround:
  best=3.1168 ms median=3.1685 ms
  useful=26.26 TF/s roof70=37.5%
```

Correctness:

```text
checksum baseline = 4080.0000
checksum pair2    = 4080.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
baseline W1=2,W2=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

hidden-pair x2:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2504 HMMA=256 LDSM=112 LDGSTS=72 LDG=124 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=31 DEPBAR=28 IMAD=239 IADD3=123 PRMT=20 LOP3=126 SHF=163
```

Interpretation:

- This probe is different from `W2 streaming` and `W2 pairwise`: it preserves
  `HMMA=256`, so the failure is not a Tensor Core fallback or a halved MMA
  body.
- It still keeps the same A10G residency cliff:
  `SHARED=98304B`, `REG=255`, and one resident CTA per SM. Therefore grouping
  two hidden pairs does not solve the primary resource limit.
- The runtime regression is large despite slightly fewer static instructions
  and fewer `LDSM/LDGSTS`. The likely bottom-level cause is that the larger
  source loop body extends hidden/W1/W2 live ranges and reduces the CUDA Tile
  compiler's ability to schedule the same `HMMA=256` body efficiently.
- This is an important negative result: preserving the HMMA count is necessary
  but not sufficient. For FFN12 on A10G, the current one-hidden-pair body is a
  better Tile lowering shape than a larger hidden-pair x2 body.

Decision:

- Do not promote hidden-pair x2 scheduling to source.
- Keep the source-relevant FFN12 fused shape on the current one-hidden-pair
  loop body.
- Future FFN12 work should not simply enlarge the loop body. A useful next
  direction must either reduce the `98KB` shared-memory allocation while
  keeping the current schedule quality, or change W2/layout staging in a way
  that keeps both `HMMA=256` and measured issue efficiency.

### 2026-06-05 - Time Attention Tail 16+8 Tile Split Probe

Hypothesis:

- The current exact split-contiguous main1280 time-attention kernel handles
  keys `1280..1300` with one masked `KTile=32` tail. That tail computes 21
  useful columns plus 11 invalid columns.
- CUDA Tile shapes must stay compile-time tile-compatible powers of two, so a
  direct 21-column tile is not legal. This probe splits the tail into a full
  16-key segment plus an 8-key segment with only three invalid columns.
- If masked-tail padding/control is the bottom limiter, `16+8` should reduce
  tail control/static work while keeping exact output.

Change:

- Added bench-only variant
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail16_8`.
- Source inference is unchanged.
- The probe remains CUDA Tile-first: `__tile_global__`,
  `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::irange`, `ct::mma`,
  `ct::load_masked`, and `ct::select`. No cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN/raw WMMA path
  was added.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail16_8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail16_8 \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input|tail16_8)_kernel<64, 32' 140

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
baseline exact keytail:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0
  attr_regs=233 static_shared=40960B
  shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2 local=0B

tail16_8:
  grid=(20,480,1) waves/SM=120.0
  attr_regs=255 static_shared=38912B
  shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2 local=0B
```

Timing and correctness:

```text
baseline exact keytail:
  best=3.387 ms median=3.396 ms
  real_math=60.41 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.41 TF/s roof70=86.3%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

tail16_8:
  best=3.394 ms median=3.402 ms
  real_math=60.28 TF/s logical_tile_math=60.42 TF/s roof70=86.1%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
baseline exact keytail:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

tail16_8:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=38912 LOCAL=0
  instr=2672 HMMA=156 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=50 STG=1
  F2FP=54 FFMA=374 FADD=198 FMUL=164 MUFU=122
  BAR=17 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=14 IADD3=118 PRMT=30 LOP3=134 SHF=145
```

Interpretation:

- The hypothesis is only partly true. The `16+8` split does reduce some
  tail index/control work: `IMAD 58 -> 14`, `PRMT 80 -> 30`, and logical
  padded columns drop from `1280+32` to `1280+24`.
- That saving is not the bottom limiter. The extra tail segment creates a
  second online-softmax merge and pushes the kernel to `REG=255`. It also
  increases `BAR 15 -> 17`, `STS 44 -> 50`, and `FFMA 340 -> 374`.
- The SASS has `HMMA=156` instead of `160`, which is consistent with the
  smaller tile-compatible tail work, but the measured time is still slightly
  slower. Less nominal Tensor Core tail work does not help when schedule and
  live-range pressure get worse.

Decision:

- Do not promote `tail16_8` to source.
- Keep the source-relevant exact main1280 time-attention path on the current
  single `KTile=32` tail.
- Future time-attention work should target a lower-register way to retain
  no-keytail-like resources while preserving the 21 real keys, rather than
  splitting the tail into more in-kernel online-softmax segments.

### 2026-06-05 - Time Attention Tail Probability-Only Mask Probe

Hypothesis:

- The exact split-contiguous keytail currently masks invalid score columns to
  `-inf` before `reduce_max`, then masks invalid probabilities to zero.
- Online softmax remains mathematically valid if `new_m` is larger than the
  true valid-key max, as long as numerator and denominator use the same scale.
  Therefore the score-stage `-inf` mask might be removable while keeping the
  probability mask.
- If score-stage mask codegen is the bottom limiter, this should reduce tail
  control without changing the output.

Change:

- Added bench-only variant
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_prob_mask_only`.
- Source inference is unchanged.
- The probe keeps the same `QRows=64,KTile=32`, single keytail segment, and
  `ct::mma` schedule as the baseline. It removes only the tail score
  `ct::select(valid, scores, -inf)` step and keeps probability masking.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_prob_mask_only

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_prob_mask_only \
  --warmup 30 --iters 300 --compare-baseline

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input|tail_prob_mask_only)_kernel<64, 32' 140

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
baseline exact keytail:
  attr_regs=233 static_shared=40960B
  shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2 local=0B

tail_prob_mask_only:
  attr_regs=233 static_shared=40960B
  shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2 local=0B
```

Timing and correctness:

```text
baseline exact keytail:
  best=3.388 ms median=3.394 ms
  real_math=60.39 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.39 TF/s roof70=86.3%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

tail_prob_mask_only:
  best=3.438 ms median=3.444 ms
  real_math=59.53 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.50 TF/s roof70=85.0%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
baseline exact keytail:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

tail_prob_mask_only:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2704 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=57 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=131 SHF=123
```

Interpretation:

- The output is exact, so the mathematical shortcut is valid for this fixed
  online-softmax recurrence.
- It is still slower. Static SASS barely changes: same `REG`, `HMMA`, `LDSM`,
  `LDGSTS`, `BAR`, and `DEPBAR`; only `instr 2736 -> 2704`, `IMAD 58 -> 57`,
  and `LOP3 134 -> 131`.
- Therefore the score-stage `-inf` mask is not the bottom limiter. Removing it
  does not change the resource shape and likely perturbs tail-row scaling/order
  enough to lose runtime despite exact final BF16 output.

Decision:

- Do not promote `tail_prob_mask_only` to source.
- Stop spending A10G cycles on tail mask spelling by itself. Both
  `tail_col_broadcast` and `tail_prob_mask_only` show that local mask-code
  reductions do not solve the time-attention gap.
- Future time-attention work should attack the online-softmax state/live-range
  problem or shift focus to higher-headroom hotspots such as FFN12 and QKV.

### 2026-06-05 - QKV Split-Contig A/B Load-Hint Sweep Closure

Goal:

- Close the remaining small QKV hint-space around the source-relevant
  `TM32 x TN256 x TK16` split-contiguous producer.
- Keep the same CUDA Tile dataflow, BF16 input/output, split Q/K/V stores,
  `ct::mma`, and `HMMA=256` schedule. Change only A/B load latency hints.
- This tests whether the current `49 TF/s` QKV plateau is still a simple
  `cutile::hint` scheduling issue, after `TN/TK/TM`, pair-K, grid.z, and store
  hints were already closed.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The variants use the existing `qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel` template with
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::irange`,
  `ct::mma`, and `cutile::hint`.
- No cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN/raw WMMA path was added.

Change:

- Added bench-only variants:
  - `split_contig_a1_b2_s2`
  - `split_contig_a2_b1_s2`
  - `split_contig_a1_b1_s2`
  - `split_contig_a0_b2_s2`
  - `split_contig_a2_b0_s2`
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant all | \
  rg -e 'GPU:|split_contig_(lat2|a1_b2_s2|a2_b1_s2|a1_b1_s2|a0_b2_s2|a2_b0_s2|a2_b2_s0)'

for v in split_contig_lat2 split_contig_a1_b2_s2 split_contig_a2_b1_s2 \
         split_contig_a1_b1_s2 split_contig_a0_b2_s2 split_contig_a2_b0_s2 \
         split_contig_a2_b2_s0; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done

for r in 1 2 3; do
  for v in split_contig_lat2 split_contig_a2_b1_s2 split_contig_a2_b2_s0; do
    ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
  done
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, (0|1|2), (0|1|2), 2>' 140

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
```

Resource/occupancy:

```text
split_contig_lat2:      REG=165 SHARED=98304 active_cta_per_sm=1
split_contig_a2_b2_s0:  REG=165 SHARED=98304 active_cta_per_sm=1
split_contig_a1_b2_s2:  REG=166 SHARED=98304 active_cta_per_sm=1
split_contig_a2_b1_s2:  REG=164 SHARED=98304 active_cta_per_sm=1
split_contig_a1_b1_s2:  REG=166 SHARED=98304 active_cta_per_sm=1
split_contig_a0_b2_s2:  REG=156 SHARED=98304 active_cta_per_sm=1
split_contig_a2_b0_s2:  REG=178 SHARED=98304 active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
first pass:
  split_contig_lat2:      best=1.252 ms median=1.259 ms 49.04 TF/s roof70=70.1%
  split_contig_a1_b2_s2:  best=1.285 ms median=1.303 ms 47.75 TF/s roof70=68.2%
  split_contig_a2_b1_s2:  best=1.264 ms median=1.285 ms 48.57 TF/s roof70=69.4%
  split_contig_a1_b1_s2:  best=1.276 ms median=1.308 ms 48.11 TF/s roof70=68.7%
  split_contig_a0_b2_s2:  best=1.346 ms median=1.363 ms 45.60 TF/s roof70=65.1%
  split_contig_a2_b0_s2:  best=1.405 ms median=1.412 ms 43.67 TF/s roof70=62.4%
  split_contig_a2_b2_s0:  best=1.274 ms median=1.281 ms 48.16 TF/s roof70=68.8%

repeat top candidates:
  split_contig_lat2:
    best=1.263/1.275/1.277 ms
    median=1.272/1.286/1.289 ms
    TF/s=48.61/48.13/48.06

  split_contig_a2_b1_s2:
    best=1.260/1.278/1.246 ms
    median=1.289/1.292/1.287 ms
    TF/s=48.72/48.03/49.27

  split_contig_a2_b2_s0:
    best=1.275/1.263/1.274 ms
    median=1.284/1.286/1.281 ms
    TF/s=48.14/48.61/48.19
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  REG=165 SHARED=98304
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_a1_b2_s2:
  REG=166 SHARED=98304
  instr=1136 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=31 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_a2_b1_s2:
  REG=164 SHARED=98304
  instr=1104 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=18 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_a1_b1_s2:
  REG=166 SHARED=98304
  instr=1160 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_a0_b2_s2:
  REG=156 SHARED=98304
  instr=1112 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=20 DEPBAR=31 IMAD=138 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_a2_b0_s2:
  REG=178 SHARED=98304
  instr=1104 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=21 DEPBAR=31 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63
```

Interpretation:

- All hint variants preserve Tensor Core lowering:
  `HMMA=256`, `LDSM=96`, and `LDGSTS=80`.
- The lower-barrier SASS is not a win. `a1_b2_s2`, `a2_b1_s2`, `a0_b2_s2`,
  and `a2_b0_s2` reduce static `BAR` from `33` to `18-21`, but none gives a
  stable runtime improvement.
- `a0_b2_s2` is the important diagnostic: it lowers registers to `156` and
  cuts `BAR`, yet slows to `45.60 TF/s`. Therefore the A-load latency hint is
  doing real scheduling work; removing it improves some static counters while
  hurting the generated pipeline.
- `a2_b0_s2` is worse and raises registers to `178`, so B-load hint removal is
  clearly negative.
- `a2_b1_s2` occasionally posts a slightly better best sample, but median and
  repeat runs are not better than baseline. It also leaves the core resource
  wall unchanged: `SHARED=98304`, `active_cta_per_sm=1`.

Decision:

- Do not promote any A/B load-hint variant to source.
- Keep QKV split-contig producer on the current all-site `latency=2` spelling.
- Close simple QKV hint-space. The remaining QKV gap is not a `cutile::hint`
  knob; it needs a real staging/fusion change that breaks the `98KB`/one-CTA
  resource wall without losing the current `HMMA=256` schedule.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 bias broadcast tile

Goal:

- Test whether FFN12 pair-H32/TK64 is paying avoidable row-duplicated bias
  address/load cost.
- Keep the source-relevant fused CUDA Tile dataflow unchanged: `TM32`,
  `TK64`, `THidden32`, two `32x128` output accumulators, W1/W2
  `latency=2`, BF16 input/output, FP32 `ct::mma` accumulators, and the same
  W1/W2 load/MMA order.
- Change only the hidden/output bias loads from `[M,C]`-shaped repeated index
  tiles to one-row bias tiles plus `ct::broadcast`.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- This probe uses only documented CUDA Tile C++ constructs:
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::tile`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`,
  `ct::irange`, `ct::mma`, `ct::load`, `ct::broadcast`, and
  `cutile::hint`.
- Tile shapes remain compile-time powers of two, including the one-row
  broadcast shapes `shape<1,32>` and `shape<1,128>`.
- No cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN/raw WMMA/inline MMA path was added.

Change:

- Added bench-only variant `fused_pairh32_bias_broadcast_lat2` to
  `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_lat2

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_bias_broadcast_lat2

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_bias_broadcast_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|bias_broadcast)' 80

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
  local=8B

fused_pairh32_bias_broadcast_lat2:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=94208B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
  local=0B
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.8012 ms median=1.8206 ms
  useful=45.44 TF/s roof70=64.9%
  checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_bias_broadcast_lat2:
  best=1.9504 ms median=1.9565 ms
  useful=41.96 TF/s roof70=59.9%
  checksum=4080.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
baseline:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

bias_broadcast:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=94208
  instr=2248 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=50 STS=14 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=22 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=151 IADD3=130 PRMT=28 LOP3=110 SHF=123
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- The probe proves the one-row bias tile plus `ct::broadcast` spelling is legal
  CUDA Tile C++ and keeps the desired Tensor Core body: `HMMA=256`,
  `LDSM=128`, and `LDGSTS=80`.
- It also removes the expected row-duplicated bias traffic and staging:
  `SHARED 98304 -> 94208`, `LDG 128 -> 50`, `STS 44 -> 14`, and
  `instr 2536 -> 2248`.
- The runtime still regresses by about `8.3%`. The core A10G residency wall is
  unchanged: `REG=255` and one resident CTA per SM. The new broadcast lowering
  also raises `PRMT 16 -> 28` and `DEPBAR 29 -> 32`, so the cleaner static load
  count does not translate into better issue scheduling.
- Bottom-level conclusion: row-duplicated bias gather is not the primary FFN12
  limiter. For this CUDA Tile lowering, preserving the current bias expression
  gives a better dynamic schedule than explicitly broadcasting a one-row bias
  tile.

Decision:

- Do not promote `bias_broadcast` to source.
- Keep it as a bench-only negative result because it explains why a seemingly
  cleaner bias broadcast is not enough.
- Future FFN12 work should stop treating bias-load cleanup as the next lever.
  The remaining useful directions are structural: reduce the `98KB`/`REG=255`
  residency cliff or change data layout/fusion boundaries while preserving the
  current `HMMA=256` schedule.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: QKV compound 2x128 split-contig output

Goal:

- Test a QKV structure that keeps one launch, keeps one A-tile load per CTA,
  and keeps the source-relevant `TM32 x TN256 x TK16` work density, while
  replacing the single `32x256` accumulator/store with two `32x128`
  accumulators/stores inside the same CTA.
- This differs from the already-closed `TN128` path because it does not double
  the number of CTAs or reread A. It also differs from the existing packed
  `compound2x128_lat2` diagnostic because this new probe writes the real
  split-contiguous Q/K/V outputs.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The probe uses only CUDA Tile C++ constructs:
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::tile`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`,
  `ct::layout_strided`, `ct::irange`, `ct::mma`, and `cutile::hint`.
- Tile shapes stay compile-time powers of two:
  `TM=32`, `TN=128`, and `TK=16`.
- No cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN/raw WMMA/inline MMA path was added.

Change:

- Added bench-only variant `split_contig_compound2x128_lat2` to
  `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --describe --variant split_contig_lat2

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --describe --variant split_contig_compound2x128_lat2

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --variant split_contig_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --variant split_contig_compound2x128_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_(split_contig_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, 2, 2, 2>|compound2x128_split_contig_kernel)' 100

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=165
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

split_contig_compound2x128_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=242
  static_shared=97304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  best=1.273 ms median=1.280 ms
  useful=48.20 TF/s roof70=68.9%
  checksum=92.0000

split_contig_compound2x128_lat2:
  best=1.927 ms median=1.932 ms
  useful=31.85 TF/s roof70=45.5%
  checksum=92.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

split_contig_compound2x128_lat2:
  REG=242 STACK=0 SHARED=97304
  instr=1216 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=48 STS=8 STG=1
  F2FP=32 BAR=45 DEPBAR=45 IMAD=90 IADD3=97 PRMT=0 LOP3=112 SHF=88
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- The probe keeps Tensor Core lowering: `HMMA=256`, `LDSM=96`, and
  `LDGSTS=80`, so the slowdown is not a Tensor Core fallback.
- It barely reduces shared memory: `98304B -> 97304B`. That does not cross the
  A10G residency threshold, so occupancy remains one CTA per SM.
- The split 2x128 structure sharply worsens live state and dependency behavior:
  registers rise `165 -> 242`, barriers rise `BAR 33 -> 45`, dependency
  barriers rise `DEPBAR 32 -> 45`, and logical/shift work rises
  `LOP3 79 -> 112`, `SHF 63 -> 88`.
- The lower `IMAD` and lower `STG` counts do not help because the two strided
  output views and two live accumulators make CUDA Tile generate a worse dynamic
  schedule.

Decision:

- Do not promote `split_contig_compound2x128_lat2` to source.
- Treat same-CTA half-width accumulator splitting as closed for the QKV
  split-contiguous producer unless a new CUDA Tile spelling can prove a real
  shared-memory drop below the one-CTA threshold while keeping registers near
  the baseline.
- The QKV producer gap is still a staging/live-range problem around the current
  `TM32 x TN256 x TK16` body, not a simple accumulator-width or store-view
  problem. The next useful work should target producer-consumer fusion or a
  different data layout that removes the Q/K/V global-memory round trip without
  rereading A or rerotating keys.

### CUDA Tile Purity Cleanup: Bench Init Helper

Goal: remove the last raw `__global__` kernel from the checked source and bench
tree so the CUDA Tile-first purity gate can be used as a strict signal for both
`src` and active benchmark programs.

Official CUDA Tile reference checked first:

- CUDA 13.3 `Writing Tile Kernels` says CUDA Tile C++ is exposed by
  `cuda_tile.h` / `cuda::tiles`.
- Tile kernels use `__tile_global__`, query block position with `ct::bid()`,
  and launch with the second chevron argument fixed to `1`.
- Tile factories such as `ct::iota<Tile>()` and masked stores are the intended
  way to express per-tile element coverage and tails.

Change:

- Renamed historical local variables `wmma_out` to `tile_out` in
  `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`. This is behavior-neutral but prevents false positive
  purity scans against the no-raw-WMMA requirement.
- Replaced `bench/bf16_mask_hidden_cutile.cu`'s raw CUDA
  `init_bf16_kernel<<<grid,256>>>` helper with a CUDA Tile
  `__tile_global__` kernel launched as `<<<grid,1>>>`.
- The new init helper uses `ct::tile`, `ct::bid()`, `ct::iota`, and
  `ct::store_masked`. It is benchmark input generation only, so exact random
  initialization values are not part of the measured operator contract.

Validation:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile -j8
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile
./build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile --variant t32x64x64 --k 1024 --warmup 3 --iters 5
git diff --check -- src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu bench/bf16_mask_hidden_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Results:

```text
build-min/cudasep_infer:
  audit PASS
  dynamic dependencies: libcudart.so.13, libstdc++, libm, libgcc_s, libc
  no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN dependency
  no forbidden dynamic symbols or binary strings

build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile:
  audit PASS
  dynamic dependencies: libcudart.so.13, libstdc++, libgcc_s, libc
  no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN dependency
  no forbidden dynamic symbols or binary strings

bench short run:
  t32x64x64 K=1024 tile=32x64x64 grid=(41,16) MPad=1312
  best=0.0846 ms median=0.0858 ms
  useful=32.24 TF/s issued=32.51 TF/s roof=46.1%
  checksum=5.3438
```

Decision:

- Keep the cleanup. It moves the experiment harness closer to the project-level
  CUDA Tile-first requirement without changing the production inference math.
- Do not interpret the short `mask_hidden` timing as a new optimization result;
  it only proves the converted bench still runs after the init helper became a
  Tile kernel.

### CUDA Tile Purity Gate Hardening and Current Hotspot Triage

Goal: make the purity gate fail on raw CUDA kernels too, then re-rank the
current default BF16 path by runtime and static SASS/resource evidence.

Change:

- Extended `bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh` source scan to reject raw
  `__global__` in addition to cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN, raw WMMA, inline `mma.sync`,
  and inline asm patterns.
- This does not reject `__tile_global__`, so CUDA Tile kernels remain valid.

Validation:

```bash
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_mask_hidden_cutile
rg -n "__global__|cuBLAS|cublas|cuFFT|cufft|cuDNN|cudnn|nvcuda::wmma|wmma::|mma\\.sync|asm\\s*\\(" \
  CMakeLists.txt src bench \
  -g '!bench/README.md' \
  -g '!bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh' \
  -g '!bench/.ipynb_checkpoints/**'
git diff --check -- bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh bench/README.md \
  bench/bf16_mask_hidden_cutile.cu src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu
./bench/nsys_kernel_summary.sh bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite 35
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel<6, false, 64, false, false, 2, true, true, true, false>|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true>|linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, false, false, 0, false, 2>|qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, 2>|freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16, false>|split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64_kernel<__nv_bfloat16>|rotary_time_split_contig_inplace_kernel<128, __nv_bfloat16>' \
  40
```

```text
  scalar math: FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160

qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full<32,256,16,...>:
  REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16<32,256,16,...>:
  REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=992 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=1
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 LOP3=77 SHF=61

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input<64,32,true,true>:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=64 IADD3=134 PRMT=81 LOP3=145 SHF=124
  scalar math: FFMA=344 FADD=202 FMUL=166 MUFU=124

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60<16,false>:
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528 LOCAL=0
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 LDG=9 STS=22 STG=1 F2FP=8
  FFMA=88 FADD=32 FMUL=10 MUFU=18 BAR=16 IMAD=180 PRMT=72
```

Interpretation:

- FFN12 is now the largest end-to-end kernel group. It keeps `HMMA=256`, but
  `REG=255` and `SHARED=98304` make it a one-CTA-per-SM kernel. Its scalar GELU
  math is also large, so the limiting factor is live state plus epilogue math,
  not missing Tensor Cores.
- The long BKN linear and QKV producer both keep `HMMA=256`, but both use
  `SHARED=98304`, so their current A10G ceiling is constrained by CTA residency
  before dense BF16 Tensor Core peak becomes reachable.
- Time attention is a different class: it only has `HMMA=160` and mixes online
  softmax work (`MUFU=124`, many FFMA/FADD/FMUL) with QK/AV. It should not be
  judged as a pure 70 TFLOP/s dense GEMM kernel.
- Freq attention has very low `HMMA=16` and heavy address/control work relative
  to math, so its useful TFLOP/s ceiling is inherently lower unless the dataflow
  changes.

Next experiment priority:

1. FFN12: look for a dataflow that reduces shared/register residency pressure
   without undoing the current `HMMA=256` schedule.
2. Time attention: optimize as a fused attention/softmax kernel, not as a dense
   GEMM clone. The target is lower scalar/control overhead and better occupancy,
   not simply higher HMMA count.
3. QKV/long linear: only revisit if a candidate can reduce `98304B` shared or
   eliminate a producer-consumer global-memory boundary without raising live
   registers. Repeating `TN/TK/hint/store` variants is already closed.

### FFN12 Next-Probe Selection Audit

Goal: choose the next FFN12 experiment from evidence instead of repeating
already-closed Tile shape changes.

Current source-relevant FFN12 baseline:

```text
ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile<6,false,64,false,false,2,true,true,true,false>
runtime: 96 calls, 177.050 ms total in current default BF16 profile
resource: REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
SASS: instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
scalar math: FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
control/address: BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=192 IADD3=147 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=182
```

Closed simple-shape directions:

```text
THidden=16:
  H32 best=1.795 ms, 45.60 TF/s, HMMA=256, REG=255, SHARED=98304
  H16 best=2.294 ms, 35.68 TF/s, HMMA=192, REG=255, SHARED=96256
  result: shared drops only 2 KiB, still one CTA/SM, and HMMA/control shape worsens.

TM=16 with H32:
  M32/H32 best=1.795 ms, 45.60 TF/s, HMMA=256, REG=255, SHARED=98304
  M16/H32 best=2.906 ms, 28.16 TF/s, HMMA=128, REG=228, SHARED=100352
  result: registers improve, but shared gets worse and HMMA halves.

hidden-pair x2:
  baseline best=1.8035 ms, 45.38 TF/s, HMMA=256, REG=255, SHARED=98304
  pair2 best=3.1168 ms, 26.26 TF/s, HMMA=256, REG=255, SHARED=98304
  result: preserving HMMA is not sufficient when the larger Tile loop body
  worsens scheduling and does not break the residency wall.

bias broadcast:
  baseline best=1.8012 ms, 45.44 TF/s, REG=255, SHARED=98304
  broadcast best=1.9504 ms, 41.96 TF/s, REG=255, SHARED=94208
  result: modest shared reduction does not cross the one-CTA threshold and the
  generated schedule is slower.

two-stage FFN1+FFN2:
  fused best=1.7930 ms, 45.64 TF/s
  two-stage best=2.4598 ms, 33.27 TF/s, hidden_rw=0.298 GiB
  result: unfusing reduces the fused live set but pays a hidden global-memory
  round trip and still leaves FFN2 as a 98 KiB one-CTA kernel.
```

Selection rule for the next FFN12 experiment:

- Do not add another simple `TM`, `THidden`, output split, bias-load, or
  latency-hint variant unless it can plausibly change the one-CTA wall.
- A useful candidate must target at least one of these structural changes:
  - reduce static shared from `98304B` below a real A10G residency threshold
    while keeping close to `HMMA=256`;
  - keep the current H32 Tensor Core schedule but reduce the `REG=255` cliff
    without increasing shared or adding stack/local memory;
  - change W2/output ownership so the output accumulator footprint is smaller
    without materializing the full `M x 1024` hidden tensor in global memory.

Next concrete FFN12 hypothesis:

- Prototype a benchmark-only W2/output-ownership change, not a smaller hidden
  or row tile. The target is to keep the current `TM32`, `THidden32`, `TK64`,
  one-hidden-pair body, but reduce how much output accumulator state is live at
  once. The acceptance gate is strict:
  `HMMA` must stay near 256, `SHARED` must move meaningfully below 98 KiB or
  `REG` must move below the cliff without new stack/local memory, and measured
  time must beat the current `~1.80 ms` FFN12 microbench baseline.

### FFN12 TN64/y2 Describe Coverage

Goal: close a diagnostics gap in the standalone FFN12 benchmark. The
`fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2_outnoround` variant already had timing and SASS
evidence, but `--describe` did not print its CUDA runtime resource line.

Change:

- Added the missing `describe_kernel()` entry for
  `fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2_outnoround` in
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.
- Source inference code is unchanged.

Validation:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2_outnoround
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|tn64_y2)' 30
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu
```

Runtime resource result:

```text
fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,2,1) waves/SM=61.0
  attr_regs=254 static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=0B
```

Static SASS/resource summary:

```text
fused_pairh32_tn64_y2_lat2:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2944 HMMA=288 LDSM=168 LDGSTS=96 LDG=128 STS=54 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=384 FADD=176 FMUL=240
  BAR=27 DEPBAR=48 IMAD=145 IADD3=144 PRMT=92 LOP3=129 SHF=183

source-style fused_pairh32_lat2 W1=2/W2=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
```

Interpretation:

- `TN64/y2` does not move the real A10G residency limiter. Shared memory stays
  at `98304B`, so occupancy remains one CTA per SM.
- The output split adds `grid.y=2` and duplicates FFN1/GELU work. Static HMMA
  rises to `288` per CTA instance while useful end-to-end FFN work per logical
  M tile gets worse.
- Keep this as a closed negative result. The next FFN12 probe should not be
  another output-N split; it needs to change the compiler-visible shared staging
  or output accumulator live state without duplicating FFN1.

### Time Attention Lower-Bound Describe Coverage and State-Cost Audit

Goal: close the `--describe` coverage gap for time-attention variants that are
most useful for explaining why the source-relevant `Q64/K32` path is below the
dense BF16 GEMM ceiling.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The existing kernels already use documented CUDA Tile C++ entry points
  (`__tile_global__`) and launches with `<<<grid, 1>>>`.
- This change only improves host-side resource diagnostics for existing Tile
  kernels. It does not change Tile math, launch dimensions, or source inference.

Change:

- Added `describe_kernel_entry(filter_name, print_name, ...)` in
  `bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu` so one benchmark variant can print
  multiple kernel resource lines.
- Added `--describe` entries for:
  `score_av_lb`, `tile_local_softmax_lb`, `two_pass_state`,
  `final_rcp`, `first_init`, `row_l_bf16`, `row_state_bf16`, and no-keytail
  forms where relevant.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_score_av_lb
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tile_local_softmax_lb
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_two_pass_state
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_row_state_bf16
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_final_rcp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_first_init
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false>|score_av_lb_kernel<64, 32, true>|tile_local_softmax_lb_kernel<64, 32, true>|state1280_kernel<64, 32, true>|tail_finalize_kernel<64, 32, true>|first_init_kernel<64, 32, true>|row_state_bf16_kernel<64, 32, true, false>|input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, true, false, false>)' 120
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Timing, same run settings `--warmup 10 --iters 100`:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.392 ms, real_math=60.32 TF/s, roof70=86.2%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

score_av_lb:
  best=3.819 ms, real_math=53.59 TF/s, roof70=76.6%

tile_local_softmax_lb:
  best=4.249 ms, real_math=48.16 TF/s, roof70=68.8%

two_pass_state:
  best=3.798 ms, real_math=53.87 TF/s, roof70=77.0%

final_rcp:
  best=3.397 ms, real_math=60.24 TF/s, roof70=86.1%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

first_init:
  best=3.454 ms, real_math=59.24 TF/s, roof70=84.6%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

row_l_bf16:
  best=3.502 ms, real_math=58.42 TF/s, roof70=83.5%
  compare max_abs=0.009765625 rms=0.0054707089 rel_rms=0.0220701939
```

Runtime resource summary:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 local=0 occupancy=2 CTA/SM

score_av_lb:
  REG=255 SHARED=57344 local=8 occupancy=1 CTA/SM

tile_local_softmax_lb:
  REG=255 SHARED=45056 local=152 occupancy=2 CTA/SM

two_pass_state1280:
  REG=215 SHARED=40960 local=0 occupancy=2 CTA/SM

two_pass_tail_finalize:
  REG=124 SHARED=42240 local=0 occupancy=2 CTA/SM

final_rcp:
  REG=235 SHARED=40960 local=0 occupancy=2 CTA/SM

first_init:
  REG=234 SHARED=49152 local=0 occupancy=2 CTA/SM

row_l_bf16:
  REG=238 SHARED=40960 local=0 occupancy=2 CTA/SM
```

Static SASS/resource highlights:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 MUFU=124 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 PRMT=80

score_av_lb:
  instr=1600 HMMA=224 MUFU=0 FFMA=0 FADD=224 FMUL=112
  BAR=19 DEPBAR=12 IMAD=151 PRMT=81

tile_local_softmax_lb:
  instr=3336 HMMA=192 MUFU=130 FFMA=170 FADD=434 FMUL=204
  BAR=17 DEPBAR=7 IMAD=175 PRMT=81

two_pass_state1280:
  instr=1576 HMMA=128 MUFU=72 FFMA=136 FADD=160 FMUL=136
  BAR=13 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=17 PRMT=0

two_pass_tail_finalize:
  instr=1664 HMMA=32 MUFU=52 FFMA=204 FADD=42 FMUL=34
  BAR=4 DEPBAR=0 IMAD=254 PRMT=81

final_rcp:
  instr=2328 HMMA=160 MUFU=94 FFMA=190 FADD=202 FMUL=202
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=57 PRMT=80

row_l_bf16:
```
