```text
  instr=2968 HMMA=160 MUFU=124 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=182 PRMT=173
```

Interpretation:

- The current exact `Q64/K32` path is already a strong CUDA Tile attention
  kernel at about `60.3 TF/s` real math, roughly `86%` of the local dense BF16
  reference. Its remaining gap is not explained by Tensor Core absence:
  SASS keeps `HMMA=160`.
- `score_av_lb` removes online softmax semantics, but compiles to a worse
  resource shape (`REG=255`, `SHARED=57344`, one CTA/SM). It is slower despite
  higher `HMMA=224`. This proves "less math" is not the bottom criterion; Tile
  codegen/live state and staging dominate.
- `tile_local_softmax_lb` has even more scalar work and spills (`local=152`),
  so local per-tile softmax is not a useful approximation path.
- `two_pass_state` proves that the main online-softmax state can compile much
  lighter (`REG=215`, `PRMT=0`, `IMAD=17`), but storing `state_acc/state_m/state_l`
  to global memory and launching `tail_finalize` loses the saved time.
- `final_rcp` reduces static instruction count but does not improve runtime,
  so final divide/reciprocal is not the limiting factor.
- `row_l_bf16` and row-state BF16 variants are not safe for this path: they are
  slower and produce visible numeric drift in BF16 output. The online softmax
  normalization state should stay FP32 for now.

Decision:

- Keep source time attention on the current exact `Q64/K32` split-contiguous
  kernel.
- The next useful time-attention probe should try to get the `two_pass_state`
  low-register schedule without global state materialization. A candidate must
  preserve `HMMA=160`, keep occupancy at two CTA/SM, and reduce the baseline
  `REG=233`/scalar-control footprint inside one kernel.

### Time Attention Tail Helper Live-Range Probe

Goal: test whether moving the keytail merge into a `__tile__` helper can make
the CUDA Tile compiler shorten tail/live ranges inside a single kernel, without
materializing `state_acc/state_m/state_l` in global memory.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- The new helper is a CUDA Tile function called from a CUDA Tile kernel.
- The new benchmark kernel still launches with `<<<grid, 1>>>` and uses the
  same compile-time `QRows=64`, `KTile=32`, and `D=64` Tile shapes as the
  source-relevant baseline.

Change:

- Added `merge_keytail_helper()` and
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_tail_helper_kernel` to
  `bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu`.
- Added focused runner, `--describe` coverage, and `--help` entry for
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_helper`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_helper
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_helper \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false>|tail_helper_kernel<64, 32, true>|state1280_kernel<64, 32, true>|tail_finalize_kernel<64, 32, true>)' 100
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu
```

Timing and correctness:

```text
tail_helper:
  best=3.393 ms median=3.395 ms
  real_math=60.31 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.31 TF/s roof70=86.2%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

tail_helper:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

two_pass_state1280 reference:
  REG=215 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=1576 HMMA=128 BAR=13 IMAD=17 PRMT=0
```

Interpretation:

- The helper version is exact and performance-neutral. It compiles to the same
  resource usage and identical static SASS counts as the baseline.
- Therefore a normal `__tile__` helper boundary is not enough to expose the
  `two_pass_state` low-register schedule to the compiler. The helper is
  effectively inlined or optimized into the same live-range shape.
- The next useful single-kernel probe must change the real live set, not just
  source organization. Candidate directions are a different tail/state
  recurrence that does not keep tail index/mask state live with the full main
  loop, or a legal Tile dataflow that computes tail contribution with less
  simultaneous `out_acc/row_l/row_m` pressure.

Decision:

- Keep `tail_helper` as a negative codegen diagnostic.
- Do not promote it to source; it proves the gap cannot be solved by simple
  `__tile__` refactoring alone.

### Time Attention Existing Tail-Dataflow Resource Audit

Goal: fill `--describe` coverage for existing single-kernel variants that
actually change the tail/state dataflow, then compare them against the exact
`Q64/K32` baseline.

Change:

- Added `--describe` entries for:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_seg2`,
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_seg2_no_keytail`,
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_first`, and
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_padded_tail_load`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_seg2
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_first
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_padded_tail_load
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_first --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_padded_tail_load --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_seg2 --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false>|tail_first_kernel<64, 32, true>|padded_tail_load_kernel<64, 32, true>|seg2_kernel<64, 32, true, true>)' 120
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing, same run settings:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.387 ms real_math=60.41 TF/s roof70=86.3%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

tail_first:
  best=3.467 ms real_math=59.02 TF/s roof70=84.3%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

padded_tail_load:
  best=3.416 ms real_math=59.90 TF/s roof70=85.6%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

seg2:
  best=3.719 ms real_math=55.02 TF/s roof70=78.6%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 PRMT=80

tail_first:
  REG=221 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2656 HMMA=160 FFMA=306 FADD=200 FMUL=168 MUFU=122
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=78 PRMT=81

padded_tail_load:
  REG=235 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2704 HMMA=160 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=48 PRMT=80

seg2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2856 HMMA=160 FFMA=342 FADD=208 FMUL=278 MUFU=130
  BAR=9 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=20 PRMT=81

seg2_no_keytail describe-only:
  REG=230 SHARED=32768 occupancy=3 CTA/SM
```

Interpretation:

- `tail_first` is the most informative negative result. It proves a single
  kernel can reduce registers from `233` to `221` while preserving exact output
  and `HMMA=160`, but the changed recurrence order still loses runtime
  (`3.467 ms` vs `3.387 ms`). Lower registers alone are not enough.
- `padded_tail_load` reduces `IMAD` and static instruction count but is still
  slower, so masked tail load form is not the dominant cost.
- `seg2` is clearly bad for exact keytail: it pushes the kernel to
  `REG=255`, adds stack, and increases scalar math. Its no-keytail resource
  line is useful only as a lower-bound clue: no-tail dataflow can reach
  `32768B` shared and 3 CTA/SM, but adding exact tail state destroys that shape.

Decision:

- Do not promote `tail_first`, `padded_tail_load`, or `seg2`.
- The remaining time-attention path should target a hybrid of the exact
  baseline and `tail_first`: keep the baseline recurrence order and `HMMA=160`,
  but reduce tail index/state live range without changing the merge order or
  introducing global state.

### Time Attention Tail-First Padded-Tail Hybrid Probe

Goal: test whether combining `tail_first`'s lower live-state pressure with
`padded_tail_load`'s cheaper tail load/index path can beat the exact
`Q64/K32` split-contiguous baseline without losing `HMMA=160` or exact output.

Change:

- Added
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_tail_first_padded_tail_load_kernel`.
- Added focused runner, `--describe` entry, help text, and variant dispatch:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_first_padded_tail_load`.
- The variant uses padded split-contiguous Q/K/V buffers and unmasked tail
  tile loads, while preserving invalid-key probability masking.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_first_padded_tail_load
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_first_padded_tail_load \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_first --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_padded_tail_load --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false>|tail_first_kernel<64, 32, true>|padded_tail_load_kernel<64, 32, true>|tail_first_padded_tail_load_kernel<64, 32, true>)' 120
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and correctness:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.393 ms real_math=60.31 TF/s roof70=86.2%

tail_first:
  best=3.473 ms real_math=58.92 TF/s roof70=84.2%

padded_tail_load:
  best=3.412 ms real_math=59.98 TF/s roof70=85.7%

tail_first_padded_tail_load:
  best=3.428 ms median=3.430 ms
  real_math=59.69 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.66 TF/s roof70=85.3%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

tail_first:
  REG=221 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2656 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=306 FADD=200 FMUL=168 MUFU=122
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=78 IADD3=132 PRMT=81 LOP3=129 SHF=119

padded_tail_load:
  REG=235 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2704 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=12 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=48 IADD3=130 PRMT=80 LOP3=139 SHF=116

tail_first_padded_tail_load:
  REG=215 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2624 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=12 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=306 FADD=200 FMUL=168 MUFU=122
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=63 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=124 SHF=111
```

Interpretation:

- The hybrid is exact and preserves Tensor Core lowering (`HMMA=160`).
- It reaches the lowest register count among the exact single-kernel variants
  tested here (`REG=215`) and the lowest static instruction count
  (`instr=2624`).
- Runtime still loses to the baseline (`3.428 ms` vs `3.393 ms`). This
  falsifies the simple hypothesis that reducing live registers and static
  scalar instruction count is sufficient for this path.
- The remaining penalty is more likely tied to recurrence order and instruction
  scheduling around the online softmax state, not only tail address arithmetic.

Decision:

- Do not promote `tail_first_padded_tail_load` to inference source.
- Keep it as evidence that the next useful probe should preserve the baseline
  main-then-tail merge order while shortening the tail mask/index live range.

### Time Attention Tail-Col Broadcast Padded-Tail Probe

Goal: keep the baseline main-then-tail recurrence order, then combine the
shorter `tail_col_broadcast` validity mask with padded tail loads. This tests
whether tail mask/index live range and masked tail loads are the remaining
runtime limiter after the failed tail-first hybrid.

Change:

- Added a `TailColBroadcast` template branch to
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_padded_tail_load_kernel`.
- Added help text, `--describe` entry, and variant dispatch:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load`.
- The new variant uses padded split-contiguous Q/K/V buffers and a `1 x KTile`
  tail validity mask.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_tail_col_broadcast --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_padded_tail_load --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, (false|true), false>|padded_tail_load_kernel<64, 32, true, (false|true)>)' 140
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and correctness, same run settings:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.388 ms real_math=60.40 TF/s roof70=86.3%

tail_col_broadcast:
  best=3.396 ms real_math=60.25 TF/s roof70=86.1%

padded_tail_load:
  best=3.411 ms real_math=59.99 TF/s roof70=85.7%

tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load:
  best=3.410 ms median=3.412 ms
  real_math=60.01 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.00 TF/s roof70=85.7%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

tail_col_broadcast:
  REG=239 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2592 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=344 FADD=198 FMUL=170 MUFU=120
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=30 IADD3=122 PRMT=80 LOP3=112 SHF=122

padded_tail_load:
  REG=235 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2704 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=12 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=48 IADD3=130 PRMT=80 LOP3=139 SHF=116

tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2560 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=12 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=344 FADD=198 FMUL=170 MUFU=120
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=11 IADD3=112 PRMT=80 LOP3=103 SHF=111
```

Interpretation:

- The combined variant is exact and preserves Tensor Core lowering
  (`HMMA=160`).
- It gives the cleanest tail-index SASS so far: `IMAD=11`, `instr=2560`, and
  `REG=230`.
- Runtime still does not beat the baseline (`3.410 ms` vs `3.388 ms`). This
  rules out tail index arithmetic and masked tail loads as the dominant
  remaining limiter for the exact single-kernel path.
- The baseline's slower-looking tail SASS may schedule better around the online
  softmax state, or the remaining cost is dominated by `MUFU`/state dependency
  latency and synchronization rather than integer address work.

Decision:

- Do not promote `tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load` to inference source.
- Next probes should target online softmax state scheduling or row-state
  lifetime, not further tail index arithmetic cleanup.

### Time Attention First-Init Tail-Col Padded-Tail Probe

Goal: test whether removing the first online-softmax iteration's `-inf`
initial-state dependency can help scheduling when the tail path is already
cleaned up with a column-broadcast validity mask and padded tail loads.

Change:

- Added `TailColBroadcastPaddedTailLoad` as a template branch on
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_first_init_kernel`.
- Added help text, `--describe` entry, and focused dispatch:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_first_init_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load`.
- The new branch uses padded split-contiguous Q/K/V buffers and a `1 x KTile`
  tail validity mask.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_first_init_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_first_init_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_first_init \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_(input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false>|first_init_kernel<64, 32, true, (false|true)>|padded_tail_load_kernel<64, 32, true, true>)' 140
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and correctness:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.393 ms real_math=60.31 TF/s roof70=86.2%

first_init:
  best=3.455 ms real_math=59.22 TF/s roof70=84.6%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0

first_init_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load:
  best=3.481 ms median=3.482 ms
  real_math=58.79 TF/s logical_tile_math=60.74 TF/s roof70=84.0%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

first_init:
  REG=234 STACK=0 SHARED=49152 LOCAL=0
  instr=3080 HMMA=192 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=15 STS=56 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=340 FADD=240 FMUL=202 MUFU=140
  BAR=17 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=87 IADD3=141 PRMT=160 LOP3=161 SHF=130

tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2560 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=12 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=344 FADD=198 FMUL=170 MUFU=120
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=11 IADD3=112 PRMT=80 LOP3=103 SHF=111

first_init_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load:
  REG=234 STACK=0 SHARED=49152 LOCAL=0
  instr=2888 HMMA=192 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=16 STS=56 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=344 FADD=236 FMUL=202 MUFU=136
  BAR=17 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=41 IADD3=133 PRMT=160 LOP3=122 SHF=123
```

Interpretation:

- The variant is exact but clearly slower than both baseline and plain
  `first_init`.
- The cleaned tail cuts `first_init` integer/control code (`IMAD=87 -> 41`,
  `instr=3080 -> 2888`), but it cannot remove the structural cost introduced by
  first-tile initialization.
- `first_init` changes the CUDA Tile lowering shape: `HMMA=160 -> 192`,
  `SHARED=40960 -> 49152`, `STS=44 -> 56`, `BAR=15 -> 17`, and `MUFU=124 -> 136`
  or higher. This is the opposite of the desired two-pass main-state shape.
- Removing the first `-inf` recurrence dependency is not a useful path on this
  A10G/CUDA Tile codegen. The main loop should keep the baseline initialization
  form unless a different phase boundary can reduce state without extra staging.

Decision:

- Do not promote `first_init_tail_col_broadcast_padded_tail_load` to source.
- Next state probes should not start from `first_init`; they should target
  reducing `MUFU`/state dependency latency inside the baseline loop or finding a
  Tile-native phase boundary that does not increase shared staging/barriers.

### Time Attention Alpha-One State-Rescale Probe

Goal: isolate the cost of the online-softmax history rescale
`alpha = exp(row_m - new_m)` inside the exact baseline loop. This diagnostic
keeps QK, row max, probability exp, BF16 probability AV, and row denominator
updates, but replaces the state-rescale alpha with constant `1.0f`.

This is not a candidate for source correctness; it is a controlled
MUFU/dependency experiment.

Change:

- Added `SkipAlphaExp` as a template branch on
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel`.
- Added help text, `--describe` entry, and focused dispatch:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_alpha_one`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_alpha_one
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_alpha_one \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, (false|true)>' 140
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.387 ms real_math=60.41 TF/s roof70=86.3%

alpha_one:
  best=3.414 ms median=3.416 ms
  real_math=59.93 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.91 TF/s roof70=85.6%
  compare max_abs=0 rms=0 rel_rms=0
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

alpha_one:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2680 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=170 FADD=362 FMUL=160 MUFU=114
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=88 IADD3=134 PRMT=80 LOP3=155 SHF=121
```

Interpretation:

- The deterministic bench input remains BF16-output exact even with
  `alpha=1.0f`, but this should not be treated as a general correctness proof.
- Removing the alpha exp only reduces `MUFU` by `10` static instructions
  (`124 -> 114`). The dominant exp work is still the per-score probability path,
  not the row-state alpha path.
- Codegen gets worse in other places: `REG=233 -> 237`, `IMAD=58 -> 88`,
  `LOP3=134 -> 155`, and runtime regresses (`3.414 ms` vs `3.387 ms`).
- Therefore the row-history alpha exp is not the main limiter. The next useful
  MUFU probe must target the per-score probability exp or the dependency chain
  created by `scores - new_m`, not just `row_m - new_m`.

Decision:

- Keep `alpha_one` as a diagnostic only.
- Do not promote it to source.

### Time Attention Probability Linear Approx Probe

Goal: directly test the per-score probability exp path after `alpha_one` showed
that row-history alpha exp is not the dominant cost. This diagnostic replaces

```text
probs = exp(scores - new_m)
```

with a no-MUFU linear clamp:

```text
probs = max(0, 1 + 0.25 * (scores - new_m))
```

The alpha state rescale remains exact. This is not a correctness candidate; it
is an A10G/CUDA Tile codegen diagnostic.

Change:

- Added `softmax_prob<UseExp2, LinearProb>()`.
- Added `LinearProb` as a template branch on
  `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel`.
- Added help text, `--describe` entry, and focused dispatch:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_linear`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_linear
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_linear \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (false|true)>' 140
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.387 ms real_math=60.42 TF/s roof70=86.3%

prob_linear:
  best=4.281 ms median=4.302 ms
  real_math=47.80 TF/s logical_tile_math=49.38 TF/s roof70=68.3%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.00090637243
  rel_rms=0.00365653074
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_linear:
  REG=196 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
  instr=1744 HMMA=64 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=4 LDG=5 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=32 FFMA=272 FADD=82 FMUL=68 MUFU=38
  BAR=6 DEPBAR=2 IMAD=179 IADD3=85 PRMT=81 LOP3=112 SHF=78
```

Interpretation:

- The approximation lowers resource pressure substantially (`REG=196`,
  `SHARED=32768`, 3 CTA/SM) and reduces `MUFU` (`124 -> 38`).
- Runtime is much worse because CUDA Tile lowering changes the Tensor Core
  schedule: `HMMA=160 -> 64`, `LDSM=64 -> 16`, and `LDGSTS=16 -> 4`.
- This proves that "remove probability exp" is not sufficient. The probability
  path is entangled with the Tile compiler's MMA/staging schedule; a useful
  approximation must preserve the baseline `HMMA=160` issue structure.
- Output drift on the deterministic bench is moderate at BF16 output precision
  (`rel_rms=0.00366`), but performance already disqualifies this simple linear
  clamp as an acceleration path.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_linear` as a diagnostic only.
- The next probability-exp experiment should preserve the baseline
  probability tile type and AV MMA shape, or approximate exp in a way that does
  not collapse `HMMA` count.

### Time Attention Probability Poly4 Approx Probe

Goal: follow up on `prob_linear`, which removed probability `MUFU` but collapsed
the Tensor Core schedule from `HMMA=160` to `HMMA=64`. This probe uses a heavier
no-exp approximation:

```text
probs = max(0, 1 + 0.25 * (scores - new_m))^4
```

The hypothesis is that a richer probability expression may keep CUDA Tile's
baseline AV MMA/staging schedule while still reducing `MUFU`.

Change:

- Added `Poly4Prob` as a template branch in `softmax_prob()`.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4 \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (false|true), (false|true)>' 140
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.392 ms real_math=60.33 TF/s roof70=86.2%

prob_poly4:
  best=3.423 ms median=3.432 ms
  real_math=59.78 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.76 TF/s roof70=85.4%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000245217274
  rel_rms=0.000989267181
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_linear:
  REG=196 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
  instr=1744 HMMA=64 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=4 LDG=5 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=32 FFMA=272 FADD=82 FMUL=68 MUFU=38
  BAR=6 DEPBAR=2 IMAD=179 IADD3=85 PRMT=81 LOP3=112 SHF=78

prob_poly4:
  REG=239 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2840 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=330 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=66 IADD3=132 PRMT=80 LOP3=152 SHF=122
```

Interpretation:

- `prob_poly4` preserves the critical baseline Tensor Core schedule:
  `HMMA=160`, `LDSM=64`, `LDGSTS=16`, and shared memory `40960B`.
- It cuts `MUFU` substantially (`124 -> 44`) and has much smaller output drift
  than `prob_linear` (`rel_rms=0.00099` vs `0.00366`).
- Runtime is still slower than baseline (`3.423 ms` vs `3.392 ms`) because the
  saved `MUFU` is replaced by much more scalar arithmetic:
  `FFMA=340 -> 420`, `FMUL=170 -> 330`, `REG=233 -> 239`.
- This is the first useful probability-approx direction: it proves a no-exp
  probability path can keep the baseline HMMA schedule. The remaining task is
  reducing polynomial arithmetic/register pressure enough to beat exact exp.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly4` as a diagnostic and possible starting point.
- Do not promote it to source yet.
- Next probability approximation should preserve `HMMA=160` like `prob_poly4`
  but use fewer FMUL/FFMA or a better-fitted lower-order form.

### Time Attention Probability Poly2 Approx Probe

Goal: reduce the scalar arithmetic cost of `prob_poly4` while preserving the
baseline Tensor Core schedule. The approximation is:

```text
probs = max(0, 1 + 0.5 * (scores - new_m))^2
```

Change:

- Added `Poly2Prob` as another `softmax_prob()` template branch.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly2`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly2
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly2 \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, (false|true), (false|true)>' 160
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.387 ms real_math=60.42 TF/s roof70=86.3%

prob_poly2:
  best=3.463 ms median=3.465 ms
  real_math=59.10 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.05 TF/s roof70=84.4%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000290638016
  rel_rms=0.0011725057
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_poly4:
  REG=239 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2840 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=330 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=66 IADD3=132 PRMT=80 LOP3=152 SHF=122

prob_poly2:
  REG=240 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2760 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=108 IADD3=125 PRMT=80 LOP3=153 SHF=119
```

Interpretation:

- `prob_poly2` preserves the critical `HMMA=160` schedule, so the minimum
  complexity needed to avoid the `prob_linear` lowering collapse is no higher
  than this two-power form.
- It reduces `FMUL` versus `prob_poly4` (`330 -> 250`) but increases integer
  address/control work (`IMAD=66 -> 108`) and register pressure
  (`REG=239 -> 240`).
- Runtime is worse than `prob_poly4` (`3.463 ms` vs `3.423 ms`) and output drift
  is also slightly worse. Lower polynomial order alone is not enough; the
  generated integer/control schedule matters more than the saved FMULs here.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly2` as a negative diagnostic.
- Continue from `prob_poly4`-style HMMA-preserving approximations, but optimize
  expression shape/codegen rather than simply lowering polynomial order.

### Time Attention Probability Poly4 No-Clamp Probe

Goal: isolate whether the clamp in `prob_poly4` causes extra control/integer
codegen. This variant keeps the same fourth-power approximation but removes the
`ct::select` clamp:

```text
probs = (1 + 0.25 * (scores - new_m))^4
```

This is a diagnostic only; removing the clamp is not generally safe.

Change:

- Added `Poly4NoClampProb` as another `softmax_prob()` template branch.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, (false|true), false, (false|true)>' 180
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.393 ms real_math=60.31 TF/s roof70=86.2%

prob_poly4:
  best=3.423 ms real_math=59.78 TF/s roof70=85.4%
  rel_rms=0.000989267181

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  best=3.417 ms median=3.425 ms
  real_math=59.89 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.87 TF/s roof70=85.6%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000245217274
  rel_rms=0.000989267181
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_poly4:
  REG=239 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2840 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=330 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=66 IADD3=132 PRMT=80 LOP3=152 SHF=122

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  REG=229 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2592 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=21 IADD3=137 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=125
```

Interpretation:

- Removing the clamp preserves the critical Tensor Core schedule (`HMMA=160`)
  and substantially improves codegen over clamped poly4:
  `REG=239 -> 229`, `instr=2840 -> 2592`, `IMAD=66 -> 21`, `LOP3=152 -> 134`.
- Output drift on the deterministic bench is unchanged from clamped `poly4`
  at BF16 output precision.
- Runtime improves slightly versus `prob_poly4` (`3.417 ms` vs `3.423 ms`) but
  still loses to exact exp (`3.393 ms`). The remaining gap is now mostly the
  extra polynomial arithmetic: `FFMA=420`, `FMUL=250` versus baseline
  `FFMA=340`, `FMUL=170`.
- This is the best probability-approx codegen shape so far, and proves the
  clamp was a real scalar/control cost. It is still not enough to beat exact
  exp on A10G.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly4_noclamp` as the current best approximation diagnostic.
- Do not promote it to source.
- Next approximation work should keep this no-clamp/HMMA-preserving structure
  but reduce the additional multiply/FFMA count.

### Time Attention Probability Poly2 No-Clamp Probe

Goal: reduce arithmetic further from `prob_poly4_noclamp` by using the no-clamp
quadratic form:

```text
probs = (1 + 0.5 * (scores - new_m))^2
```

Change:

- Added `Poly2NoClampProb` as another `softmax_prob()` template branch.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly2_noclamp`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly2_noclamp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly2_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, (false|true), (false|true)>' 200
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.388 ms real_math=60.40 TF/s roof70=86.3%

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  best=3.417 ms real_math=59.89 TF/s roof70=85.6%
  rel_rms=0.000989267181

prob_poly2_noclamp:
  best=3.535 ms median=3.543 ms
  real_math=57.88 TF/s logical_tile_math=59.79 TF/s roof70=82.7%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000290638016
  rel_rms=0.0011725057
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  REG=229 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2592 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=21 IADD3=137 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=125

prob_poly2_noclamp:
  REG=255 STACK=72 SHARED=45056 LOCAL=0
  instr=2896 HMMA=192 LDSM=80 LDGSTS=20 LDG=13 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=470 FADD=242 FMUL=204 MUFU=46
  BAR=17 DEPBAR=7 IMAD=55 IADD3=141 PRMT=80 LOP3=136 SHF=142
```

Interpretation:

- The no-clamp quadratic is a clear negative result. It does not preserve the
  clean `prob_poly4_noclamp` schedule.
- CUDA Tile lowering changes shape: `HMMA=160 -> 192`, `SHARED=40960 -> 45056`,
  `BAR=15 -> 17`, and it spills (`STACK=72`, `REG=255`).
- Although it has fewer `FMUL` than `prob_poly4_noclamp`, the changed lowering
  and spill dominate runtime.
- This reinforces that expression shape, not only nominal operation count,
  controls the useful CUDA Tile codegen.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly2_noclamp` as a negative diagnostic.
- The current best approximation starting point remains `prob_poly4_noclamp`.

### Time Attention Probability Linear No-Clamp Probe

Goal: determine whether the `prob_linear` Tensor Core collapse (`HMMA=64`) was
caused by its clamp/select, or by the linear probability expression itself. This
variant removes the clamp:

```text
probs = 1 + 0.25 * (scores - new_m)
```

Change:

- Added `LinearNoClampProb` as another `softmax_prob()` template branch.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_linear_noclamp`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_linear_noclamp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_linear_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (false|true), false, false, false, false, (false|true)>' 200
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.391 ms real_math=60.34 TF/s roof70=86.2%

prob_linear:
  best=4.281 ms real_math=47.80 TF/s roof70=68.3%
  rel_rms=0.00365653074

prob_linear_noclamp:
  best=4.140 ms median=4.168 ms
  real_math=49.43 TF/s logical_tile_math=51.06 TF/s roof70=70.6%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.00090637243
  rel_rms=0.00365653074
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_linear:
  REG=196 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
  instr=1744 HMMA=64 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=4 LDG=5 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=32 FFMA=272 FADD=82 FMUL=68 MUFU=38
  BAR=6 DEPBAR=2 IMAD=179 IADD3=85 PRMT=81 LOP3=112 SHF=78

prob_linear_noclamp:
  REG=193 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
  instr=1640 HMMA=64 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=4 LDG=5 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=32 FFMA=272 FADD=82 FMUL=36 MUFU=38
  BAR=6 DEPBAR=2 IMAD=158 IADD3=88 PRMT=81 LOP3=112 SHF=80
```

Interpretation:

- Removing the clamp improves the linear variant's static code slightly
  (`instr=1744 -> 1640`, `REG=196 -> 193`, `FMUL=68 -> 36`,
  `IMAD=179 -> 158`) and runtime improves (`4.281 ms -> 4.140 ms`).
- It still keeps the bad Tensor Core schedule: `HMMA=64`, `LDSM=16`,
  `LDGSTS=4`, and 3 CTA/SM. Therefore the `prob_linear` collapse is not caused
  primarily by clamp/select; the linear expression itself is too simple or too
  different for CUDA Tile to keep the baseline AV MMA/staging schedule.
- This confirms that a useful no-exp probability approximation must have enough
  expression structure to preserve `HMMA=160`. Current evidence points back to
  `prob_poly4_noclamp`.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_linear_noclamp` as a negative diagnostic.
- Continue from `prob_poly4_noclamp`, not from linear forms.

### Time Attention Probability Poly4 No-Clamp Bias Probe

Goal: test an algebraically equivalent expression shape for
`prob_poly4_noclamp`. Instead of:

```text
t = 1 + 0.25 * (scores - new_m)
```

this probe uses a row-bias form:

```text
t = 0.25 * scores + (1 - 0.25 * new_m)
probs = t^4
```

The intended diagnostic is whether hoisting the row term changes CUDA Tile
codegen while keeping the same output drift.

Change:

- Added `softmax_probs_from_scores()` as a wrapper around the probability helper.
- Added `Poly4NoClampBiasProb` branch.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp_bias`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp_bias
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp_bias \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, (true|false), false, false, (true|false)>' 220
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.388 ms real_math=60.40 TF/s roof70=86.3%

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  best=3.417 ms real_math=59.89 TF/s roof70=85.6%
  rel_rms=0.000989267181

prob_poly4_noclamp_bias:
  best=3.570 ms median=3.575 ms
  real_math=57.32 TF/s logical_tile_math=59.21 TF/s roof70=81.9%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000245100476
  rel_rms=0.000988795989
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  REG=229 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2592 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=21 IADD3=137 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=125

prob_poly4_noclamp_bias:
  REG=255 STACK=80 SHARED=45056 LOCAL=0
  instr=2920 HMMA=192 LDSM=80 LDGSTS=20 LDG=13 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=470 FADD=158 FMUL=312 MUFU=46
  BAR=17 DEPBAR=7 IMAD=41 IADD3=143 PRMT=80 LOP3=136 SHF=144
```

Interpretation:

- The row-bias expression is algebraically equivalent enough to produce the
  same BF16-output drift, but it is a severe codegen regression.
- CUDA Tile lowering changes from the clean `HMMA=160/SHARED=40960/STACK=0`
  shape into `HMMA=192/SHARED=45056/STACK=80`, with `REG=255` and more scalar
  math.
- Hoisting the row term is not a safe optimization for this Tile compiler path.
  The original `(scores - new_m)` form is much better even though it looks less
  algebraically optimized.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly4_noclamp_bias` as a negative diagnostic.
- The current best probability approximation remains the direct
  `prob_poly4_noclamp` form.

### Time Attention Poly4 No-Clamp BF16 Sum Probe

Goal: test whether reducing the softmax denominator from BF16 probabilities
shortens F32 probability live ranges or reduces scheduling pressure without
changing the currently best approximate probability shape.

Change:

- Added `softmax_tile_sum<ProbSumBf16>()`.
- Added `ProbSumBf16` to the fixed-shape split-contig input kernel template.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16 \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false>|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, true, false, false, false, false>|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, true, false, false, false, true>' 300
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.387 ms median=3.394 ms
  real_math=60.41 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.41 TF/s roof70=86.3%

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  best=3.416 ms median=3.424 ms
  real_math=59.90 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.88 TF/s roof70=85.6%
  rel_rms=0.000989267181

prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16:
  best=4.290 ms median=4.339 ms
  real_math=47.70 TF/s logical_tile_math=49.28 TF/s roof70=68.1%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000289552253
  rel_rms=0.00116812546
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  REG=229 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2592 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=21 IADD3=137 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=125

prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16:
  REG=184 STACK=0 SHARED=32768 LOCAL=0
  instr=1776 HMMA=64 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=4 LDG=5 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=32 FFMA=272 FADD=82 FMUL=100 MUFU=38
  BAR=6 DEPBAR=2 IMAD=161 IADD3=91 PRMT=113 LOP3=112 SHF=92
```

Interpretation:

- BF16 denominator sum does reduce apparent resource pressure
  (`REG=229 -> 184`, `SHARED=40960 -> 32768`, 3 CTA/SM), but this is a bad
  CUDA Tile lowering outcome, not an optimization.
- The main Tensor Core schedule collapses from `HMMA=160/LDSM=64/LDGSTS=16` to
  `HMMA=64/LDSM=16/LDGSTS=4`, matching the earlier `prob_linear` failure mode.
- The runtime loss (`3.416 ms -> 4.290 ms`) is explained by the lost AV MMA
  staging shape. Lower register pressure alone is not a valid objective for
  this A10G path; preserving the `HMMA=160` schedule matters more.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16` as a negative diagnostic.
- Do not use BF16 denominator sum for this fused attention path unless a later
  expression or staging change preserves `HMMA=160`.
- Continue from direct `prob_poly4_noclamp`, and treat any future BF16
  probability experiment as invalid if it collapses HMMA/staging.

### Time Attention Probability Poly3 No-Clamp Probe

Goal: test a lower-degree no-exp probability approximation that may preserve
the clean `HMMA=160` Tensor Core schedule while reducing the scalar work of
`prob_poly4_noclamp`.

Change:

- Added `Poly3NoClampProb` branch:

```text
t = 1 + (scores - new_m) / 3
probs = t^3
```

- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, true>|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, true, false, false, false, false, false>|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false>' 320
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.391 ms median=3.393 ms
  real_math=60.34 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.33 TF/s roof70=86.2%

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  best=3.417 ms median=3.424 ms
  real_math=59.89 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.87 TF/s roof70=85.6%
  rel_rms=0.000989267181

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  best=3.398 ms median=3.405 ms
  real_math=60.21 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.20 TF/s roof70=86.0%
  compare max_abs=0.001953125 rms=0.000265943812
  rel_rms=0.00107288317
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=58 IADD3=129 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=122

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  REG=229 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2592 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=21 IADD3=137 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=125

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  REG=234 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2584 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=56 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8 IMAD=17 IADD3=137 PRMT=80 LOP3=134 SHF=125
```

Interpretation:

- `prob_poly3_noclamp` preserves the good Tensor Core/staging shape:
  `HMMA=160/LDSM=64/LDGSTS=16`.
- CUDA Tile lowering does not reduce FFMA/FMUL compared with `poly4_noclamp`,
  even though the source expression is lower degree. It does reduce a small
  amount of address/integer work (`instr=2592 -> 2584`, `IMAD=21 -> 17`) but
  raises registers (`REG=229 -> 234`).
- Runtime improves over `prob_poly4_noclamp` (`3.417 ms -> 3.398 ms`) and is
  very close to exact exp (`3.391 ms`) while cutting `MUFU=124 -> 44`.
- Output drift is slightly larger than poly4 (`rel_rms=0.00107` vs
  `0.00099`) but still small in this deterministic BF16-output bench.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly3_noclamp` as the current best no-exp probability candidate.
- Continue expression-shape search only if the new form preserves
  `HMMA=160/LDSM=64/LDGSTS=16`; otherwise classify it as a codegen regression
  instead of a real A10G optimization.

### Time Attention Probability Mode Template Cleanup

Goal: remove a measurement-risk source from the split-contig attention bench.
The probability variants had grown into a long chain of boolean template
arguments, and a previous probe briefly mis-instantiated `ProbSumBf16=true`
instead of `Poly3NoClampProb=true`. That made the benchmark easy to misread.

Change:

- Replaced the probability-shape boolean pack with one `ProbMode` enum:

```text
kProbExp = 0
kProbLinear = 1
kProbPoly4 = 2
kProbPoly2 = 3
kProbPoly4NoClamp = 4
kProbPoly2NoClamp = 5
kProbLinearNoClamp = 6
kProbPoly4NoClampBias = 7
kProbPoly3NoClamp = 8
```

- Kept `ProbSumBf16` as the one independent diagnostic switch.
- Updated `--describe` and `--variant` dispatch to use named `kProb*`
  template arguments.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16 \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (0, false|8, false|4, false|4, true)>' 260
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Post-cleanup timing:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.387 ms median=3.389 ms
  real_math=60.42 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.42 TF/s roof70=86.3%

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  best=3.399 ms median=3.400 ms
  real_math=60.21 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.20 TF/s roof70=86.0%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_poly4_noclamp:
  best=3.423 ms median=3.426 ms
  real_math=59.79 TF/s logical_tile_math=61.76 TF/s roof70=85.4%
  rel_rms=0.000989267181

prob_poly4_noclamp_sum_bf16:
  best=4.306 ms median=4.343 ms
  real_math=47.52 TF/s logical_tile_math=49.09 TF/s roof70=67.9%
  rel_rms=0.00116812546
```

SASS/resource check:

```text
ProbMode 0, exact:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 MUFU=124 instr=2736

ProbMode 8, poly3_noclamp:
  REG=234 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 MUFU=44 instr=2584

ProbMode 4, poly4_noclamp:
  REG=229 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 MUFU=44 instr=2592

ProbMode 4 + ProbSumBf16:
  REG=184 SHARED=32768 HMMA=64 MUFU=38 instr=1776
```

Interpretation:

- The cleanup did not change the measured kernel shapes or performance
  conclusions.
- Demangled kernel names now expose the key experimental axis directly:
  `..., 8, false` means `poly3_noclamp`, and `..., 4, true` means
  `poly4_noclamp + ProbSumBf16`.
- Future probability experiments should add a new `kProb*` mode instead of a new
  boolean. This reduces the chance of measuring the wrong CUDA Tile lowering.

Decision:

- Keep `ProbMode` for future attention probability experiments.
- Next useful performance probe is alpha update approximation: after no-exp
  probability, the remaining `MUFU=44` is likely from online-softmax alpha
  updates. Test it only through named modes/switches and reject any variant that
  loses `HMMA=160`.

### Time Attention Poly3 Probability With Clamped Poly3 Alpha

Goal: reduce the remaining scalar special-function work in the current best
no-exp probability path. `prob_poly3_noclamp` still has `MUFU=44`, mostly from
online-softmax alpha updates:

```text
alpha = exp(row_m - new_m)
```

Direct no-clamp alpha is unsafe because the first tile has `row_m=-inf`, and
`0 * -inf` can produce NaNs. This probe uses a clamped cubic alpha:

```text
t = max(1 + (row_m - new_m) / 3, 0)
alpha = t^3
```

Change:

- Added `softmax_alpha_approx()`.
- Added `ApproxAlpha` to the split-contig input kernel template.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (0, false, false|8, false, false|8, false, true)>' 260
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.392 ms median=3.394 ms
  real_math=60.32 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.32 TF/s roof70=86.2%

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  best=3.398 ms median=3.406 ms
  real_math=60.21 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.20 TF/s roof70=86.0%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  first run:
    best=3.373 ms median=3.374 ms
    real_math=60.67 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.68 TF/s roof70=86.7%
    rel_rms=0.00107288317
  repeat:
    best=3.368 ms median=3.375 ms
    real_math=60.75 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.76 TF/s roof70=86.8%
    rel_rms=0.00107288317
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2736 FFMA=340 FMUL=170 MUFU=124

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  REG=234 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2584 FFMA=420 FMUL=250 MUFU=44

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  REG=230 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2600 FFMA=430 FMUL=270 MUFU=34
```

Interpretation:

- The alpha approximation preserves the good Tensor Core/staging shape:
  `HMMA=160/LDSM=64/LDGSTS=16`.
- It does not remove all special-function instructions (`MUFU=44 -> 34`), so
  some MUFU still remains in CUDA Tile lowering. However it lowers register
  pressure (`REG=234 -> 230`) and gives a net runtime win.
- The scalar instruction count increases (`FFMA=420 -> 430`, `FMUL=250 -> 270`,
  `instr=2584 -> 2600`), but this cost is outweighed by the lower MUFU/register
  pressure on A10G for this shape.
- Output drift is identical to `prob_poly3_noclamp` in this deterministic
  BF16-output bench, so the clamped alpha did not introduce additional measured
  drift here.

Decision:

- Current best fixed-shape no-exp time-attention candidate:
  `prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp`.
- Next useful probe is to locate the remaining `MUFU=34` source in SASS/PTX or
  test a simpler clamped linear alpha that may reduce scalar FMUL/FFMA while
  preserving `HMMA=160`.

### Time Attention AlphaMode Cleanup And Linear Alpha Probe

Goal: avoid adding more alpha booleans and test whether a cheaper clamped
linear alpha can beat the current clamped cubic alpha.

Change:

- Replaced the alpha approximation boolean with `AlphaMode`:

```text
kAlphaExact = 0
kAlphaProbClamp = 1
kAlphaLinearClamp = 2
```

- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (0, false, 0|8, false, 0|8, false, 1|8, false, 2)>' 280
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 100
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig:
  best=3.391 ms median=3.393 ms
  real_math=60.35 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.35 TF/s roof70=86.2%

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  best=3.367 ms median=3.401 ms
  real_math=60.77 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.78 TF/s roof70=86.8%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp:
  best=3.407 ms median=3.408 ms
  real_math=60.06 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.05 TF/s roof70=85.8%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2736 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  REG=234 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2584 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  REG=230 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2600 FFMA=430 FADD=202 FMUL=270 MUFU=34

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp:
  REG=235 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2576 FFMA=420 FADD=212 FMUL=250 MUFU=34
```

Interpretation:

- Linear alpha preserves `HMMA=160` and keeps `MUFU=34`, so it is a valid CUDA
  Tile lowering shape.
- It reduces total static instruction count compared with clamped cubic alpha
  (`2600 -> 2576`) and avoids the extra cubic FMUL/FFMA, but raises register
  pressure (`REG=230 -> 235`).
- On A10G this loses clearly (`3.407 ms` vs `3.367 ms`). For this shape,
  lower register pressure and better scheduling matter more than fewer scalar
  arithmetic instructions.

Decision:

- Keep `kAlphaProbClamp` / clamped cubic alpha as the best alpha mode.
- Keep `kAlphaLinearClamp` as a negative diagnostic.
- The remaining `MUFU=34` is probably not alpha exp anymore; next probe should
  inspect final normalization/division or reciprocal lowering.

### Time Attention Final Reciprocal Probe

Goal: test whether the remaining `MUFU=34` in
`prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp` comes from final normalization:

```text
out = out_acc / row_l
```

The kernel already has a `UseFinalReciprocal` path:

```text
inv_l = 1 / row_l
out = out_acc * inv_l
```

Change:

- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp`.
- No source inference change.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, (false|true), false, false, false, 8, false, 1>|time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, 0, false, 0>' 280
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 100 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 200
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

Timing and output comparison:

```text
baseline exact split-contig, 200 iters:
  best=3.359 ms median=3.393 ms
  real_math=60.92 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.93 TF/s roof70=87.0%

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp, 200 iters:
  best=3.339 ms median=3.374 ms
  real_math=61.28 TF/s logical_tile_math=63.31 TF/s roof70=87.5%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp, 100 iters:
  best=3.377 ms median=3.379 ms
  real_math=60.59 TF/s logical_tile_math=62.59 TF/s roof70=86.6%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp, 200 iters:
  best=3.344 ms median=3.379 ms
  real_math=61.20 TF/s logical_tile_math=63.22 TF/s roof70=87.4%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline exact:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2736 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  REG=230 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2600 FFMA=430 FADD=202 FMUL=270 MUFU=34

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp:
  REG=230 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2184 FFMA=280 FADD=202 FMUL=302 MUFU=4
```

Interpretation:

- Final normalization is the major remaining MUFU source:
  `MUFU=34 -> 4` when switching from tile divide to explicit reciprocal +
  multiply.
- The Tensor Core/staging path remains intact:
  `HMMA=160/LDSM=64/LDGSTS=16`.
- Static instruction count drops sharply (`2600 -> 2184`), but FMUL increases
  (`270 -> 302`) and runtime is not consistently better than direct divide.
- With 200 iterations, direct divide still has the best observed best/median
  pair (`3.339/3.374 ms`) versus final reciprocal (`3.344/3.379 ms`), so
  final reciprocal explains MUFU but is not the current best runtime path.

Decision:

- Keep direct final divide as the current best runtime path.
- Keep final reciprocal as a diagnostic showing that final normalization
  accounts for most remaining MUFU.
- Next useful probe should target final normalization without increasing FMUL,
  or move from bench-only attention into the production fused attention path
  once this best candidate is stable enough.

### Production Time Attention Approx Softmax Probe

Goal: migrate the current best bench-only non-qrot split-contig main attention
candidate into the production CLI path behind an explicit experiment flag.

Important scope:

- This only changes the non-qrot split-contig main-q kernel
  (`q rows 0..1279`).
- Tail-q rows and qrot fused attention kernels remain exact in this probe.
- The experiment is off by default and requires:

```bash
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1
```

Change:

- Added `ProbMode`/`AlphaMode` helpers to `src/mbr_time_attention_cutile.cu`.
- Added `approx_softmax` to
  `launch_time_attention1301_split_contig_main_cutile()`.
- Added env gate in `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`:
  `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX`.
- Production approximate instance uses:

```text
Prob = kProbPoly3NoClamp
Alpha = kAlphaProbClamp
final normalization = direct divide
```

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min -j8
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_test_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_baseline_test_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_baseline_test_clean_repeat \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
git diff --check -- src/mbr_time_attention_cutile.cu src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu \
  src/mbr_cuda_tile.h bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu bench/README.md
```

End-to-end timing:

```text
test_clean.wav baseline:
  infer time=909.5 ms

test_clean.wav baseline repeat:
  infer time=908.9 ms

test_clean.wav approx env:
  infer time=908.8 ms

test.wav approx env:
  infer time=5322.5 ms
```

Output comparison for `test_clean.wav`:

```text
baseline vs baseline repeat:
  Vocals rel_rms=5.40395348e-08
  Instrumental rel_rms=5.4444837e-08

baseline vs approx env:
  Vocals rel_rms=0.954210278
  Instrumental rel_rms=0.732681987
```

Interpretation:

- The production experiment path builds and runs both test WAVs.
- Runtime is nearly unchanged at full CLI level for `test_clean.wav`
  (`909.5 ms -> 908.8 ms`), because this probe only changes the main-q part of
  one time-attention path, while the whole model contains many other kernels and
  audio stages.
- The output drift is very large end-to-end. Baseline repeat is essentially
  deterministic, so the drift is caused by the approximate attention path, not
  measurement noise.
- This explains an important gap between bench and production: tiny BF16-output
  drift in one isolated attention bench can amplify heavily across 12 depth
  blocks and chunked inference.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX` off by default.
- Keep the production path as a runnable risk experiment, not a default
  optimization.
- Before wider production migration, test milder approximations or apply the
  bench candidate only under a layer/call limit to locate where drift amplifies.

### Default FFN12 Residual Two-Kernel Promotion

Goal: make the current best FFN12 residual decomposition the default path for
the fixed MBR BF16 shape.

Change:

- `ffn12_fused_residual_enabled()` is now enabled by default whenever the FFN12
  CUDA Tile path is enabled.
- `ffn12_residual_two_kernel_enabled()` is now the default residual strategy.
- `CUDASEP_DISABLE_FFN12_FUSED_RESIDUAL=1` returns to the older default
  residual/RMS flow.
- `CUDASEP_DISABLE_FFN12_RESIDUAL_TWO_KERNEL=1` keeps the legacy one-kernel
  fused-residual experiment available for comparison.

Reason:

- Earlier bench/source probes showed that `AddResidual=true` changes the Tile
  compiler schedule enough to drop the dominant FFN12 kernel from `HMMA=256` to
  `HMMA=128`.
- The two-kernel path preserves the fast no-residual FFN12 body and pays a
  small BF16 residual-add kernel afterwards.
- Against the real default BF16 path, this output is effectively unchanged
  (`~5.5e-8` relative RMS) and was a small end-to-end win.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

nsys profile --trace=cuda --sample=none --cpuctxsw=none \
  --force-overwrite=true -o outputs/nsys_ffn12_residual_default_clean \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/ffn12_residual_default_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

nsys export --type sqlite --force-overwrite=true \
  --output outputs/nsys_ffn12_residual_default_clean.sqlite \
  outputs/nsys_ffn12_residual_default_clean.nsys-rep

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_residual_default_clean_plain \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_residual_default_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel<6, false, 64, false, false, 2, true, true, true, false>|residual_add_bf16_(main|tail)_kernel' 12
git diff --check -- src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu bench/README.md
```

```text
  infer time=917.6 ms

test_clean.wav, default plain CLI:
  infer time=904.5 ms

test.wav, default plain CLI:
  infer time=5276.5 ms
```

```text
  calls=96 total_ms=175.753 avg_us=1830.759
```

```text
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
```

Output difference versus the previous default output:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  Vocals:       rel_rms=5.46816697e-08 snr=145.243 dB
  Instrumental: rel_rms=5.49085513e-08 snr=145.207 dB

test.wav:
  Vocals:       rel_rms=5.48075701e-08 snr=145.223 dB
```

Audit:

```text
[audit] PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed for build-min/cudasep_infer
```

Interpretation:

- The default path now uses the CUDA Tile two-kernel residual strategy verified
  by earlier bench work.
- This is an example where "deeper fusion" was wrong for A10G because it hurt
  the generated Tensor Core schedule. The best current decomposition is one
  fast `HMMA=256` FFN kernel plus a separate BF16 residual-add Tile kernel.
- The remaining FFN12 work is not residual fusion; it is the main FFN schedule:
  reducing the `REG=255`/`STACK=8` pressure, preserving `HMMA=256`, and finding
  whether a split/TK/latency spelling can move the average `1.83 ms` launch
  closer to the A10G dense-BF16 ceiling without creating more global-memory
  traffic.

numbers for choosing the next CUDA Tile/A10G experiment.

```text
outputs/nsys_ffn12_residual_default_clean.sqlite
```

```text
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=344 FADD=202 FMUL=166 MUFU=124

  REG=163 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
  instr=992 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=1
  BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=72 IADD3=75 PRMT=2 LOP3=77 SHF=61

qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full<32,256,16,...>
  REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

linear_cutile_static_full_bf16<32,128,32,...>
  REG=243 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
  instr=1040 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=1
  BAR=26 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=72 IADD3=88 LOP3=102 SHF=82

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60<16,false>
  REG=92 STACK=0 SHARED=22528
  instr=1008 HMMA=16 LDG=9 STS=22 STG=1
  FFMA=88 FADD=32 FMUL=10 MUFU=18
  BAR=16 IMAD=180 IADD3=41 PRMT=72 LOP3=98 SHF=71

split_qkv_heads_rotary_qkv_bf16_freq60_pad64<bf16>
  REG=40 STACK=0 SHARED=0
  instr=536 HMMA=0 F2FP=15 FFMA=8 FMUL=10 MUFU=4
  BAR=3 IMAD=154 IADD3=62 PRMT=38 LOP3=14 SHF=18

rotary_time_split_contig_inplace<128,bf16>
  REG=20 STACK=0 SHARED=0
  instr=120 HMMA=0 F2FP=14 FFMA=4 FMUL=4
  BAR=6 IMAD=26 IADD3=9 PRMT=6 LOP3=5 SHF=5
```

Bottom-level triage:

- FFN12 remains the largest group and the hardest Tensor Core schedule:
  `HMMA=256` is present, but `REG=255`, `STACK=8`, and `98KB` shared put it on
  the A10G one-CTA/resource cliff. The previous simple fixes failed because
  they either kept the cliff or lost `HMMA=256`.
- Time attention is not a dense GEMM problem. It reaches a good fused-attention
  shape but mixes `HMMA=160` with online-softmax scalar work
  (`MUFU=124`, large FFMA/FADD/FMUL) and row state.
- Long BKN linear and QKV both keep `HMMA=256`, but both use `98KB` shared.
  Smaller `TN` can cross the residency threshold only by destroying per-CTA
  work density, so the next viable work is producer-consumer fusion or a true
  staging-layout change.
- Frequency attention is low-density by construction at `N=60`: only
  `HMMA=16` per CTA with substantial fixed address/control and softmax work.
  It should be optimized as tiny fixed attention, not benchmarked against dense
  GEMM expectations.
- Standalone rotary/gate/RMSNorm are now lower-order memory/elementwise
  boundaries. Their next gains likely require fusion into neighboring producers
  or consumers, not standalone tile-size sweeps.

Next priority from this refreshed default:

1. FFN12 structural schedule work if a candidate can keep `HMMA=256` and move
   either `SHARED` or `REG/STACK` off the cliff.
