2. Time attention exact-path state/live-range work; approximate softmax remains
   off by default because production drift was too large.
3. Producer-consumer fusion around QKV/rotary/attention only when it removes a
   real global-memory boundary without recreating the previous direct-QKV load
   schedule collapse.

### Current Residual-Default FFN12 Odd5 Recheck

Goal: recheck the aggressive FFN12 `odd5` GELU approximation under the current
default residual two-kernel path. The earlier `odd5` source experiment was run
before the FFN12 residual decomposition became default; this rerun verifies
whether the same speed/quality tradeoff still holds.

No source changed. The opt-in remains:

```bash
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1
```

Commands:

```bash
CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_odd5_current_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_odd5_current_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Timing:

```text
test_clean.wav current default: 904.5 ms
test_clean.wav odd5 opt-in:    886.6 ms

test.wav current default:       5276.5 ms
test.wav odd5 opt-in:           5192.9 ms
```

Nsight Systems on `test_clean.wav`:

```text
odd5 opt-in under Nsight: 901.9 ms
kernel launches: 6602

ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel<8,false,64,false,false,2,true,true,true,false>
  96 calls, 162.377 ms total, 1691.425 us avg

current default poly9 reference:
  96 calls, 175.753 ms total, 1830.759 us avg
```

SASS/resource for the odd5 FFN12 main kernel:

```text
REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
instr=2392 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
F2FP=64 FFMA=64 FADD=160 FMUL=160 MUFU=0
BAR=35 DEPBAR=30 IMAD=346 IADD3=172 PRMT=50 LOP3=186 SHF=138
```

Output difference, odd5 opt-in versus current residual-default output:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.131668031 rms=0.00782591151 rel_rms=0.0425663119 snr=27.419 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.143223479 rms=0.00821282886 rel_rms=0.0341177768 snr=29.340 dB

test.wav:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.119069606 rms=0.00464570921 rel_rms=0.0245761365 snr=32.190 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.138709854 rms=0.00512313498 rel_rms=0.0267228046 snr=31.462 dB
```

Interpretation:

- `odd5` still gives a real end-to-end speed win under the new default
  residual decomposition: about `2.0%` on `test_clean.wav` and `1.6%` on
  `test.wav`.
- Nsight confirms the win is localized to the FFN12 main kernel:
  `175.753 -> 162.377 ms` over 96 calls. The CUDA Tile lowering still keeps
  `HMMA=256`, so this is not a Tensor Core regression trade.
- The kernel remains on the same A10G resource wall (`REG=255`, `98KB`
  shared). The speedup comes from reducing scalar GELU work/static instruction
  count, not from solving occupancy or shared-memory residency.
- The output drift is unchanged in character from the earlier source opt-in:
  it is percent-level model math drift, not BF16 write-noise. This makes it a
  useful aggressive experiment switch, but not a default quality-preserving
  CUDA Tile optimization.
- This closes the simple "lower GELU polynomial degree" route for default
  FFN12. The remaining FFN12 work should keep the current poly9 fast spelling
  unless the goal is explicitly to trade model output for speed.

### Current Recheck: FFN12 W2 View Split And Manual Load Closure

Goal: recheck the existing FFN12 W2 split-span/manual-load diagnostics against
the current `bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile` binary, without repeating
simple shape sweeps. Prior W2 streaming and W2 pairwise probes proved that
shortening W2 live ranges in source can drop the generated Tensor Core body
from `HMMA=256` to `HMMA=128`. This recheck verifies two older candidate
spellings are still closed:

- `w2splitspan`: split the W2 tensor view into two half-output spans while
  keeping the original four W2 MMA pattern.
- `w2manual`: bypass `partition_view` for W2 and form manual column-major
  pointer loads for the four W2 tiles.

Official CUDA Tile doc check:

- Both variants keep legal compile-time power-of-two tile shapes:
  `TM=32`, `TK=64`, `THidden=32`, `OutHalf=128`.
- Both stay inside official CUDA Tile C++ APIs: `__tile_global__`,
  `tensor_span`/`partition_view` or direct `ct::load`, and `ct::mma`.
- This is a compiler-visible staging/data-layout spelling test, not a dense
  BF16 roofline rerun.

No source changed in this step; the variants already existed in
`bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile`. The original detailed diagnostics are
also recorded earlier under the 2026-06-04 W2 split-span/manual-load sections;
this section refreshes the evidence after later FFN12 candidate churn.

Commands:

```bash
for v in \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64 \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w2splitspan \
  fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w2manual; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 10 --iters 120
done

for r in 1 2 3; do
  for v in fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64 \
           fused_h32_poly9_split2_pairh32_tk64_w2splitspan; do
    ./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
      --variant "$v" --warmup 20 --iters 300
  done
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_split2_pairh32_w2(splitspan|manual)_bf16_kernel<32, 6, 64, 32>|ffn12_fused_split2_pairh32_bf16_kernel<32, 6, 64, 32, false, false, true, true, false, true, true, long long, false, false, false, false, 8, 8, false, 8, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false, false>' \
  60

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_candidates_cutile
```

Timing:

```text
120-iter pass:
  baseline:
    best=1.855 ms median=1.858 ms useful=44.12 TF/s roof70=63.0%
  w2splitspan:
    best=1.856 ms median=1.858 ms useful=44.10 TF/s roof70=63.0%
  w2manual:
    best=3.730 ms median=3.734 ms useful=21.94 TF/s roof70=31.3%

300-iter repeats:
  baseline best=1.856/1.856/1.855 ms, median=1.858/1.858/1.858 ms
  w2splitspan best=1.839/1.855/1.855 ms, median=1.858/1.858/1.858 ms
```

Resource and SASS:

```text
baseline:
  REG=242 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2760 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=96 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=27 DEPBAR=27 IMAD=350 IADD3=171 PRMT=115 LOP3=175 SHF=188

w2splitspan:
  REG=242 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2760 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=96 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=27 DEPBAR=27 IMAD=350 IADD3=171 PRMT=115 LOP3=175 SHF=188

w2manual:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2464 HMMA=128 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=24 LDG=220 STS=66 STG=1
  F2FP=80 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=26 DEPBAR=12 IMAD=555 IADD3=245 PRMT=175 LOP3=201 SHF=101
```

Interpretation:

- `w2splitspan` is still an equivalence diagnostic, not a win. Under the
  current candidates-only baseline it compiles to the same resource/SASS shape
  and the same median time. Against the faster source-style FFN12 instance, it
  still shows the bottom problem: `SHARED=98304B` remains the A10G one-CTA wall.
- `w2manual` is clearly wrong for CUDA Tile 13.3 codegen: manual W2 pointer
  loads destroy the Tensor Core schedule (`HMMA=256 -> 128`), increase global
  load/address work (`LDG=128 -> 220`, `IMAD=555`), and halve useful throughput.
- Bottom-level cause stays unchanged after the current recheck: splitting W2
  view metadata is optimized into the same Tile staging shape, while bypassing
  structured `partition_view` W2 loads prevents the compiler from generating
  the full staged MMA pattern.

Decision:

- Do not promote `w2splitspan` or `w2manual`.
- Future FFN12 work must either actually reduce static shared below the A10G
  residency threshold while preserving `HMMA=256`, or change a larger W2
  staging/layout strategy without manual pointer loads.

### A10G CUDA Tile Bottleneck Report Script

Goal: stop manually stitching Nsight Systems hotspot tables together with
`cuobjdump` resource/SASS summaries. The new report script uses an existing
trace plus the current binary to produce one per-kernel static bottleneck
table. It does not rerun or remeasure the dense BF16 roofline.

Change:

- Added `bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py`.
- The script joins:
  - Nsight kernel calls/total time/average time from a `.sqlite` trace.
  - `cuobjdump --dump-resource-usage` register/stack/shared/local usage.
  - `cuobjdump --dump-sass` static instruction-class counts.
- Function names are normalized across Nsight and `c++filt` output, including
  `<unnamed>` versus `(anonymous namespace)` and `(int)N`/`(bool)0` template
  spelling differences.

Command:

```bash
./bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py \
  outputs/nsys_ffn12_residual_default_clean.sqlite \
  build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --limit 18
```

Current default report highlights:

```text
ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  calls=96 total_ms=175.753 avg_us=1830.759
  REG=255 STACK=8 SMEM=98304 HMMA=256 MUFU=0 scalar=608 BAR=35 STG=8
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; high reg/stack pressure;
         large scalar side work; barrier-heavy staging;
         address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel
  calls=48 total_ms=162.317 avg_us=3381.611
  REG=237 STACK=0 SMEM=40960 HMMA=160 MUFU=124 scalar=1050 BAR=15 STG=4
  cause: transcendental/softmax MUFU; large scalar side work;
         multiple global-store/layout writes

linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel
  calls=48 total_ms=63.975 avg_us=1332.818
  REG=163 STACK=0 SMEM=98304 HMMA=256 MUFU=0 scalar=0 BAR=33 STG=8
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; barrier-heavy staging;
         multiple global-store/layout writes

qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel
  calls=48 total_ms=60.652 avg_us=1263.583
  REG=165 STACK=0 SMEM=98304 HMMA=256 MUFU=0 scalar=0 BAR=33 STG=24
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; barrier-heavy staging;
         address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes

freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel
  calls=48 total_ms=38.527 avg_us=802.645
  REG=92 STACK=0 SMEM=22528 HMMA=16 MUFU=18 scalar=166 BAR=16 STG=8
  cause: low HMMA density; large scalar side work;
         address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes
```

Interpretation:

- This turns the current "why below 70 TF/s" discussion into a repeatable
  evidence gate for every future CUDA Tile experiment.
- FFN12/long linear/QKV are still dominated by 98KB shared staging and, for
  FFN12, high register/stack pressure.
- Time attention is a mixed Tensor Core plus exact softmax kernel; the bottom
  limiter is scalar softmax/state work, not missing BF16 Tensor Core lowering.
- Frequency attention is a tiny fixed-shape attention kernel with low HMMA
  density by construction; its next experiments should reduce fixed overhead
  and memory/layout cost rather than judge it like dense GEMM.
- Elementwise/RMSNorm/STFT rows in the report are intentionally marked as
  non-HMMA paths. They should be tracked for launch/memory fusion, not for
  Tensor Core TF/s.

### Production Time Attention Approx Softmax Call-Limit Sweep

Goal: localize why the production approximate time-attention softmax experiment
creates huge end-to-end drift even though the isolated BF16 attention bench only
showed small BF16-output differences. This keeps the experiment off by default
and adds a call-limit gate so only the first N non-qrot split-contiguous main
attention launches use the approximate kernel.

Change:

- Added host-side env parsing helper in `src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu`:
  `env_nonnegative_int_or(...)`.
- Added `time_attention_approx_softmax_enabled_for_call()`.
- Existing behavior is unchanged unless both env vars are set:

```bash
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=N
```

Semantics:

- unset `CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS`: same as the previous
  all-calls approximate experiment.
- `N=0`: approximate gate is enabled but no non-qrot main calls use it.
- `N>0`: only the first N non-qrot split-contig main attention calls use the
  approximate softmax path; later calls return to exact softmax.
- Tail rows and q-rotary attention remain exact, as in the previous production
  approximate experiment.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_limit_baseline_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

for limit in 0 1 2 4 8 10 12 14 16 24 48; do
  CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
  CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=$limit \
    ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
      --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
      --input test_clean.wav \
      --output outputs/time_approx_limit_${limit}_clean \
      --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
done

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_limit_default_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
git diff --check -- src/mbr_cuda_tile.cu bench/README.md
```

End-to-end timing:

```text
test_clean baseline: 903.5 ms
MAX_CALLS=0:         902.2 ms
MAX_CALLS=1:         904.5 ms
MAX_CALLS=2:         901.5 ms
MAX_CALLS=4:         899.8 ms
MAX_CALLS=8:         900.8 ms
MAX_CALLS=10:        901.2 ms
MAX_CALLS=12:        900.0 ms
MAX_CALLS=14:        904.4 ms
MAX_CALLS=16:        903.3 ms
MAX_CALLS=24:        904.0 ms
MAX_CALLS=48:        904.7 ms

default test.wav after source change: 5292.0 ms
```

Output difference versus current baseline on `test_clean.wav`:

```text
MAX_CALLS  Vocals rel_rms       Instrumental rel_rms
0          5.39916333e-08       5.44605177e-08
1          8.27349259e-05       0.00282765051
2          0.00103579492        0.00354132640
4          0.000294167062       0.00527748598
8          0.000126778089       0.00389222353
10         0.00128261408        0.00882440489
12         0.08326002900        0.04489227610
14         0.69600364400        0.51392824400
16         0.69597641100        0.51400382700
24         0.66428271300        0.50303189100
48         0.95421026600        0.73268199100
```

Interpretation:

- `MAX_CALLS=0` is equivalent to baseline (`~5.4e-8 rel_rms`), so the new gate
  has no measurable side effect when it disables all approximate calls.
- `MAX_CALLS=48` reproduces the previous all-calls production drift
  (`~0.95` vocals, `~0.73` instrumental), confirming the new limiter is
  selecting the same production path.
- The drift jump happens between `MAX_CALLS=10` and `MAX_CALLS=14`:
  - at `10`: still `0.13%` vocals / `0.88%` instrumental.
  - at `12`: jumps to `8.3%` vocals / `4.5%` instrumental.
  - at `14`: jumps to `69.6%` vocals / `51.4%` instrumental.
- The default trace has 48 non-qrot main attention calls on `test_clean.wav`,
  matching 4 chunks times 12 depth blocks. This strongly suggests that applying
  the approximate main attention through a full first chunk/depth stack is the
  first severe amplification point.
- Runtime is noise-level at this granularity. Limiting approximate softmax does
  not provide an end-to-end speed win by itself; its value is diagnostic for
  future approximate/full-BF16 attention experiments.

Decision:

- Keep approximate softmax off by default.
- Keep the call-limit env gate as the production drift-localization tool.
- Next useful production experiment is not "all layers approximate"; it is a
  layer/call placement strategy, for example approximate only selected early
  calls below the `MAX_CALLS=10` safe-ish region, or make the approximate path
  milder before crossing a full 12-layer chunk.
- For performance work, continue treating exact time attention as the default
  optimization target; approximate softmax remains an aggressive experiment.

### Production Time Attention Approx Softmax Range-Gate Probe

Goal:

- Continue localizing the production approximate-softmax drift jump around
  non-qrot main attention calls 10 to 14.
- Distinguish cumulative drift from specific sensitive call windows by adding
  a start offset to the existing call-limit gate.
- Keep the exact time-attention path as the default production path.

Change:

- Extended `time_attention_approx_softmax_enabled_for_call()` with:

```bash
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_START_CALL=S
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=N
```

Semantics:

- Calls are 0-based non-qrot split-contiguous main attention launches.
- Default `START_CALL=0`, so previous `MAX_CALLS=N` behavior is unchanged.
- With both variables set, approximate softmax applies to calls
  `[START_CALL, START_CALL + MAX_CALLS)`.
- `MAX_CALLS=0` still means no approximate calls.
- If `MAX_CALLS` is unset and `START_CALL=S`, approximate softmax applies to
  all calls from `S` onward.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_range_baseline_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=0 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_approx_range_max0_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_START_CALL=10 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=2 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_approx_range_10_2_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_START_CALL=12 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=2 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_approx_range_12_2_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

End-to-end timing:

```text
baseline: 901.2 ms
MAX_CALLS=0: 904.6 ms
START_CALL=10 MAX_CALLS=2: 901.1 ms
START_CALL=12 MAX_CALLS=2: 902.3 ms
```

Output difference versus `time_approx_range_baseline_clean`:

```text
variant                         Vocals rel_rms       Instrumental rel_rms
time_approx_range_max0_clean     5.39130923e-08       5.47425867e-08
time_approx_range_10_2_clean     0.171273649          0.0920673731
time_approx_range_12_2_clean     0.691005715          0.511963792
```

Interpretation:

- `MAX_CALLS=0` is equivalent to baseline, confirming the new start offset has
  no side effect when no approximate calls are selected.
- Calls `[10,12)` alone already create large drift:
  `17.1%` vocals / `9.2%` instrumental.
- Calls `[12,14)` alone create the severe drift seen in the previous sweep:
  `69.1%` vocals / `51.2%` instrumental.
- The jump is therefore not only accumulated error from calls 0 to 9. Specific
  calls around 10 to 13 are individually sensitive to the approximate softmax
  polynomial, probably because this region crosses a chunk/depth placement
  where attention errors are strongly amplified by later blocks.

Decision:

- Keep approximate softmax off by default.
- Keep `START_CALL` plus `MAX_CALLS` as the next placement-localization tool.
- Next probe should isolate single calls 10, 11, 12, and 13 before designing a
  milder approximation or a selective placement policy.

### Production Time Attention Approx Softmax Single-Call Probe

Goal:

- Isolate which individual non-qrot main attention call explains the severe
  drift from the `[12,14)` range-gate window.
- Keep the source unchanged. This only uses the existing `START_CALL` and
  `MAX_CALLS` gate.

Commands:

```bash
for call in 10 11 12 13; do
  CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
  CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_START_CALL=$call \
  CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=1 \
    ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
      --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
      --input test_clean.wav \
      --output outputs/time_approx_single_${call}_clean \
      --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
done
```

End-to-end timing:

```text
START_CALL=10 MAX_CALLS=1: 903.6 ms
START_CALL=11 MAX_CALLS=1: 902.3 ms
START_CALL=12 MAX_CALLS=1: 904.4 ms
START_CALL=13 MAX_CALLS=1: 902.5 ms
```

Output difference versus `time_approx_range_baseline_clean`:

```text
variant                         Vocals rel_rms       Instrumental rel_rms
time_approx_single_10_clean      0.0130260909         0.0132983586
time_approx_single_11_clean      0.0440961975         0.0312447933
time_approx_single_12_clean      0.158593373          0.124237334
time_approx_single_13_clean      0.690974347          0.511827167
```

Interpretation:

- The severe `[12,14)` drift is almost entirely explained by call 13 alone:
  `69.10%` vocals / `51.18%` instrumental.
- Call 12 alone is already risky but much smaller:
  `15.86%` vocals / `12.42%` instrumental.
- Calls 10 and 11 are not harmless, but they are not the bottom cause of the
  catastrophic jump.
- For the current command line, `--chunk-batch-size 1` makes
  `process_chunked()` call `model.forward()` once per chunk. Inside each
  forward, `MelBandRoformer::forward()` iterates `cfg_.depth=12` blocks, and
  each block's time transformer has depth 1. Therefore call 12 is chunk 1,
  depth block 0, and call 13 is chunk 1, depth block 1 in 0-based indexing.
- The next question is now specific: why does approximate softmax in the second
  chunk's early time-depth blocks amplify much more than the same approximate
  kernel in the first chunk?

Decision:

- Do not invest in all-call approximate softmax as a speed path.
- For future BF16/full-approx attention work, add a chunk/depth placement gate
  so selective experiments do not rely on call counting.
- The bottom-cause investigation should compare exact vs approximate internal
  attention statistics for call 13: score range, max-subtracted logits,
  normalization denominator, and output delta before residual propagation.

### Production Time Attention Approx Softmax Chunk/Depth Gate

Goal:

- Replace call-order-only diagnosis with an explicit model-placement selector.
- Keep the old call window gate intact for compatibility.
- Verify that selecting `chunk 1, depth 1` is equivalent to selecting call 13
  for the current `test_clean.wav --chunk-batch-size 1` run.

Change:

- Added host-side placement context:
  `mbr_tile::set_time_attention_context_chunk(...)` and
  `mbr_tile::set_time_attention_context_depth(...)`.
- `process_chunked()` sets the chunk context before each `model.forward()`.
  With `--chunk-batch-size 1`, this is the exact chunk index. For larger
  chunk batches, it is the group start index because one forward contains
  multiple chunks.
- `MelBandRoformer::forward()` sets the time-depth context before each time
  transformer and clears it before frequency attention.
- `time_attention_approx_softmax_enabled_for_call()` now also accepts:

```bash
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CHUNK=C
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_DEPTH=D
```

Selector semantics:

- `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1` is still required.
- `START_CALL/MAX_CALLS` keep their previous behavior.
- `CHUNK` and `DEPTH` are optional extra filters. If both are set, both must
  match.
- `MAX_CALLS=0` still disables all approximate calls.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_place_baseline_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_START_CALL=13 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_approx_place_call13_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_DEPTH=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_approx_place_chunk1_depth1_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

End-to-end timing:

```text
baseline: 901.4 ms
START_CALL=13 MAX_CALLS=1: 896.8 ms
CHUNK=1 DEPTH=1: 898.8 ms
```

Output difference:

```text
variant / comparison                    Vocals rel_rms       Instrumental rel_rms
call13 vs baseline                       0.690974347          0.511827167
chunk1_depth1 vs baseline                0.690974347          0.511827167
chunk1_depth1 vs call13                  5.36796764e-08       5.42576787e-08
```

Interpretation:

- The placement gate selects the same production attention launch as the call
  gate. `chunk1_depth1` and `call13` differ only at baseline repeat noise.
- The sensitive point is now explicitly identified as time attention at
  `chunk 1, depth block 1` for the current command line.
- Future bottom-cause probes can target this exact model placement without
  brittle call counting.

Decision:

- Keep call gating for coarse sweeps.
- Use chunk/depth gating for all future call-13 bottom-cause experiments.
- Next instrumentation should compare exact and approximate internal state at
  `chunk 1, depth 1`: logits range, max value, denominator, attention output,
  and post-residual amplification.

### Production Time Attention Approx Softmax Internal Stats

Goal:

- Find the bottom cause of the call-13 drift at the now explicit placement:
  `chunk 1, depth 1`.
- Compare exact softmax, the previous `prob_poly3_noclamp` approximate path,
  and a new clamped-probability variant.
- Keep stats and clamped approximation off by default.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The stats path is implemented inside the existing CUDA Tile main attention
  kernel using `__tile_global__`, `ct::bid()`, `ct::iota`, `ct::sum`,
  `ct::reduce_max`, and `ct::store`.
- The launch remains `<<<grid, 1>>>`, matching the official Tile kernel model.
- Reference:
  https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html

Change:

- Added optional stats gate:

```bash
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS=1
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_CHUNK=C
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_DEPTH=D
```

- The stats buffer records one row per `bh/q` row in the main non-qrot
  attention kernel:
  `row_m`, `row_l`, output row L2, and output row max-abs.
- Added optional clamped-probability approximation:

```bash
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CLAMP_PROB=1
```

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_DEPTH=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_stats_exact_chunk1_depth1_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_DEPTH=1 \
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_DEPTH=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_stats_approx_chunk1_depth1_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_DEPTH=1 \
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_DEPTH=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CLAMP_PROB=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_stats_approx_clamp_chunk1_depth1_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Internal stats:

```text
exact:
  row_m_min=-1.14113 row_m_max=9.2589 row_m_mean=2.15386
  row_l_min=1.01926 row_l_max=1067.37 row_l_mean=114.249
  out_l2_max=16.2303 out_l2_mean=3.6149
  out_max_abs_max=1.48143 out_max_abs_mean=0.623957

approx prob_poly3_noclamp:
  row_m_min=-1.14113 row_m_max=9.2589 row_m_mean=2.15386
  row_l_min=-300310 row_l_max=1059.86 row_l_mean=-8477.72
  out_l2_max=1.10498e+10 out_l2_mean=25367.6
  out_max_abs_max=37123.3 out_max_abs_mean=1.12118

approx prob_poly3_clamp:
  row_m_min=-1.14113 row_m_max=9.2589 row_m_mean=2.15386
  row_l_min=1.00636 row_l_max=1067.35 row_l_mean=111.359
  out_l2_max=17.3009 out_l2_mean=3.74394
  out_max_abs_max=1.52348 out_max_abs_mean=0.632915
```

Output difference versus exact at the same placement:

```text
variant                  Vocals rel_rms       Instrumental rel_rms
prob_poly3_noclamp        0.690974347          0.511827167
prob_poly3_clamp          0.016294024          0.0134902105
```

Interpretation:

- The score max distribution is identical across exact, no-clamp, and clamped
  approximate paths. The QK score range is not the bottom cause.
- The no-clamp polynomial is the direct failure point. For `scores - max < -3`,
  `t = x / 3 + 1` becomes negative, so `t^3` creates negative probabilities.
- Those negative probabilities make the online softmax denominator invalid:
  `row_l_min=-300310`, `row_l_mean=-8477.72`.
- Once `row_l` is negative or near cancellation, output normalization explodes:
  `out_l2_max=1.10498e+10`, `out_max_abs_max=37123.3`.
- Clamping the probability polynomial before cubing fixes the denominator and
  keeps output stats close to exact. End-to-end drift at the same placement
  drops from `69.1%/51.2%` to `1.63%/1.35%`.

Decision:

- Close `prob_poly3_noclamp` as a production approximation candidate.
- Keep `CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CLAMP_PROB=1` as the next
  approximate-softmax experiment.
- The next speed/quality experiment should run a call/window sweep with
  clamped probabilities and compare SASS/resource impact against exact.

### Production Time Attention Clamped Approx Softmax Call-Limit Sweep

Goal:

- Check whether the denominator fix from `prob_poly3_clamp` remains stable when
  applied across wider production call windows.
- Compare quality drift against the previous no-clamp call-limit sweep.
- Inspect static resource/SASS impact to see whether the clamped fix preserves
  the intended approximate-softmax performance benefit.

Commands:

```bash
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_clamp_limit_baseline_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

for limit in 0 1 2 4 8 10 12 14 16 24 48; do
  CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
  CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=$limit \
  CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CLAMP_PROB=1 \
    ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
      --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
      --input test_clean.wav \
      --output outputs/time_approx_clamp_limit_${limit}_clean \
      --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, (0|8|9), (0|1)' \
  160
```

End-to-end timing:

```text
baseline: 909.1 ms
MAX_CALLS=0: 905.5 ms
MAX_CALLS=1: 910.3 ms
MAX_CALLS=2: 906.2 ms
MAX_CALLS=4: 908.2 ms
MAX_CALLS=8: 917.3 ms
MAX_CALLS=10: 906.3 ms
MAX_CALLS=12: 907.2 ms
MAX_CALLS=14: 907.2 ms
MAX_CALLS=16: 911.5 ms
MAX_CALLS=24: 908.1 ms
MAX_CALLS=48: 910.5 ms
```

Output difference versus `time_approx_clamp_limit_baseline_clean`:

```text
MAX_CALLS  Vocals rel_rms       Instrumental rel_rms
0          5.4060427542e-08     5.4256565467e-08
1          6.4280797423e-06     0.00075911772652
2          1.2848102163e-05     0.0011118114538
4          4.1039841356e-05     0.0022295834481
8          5.9916404603e-05     0.0025659245273
10         6.2477254088e-05     0.0026162404401
12         6.5565993961e-05     0.0027045677016
14         0.017306017986       0.014827995137
16         0.040613015697       0.054467659726
24         0.046332165553       0.059168477181
48         0.11790419414        0.10810693356
```

Static SASS/resource for `QRows=64, KTile=32, UseExp2=true, IncludeKeyTail=true`:

```text
exact:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=344 FADD=202 FMUL=166 MUFU=124

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  REG=232 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2608 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=434 FADD=202 FMUL=266 MUFU=34

prob_poly3_clamp:
  REG=228 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2784 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=354 FADD=202 FMUL=186 MUFU=114
```

Interpretation:

- Clamping removes the catastrophic call-13 denominator failure. At
  `MAX_CALLS=14`, drift is now `1.73%` vocals / `1.48%` instrumental instead
  of the previous no-clamp `69.6%` vocals / `51.4%` instrumental.
- The full 48-call clamped run is still not production-quality:
  `11.8%` vocals / `10.8%` instrumental. After fixing the denominator, the
  remaining issue is normal approximation error accumulating through many
  depth/chunk placements.
- Runtime is noise-level at full CLI scope. The clamped path does not show an
  end-to-end speed win in this production sweep.
- The Tensor Core body remains intact for all variants:
  `HMMA=160`, `LDSM=64`, `LDGSTS=16`, `SHARED=40960`.
- The no-clamp path's static win came from reducing MUFU
  (`124 -> 34`) and cutting instructions (`2792 -> 2608`). The clamped path
  mostly loses the MUFU advantage: `MUFU=114`, close to exact. This explains
  why the safe clamp is a correctness fix, not yet a good speed fix.

Decision:

- Keep `prob_poly3_clamp` as a safe diagnostic candidate, but do not promote it
  as a performance optimization.
- The next approximate-softmax work should search for a non-negative
  approximation that keeps MUFU low, or a placement policy that uses clamped
  approximation only where the drift stays below the early-window region.
- Exact time attention remains the default optimization target.

### Production Time Attention Negative Probe: Poly2 L4 Approx Softmax

Goal:

- Test a non-negative approximate probability without per-score clamp:
  `prob = (1 + x / 4)^2`.
- Keep alpha clamped at row granularity, where the tile is only `QRows x 1`.
- Check whether this preserves the low-MUFU property of `prob_poly3_noclamp`
  while avoiding negative denominators.

Change:

- Added `kProbPoly2NoClampL4` behind:

```bash
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_POLY2_L4=1
```

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, 10, 1' \
  120

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_DEPTH=1 \
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_DEPTH=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_POLY2_L4=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_stats_approx_poly2_l4_chunk1_depth1_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_MAX_CALLS=48 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_APPROX_SOFTMAX_POLY2_L4=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_approx_poly2_l4_limit_48_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
prob_poly2_l4:
  REG=236 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=354 FADD=202 FMUL=176 MUFU=114
```

Internal stats at `chunk 1, depth 1`:

```text
prob_poly2_l4:
  row_m_min=-1.14113 row_m_max=9.2589 row_m_mean=2.15386
  row_l_min=1.00808 row_l_max=1072.35 row_l_mean=128.927
  out_l2_max=17.3009 out_l2_mean=3.63614
  out_max_abs_max=1.52348 out_max_abs_mean=0.625074
```

Output difference:

```text
chunk 1 depth 1 vs exact:
  Vocals rel_rms=0.0108765668
  Instrumental rel_rms=0.0109531397

MAX_CALLS=48 vs baseline:
  Vocals rel_rms=0.99733867
  Instrumental rel_rms=0.752540387
```

Interpretation:

- Poly2 L4 fixes the local denominator problem: `row_l_min=1.00808`, so it
  does not have the negative-probability failure mode.
- It fails the static-performance goal. CUDA Tile lowering gives
  `MUFU=114`, close to clamped poly3 and exact, not the desired no-clamp
  `MUFU=34`.
- It also fails the full-window quality goal. Although single placement drift
  is only around `1.09%`, all 48 calls drift to `99.7%` vocals /
  `75.3%` instrumental.

Decision:

- Do not pursue `prob_poly2_l4`.
- Non-negative-by-squaring is not a useful route for this production attention
  path: it neither keeps MUFU low nor remains stable under full-depth
  accumulation.
- The next approximate candidate should be designed from SASS constraints
  first: no per-score clamp lowering to high MUFU, no far-tail growth, and
  explicit non-negativity.

### Production Time Attention Stats Instrumentation SASS Cleanup

Goal:

- Ensure the optional stats instrumentation does not pollute the default exact
  time-attention kernel.
- Preserve the stats diagnostic path behind
  `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS=1`.

Change:

- Converted the stats writeout from a runtime `stats != nullptr` branch inside
  every main attention kernel to a compile-time `WriteStats` template parameter.
- The launcher now instantiates separate stats and no-stats kernels.
- Follow-up split: no-stats and stats wrappers now have separate kernel
  signatures. The default no-stats kernel no longer has a `float* stats`
  argument.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, 0, 0, false' \
  120

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, 0, 0>\(' \
  120

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_CHUNK=1 \
CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_STATS_DEPTH=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_stats_split_signature_exact_chunk1_depth1_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_split_signature_default_sanity_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Static SASS/resource for default exact no-stats:

```text
before WriteStats cleanup:
  REG=239 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2984 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 MUFU=124

after WriteStats cleanup:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 MUFU=124

after split stats/no-stats signatures:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 MUFU=124
  signature=(q, k, v, out, scale)
```

Stats smoke test:

```text
[time-attn-stats] approx=0 clamp_prob=0 poly2_l4=0 use_exp2=1 skip_keytail=0
  rows=614400
  row_m_min=-1.14113 row_m_max=9.2589 row_m_mean=2.15386
  row_l_min=1.01926 row_l_max=1067.37 row_l_mean=114.249
  out_l2_min=0.66644 out_l2_max=16.2303 out_l2_mean=3.6149
  out_max_abs_min=0.217309 out_max_abs_max=1.48143 out_max_abs_mean=0.623957
```

Interpretation:

- The default exact no-stats kernel no longer carries the stats reductions and
  stores in its SASS. Static instruction count drops from `2984` to `2792`.
- The stats path still runs and reproduces the previous exact stats for
  `chunk 1, depth 1`.
- Splitting stats/no-stats kernel signatures does not reduce SASS further.
  This disproves the unused-parameter hypothesis: the remaining gap versus the
  older pre-stats historical record (`~2736` instructions, `REG=233`) is not
  caused by the `float* stats` argument.
- The remaining difference is likely from the shared `__tile__` body/helper
  spelling or later approximate-mode additions changing CUDA Tile lowering.

Decision:

- Keep the compile-time `WriteStats` split.
- Keep the split stats/no-stats signatures.
- If exact attention becomes the next active target, compare the shared-body
  spelling against the original inline `__tile_global__` body to recover the
  remaining `~56` instructions and `~4` registers if possible.

### Production Time Attention Negative Probe: Inline Exact Body

Goal:

- Test whether the remaining exact-attention SASS gap comes from the shared
  `__tile__` body/helper spelling.
- Add a temporary hardcoded inline exact kernel for the default production
  shape: `QRows=64`, `KTile=32`, `UseExp2=true`, `IncludeKeyTail=true`.
- Compare it against the shared-body no-stats exact kernel.

Temporary change:

- Added a source-local `time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_exact_inline_kernel`
  behind `CUDASEP_TIME_ATTENTION_INLINE_EXACT=1`.
- Removed it after measurement because it produced identical SASS.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min cudasep_infer -j8

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_exact_inline_kernel' \
  120

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, 0, 0>\(' \
  120
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
inline exact:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=344 FADD=202 FMUL=166 MUFU=124

shared-body exact:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=344 FADD=202 FMUL=166 MUFU=124
```

Interpretation:

- The shared-body spelling is not the bottom cause of the remaining exact
  SASS gap. Inline and shared-body exact kernels are identical after lowering.
- The earlier historical `instr=2736/REG=233` difference must come from another
  source change or measurement context, not the `__tile__` body extraction.

Decision:

- Do not keep the inline exact source path.
- Stop pursuing shared-body vs inline spelling for this kernel.
- Next exact-attention work should look at the actual remaining instruction
  mix: final normalization, MUFU count, row-state updates, or alternative
  exact softmax formulations.

### Production Time Attention Exact Softmax: Exp2 vs Exp

Goal:

- Check whether the default exact softmax should keep `exp2` or use `exp`.
- Use the existing production env gate:

```bash
CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_EXP2=1
```

Commands:

```bash
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, 0, 0>\(' \
  120

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/cudasep_infer \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, false, true, 0, 0>\(' \
  120

CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_EXP2=1 \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/time_exact_exp_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
exact exp2:
  REG=237 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=344 FADD=202 FMUL=166 MUFU=124

exact exp:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=49152 LOCAL=0
  instr=3016 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16 LDG=11 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=704 FADD=292 FMUL=166 MUFU=124
```

Runtime and output:

```text
CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_ATTENTION_EXP2=1:
  test_clean.wav infer time=927.2 ms

exact exp vs exact exp2 baseline:
  Vocals rel_rms=0.00349222208
  Instrumental rel_rms=0.0035054319
```

Interpretation:

- `exp` does not reduce MUFU. It keeps `MUFU=124` but increases register
  pressure, shared memory, instruction count, and FFMA count.
- End-to-end runtime is slower (`927.2 ms`) than the normal exact `exp2`
  baseline range (`~900-909 ms`).
- Output drift is small but nonzero because `exp` and `exp2` paths are not
  bit-identical under BF16/FP32 mixed accumulation.

Decision:

- Keep `exp2` as the exact time-attention default.
- Do not pursue `exp` as an A10G exact-softmax optimization.
- The next exact experiment should target final normalization or row-state
  update spelling, not replacing `exp2` with `exp`.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: QKV head-major producer layout

Goal:

- Test whether the time-QKV producer should write attention-friendly
  head-major buffers directly as `[B,H,N,D]` instead of the current
  split-contiguous `[B,N,H,D]` layout.
- Keep the source-relevant Tensor Core body fixed:
  `TM32 x TN256 x TK16`, BF16 input/output, FP32 `ct::mma` accumulators, and
  A/B `latency=2` load hints.
- Change only the output store mapping. This isolates whether producer-side
  store cost can be traded for the compact/head-major attention consumer.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The probe uses only official CUDA Tile constructs:
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::irange`,
  `ct::mma`, tile `ct::store`, and `cutile::hint`.
- Tile dimensions remain compile-time powers of two:
  `TM=32`, `TN=256`, `TK=16`, and `D=64`.
- The launch remains the required Tile-kernel form `<<<grid, 1>>>`.

Change:

- Added bench-only variant `headmajor_lat2` to
  `bench/bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant all | \
  rg -e 'GPU:|split_contig_lat2|headmajor_lat2'

for v in split_contig_lat2 headmajor_lat2; do
  ./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
    --variant "$v" --warmup 30 --iters 500
done

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_(split_contig|headmajor)_kernel<32, 256, 16, 78048, 1536, 256, 2' \
  80

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
GPU: NVIDIA A10G SM 8.6 SMs=80 shared_per_sm=102400B

split_contig_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=165
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

headmajor_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9 attr_regs=170
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  best=1.270 ms median=1.281 ms
  useful=48.34 TF/s roof70=69.1% checksum=92.0000

headmajor_lat2:
  best=5.950 ms median=5.995 ms
  useful=10.32 TF/s roof70=14.7% checksum=92.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  REG=165 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 IADD3=82 PRMT=1 LOP3=79 SHF=63

headmajor_lat2:
  REG=170 STACK=0 SHARED=98304
  instr=1728 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=192
  F2FP=32 BAR=17 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=75 IADD3=92 PRMT=129 LOP3=148 SHF=69
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- Head-major producer output keeps Tensor Core lowering: `HMMA=256`, `LDSM=96`,
  and `LDGSTS=80` are unchanged. The failure is not a Tensor Core fallback.
- It does not reduce the A10G resource wall: shared memory remains `98304B`, so
  occupancy stays at one resident CTA per SM. Registers increase slightly
  (`165 -> 170`).
- The head-major store mapping destroys the epilogue. Static stores explode
  from `STG=3` to `STG=192`, and permutation/logical work also rises sharply
  (`PRMT 1 -> 129`, `LOP3 79 -> 148`).
- This matches the layout tradeoff: writing `[B,H,N,D]` from a `32x256` output
  tile scatters the four-head columns across distant head-major regions, while
  the current `[B,N,H,D]` split-contiguous store is compiler-friendly.
- The attention consumer does not offer enough upside to pay this cost. The
  compact `[BH,N,D]` attention bench and the split-contiguous consumer are
  already essentially equal (`~3.40 ms` each on this A10G).

Decision:

- Do not change the time-QKV producer to emit `[B,H,N,D]` head-major buffers.
- Keep the source default on split-contiguous `[B,N,H,D]` Q/K/V stores.
- Close this producer-consumer layout direction unless a future design changes
  the producer tile shape itself. A useful QKV/attention fusion must preserve
  coalesced split-contiguous stores or remove the global boundary without
  turning the producer epilogue into scattered head-major stores.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 TN64x4 Output Ownership

Goal:

- Finish checking the more aggressive existing FFN12 output-ownership split
  after the `tn64_y2` result showed no shared-memory improvement.
- Test whether four `64`-wide output slices can reduce the live output
  accumulator/staging footprint enough to move the fused FFN12 kernel below the
  A10G one-CTA shared-memory wall.
- Keep this bench-only. Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround --warmup 20 --iters 300

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|tn64x4)' 120

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=254
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
  local=0B
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.7909 ms median=1.7978 ms
  useful=45.70 TF/s roof70=65.3% checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround:
  best=3.7111 ms median=3.7153 ms
  useful=22.05 TF/s roof70=31.5% checksum=4080.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
source-like fused_pairh32_lat2 W1/W2 latency=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

tn64x4:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=1800 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=100 STS=58 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=26 DEPBAR=20 IMAD=281 IADD3=135 PRMT=76 LOP3=146 SHF=116
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- `tn64x4` does not move the real A10G limiter. Static shared memory stays at
  `98304B`, so occupancy remains one resident CTA per SM.
- The four-slice output ownership also breaks the main CUDA Tile Tensor Core
  schedule: `HMMA=256 -> 128`, `LDSM=128 -> 64`, and `LDGSTS=80 -> 40`.
- The apparent reductions in total instructions and scalar GELU work are not a
  win because the useful MMA body is halved and address/control work rises
  (`IMAD=194 -> 281`, `PRMT=16 -> 76`).
- This closes the simple output-width split family for FFN12. Splitting output
  ownership inside the same source-style fused kernel does not reduce shared
  staging below the residency threshold and can easily make CUDA Tile generate a
  weaker MMA pipeline.

Decision:

- Do not promote `fused_pairh32_tn64x4_lat2_outnoround`.
- Do not continue with same-CTA `TN64`/`TN64x4`/half-output style splits unless
  a new spelling can first prove a real shared-memory drop below `51200B` or
  preserve `HMMA=256` while reducing the `REG=255` cliff.
- The next FFN12 structural work should target a different phase boundary or
  W2/output dataflow, not narrower output ownership with duplicated FFN1 work.

### 2026-06-05 - Negative Probe: FFN12 Persistent A-Tile Reuse

Goal:

- Test whether the fused FFN12 kernel is paying a meaningful cost by reloading
  the same input activation `A[32x64]` tiles for every hidden-pair loop.
- Keep the source-relevant `TM32/TK64/THidden32`, pair-H32, BF16 input/output,
  FP32 `ct::mma` accumulators, and W1/W2 latency hints.
- Move only the four `A` K-tiles outside the hidden-pair loop so they stay live
  across all hidden pairs. This is a CUDA Tile dataflow probe for A10G, not a
  production source change.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The probe uses the documented CUDA Tile programming model:
  `#include <cuda_tile.h>`, `__tile_global__`, `cuda::tiles`,
  `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::irange`, `ct::mma`, and launch
  with `<<<grid, 1>>>`.
- Tile shapes remain compile-time fixed: `TM=32`, `TK=64`, `THidden=32`, and
  the four `A` tiles cover the fixed `K=256` input.
- Reference:
  https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html

Change:

- Added bench-only variant:
  `fused_pairh32_a_persist_lat2_outnoround`
  in `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_a_persist_lat2_outnoround

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_a_persist_lat2_outnoround --warmup 20 --iters 300

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|a_persist)' 140

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
fused_pairh32_a_persist_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
  local=0B
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.7862 ms median=1.7942 ms
  useful=45.82 TF/s roof70=65.5% checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_a_persist_lat2_outnoround:
  best=2.8966 ms median=2.8989 ms
  useful=28.25 TF/s roof70=40.4% checksum=4080.0000
```

Static SASS/resource:

```text
source-like fused_pairh32_lat2 W1/W2 latency=2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

a_persist:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=1744 HMMA=128 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=32 LDG=92 STS=66 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80
  BAR=18 DEPBAR=12 IMAD=264 IADD3=121 PRMT=8 LOP3=141 SHF=129
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- Persisting the four `A` tiles reduces static global-load count
  (`LDG=128 -> 92`), so the compiler can see the intended A reuse.
- That saving is not useful. The live-range extension makes CUDA Tile lower the
  kernel to a weaker Tensor Core/staging schedule:
  `HMMA=256 -> 128`, `LDSM=128 -> 48`, `LDGSTS=80 -> 32`.
- The real A10G limiter is unchanged: shared memory stays at `98304B`,
  registers remain at `255`, and occupancy remains one resident CTA per SM.
- Runtime falls from `1.786 ms` to `2.897 ms`, so repeated A loads are not the
  bottom cause of the FFN12 gap. Preserving A tiles across the hidden loop costs
  more in live state and scheduling than it saves in memory traffic.

Decision:

- Do not pursue persistent A-tile reuse for FFN12.
- Future FFN12 variants must reject any spelling that lowers the main Tensor
  Core body from `HMMA=256`, even if it improves `LDG`, stack, or local memory.
- The next FFN12 experiment should target a phase boundary that changes shared
  staging itself, not longer-lived input or W2 tile temporaries inside the same
  CTA.

### 2026-06-06 - Bottleneck Report Template-Prefix Match Fix

Goal:

- Keep the A10G CUDA Tile bottleneck report usable as the default evidence gate
  for future experiments.
- The report must explain current hotspot causes from Nsight timing plus current
  SASS/resource data, not silently drop a hotspot when template spellings drift.

Problem:

- `bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite` records the production exact
  time-attention kernel as:

```text
time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64,32,true,true>
```

- The current binary contains the same source path with later experimental
  probability/alpha template parameters:

```text
time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64,32,true,true,0,0>
```

- Because the report only performed exact-name lookup, the second largest
  default hotspot was printed as `no SASS match`.

Change:

- Updated `bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py` to match resource/SASS
  entries by:
  - exact normalized name first;
  - then namespace-suffix-compatible template base name;
  - then template-argument prefix;
  - if multiple current instantiations match, prefer the one whose extra
    template arguments are all `0` or `false`.
- This keeps the default exact production path mapped to the current exact SASS
  while avoiding accidental selection of approximate-softmax instantiations.
- No inference source or CUDA Tile kernel body changed.

Commands:

```bash
python3 -m py_compile bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py

./bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py \
  bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite \
  build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --limit 5
```

Current top-5 report after the fix:

```text
ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  calls=96 total_ms=177.050 avg_us=1844.274
  REG=255 STACK=8 SMEM=98304 HMMA=256 MUFU=0 scalar=608 BAR=35 STG=8
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; high reg/stack pressure;
         large scalar side work; barrier-heavy staging;
         address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel
  calls=48 total_ms=163.238 avg_us=3400.800
  REG=237 STACK=0 SMEM=40960 HMMA=160 MUFU=124 scalar=1050 BAR=15 STG=4
  cause: transcendental/softmax MUFU; large scalar side work;
         multiple global-store/layout writes

linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<32,256,16,...>
  calls=48 total_ms=64.519 avg_us=1344.143
  REG=163 STACK=0 SMEM=98304 HMMA=256

qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<32,256,16,...>
  calls=48 total_ms=61.046 avg_us=1271.784
  REG=165 STACK=0 SMEM=98304 HMMA=256

linear_cutile_static_full_bf16_kernel<32,128,32,...>
  calls=96 total_ms=50.839 avg_us=529.578
  REG=243 STACK=0 SMEM=98304 HMMA=256
```

Interpretation:

- The report now correctly proves that current time attention is not missing
  Tensor Core lowering: it keeps `HMMA=160`.
- Its gap is still from exact online-softmax scalar work
  (`MUFU=124`, `scalar=1050`) plus global store/layout writes.
- FFN12, long linear, and QKV remain the `98KB` shared-staging family. Their
  next candidates must change shared residency or generated staging while
  preserving `HMMA=256`.
- This run reuses the existing Nsight trace and current binary SASS. It does not
  rerun or remeasure the dense BF16 roofline.

Decision:

- Keep `bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py` as the first triage command
  before choosing future CUDA Tile experiments.
- For source traces created before a template-axis cleanup, require this
  prefix/default-argument matching so old traces remain comparable against the
  current binary.

### 2026-06-06 - Bottleneck Report Useful TF/s Columns

Goal:

- Make the default A10G CUDA Tile hotspot report answer both parts of the
  project question:
  - how much of the accepted 70 TF/s dense BF16 reference each recognizable
    compute kernel reaches;
  - why it is below that level, using SASS/resource evidence.
- Do not rerun the dense roofline.

Change:

- Added fixed-shape useful-FLOP estimates to
  `bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py` for source-relevant kernels whose
  math shape is reliably recoverable from the kernel name or fixed MBR shape:
  - FFN12 fused pair-H32 main kernel;
  - time-attention main1280 and tail kernels;
  - static full linear / BKN linear / QKV BKN kernels;
  - padded-M static linear kernels;
  - masked-MN static linear kernels.
- Added `TF/s` and `roof%` columns to the report.
- Non-HMMA or ambiguous-shape kernels still print `-` for these columns instead
  of forcing an invalid dense-GEMM interpretation.

Command:

```bash
python3 -m py_compile bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py

./bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py \
  bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite \
  build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --limit 18
```

Current report highlights:

```text
rank calls  total_ms   avg_us    TF/s  roof%  REG STACK    SMEM  HMMA  MUFU scalar  BAR STG  kernel
   1    96   177.050 1844.274    44.4   63.4  255     8   98304   256     0    608   35   8  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
     cause: 98KB shared residency wall; high reg/stack pressure;
            large scalar side work; barrier-heavy staging;
            address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes

   2    48   163.238 3400.800    60.2   86.0  237     0   40960   160   124   1050  15   4  time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel
     cause: transcendental/softmax MUFU; large scalar side work;
            multiple global-store/layout writes

   3    48    64.519 1344.143    45.7   65.2  163     0   98304   256     0      0  33   8  linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel
     cause: 98KB shared residency wall; barrier-heavy staging;
            multiple global-store/layout writes

   4    48    61.046 1271.784    48.3   68.9  165     0   98304   256     0      0  33  24  qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel
     cause: 98KB shared residency wall; barrier-heavy staging;
            address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes

   5    96    50.839  529.578    38.6   55.2  243     0   98304   256     0      0  26   4  linear_cutile_static_full_bf16_kernel
     cause: 98KB shared residency wall; high reg/stack pressure;
            multiple global-store/layout writes
```

Interpretation:

- This now separates the two questions cleanly:
  - time attention reaches a high Tensor Core-equivalent math rate
    (`60.2 TF/s`, `86.0%`) but is still dominated by exact online-softmax scalar
    work (`MUFU=124`, `scalar=1050`);
  - FFN12, BKN linear, and QKV sit lower (`44-48 TF/s`) because they preserve
    `HMMA=256` only at the `98304B` shared-memory residency wall, with FFN12
    also hitting `REG=255` and stack;
  - tiny/masked or non-HMMA paths should be optimized as launch/memory/layout
    problems, not judged primarily by dense TF/s.
- This report is now the default triage gate before selecting the next A10G
  CUDA Tile experiment.

Decision:

- Future kernel candidates should report the same columns:
  `TF/s`, `roof%`, `REG`, `STACK`, `SMEM`, `HMMA`, `MUFU`, scalar instruction
  count, and cause classification.
- For the immediate next optimization, the report points back to FFN12 or the
  98KB BKN/QKV family, not to remeasuring the dense BF16 ceiling.

### 2026-06-06 - Negative Probe: FFN12 Serial Output Halves

Goal:

- Test whether the two live `32x128` output accumulators in the current FFN12
  pair-H32 kernel are the main cause of the `98304B` shared-memory residency
  wall and `REG=255` cliff.
- Keep this benchmark-only. Source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The probe stays inside the documented CUDA Tile model: `__tile_global__`,
  `cuda::tiles`, fixed compile-time `ct::tile` shapes, `ct::partition_view`,
  `ct::mma`, and `<<<grid, 1>>>` launch.
- Reference:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`.

Change:

- Added `fused_pairh32_serial_out_halves_lat2_outnoround` to
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.
- The variant computes output half 0 in one full hidden-pair pass, stores it,
  then recomputes the hidden path and computes output half 1.
- This intentionally reduces simultaneous output accumulator live state, while
  accepting duplicated FFN1/GELU work as the diagnostic cost.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_lat2

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_serial_out_halves_lat2_outnoround

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_serial_out_halves_lat2_outnoround \
  --warmup 20 --iters 300

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|serial_out_halves)' 140

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=255
  static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
  local=8B

fused_pairh32_serial_out_halves_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5 attr_regs=190
  static_shared=16608B shared_limit=6 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=5
  local=0B
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.7727 ms median=1.7951 ms
  useful=46.17 TF/s roof70=66.0% checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_serial_out_halves_lat2_outnoround:
  best=4.2476 ms median=4.2867 ms
  useful=19.27 TF/s roof70=27.5% checksum=4080.0000
```

SASS/resource:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160 BAR=35 DEPBAR=29
  IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

fused_pairh32_serial_out_halves:
  REG=190 STACK=0 SHARED=16608 LOCAL=0
  instr=2792 HMMA=192 LDSM=112 LDGSTS=64 LDG=124 STS=44 STG=1
  FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160 BAR=98 DEPBAR=0
  IMAD=173 IADD3=127 PRMT=24 LOP3=131 SHF=143
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- This is a useful negative result. It proves the current simultaneous output
  accumulator live set is tightly connected to the `98KB` shared-memory wall:
  serializing output halves drops shared memory from `98304B` to `16608B`,
  registers from `255` to `190`, removes stack, and allows five CTAs/SM.
- It is not a performance candidate. Runtime collapses from `1.77 ms` to
  `4.25 ms` because the hidden path is recomputed and, more importantly, the
  CUDA Tile lowering no longer preserves the baseline Tensor Core/staging
  schedule: `HMMA=256 -> 192` and `BAR=35 -> 98`.
- Bottom-level cause: simply shortening output-accumulator lifetime is not
  enough. The source spelling also has to preserve the pair-H32 `HMMA=256`
  schedule and avoid adding a barrier-heavy duplicated phase.

Decision:

- Do not promote `fused_pairh32_serial_out_halves_lat2_outnoround`.
- The next FFN12 direction should keep the evidence from this probe:
  output accumulator state is a real residency cause, but the fix cannot be
  naive serial recomputation. A useful candidate needs a phase boundary or
  accumulator decomposition that lowers shared/register pressure while keeping
  `HMMA=256` and avoiding the `BAR=98` schedule.

### 2026-06-06 - Bottleneck Report A10G Shared-Residency Column

Goal:

- Make the A10G residency threshold visible in the default CUDA Tile hotspot
  report instead of only describing it in prose.
- Do not rerun the dense BF16 roofline. Continue using the accepted 70 TF/s
  dense reference and the existing Nsight trace.

Change:

- Added an `smCTA` column to `bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py`.
- `smCTA` estimates the number of CTAs per SM allowed by static shared memory
  on the current A10G budget (`102400B` per SM), capped at the architectural
  max-blocks-per-SM value of `16`.
- This makes the real shape threshold explicit:
  - `98304B -> smCTA=1`;
  - `60416B -> smCTA=1`;
  - `40960B -> smCTA=2`;
  - `32768B -> smCTA=3`;
  - small shared-memory kernels are capped at `16`.

Commands:

```bash
python3 -m py_compile bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py

./bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py \
  bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite \
  build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --limit 18
```

Current top rows:

```text
rank calls  total_ms   avg_us    TF/s  roof%  REG STACK    SMEM smCTA  HMMA  MUFU scalar  BAR STG  kernel
   1    96   177.050 1844.274    44.4   63.4  255     8   98304    1   256     0    608   35   8  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel<6,false,64,false,false,2,true,true,true,false>
   2    48   163.238 3400.800    60.2   86.0  237     0   40960    2   160   124   1050   15   4  time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64,32,true,true>
   3    48    64.519 1344.143    45.7   65.2  163     0   98304    1   256     0      0   33   8  linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<32,256,16,78048,1536,256,false,false,0,false,2>
   4    48    61.046 1271.784    48.3   68.9  165     0   98304    1   256     0      0   33  24  qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<32,256,16,78048,1536,256,2>
   5    96    50.839  529.578    38.6   55.2  243     0   98304    1   256     0      0   26   4  linear_cutile_static_full_bf16_kernel<32,128,32,78048,256,512,false,false,0,false,true>
   6   480    42.021   87.543    31.2   44.5  116     0   32768    3    32     0     16    6  18  linear_cutile_static_padded_m_bf16_kernel<32,64,64,1312,1301,1024,1024,true,false,0,false,true>
   7    48    38.840  809.164    11.9   16.9   92     0   22528    4    16    18    166   16   8  freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false>
```

Interpretation:

- For FFN12, BKN linear, QKV, and the large static full linear, the problem is
  now visible as a hard A10G residency threshold, not just a large byte count:
  `98304B` leaves only `4096B` headroom under `102400B`, so these kernels are
  one-CTA-per-SM shapes.
- Future candidates that only reduce shared memory to `60-80KB` should be
  treated skeptically: they may still report `smCTA=1` and fail to change the
  residency regime.
- To materially change the A10G occupancy side of these Tensor Core kernels,
  a candidate must either get below roughly `51200B` static shared for
  `smCTA>=2`, or prove by timing/SASS that a one-CTA schedule is faster despite
  staying at `smCTA=1`.

Decision:

- Keep `smCTA` in the default triage report.
- Future FFN12/QKV/BKN experiments must report whether their shared-memory
  change crosses the `smCTA` threshold, not only whether `SMEM` decreased.

### 2026-06-06 - Bottleneck Report Freq Attention Useful TF/s

Goal:

- Stop leaving the source-relevant frequency attention hotspot without a
  `TF/s`/`roof%` value in the default A10G CUDA Tile report.
- Use logical model work, not padded implementation work, so padding waste and
  softmax/control overhead remain visible.

Change:

- Added fixed-shape useful-FLOP estimation for
  `freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel`.
- The formula is logical QK plus AV work:
  `4 * BH * N * N * D`, with `BH=10408`, `N=60`, and `D=64`.
- The implementation pads N to 64 internally, but the report intentionally
  counts only logical `N=60` work.

Commands:

```bash
python3 -m py_compile bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py

./bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py \
  bench/nsys_current_default_bf16_clean.sqlite \
  build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --limit 8
```

Relevant row:

```text
rank calls  total_ms   avg_us    TF/s  roof%  REG STACK    SMEM smCTA  HMMA  MUFU scalar  BAR STG  kernel
   7    48    38.840  809.164    11.9   16.9   92     0   22528    4    16    18    166   16   8  freq_attention60_cutile_padded_out60_kernel<16,false>
     cause: low HMMA density; large scalar side work; address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes
```

Interpretation:

- Frequency attention is not in the FFN12/QKV `98KB` residency family:
  `SMEM=22528` gives `smCTA=4`.
- Its low roof percentage comes from tiny logical attention shape
  (`N=60`, padded to `64`), low HMMA density (`HMMA=16`), exact softmax/MUFU,
  scalar control, and layout/global-store overhead.
- Therefore the next frequency-attention work should not chase shared-memory
  residency. It should either reduce launch/layout overhead around the small
  attention path or test a more aggressive fused/probability approximation path
  if quality risk is acceptable.

Decision:

- Keep this frequency-attention FLOP estimate in the default report.
- Continue prioritizing FFN12/BKN/QKV for Tensor Core residency work, while
  treating frequency attention as a small-N attention/control-fusion problem.

### 2026-06-06 - Current Recheck: Time Attention No-Exp Upper Candidate

Goal:

- Recheck the current-tree standalone time-attention upper candidate after the
  shared-residency report update.
- Do not change source inference. This is a CUDA Tile bench-only measurement.
- The question is whether removing most softmax MUFU can push the source-like
  time-attention kernel closer to the accepted 70 TF/s A10G dense BF16 ceiling
  while preserving the `HMMA=160` Tensor Core schedule.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The measured kernels are `__tile_global__` CUDA Tile kernels launched as
  `<<<grid, 1>>>`.
- The hot loop uses `ct::partition_view`, `ct::mma`, tile reductions, and
  masked stores. This matches the CUDA Tile programming model used elsewhere in
  the project.
- Reference:
  https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, (false|true), false, false, false, (0|8), false, (0|1)>' \
  180

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
```

Runtime resource:

```text
exact split-contig:
  attr_regs=233 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  attr_regs=230 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp:
  attr_regs=230 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2
```

Timing:

```text
exact split-contig:
  best=3.388 ms median=3.392 ms
  real_math=60.40 TF/s roof70=86.3% checksum=0.242188

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  best=3.340 ms median=3.373 ms
  real_math=61.27 TF/s roof70=87.5%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp:
  best=3.344 ms median=3.377 ms
  real_math=61.19 TF/s roof70=87.4%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317
```

SASS/resource:

```text
exact split-contig:
  REG=233 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2736 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  REG=234 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2584 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2600 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  FFMA=430 FADD=202 FMUL=270 MUFU=34

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp_final_rcp:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=40960 LOCAL=0
  instr=2184 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  FFMA=280 FADD=202 FMUL=302 MUFU=4
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- The exact source-like bench remains a strong CUDA Tile Tensor Core kernel:
  `60.40 TF/s`, about `86.3%` of the accepted 70 TF/s dense BF16 reference.
- The fastest no-exp bench candidate preserves the Tensor Core/staging shape:
  `HMMA=160`, `LDSM=64`, `LDGSTS=16`, and `SMEM=40960`.
- Its speedup is explained by SASS/resource changes, not by more Tensor Core
  work: `MUFU=124 -> 34`, `REG=233 -> 230`, and
  `2736 -> 2600` static instructions.
- The final reciprocal variant proves that final normalization accounts for
  most remaining MUFU (`34 -> 4`), but it increases `FMUL` enough that runtime
  is slightly worse than direct final divide on this A10G run.
- This is a bench upper candidate, not a production promotion candidate. Earlier
  production stats showed the no-clamp probability form can create negative
  denominators at sensitive placements. The safe clamped production form fixes
  the denominator but mostly loses the MUFU advantage.

Decision:

- Keep exact time attention as the production default.
- Keep `prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp` as the current CUDA Tile
  standalone performance upper candidate for the time-attention kernel.
- Future time-attention work should search for a non-negative approximation
  that preserves the low-MUFU/no-clamp SASS shape, or improve exact softmax
  row-state/final-normalization spelling without changing the `HMMA=160`
  schedule.

### 2026-06-06 - Negative Probe: Time Attention Poly3 Output Clamp

Goal:

- Test whether changing the clamp placement can preserve the low-MUFU no-exp
  SASS shape while avoiding negative probabilities.
- The previous production stats showed that no-clamp cubic probability can
  create negative denominators at sensitive placements. Mathematically,
  `max(t^3, 0)` is equivalent to `max(t, 0)^3` for
  `t = 1 + (score - max) / 3`, but CUDA Tile lowering may differ.
- Keep this benchmark-only. Source inference is unchanged.

Change:

- Added `kProbPoly3OutputClamp` to `bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu`.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_outclamp_alpha_outclamp`.
- The probability path is:

```text
t = 1 + (score - max) / 3
```
