```text
p = t * t * t
prob = max(p, 0)
```

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_outclamp_alpha_outclamp

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_outclamp_alpha_outclamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (0|8|9), false, (0|1)>' \
  180

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu
```

Runtime resource:

```text
prob_poly3_outclamp_alpha_outclamp:
  attr_regs=238 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2
```

Timing:

```text
prob_poly3_outclamp_alpha_outclamp:
  best=3.478 ms median=3.512 ms
  real_math=58.84 TF/s roof70=84.1%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317
```

SASS/resource:

```text
exact split-contig:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160
  instr=2736 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124 IMAD=58 LOP3=134

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  REG=230 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160
  instr=2600 FFMA=430 FADD=202 FMUL=270 MUFU=34 IMAD=23 LOP3=142

prob_poly3_outclamp_alpha_outclamp:
  REG=238 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160
  instr=2856 FFMA=430 FADD=202 FMUL=350 MUFU=34 IMAD=69 LOP3=152
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- The candidate keeps the Tensor Core body (`HMMA=160`) and preserves the
  low-MUFU property (`MUFU=34`), so changing clamp placement does not force the
  exact softmax special-function path back into SASS.
- However the output-clamp spelling is much worse for A10G scheduling:
  `REG=238`, `instr=2856`, `FMUL=350`, `IMAD=69`, and `LOP3=152`.
- Runtime drops below even the exact baseline:
  `58.84 TF/s` versus exact `60.40 TF/s` and no-exp upper candidate
  `61.27 TF/s`.
- Bench output drift is unchanged relative to the no-exp candidate, but the
  performance cost makes this spelling a dead end for the current CUDA Tile
  compiler.

Decision:

- Do not promote `kProbPoly3OutputClamp`.
- Close "move cubic clamp from input `t` to output `p`" as a time-attention
  performance direction.
- The next approximate-softmax candidate must avoid both negative
  probabilities and the extra FMUL/control footprint seen in this output-clamp
  lowering.

### 2026-06-06 - QKV BKN M64 Residency Shape Probe

Goal:

- Revisit the QKV BKN `98KB` shared-memory family using the new `smCTA`
  threshold lens.
- Test whether a taller `M64` tile can offset the CTA-count cost of smaller
  `N` tiles, while preserving the source-relevant BF16 Tensor Core schedule.
- Keep this benchmark-only. Source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The probe uses the existing CUDA Tile BKN split-contiguous kernel body:
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::irange`,
  `ct::mma`, and Tile `store`.
- The launch remains `<<<grid, 1>>>`.

Change:

- Added `kFullM64 = 78016` for a 64-row-aligned main region.
- Added generic bench helpers for `TM/TN/TK/MShape` split-contiguous QKV BKN
  shapes.
- Added focused variants:
  - `split_contig_m64_tn64_lat2`
  - `split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2`
- These variants intentionally cover only `M=78016`. The current source path
  covers `M=78048`, so source promotion would require a separate 32-row tail.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant split_contig_lat2
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant split_contig_tn64_lat2
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant split_contig_m64_tn64_lat2
./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --describe --variant split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --variant split_contig_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --variant split_contig_tn64_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --variant split_contig_m64_tn64_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  --variant split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2 --warmup 30 --iters 500

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile \
  'qkv_bkn_split_contig_kernel<(32, 256, 16, 78048|64, 64, 16, 78016|64, 128, 16, 78016)' \
  140

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
git diff --check -- bench/bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu
```

Runtime resource:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) attr_regs=165 static_shared=98304B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

split_contig_tn64_lat2:
  grid=(2439,24,1) attr_regs=90 static_shared=53248B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

split_contig_m64_tn64_lat2:
  grid=(1219,24,1) attr_regs=98 static_shared=73728B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2:
  grid=(1219,12,1) attr_regs=163 static_shared=100352B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 M=78048
  best=1.264 ms median=1.280 ms
  48.55 TF/s roof=69.4%

split_contig_tn64_lat2:
  tile=32x64x16 M=78048
  best=2.071 ms median=2.083 ms
  29.64 TF/s roof=42.3%

split_contig_m64_tn64_lat2:
  tile=64x64x16 M=78016
  best=1.831 ms median=1.833 ms
  33.51 TF/s roof=47.9%

split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2:
  tile=64x128x16 M=78016
  best=1.248 ms median=1.260 ms
  49.18 TF/s roof=70.3%
```

SASS/resource:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  REG=165 SHARED=98304
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80
  BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 PRMT=1 LOP3=79

split_contig_m64_tn64_lat2:
  REG=98 SHARED=73728
  instr=880 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=32
  BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=50 PRMT=32 LOP3=102

split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2:
  REG=163 SHARED=100352
  instr=1056 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48
  BAR=29 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=57 PRMT=64 LOP3=72
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- `M64/TN64` does not solve the problem. It lowers registers and shared memory
  versus the baseline, but still does not cross the `smCTA>=2` threshold and
  halves per-CTA Tensor Core work (`HMMA=256 -> 128`). Runtime collapses to
  `33.51 TF/s`.
- `M64/TN128` is the useful signal. It still does not solve residency
  (`100352B`, one CTA/SM), but it preserves `HMMA=256` and improves codegen:
  `instr=1144 -> 1056`, `LDGSTS=80 -> 48`, `IMAD=143 -> 57`, and
  `BAR=33 -> 29`.
- The cost is `PRMT=1 -> 64` and a slightly larger shared allocation
  (`98304B -> 100352B`). Despite that, the standalone main region improves from
  `48.55 TF/s` to `49.18 TF/s`.
- This is not directly source-ready because it covers `M=78016`; the source
  QKV BKN default path currently uses `M=78048`. A source experiment needs an
  opt-in `M64/TN128` main kernel plus a separate tail path. For the current
  fixed shape, that source tail is 44 rows.

Decision:

- Close `M64/TN64` as a negative shape.
- Keep `M64/TN128` as a positive bench signal and candidate for source opt-in
  evaluation.
- Next QKV source experiment should test whether `M64/TN128` main +
  tail can beat the current `M32/TN256` source path end to end.
  For the current fixed shape, `M64/TN128` covers `78016` rows, so the
  source tail is 44 rows instead of the default 12-row tail.

## Time QKV BKN M64/TN128 source opt-in

Source opt-in:

```bash
CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_BKN_M64_TN128=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/qkv_m64_tn128_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Implementation:

- `qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel` now routes Q/K/V output by
  `kComponentTiles = (N / 3) / TN` instead of the old `TN=256`-only
  hardcoded component span.
- The default source path still launches `M32/TN256/TK16` over `78048` rows.
- The opt-in path launches `M64/TN128/TK16` over `78016` rows, then uses the
  existing scalar tail kernel for the final 44 rows.

Standalone QKV BKN bench, current rebuild:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6) M=78048
  best=1.275 ms median=1.285 ms
  48.16 TF/s roof=68.8%

split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2:
  tile=64x128x16 grid=(1219,12) M=78016
  best=1.257 ms median=1.268 ms
  48.81 TF/s roof=69.7%
```

Source SASS/resource:

```text
M32/TN256/TK16:
  REG=165 SHARED=98304
  instr=1144 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80
  BAR=33 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=143 PRMT=1 LOP3=79

M64/TN128/TK16:
  REG=163 SHARED=100352
  instr=1056 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48
  BAR=29 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=57 PRMT=64 LOP3=72
```

CLI timing:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  default:          900.6 ms
  M64/TN128 opt-in: 908.9 ms

test.wav:
  default:          5292.9 ms
  M64/TN128 opt-in: 5301.2 ms
```

Output difference versus the current default:

```text
test_clean.wav:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.00987094641 rms=0.000723393831 rel_rms=0.00393464805 snr=48.102 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.0094947964  rms=0.000874440642 rel_rms=0.0036326059  snr=48.796 dB

test.wav:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.0150471628 rms=0.000539953283 rel_rms=0.00285639178 snr=50.884 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.0138685405 rms=0.000703524123 rel_rms=0.00366965496 snr=48.707 dB
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Decision:

- Superseded by the masked Tile tail experiment below.
- The scalar-tail source integration should not be promoted. The main kernel
  is better in isolation (`48.16 -> 48.81 TF/s`) and SASS is cleaner, but the
  scalar-tail integration increases the scalar tail from 12 rows to 44 rows
  and does not improve end-to-end CLI timing.
- The next source-side QKV experiment should remove or reduce the enlarged
  tail penalty, for example by adding a dedicated 44-row/64-row tail tile path
  or by changing the phase boundary so the full source shape can keep the
  cleaner `M64/TN128` schedule without moving more rows into the scalar tail.

## Time QKV BKN M64/TN128 masked Tile tail promotion

Change:

- Added `qkv_bkn_split_contig_masked_tail_kernel<64,128,16,...>`.
- The masked tail covers rows `78016..78059` with CUDA Tile MMA instead of
  the scalar per-element tail loop.
- `M64/TN128 + masked Tile tail` is now the BF16 default time-QKV BKN path.
- `CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_QKV_BKN_M64_TN128=1` restores the previous
  `M32/TN256` source path.

Tail SASS/resource:

```text
qkv_bkn_split_contig_masked_tail_kernel<64,128,16,78060,1536,256,78016,2>:
  REG=164 SHARED=100352
  instr=1024 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48
  BAR=29 DEPBAR=32 IMAD=57 PRMT=56 LOP3=72
```

The tail is now Tensor Core work rather than scalar FP32 loop work.

CLI timing:

```text
test_clean.wav, before promotion:
  old default:          900.7 ms
  M64/TN128 Tile tail:  897.3 ms

test_clean.wav, after promotion:
  promoted default:     899.1 ms
  disable fallback:     901.9 ms

test.wav, before promotion:
  old default:          5294.9 ms
  M64/TN128 Tile tail:  5267.7 ms

test.wav, after promotion:
  promoted default:     5262.8 ms
```

Output difference versus previous default/fallback:

```text
test_clean.wav promoted vs disable fallback:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.00886759162 rms=0.000625169229 rel_rms=0.00340038964 snr=49.369 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.00880484655 rms=0.000763540531 rel_rms=0.00317190408 snr=49.974 dB

test.wav promoted vs previous default:
  Vocals.wav:       max_abs=0.00735065341 rms=0.000487089545 rel_rms=0.00257673879 snr=51.779 dB
  Instrumental.wav: max_abs=0.00763860345 rms=0.000650142044 rel_rms=0.00339120848 snr=49.393 dB
```

Decision:

- Promote `M64/TN128 + masked Tile tail` to the BF16 default. It is aligned
  with the CUDA Tile-first goal because both the main and tail regions use
  `ct::mma`, and it is positive on both validation WAVs.
- Keep the disable fallback until the next Nsight trace confirms the expected
  per-kernel reduction inside full inference.
- Next QKV step: profile the promoted default under Nsight and quantify the
  time-QKV BKN group reduction. If the reduction is still smaller than the
  standalone bench predicts, inspect launch count, tail grid overhead, and
  shared-memory one-CTA/SM residency as the likely remaining causes.

## Time QKV BKN M64/TN128 masked Tile tail Nsight follow-up

Commands:

```bash
nsys profile --trace=cuda --sample=none --cpuctxsw=none --stats=false \
  --force-overwrite=true -o bench/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_promoted_clean \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_promoted_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_QKV_BKN_M64_TN128=1 \
nsys profile --trace=cuda --sample=none --cpuctxsw=none --stats=false \
  --force-overwrite=true -o bench/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_disabled_clean \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_disabled_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Trace files:

```text
bench/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_promoted_clean.nsys-rep
bench/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_promoted_clean.sqlite
bench/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_disabled_clean.nsys-rep
bench/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_disabled_clean.sqlite
```

Nsight CLI timing:

```text
promoted default: 913.8 ms
disable fallback: 914.5 ms
```

Total GPU kernel time:

```text
promoted default: 872.324 ms over 6602 launches
disable fallback: 874.133 ms over 6602 launches
delta:            -1.809 ms
```

QKV BKN group:

```text
promoted default:
  qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<64,128,16,78016,...>:
    48 calls, 60.134 ms total, 1252.785 us avg, grid=(1219,12), SMEM=100352, REG=163
  qkv_bkn_split_contig_masked_tail_kernel<64,128,16,78060,...>:
    48 calls, 0.379 ms total, 7.899 us avg, grid=(1,12), SMEM=100352, REG=164
  group total: 60.513 ms

disable fallback:
  qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<32,256,16,78048,...>:
    48 calls, 60.364 ms total, 1257.587 us avg, grid=(2439,6), SMEM=98304, REG=165
  qkv_bkn_split_contig_tail_kernel<1536,256>:
    48 calls, 2.288 ms total, 47.670 us avg, grid=(72,1), SMEM=0, REG=36
  group total: 62.652 ms

QKV group delta: -2.139 ms
```

A10G bottleneck report:

```text
promoted qkv main:
  60.134 ms, 49.0 TF/s, 70.0% roof, SMEM=100352, smCTA=1
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; multiple global-store/layout writes

disable qkv main:
  60.364 ms, 48.8 TF/s, 69.7% roof, SMEM=98304, smCTA=1
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; barrier-heavy staging; address/control overhead; multiple global-store/layout writes
```

Interpretation:

- The promoted main kernel only saves `0.230 ms` in the full graph
  (`60.364 -> 60.134 ms`), despite the standalone bench showing a clearer
  improvement. The full-graph main path is still one CTA/SM due to about
  98-100KB shared memory, so `M64/TN128` mostly improves codegen/instruction
  mix rather than residency.
- Most of the measured QKV group win comes from the tail conversion:
  `2.288 -> 0.379 ms`, saving `1.909 ms`.
- The whole GPU kernel-time reduction is `1.809 ms`, close to the QKV group
  reduction after normal trace noise. No other kernel showed a meaningful
  reverse delta; non-QKV deltas were around `0.1 ms` or smaller.
- The next QKV direction is not another simple `TM/TN` retile of this same
  BKN producer. The remaining main-kernel wall is shared-memory residency plus
  Q/K/V global stores. A deeper direction should either reduce the
  shared-memory footprint enough to allow `smCTA>=2`, or remove the Q/K/V
  global-memory round trip by fusing the producer with rotary/attention.

## Time QKV BKN small-SMEM residency sweep

Goal:

- Test whether reducing shared memory enough to reach `smCTA>=2` can beat the
  promoted `M64/TN128` QKV producer.
- This tests the hypothesis from the Nsight follow-up: the main producer is
  shared-memory-residency limited, so a smaller-SMEM tile might improve
  concurrency.

Resource summary from `--describe`:

```text
split_contig_tn32_lat2:
  tile=32x32x16,  grid=(2439,48), waves/SM=1463.4
  REG=69,  SMEM=34816,  shared_limit=2, occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

split_contig_tn64_lat2:
  tile=32x64x16,  grid=(2439,24), waves/SM=731.7
  REG=90,  SMEM=53248,  shared_limit=1, occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

split_contig_tn128_lat2:
  tile=32x128x16, grid=(2439,12), waves/SM=365.9
  REG=96,  SMEM=90112,  shared_limit=1, occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2:
  tile=64x128x16, grid=(1219,12), waves/SM=182.8
  REG=163, SMEM=100352, shared_limit=1, occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
split_contig_lat2:
  tile=32x256x16, best=1.276 ms, median=1.287 ms
  48.10 TF/s, 68.7% roof

split_contig_tn32_lat2:
  tile=32x32x16, best=3.370 ms, median=7.023 ms
  18.21 TF/s, 26.0% roof

split_contig_tn64_lat2:
  tile=32x64x16, best=2.082 ms, median=2.084 ms
  29.49 TF/s, 42.1% roof

split_contig_tn128_lat2:
  tile=32x128x16, best=1.679 ms, median=1.684 ms
  36.55 TF/s, 52.2% roof

split_contig_m64_tn128_lat2:
  tile=64x128x16, best=1.237 ms, median=1.239 ms
  49.60 TF/s, 70.9% roof
```

SASS/resource:

```text
TN32:
  REG=69, SMEM=34816
  instr=576 HMMA=32 LDSM=32 LDGSTS=32 BAR=33 IMAD=47

TN64:
  REG=90, SMEM=53248
  instr=672 HMMA=64 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=32 BAR=33 IMAD=46

TN128:
  REG=96, SMEM=90112
  instr=856 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=48 BAR=33 IMAD=100

M64/TN128:
  REG=163, SMEM=100352
  instr=1056 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48 BAR=29 IMAD=57
```

Interpretation:

- `TN32` is the only tested shape that reaches `smCTA=2`, but it is much
  slower: `18.21 TF/s` versus `49.60 TF/s` for `M64/TN128`.
- The reason is Tensor Core work density. Reducing `TN` reduces shared memory,
  but also cuts per-CTA HMMA body:
  `TN32 HMMA=32`, `TN64 HMMA=64`, `TN128 HMMA=128`,
  `M64/TN128 HMMA=256`.
- The small-TN shapes explode grid.y (`48` or `24`) and pay more launch
  scheduling, barrier, address/control, and output-store overhead per unit of
  useful math. More resident CTAs do not compensate for the lower per-CTA MMA
  density.
- `TN64` is especially bad for A10G: it still misses `smCTA=2`
  (`53248B > 51200B`) while already losing half or more of the Tensor Core
  body.

Decision:

- Do not port `TN32`, `TN64`, or `TN128` to source.
- For this QKV BKN producer, the remaining path to a better main kernel is not
  a simple small-SMEM retile. It needs a different dataflow:
  reduce staging footprint without cutting HMMA density, or fuse away Q/K/V
  global stores with rotary/attention.

## Time QKV fused-rotary M64/TN128 source probe

Goal:

- Recheck the current QKV+rotary fused producer after the time-QKV default was
  promoted to `M64/TN128 + masked Tile tail`.
- The existing fused-rotary opt-in still used the old `M32/TN256` producer
  shape, so a temporary source probe ported it to `M64/TN128` plus a fused
  masked Tile tail.

Temporary source probe:

- Generalized `qkv_bkn_rotary_split_contig_static_full_kernel` to allow
  `TN=128`.
- Added a temporary `qkv_bkn_rotary_split_contig_masked_tail_kernel`.
- Routed `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_FUSED_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1` through
  `M64/TN128` when `CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_QKV_BKN_M64_TN128` was not set.
- The probe was negative and was reverted from source after measurement.

Profile commands:

```bash
CUDASEP_PROFILE_TIME_QKV_PRODUCER=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/qkvrot_m64_default_profile_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_FUSED_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1 \
CUDASEP_PROFILE_TIME_QKV_PRODUCER=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/qkvrot_m64_fused_profile_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_FUSED_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1 \
CUDASEP_DISABLE_TIME_QKV_BKN_M64_TN128=1 \
CUDASEP_PROFILE_TIME_QKV_PRODUCER=1 \
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/qkvrot_m32_fused_profile_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```

Timing:

```text
default split producer + standalone rotary:
  inference=907.7 ms
  split_producer: 48 calls, 60.790 ms total, 1.26646 ms avg
  split_rotary:   48 calls, 24.020 ms total, 0.50042 ms avg
  producer+rotary total: 84.810 ms

temporary M64/TN128 fused-rotary producer:
  inference=951.7 ms
  bkn_fused_producer: 48 calls, 141.318 ms total, 2.94412 ms avg

old M32/TN256 fused-rotary producer:
  inference=956.0 ms
  bkn_fused_producer: 48 calls, 141.141 ms total, 2.94044 ms avg
```

Output difference, M64 fused versus default:

```text
Vocals.wav:       max_abs=1.78813934e-07 rms=9.9295774e-09  rel_rms=5.39837217e-08 snr=145.355 dB
Instrumental.wav: max_abs=2.38418579e-07 rms=1.30868397e-08 rel_rms=5.43724984e-08 snr=145.292 dB
```

SASS/resource from the temporary binary:

```text
M64/TN128 QKV main:
  REG=163 SMEM=100352
  instr=1056 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48 LDG=32 STS=8 STG=3
  F2FP=32 FFMA=0 FMUL=0 BAR=29 IMAD=57 PRMT=64

M64/TN128 fused-rotary main:
  REG=171 SMEM=98304
  instr=2064 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48 LDG=96 STS=56 STG=3
  F2FP=160 FFMA=64 FMUL=64 BAR=28 IMAD=222 PRMT=335

M64/TN128 fused-rotary masked tail:
  REG=174 SMEM=98304
  instr=2040 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48 LDG=96 STS=56 STG=3
  F2FP=160 FFMA=64 FMUL=64 BAR=28 IMAD=232 PRMT=311
```

Interpretation:

- The M64 fused producer is not limited by missing Tensor Core lowering:
  it still emits `HMMA=256`.
- It loses because the rotary work explodes scalar and permutation work inside
  the already large QKV tile: `instr=1056 -> 2064`,
  `LDG=32 -> 96`, `STS=8 -> 56`, `F2FP=32 -> 160`,
  `FFMA=0 -> 64`, `FMUL=0 -> 64`, `IMAD=57 -> 222`,
  and `PRMT=64 -> 335`.
- Runtime confirms the SASS: default producer+rotary is `84.810 ms`, while
  temporary M64 fused-rotary is `141.318 ms`.
- The old M32 fused-rotary spelling is essentially the same speed
  (`141.141 ms`), so the promoted M64 QKV shape does not rescue this fusion
  boundary.

Decision:

- Revert the temporary M64 fused-rotary source probe. Do not keep failed
  source complexity.
- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_TIME_QKV_FUSED_ROTARY_PRODUCER=1` unpromoted.
- Close current-spelling QKV+rotary producer fusion again. A future fusion
  attempt must not simply move rotary math into the QKV tile; it must either
  keep the default producer's scalar/SASS footprint close to unchanged or fuse
  farther into attention so the extra scalar work replaces a larger downstream
  global-memory boundary.

## 2026-06-06 - FFN12 two-stage current recheck

Goal:

- Recheck whether splitting FFN12 into a hidden-buffer FFN1 kernel plus an FFN2
  kernel is still a viable way to escape the current fused kernel's
  `REG=255` / `98304B` shared-memory pressure.
- Use the current `bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile` binary after the QKV
  source churn, not older README numbers.

Commands:

```bash
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn32 --warmup 20 --iters 300
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64 --warmup 20 --iters 300
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn128 --warmup 20 --iters 300
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_lat2|ffn1_poly9_tk64|ffn2_tn64' 40
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  launches=1 best=1.7888 ms median=1.7956 ms
  useful=45.75 TF/s roof=65.4% hidden_rw=0.000 GiB

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn32:
  launches=2 best=3.3288 ms median=3.3368 ms
  useful=24.59 TF/s roof=35.1% hidden_rw=0.298 GiB

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn64:
  launches=2 best=2.4495 ms median=2.4659 ms
  useful=33.41 TF/s roof=47.7% hidden_rw=0.298 GiB

two_stage_poly9_tk64_ffn2_tn128:
  launches=2 best=2.5895 ms median=2.6139 ms
  useful=31.60 TF/s roof=45.1% hidden_rw=0.298 GiB
```

Resource and SASS summary:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2<32,64,32,2,2>:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SMEM=98304
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160 BAR=35 IMAD=194

ffn1_poly9_tk64:
  REG=96 STACK=0 SMEM=61440
  instr=816 HMMA=64 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=24 LDG=24 STS=12
  F2FP=16 FFMA=128 FADD=48 FMUL=80 BAR=5 IMAD=32

ffn2_tn64<32,32,64>:
  REG=138 STACK=0 SMEM=98304
  instr=928 HMMA=128 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=64 LDG=40 STS=8

ffn2_tn64<32,64,64>:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SMEM=98304
  instr=1208 HMMA=256 LDSM=192 LDGSTS=96 LDG=48 STS=12

ffn2_tn64<32,128,64>:
  REG=255 STACK=0 SMEM=98304
  instr=1864 HMMA=512 LDSM=256 LDGSTS=160 LDG=64 STS=12
```

Tile-first purity:

```text
bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile:
  PASS, no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN dependency, symbol, or string match

cudasep_infer:
  PASS, no cuBLAS/cuFFT/cuDNN dependency, symbol, or string match
```

Interpretation:

- The two-stage design is still a negative path. Its best current variant is
  `TN64` at `33.41 TF/s`, far below the fused baseline at `45.75 TF/s`.
- Splitting does reduce FFN1 register pressure, but it introduces a `0.298 GiB`
  hidden activation write/read and a second launch. That global-memory phase
  boundary dominates the benefit.
- FFN2 remains a one-resident-CTA kernel on A10G because all tested FFN2
  output tiles still use `98304B` shared memory. `TN128` emits more HMMA
  instructions (`512`) but also reaches `REG=255` and is slower than `TN64`.
- Therefore this does not solve the bottom cause of FFN12's gap to the
  `70 TF/s` dense BF16 reference. The viable direction remains a fused
  W1/GELU/W2 dataflow that preserves `HMMA=256`, avoids the hidden global-memory
  round trip, and reduces shared/register pressure inside one Tile kernel.

Decision:

- Keep the current fused pair-H32 FFN12 source path.
- Do not source-port the hidden-buffer two-stage FFN12 design.
- Future FFN12 work should look for an internal fused-kernel staging/dataflow
  change, not another simple FFN1/FFN2 phase boundary.

## 2026-06-06 - Current hotspot selection after FFN12 two-stage closure

Goal:

- Use the current QKV M64/TN128 promoted Nsight trace plus current
  `cudasep_infer` SASS to choose the next CUDA Tile optimization target.
- Do not remeasure the dense BF16 ceiling; use the established A10G reference
  of `70 TF/s`.

Command:

```bash
./bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py \
  bench/nsys_qkv_m64_tiletail_promoted_clean.sqlite \
  build-min/cudasep_infer --limit 18
```

Top current kernels:

```text
rank 1:
  ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel
  calls=96 total=175.165 ms avg=1824.637 us
  44.9 TF/s, 64.1% of 70 TF/s
  REG=255 STACK=8 SMEM=98304 smCTA=1 HMMA=256 scalar=608 BAR=35 STG=8
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; high reg/stack pressure; large scalar
         side work; barrier-heavy staging; address/control overhead; multiple
         global-store/layout writes

rank 2:
  time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel
  calls=48 total=161.438 ms avg=3363.284 us
  60.8 TF/s, 86.9% of 70 TF/s
  REG=237 SMEM=40960 smCTA=2 HMMA=160 MUFU=124 scalar=1050 STG=4
  cause: transcendental/softmax MUFU; large scalar side work; multiple
         global-store/layout writes

rank 3:
  linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel
  calls=48 total=63.682 ms avg=1326.700 us
  46.3 TF/s, 66.1% of 70 TF/s
  REG=163 SMEM=98304 smCTA=1 HMMA=256 BAR=33 STG=8
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; barrier-heavy staging; multiple
         global-store/layout writes

rank 4:
  qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<64,128,16,...>
  calls=48 total=60.134 ms avg=1252.785 us
  49.0 TF/s, 70.0% of 70 TF/s
  REG=163 SMEM=100352 smCTA=1 HMMA=256 BAR=29 STG=24
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; multiple global-store/layout writes

rank 5:
  linear_cutile_static_full_bf16_kernel
  calls=96 total=50.314 ms avg=524.107 us
  39.0 TF/s, 55.8% of 70 TF/s
  REG=243 SMEM=98304 smCTA=1 HMMA=256 STG=4
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; high reg/stack pressure; multiple
         global-store/layout writes
```

Selection:

- FFN12 stays the next primary target. It is still the largest single group and
  it is far below the A10G dense BF16 reference for clearly identified static
  reasons: `REG=255`, `STACK=8`, `98304B` shared memory, large scalar GELU/add
  side work, barriers, and multiple stores.
- Time attention is a large wall-clock group, but its main kernel already
  reaches `60.8 TF/s` / `86.9%`. The remaining gap is mostly softmax/scalar
  work, not missing Tensor Core lowering. It is worth revisiting later, but it
  is not the best immediate target for pushing GEMM-like Tile throughput.
- QKV and BKN/full linear are still important, but the latest QKV work already
  closed the scalar tail and showed that simple small-SMEM retile loses. Their
  next useful direction likely requires a different dataflow or a fusion that
  removes global-memory stores without exploding scalar work.

Decision:

- Next FFN12 experiment should not split the kernel at a global-memory phase
  boundary. It should try to change the fused kernel's internal staging so the
  main body keeps `HMMA=256` while reducing either the `98304B` shared footprint
  or the `REG=255`/stack pressure.
- The first acceptance gate for a new FFN12 candidate is static: it must keep
  legal CUDA Tile MMA lowering and avoid dropping below the current `HMMA=256`
  fused body. The second gate is microbench time versus the current
  `1.7888 ms / 45.75 TF/s` baseline.

## 2026-06-06 - Negative Probe: FFN12 full-output accumulator tile

Goal:

- Test whether the current two live `32x128` output accumulators can be
  replaced by one `32x256` accumulator tile without changing the FFN12 fusion
  boundary.
- This is a direct CUDA Tile lowering experiment: preserve `TM32`,
  `THidden32`, `TK64`, the hidden-pair body, one launch, and no hidden
  global-memory phase.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The probe stays inside the documented CUDA Tile model: `__tile_global__`,
  `cuda::tiles`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`, `ct::mma`, and
  `<<<grid, 1>>>` launch.
- Reference:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`.

Change:

- Added `fused_pairh32_out256_lat2_outnoround` to
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`.
- The new variant changes only the output accumulator spelling:
  - baseline: two `ct::tile<float, shape<32,128>>` accumulators plus four
    `32 x 128` W2 MMA updates per hidden pair;
  - probe: one `ct::tile<float, shape<32,256>>` accumulator plus two
    `32 x 256` W2 MMA updates per hidden pair.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_lat2
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --describe --variant fused_pairh32_out256_lat2_outnoround
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_out256_lat2_outnoround --warmup 20 --iters 300
./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_(lat2|out256)' 80
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Runtime resource/occupancy:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5
  attr_regs=255 static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=8B

fused_pairh32_out256_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) waves/SM=30.5
  attr_regs=254 static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=0B
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.7892 ms median=1.7974 ms
  useful=45.74 TF/s roof=65.3% checksum=4080.0000

fused_pairh32_out256_lat2_outnoround:
  best=3.3745 ms median=3.3783 ms
  useful=24.25 TF/s roof=34.6% checksum=4080.0000
```

SASS/resource:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2<32,64,32,2,2>:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80 LDG=128 STS=44 STG=1
  F2FP=64 FFMA=256 FADD=160 FMUL=160 BAR=35 DEPBAR=29
  IMAD=194 IADD3=146 PRMT=16 LOP3=142 SHF=180

fused_pairh32_out256_lat2<32,64,32,2,2>:
  REG=254 STACK=0 SHARED=98304 LOCAL=0
  instr=1640 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=40 LDG=94 STS=34 STG=1
  F2FP=48 FFMA=128 FADD=112 FMUL=80 BAR=16 DEPBAR=14
  IMAD=224 IADD3=112 PRMT=14 LOP3=118 SHF=112
```

Tile-first purity:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- This is a strong negative result. The full-output accumulator removes the
  tiny `local=8B` stack spill and reduces register count by one, but it does
  not change the real A10G residency limiter: shared memory remains `98304B`,
  so occupancy is still one CTA per SM.
- The codegen loss is decisive: CUDA Tile lowers the `32x256` accumulator form
  to `HMMA=128`, half of the baseline `HMMA=256` body. Runtime follows the
  static evidence: `45.74 -> 24.25 TF/s`.
- Therefore the current efficient FFN12 spelling depends on keeping output as
  two `32x128` accumulator tiles. Simply combining them into one larger tile is
  not a path toward the 70 TF/s A10G reference.

Decision:

- Do not promote `fused_pairh32_out256_lat2_outnoround`.
- Keep this bench variant as a closed CUDA Tile codegen diagnostic.
- Future FFN12 work should preserve the two-half output accumulator shape unless
  a candidate proves it can keep `HMMA=256` while reducing shared memory below a
  real A10G residency threshold.

## 2026-06-06 - Negative Recheck: Time Attention Alpha Linear Clamp

Goal:

- Close the existing time-attention `alpha_linear_clamp` candidate under the
  current tree before adding new softmax approximations.
- This refreshes the earlier `Time Attention AlphaMode Cleanup And Linear Alpha
  Probe` result with the current binary and a longer `200`-iteration timing run.
- This tests whether the row-state alpha path can use a cheaper linear clamp
  while preserving the low-MUFU property of the no-exp probability upper
  candidate.
- Source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The measured kernels are existing `__tile_global__` CUDA Tile kernels using
  `cuda::tiles`, `ct::partition_view`, tile reductions, `ct::mma`, and
  `<<<grid, 1>>>` launch.
- Reference:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 200
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (0|8), false, (0|1|2)>' \
  120

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
```

Runtime resource:

```text
exact split-contig:
  attr_regs=233 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  attr_regs=230 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp:
  attr_regs=235 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2
```

Timing:

```text
exact split-contig:
  best=3.362 ms median=3.393 ms
  real_math=60.86 TF/s roof70=86.9%

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  best=3.343 ms median=3.374 ms
  real_math=61.21 TF/s roof70=87.4%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp:
  best=3.377 ms median=3.408 ms
  real_math=60.59 TF/s roof70=86.6%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317
```

SASS/resource:

```text
exact split-contig:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160
  instr=2736 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  IMAD=58 IADD3=129 LOP3=134

prob_poly3_noclamp:
  REG=234 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160
  instr=2584 FFMA=420 FADD=202 FMUL=250 MUFU=44
  IMAD=17 IADD3=137 LOP3=134

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  REG=230 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160
  instr=2600 FFMA=430 FADD=202 FMUL=270 MUFU=34
  IMAD=23 IADD3=137 LOP3=142

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_linear_clamp:
  REG=235 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160
  instr=2576 FFMA=420 FADD=212 FMUL=250 MUFU=34
  IMAD=29 IADD3=136 LOP3=142
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- `alpha_linear_clamp` keeps the Tensor Core body intact (`HMMA=160`) and keeps
  the low-MUFU property (`MUFU=34`), so the candidate is still a valid CUDA Tile
  softmax-codegen experiment.
- It is not a performance win. It raises register pressure versus the current
  upper candidate (`230 -> 235`) and is slower than both exact softmax and
  `alpha_poly3_clamp` on this run: `60.59 TF/s` versus `60.86 TF/s` exact and
  `61.21 TF/s` for the upper candidate.
- Output drift is identical to the poly3-clamp upper candidate in this bench
  (`rel_rms=0.00107288317`), so alpha-linear does not buy quality.
- Therefore lower MUFU alone is not enough. The row-state alpha approximation
  also has to preserve the low-register schedule of `alpha_poly3_clamp`.

Decision:

- Do not promote or focus on `alpha_linear_clamp`.
- Keep `prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp` as the current time-attention
  performance upper candidate.
- The next time-attention approximation should target non-negative probabilities
  without increasing register pressure beyond the current upper candidate.

## 2026-06-06 - Negative Probe: Time Attention Rational4 Probability

Goal:

- Test a non-negative, far-tail-decaying probability approximation that avoids
  the denominator failure of `prob_poly3_noclamp` without using per-score clamp:

```text
prob(x) = (1 - x / 4)^-4, where x = score - row_max <= 0
```

- Use the same approximation for row-state alpha under `kAlphaProbClamp`.
- Keep this benchmark-only. Source inference is unchanged.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The new candidate remains a CUDA Tile template branch inside the existing
  `__tile_global__` attention kernel.
- It keeps `ct::partition_view`, `ct::mma`, tile reductions, and
  `<<<grid, 1>>>` launch. The only intended change is the elementwise
  probability/alpha expression.
- Reference:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`.

Change:

- Added `kProbRational4` to `bench/bf16_time_attention_cutile.cu`.
- Added focused variant:
  `main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_rational4_alpha_rational4`.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --describe --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_rational4_alpha_rational4

./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_input --warmup 10 --iters 200
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline
./build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  --variant main1280_q64k32_exp2_split_contig_prob_rational4_alpha_rational4 \
  --warmup 10 --iters 200 --compare-baseline

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile \
  'time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel<64, 32, true, true, false, false, false, false, false, (0|8|10), false, (0|1)>' \
  160

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
```

Runtime resource:

```text
exact split-contig:
  attr_regs=233 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  attr_regs=230 static_shared=40960B shared_limit=2
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

prob_rational4_alpha_rational4:
  attr_regs=192 static_shared=32768B shared_limit=3
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=3
```

Timing:

```text
exact split-contig:
  best=3.392 ms median=3.393 ms
  real_math=60.33 TF/s roof70=86.2%

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  best=3.344 ms median=3.375 ms
  real_math=61.20 TF/s roof70=87.4%
  rel_rms=0.00107288317

prob_rational4_alpha_rational4:
  best=6.046 ms median=6.116 ms
  real_math=33.84 TF/s roof70=48.3%
  rel_rms=0.00122612892
```

SASS/resource:

```text
exact split-contig:
  REG=233 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2736 FFMA=340 FADD=202 FMUL=170 MUFU=124
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8

prob_poly3_noclamp_alpha_poly3_clamp:
  REG=230 SHARED=40960 HMMA=160 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=16
  instr=2600 FFMA=430 FADD=202 FMUL=270 MUFU=34
  BAR=15 DEPBAR=8

prob_rational4_alpha_rational4:
  REG=192 SHARED=32768 HMMA=64 LDSM=16 LDGSTS=4
  instr=2352 FFMA=420 FADD=118 FMUL=140 MUFU=70
  BAR=6 DEPBAR=2
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_time_attention_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- This is a useful negative result. The rational form achieves the intended
  resource reduction: `REG=230 -> 192`, `SMEM=40960 -> 32768`, and
  `occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2 -> 3`.
- That resource improvement is not enough, because CUDA Tile lowering changes
  the Tensor Core schedule: `HMMA=160 -> 64`, `LDSM=64 -> 16`,
  `LDGSTS=16 -> 4`.
- The per-score reciprocal also leaves substantial special-function work:
  `MUFU=70`, better than exact but worse than the current upper candidate's
  `MUFU=34`.
- Runtime follows the static evidence: `33.84 TF/s`, far below exact
  `60.33 TF/s` and the current upper candidate `61.20 TF/s`.

Decision:

- Do not pursue rational per-score probability approximations for the current
  CUDA Tile attention spelling.
- Future approximate-softmax candidates must reject any expression that drops
  the main kernel below the `HMMA=160` schedule, even if it reduces registers
  or shared memory.

## 2026-06-06 - BKN QKV linear M64/TN128 TK16 probe

Purpose:

- Test whether the QKV split-contig `M64/TN128/TK16` direction also helps the
  ordinary B(K,N) linear path:
  `linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<...,1536,256,false,false,...>`.
- This path was still showing up as a source-relevant 98KB shared-staging
  linear kernel. The goal was to reduce LDGSTS/instruction overhead while
  preserving CUDA Tile Tensor Core lowering.

Code:

- Added bench variant `qkv_t64x128x16bkn`.
- Generalized the existing latency-hint bench runner so it can also run
  `qkv_t64x128x16bkn_lat2`.
- Added an opt-in inference path:
  `CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1`.
- Default inference remains the previous `32x256x16_lat2` path, because the
  new shape did not produce a stable end-to-end win.

Bench timing:

```text
No latency hint, infer_qkv, warmup=20 iters=300:

qkv_t32x128x16bkn:
  best=1.770 ms median=1.781 ms 34.68 TF/s roof70=49.5%

qkv_t64x128x16bkn:
  best=1.402 ms median=1.406 ms 43.75 TF/s roof70=62.5%

qkv_t32x256x16bkn:
  best=1.378 ms median=1.392 ms 44.55 TF/s roof70=63.6%

qkv_t64x256x16bkn:
  best=2.218 ms median=2.225 ms 27.66 TF/s roof70=39.5%
```

```text
Latency hint, infer_qkv:

qkv_t32x256x16bkn_lat2, warmup=20 iters=300:
  best=1.242 ms median=1.285 ms 49.42 TF/s roof70=70.6%

qkv_t64x128x16bkn_lat2, warmup=20 iters=300:
  best=1.236 ms median=1.263 ms 49.64 TF/s roof70=70.9%

qkv_t32x256x16bkn_lat2, warmup=50 iters=1000:
  best=1.261 ms median=1.279 ms 48.67 TF/s roof70=69.5%

qkv_t64x128x16bkn_lat2, warmup=50 iters=1000:
  best=1.243 ms median=1.254 ms 49.35 TF/s roof70=70.5%
```

SASS/resource:

```text
qkv_t32x256x16bkn_lat2:
  REG=163 SHARED=98304
  instr=992 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80

qkv_t64x128x16bkn_lat2:
  REG=161 SHARED=100352
  instr=968 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48

qkv_t64x256x16bkn:
  REG=218 SHARED=98304
  instr=1336 HMMA=512 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80
```

End-to-end checks:

```text
Temporary default M64/TN128 run before opt-in switch:
  test_clean.wav: 898.7 ms
  test_clean.wav with CUDASEP_DISABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1: 897.0 ms
  test.wav: 5283.3 ms

After changing M64/TN128 to opt-in:
  default test_clean.wav: 898.8 ms
  CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1 test_clean.wav: 903.5 ms
```

Output difference against the old `32x256x16_lat2` path:

```text
Vocals:
  rel_rms=0.00352576287 max_abs=0.00799310207

Instrumental:
  rel_rms=0.00344306234 max_abs=0.00961622596
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- `M64/TN128/TK16 + latency=2` is a real kernel-level improvement over the
  old ordinary BKN QKV linear shape. It preserves `HMMA=256`, reduces
  instructions `992 -> 968`, and reduces `LDGSTS=80 -> 48`.
- The benefit is small: about 1-2% in the isolated bench, around
  `49.35 TF/s`, or `70.5%` of the accepted A10G dense BF16 70 TF/s reference.
- `M64/TN256/TK16` is a negative result. It doubles per-CTA HMMA but cuts CTA
  count in half and becomes much slower (`27.66 TF/s`). Bigger fused tiles are
  not automatically better under CUDA Tile lowering.
- The kernel-level gain does not currently survive end-to-end inference noise,
  and the different static/tail split is not bitwise-equivalent to the old
  path.

Decision:

- Keep `qkv_t64x128x16bkn` and `qkv_t64x128x16bkn_lat2` as bench variants.
- Keep the inference path as opt-in only via
  `CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1`.
- Do not promote it to the default path until an nsys-backed end-to-end profile
  shows a real total-time win.
- The bottom cause of the remaining gap is still the 98-100KB shared-staging
  CUDA Tile lowering: both useful candidates are effectively one CTA per SM,
  so small instruction/LDGSTS improvements only weakly affect total inference
  time.

## 2026-06-06 - BKN QKV dedicated bench shape support

Purpose:

- Move future ordinary QKV BKN linear shape probes out of the large
  `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` binary.
- The large GEMM bench repeatedly took minutes to rebuild after a single CUDA
  Tile template instantiation. The dedicated `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile`
  binary rebuilt in seconds for the same ordinary BKN M64/TN128 probe.

Code:

- Added ordinary BKN variant `m64_tn128_lat2` to
  `bench/bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile.cu`.
- Added a generic C-output `run_variant_shape<TM,TN,TK,MShape>()` runner.
- Added `launch_latency_shape<TM,TN,TK,MShape,...>()`.
- Added `--describe` coverage for `m64_tn128_lat2`.

Documentation check:

- Rechecked NVIDIA CUDA 13.3 "Writing Tile Kernels" for this bench style:
  tile kernels are `__tile_global__`, launched as `<<<grid, 1>>>`, use
  `ct::bid()` for tile-block position, and rely on compile-time tile shapes.

Describe:

```text
lat2:
  grid=(2439,6,1) waves/SM=182.9
  attr_regs=163 static_shared=98304B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

m64_tn128_lat2:
  grid=(1219,12,1) waves/SM=182.8
  attr_regs=161 static_shared=100352B shared_limit=1
  occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing:

```text
bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant lat2 --warmup 50 --iters 1000:
  best=1.271 ms median=1.289 ms 48.29 TF/s roof70=69.0%

bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile --variant m64_tn128_lat2 --warmup 50 --iters 1000:
  best=1.252 ms median=1.264 ms 48.99 TF/s roof70=70.0%
```

SASS/resource:

```text
lat2:
  REG=163 SHARED=98304
  instr=992 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80

m64_tn128_lat2:
  REG=161 SHARED=100352
  instr=968 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- The smaller bench reproduces the large-bench conclusion: ordinary
  `M64/TN128/TK16 + latency=2` is a small kernel-level win, but remains
  one CTA per SM because of 98-100KB shared memory use.
- This dedicated bench is now the preferred place for ordinary QKV BKN shape
  sweep work. It gives the same timing/static evidence with much shorter
  compile feedback.

Decision:

- Use `bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile` for the next ordinary BKN linear shape
  experiments.
- Do not add more ordinary QKV BKN shape probes to the large
  `bench_bf16_cutile_mma_gemm` binary unless the shape must be compared
  against unrelated GEMM families.

## 2026-06-06 - Negative Probe: ordinary QKV BKN small-N occupancy sweep

Purpose:

- Test whether reducing ordinary BKN linear tile-N can lower shared memory
  enough to increase CTA residency and recover more A10G throughput.
- Baseline `lat2` and prior best `m64_tn128_lat2` are both one CTA/SM because
  shared memory is 98-100KB. `tn32_lat2` was the key probe because it drops
  shared to 34816B and reaches two CTA/SM.

Code:

- Added ordinary BKN variants:
  - `tn32_lat2`
  - `tn64_lat2`
  - `tn128_lat2`
  - `m16_tn256_lat2`
  - `m64_tn32_lat2`
  - `m64_tn64_lat2`

Describe:

```text
lat2:
  tile=32x256x16 grid=(2439,6,1)
  regs=163 shared=98304B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

tn32_lat2:
  tile=32x32x16 grid=(2439,48,1)
  regs=64 shared=34816B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2

tn64_lat2:
  tile=32x64x16 grid=(2439,24,1)
  regs=86 shared=53248B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

tn128_lat2:
  tile=32x128x16 grid=(2439,12,1)
  regs=96 shared=90112B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

m16_tn256_lat2:
  tile=16x256x16 grid=(4878,6,1)
  regs=118 shared=97792B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

m64_tn32_lat2:
  tile=64x32x16 grid=(1219,48,1)
  regs=100 shared=53248B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

m64_tn64_lat2:
  tile=64x64x16 grid=(1219,24,1)
  regs=94 shared=73728B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```

Timing, `warmup=50 iters=1000`:

```text
lat2:
  best=1.274 ms median=1.290 ms 48.17 TF/s roof70=68.8%

tn32_lat2:
  best=3.455 ms median=3.458 ms 17.77 TF/s roof70=25.4%

tn64_lat2:
  best=2.069 ms median=2.081 ms 29.67 TF/s roof70=42.4%

tn128_lat2:
  best=1.680 ms median=1.697 ms 36.54 TF/s roof70=52.2%

m16_tn256_lat2:
  best=2.341 ms median=2.378 ms 26.22 TF/s roof70=37.5%

m64_tn32_lat2:
  best=3.460 ms median=3.465 ms 17.73 TF/s roof70=25.3%

m64_tn64_lat2:
  best=1.836 ms median=1.837 ms 33.42 TF/s roof70=47.7%

m64_tn128_lat2:
  best=1.252 ms median=1.255 ms 49.00 TF/s roof70=70.0%
```

SASS/resource:

```text
lat2:
  REG=163 SHARED=98304 instr=992 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80

m64_tn128_lat2:
  REG=161 SHARED=100352 instr=968 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48

tn128_lat2:
  REG=96 SHARED=90112 instr=768 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=48

tn64_lat2:
  REG=86 SHARED=53248 instr=624 HMMA=64 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=32

tn32_lat2:
  REG=64 SHARED=34816 instr=544 HMMA=32 LDSM=32 LDGSTS=32

m16_tn256_lat2:
  REG=118 SHARED=97792 instr=792 HMMA=128 LDSM=80 LDGSTS=80

m64_tn64_lat2:
  REG=94 SHARED=73728 instr=816 HMMA=128 LDSM=64 LDGSTS=32

m64_tn32_lat2:
  REG=100 SHARED=53248 instr=608 HMMA=64 LDSM=48 LDGSTS=32
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- The two-CTA/SM hypothesis is negative for this ordinary BKN spelling.
  `tn32_lat2` reaches `occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=2`, but lowering collapses
  to `HMMA=32` per CTA and runtime falls to `17.77 TF/s`.
- Smaller N tiles reduce shared memory, but they reduce Tensor Core work per
  CTA faster than they reduce overhead. Approximate instruction density:
  - `lat2`: `992 / 256 = 3.9 instr/HMMA`
  - `m64_tn128_lat2`: `968 / 256 = 3.8 instr/HMMA`
  - `tn32_lat2`: `544 / 32 = 17.0 instr/HMMA`
- M16 is also negative: it keeps N=256 but drops to `HMMA=128`, losing too
  much Tensor Core density.

Decision:

- Do not pursue ordinary QKV BKN `TN32/TN64/TN128` or `M16/TN256` for this
  kernel spelling.
- The current best isolated ordinary BKN shape remains
  `m64_tn128_lat2`, but it stays opt-in at inference level because its
  end-to-end win is not proven.
- Future attempts should not chase occupancy by shrinking N below 128. The
  next useful direction is reducing the 98-100KB staging cost while preserving
  `HMMA=256` density, or changing the spelling so the same `HMMA=256` work
  uses less shared memory.

## 2026-06-06 - M64/TN128 ordinary QKV BKN latency-hint sweep

Purpose:

- Tune latency hints on the current best ordinary BKN shape
  `M64/TN128/TK16`.
- The goal is to preserve `HMMA=256` while improving register pressure or
  scheduling. This follows the small-N sweep conclusion: do not trade away
  HMMA density just to change occupancy.

Code:

- Added ordinary BKN variants:
  - `m64_tn128_lat1`
  - `m64_tn128_lat4`
  - `m64_tn128_a2_b2_s0`
  - `m64_tn128_a2_b1_s0`
  - `m64_tn128_a1_b2_s2`

```text
  regs=151 shared=100352B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1

  regs=178 shared=100352B occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1
```


Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_qkv_bkn_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- All variants preserve `HMMA=256`; this is the right search space for this
  ordinary BKN spelling.
- `lat1` is the only small positive candidate. It is not the lowest-instruction
  variant, but it has the lowest register count (`REG=151`) and repeatedly
  edges out `lat2`.
- `lat4` is a clear negative despite fewer instructions, because register
  pressure jumps to `REG=226`.
- Store latency is not the limiter: `a2_b2_s0` is effectively equal to `lat2`.
- Lowering B load or A load hints asymmetrically did not help.

Decision:

- Keep `m64_tn128_lat1` as the current isolated ordinary BKN bench upper
  candidate.
- Do not change the inference opt-in path yet. The broader `M64/TN128` path
  previously failed to show a stable end-to-end win, so the next promotion
  attempt should be nsys-backed and should compare latency 1 vs latency 2
  inside the full inference path.

## 2026-06-06 - Ordinary QKV BKN M64/TN128 latency-1 inference check

Purpose:

- Move the isolated `m64_tn128_lat1` bench candidate into the inference binary
  as an opt-in experiment and check whether its kernel-level win survives
  full `test_clean.wav` inference.
- Keep the default path unchanged.

Code:

- Added `CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128_LAT1=1`.
- This flag only has effect when
  `CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1` is also set.
- Default ordinary QKV BKN remains `32x256x16 latency=2`.

Static SASS/resource in `cudasep_infer`:

```text
default 32x256 latency=2:
  REG=163 SHARED=98304 instr=992 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=80

M64/TN128 latency=2:
  REG=161 SHARED=100352 instr=968 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48

M64/TN128 latency=1:
  REG=151 SHARED=100352 instr=984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48
```

End-to-end `test_clean.wav`:

```text
default:
  infer time=901.3 ms

CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1:
  infer time=905.4 ms

CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1
CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128_LAT1=1:
  infer time=904.2 ms
```

Nsight Systems traces:

```text
bench/nsys_qkv_bkn_lat1_default_clean.nsys-rep
bench/nsys_qkv_bkn_lat1_default_clean.sqlite
bench/nsys_qkv_bkn_lat1_m64lat1_clean.nsys-rep
bench/nsys_qkv_bkn_lat1_m64lat1_clean.sqlite
```

Nsight `infer time`:

```text
default:
  915.9 ms

M64/TN128 latency=1:
  917.4 ms
```

Ordinary BKN QKV static+tail group from sqlite:

```text
default:
  static 32x256 latency=2: 63.505 ms, 48 calls, 1323.028 us/call
  scalar tail 1536x256:    2.290 ms, 48 calls,   47.713 us/call
  group total:            65.795 ms

M64/TN128 latency=1:
  static 64x128 latency=1: 59.859 ms, 48 calls, 1247.062 us/call
  scalar tail 1536x256:     8.020 ms, 48 calls,  167.089 us/call
  group total:             67.879 ms
```

Top-kernel context:

```text
default:
  linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<32,256,16,...latency=2>
    total=63.505 ms

M64/TN128 latency=1:
  linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<64,128,16,...latency=1>
    total=59.859 ms
  linear_cutile_tail_bf16_kernel<1536,256,...>
    total=8.020 ms
```

Audit:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/cudasep_infer
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- The isolated bench result is real inside full inference: the main ordinary
  BKN QKV kernel improves from `63.505 ms` to `59.859 ms`.
- The optimization still loses end-to-end because `M64` changes the static
  split from `78048 + 12 tail rows` to `78016 + 44 tail rows`.
- The scalar tail cost rises from `2.290 ms` to `8.020 ms`, more than erasing
  the `3.646 ms` main-kernel gain.
- Bottom cause is now precise: M64/TN128 is a better full-tile spelling, but
  the ordinary BKN path still uses scalar tail handling. The tail path becomes
  the limiter for this shape.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128_LAT1=1` as an opt-in
  experiment only.
- Do not promote M64/TN128 latency 1 or latency 2 to default.
- Next useful implementation target: add a CUDA Tile masked tail for ordinary
  BKN QKV, analogous to the existing `qkv_bkn_split_contig_masked_tail_kernel`,
  so the M64/TN128 main-kernel gain is not erased by scalar tail work.

## 2026-06-06 - Ordinary QKV BKN M64/TN128 latency-1 masked tail

Purpose:

- Replace the scalar ordinary QKV BKN tail that made the earlier M64/TN128
  source experiment lose end-to-end.
- Keep the implementation fixed to the current MelBandRoformer ordinary QKV
  shape: `M=78060`, `N=1536`, `K=256`, no bias, no GELU.

Bench prototype:

```text
m64_tn128_lat1 full-only:
  best=1.242 ms median=1.250 ms 49.40 TF/s roof=70.6%

m64_tn128_lat1_masktail:
  full_grid=(1219,12), tail_grid=(1,12)
  best=1.247 ms median=1.257 ms 49.21 TF/s roof=70.3%
```

Bench SASS/resource:

```text
qkv_bkn_latency_kernel<64,128,16,...lat1>:
  REG=151 SHARED=100352 instr=984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48

qkv_bkn_masked_tail_kernel<64,128,16,78060,...TailStart=78016,lat1>:
  REG=151 SHARED=100352 instr=968 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48
```

Source integration:

- Added `linear_cutile_static_bkn_masked_tail_bf16_kernel`.
- The masked-tail path is selected only for
  `StaticM=78016`, `TM=64`, `TN=128`, `TK=16`, `N=1536`, `K=256`,
  `total_batch=78060`, no bias, and no GELU.
- The tail now runs as one masked CUDA Tile row group times 12 N tiles instead
  of falling back to the scalar K-loop tail kernel.

Source SASS/resource:

```text
linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<64,128,16,...lat1>:
  REG=151 SHARED=100352 instr=984 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48

linear_cutile_static_bkn_masked_tail_bf16_kernel<64,128,16,78060,1536,256,78016,1>:
  REG=151 SHARED=100352 instr=968 HMMA=256 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=48
```

End-to-end:

```text
test_clean.wav default before promotion:
  infer time=902.2 ms

test_clean.wav M64/TN128 latency=1 + masked tail:
  infer time=898.6 ms

test.wav default before promotion:
  infer time=5266.8 ms

test.wav M64/TN128 latency=1 + masked tail:
  infer time=5227.9 ms

test_clean.wav promoted default after code change:
  infer time=891.8 ms

test.wav promoted default after code change:
  infer time=5233.3 ms
```

Nsight Systems `test_clean.wav` ordinary BKN QKV group:

```text
old default:
  static 32x256 latency=2: 63.505 ms
  scalar tail:              2.290 ms
  group total:             65.795 ms

M64/TN128 latency=1 without masked tail:
  static 64x128 latency=1: 59.859 ms
  scalar tail:              8.020 ms
  group total:             67.879 ms

M64/TN128 latency=1 with masked tail:
  static 64x128 latency=1: 59.758 ms
  masked tail:              0.365 ms
  group total:             60.123 ms
```

Interpretation:

- The previous negative source result was not caused by the M64/TN128 full-tile
  shape. It was caused by the larger scalar tail created by the `78016 + 44`
  split.
- The masked CUDA Tile tail removes that bottom cause: tail time drops from
  `8.020 ms` to `0.365 ms` in the nsys group, while the full-tile kernel keeps
  the `64x128x16 latency=1` win.
- The isolated bench remains near `49 TF/s`, about `70%` of the accepted A10G
  dense BF16 reference.

Decision:

- Promote `M64/TN128 latency=1 + masked tail` to the BF16 default ordinary QKV
  BKN path.
- Keep rollback switches:
  `CUDASEP_DISABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128=1` returns to the old `32x256x16`
  path, and `CUDASEP_DISABLE_LINEAR_BKN_QKV_M64_TN128_LAT1=1` keeps M64/TN128
  but uses latency 2.

## 2026-06-06 - Promoted QKV hotspot refresh and FFN12 odd5 recheck

Goal:

- Refresh the current promoted default hotspot table after making ordinary QKV
  BKN `M64/TN128 latency=1 + masked tail` the default.
- Recheck the existing FFN12 odd5 GELU opt-in under the same current default
  tree. This does not remeasure the A10G dense BF16 ceiling; all roof
  percentages still use the established `70 TF/s` reference.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The next FFN12 work remains inside the documented CUDA Tile model:
  `__tile_global__`, `cuda::tiles`, `ct::partition_view`, `ct::tensor_span`,
  `ct::mma`, and `<<<grid, 1>>>` launch.
- Reference checked:
  `https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html`.

Commands:

```bash
nsys profile --trace=cuda --sample=none --cpuctxsw=none --stats=false \
  --force-overwrite=true -o bench/nsys_promoted_default_clean \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/nsys_promoted_default_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

nsys export --type sqlite --force-overwrite=true \
  --output bench/nsys_promoted_default_clean.sqlite \
  bench/nsys_promoted_default_clean.nsys-rep

./bench/nsys_kernel_summary.sh bench/nsys_promoted_default_clean.sqlite 40
./bench/a10g_cutile_bottleneck_report.py \
  bench/nsys_promoted_default_clean.sqlite build-min/cudasep_infer --limit 35

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_odd5_after_qkv_promoted_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1 ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test.wav \
  --output outputs/ffn12_odd5_after_qkv_promoted_test \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1 \
nsys profile --trace=cuda --sample=none --cpuctxsw=none --stats=false \
  --force-overwrite=true -o bench/nsys_ffn12_odd5_after_qkv_clean \
  ./build-min/cudasep_infer \
    --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
    --input test_clean.wav \
    --output outputs/nsys_ffn12_odd5_after_qkv_clean \
    --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1

nsys export --type sqlite --force-overwrite=true \
  --output bench/nsys_ffn12_odd5_after_qkv_clean.sqlite \
  bench/nsys_ffn12_odd5_after_qkv_clean.nsys-rep
```

Promoted default Nsight `test_clean.wav`:

```text
kernel launches: 6602

ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel:
  calls=96 total=175.276 ms avg=1825.789 us
  useful=44.8 TF/s roof70=64.0%
  REG=255 STACK=8 SMEM=98304 smCTA=1 HMMA=256 scalar=608 BAR=35 STG=8
  cause: 98KB shared residency wall; high reg/stack pressure; large scalar
         side work; barrier-heavy staging; address/control overhead; multiple
         global-store/layout writes

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel:
  calls=48 total=161.910 ms avg=3373.123 us
  useful=60.7 TF/s roof70=86.7%
  REG=237 SMEM=40960 smCTA=2 HMMA=160 MUFU=124 scalar=1050 STG=4
  cause: transcendental/softmax MUFU; large scalar side work; multiple
         global-store/layout writes

qkv_bkn_split_contig_static_full_kernel<64,128,16,...>:
  calls=48 total=60.274 ms avg=1255.717 us
  useful=48.9 TF/s roof70=69.8%

linear_cutile_static_full_bkn_bf16_kernel<64,128,16,...lat1>:
  calls=48 total=59.825 ms avg=1246.352 us
  useful=49.2 TF/s roof70=70.3%
```

FFN12 odd5 current A/B:

```text
promoted default, test_clean.wav:
  infer time=891.8 ms

odd5 opt-in, test_clean.wav:
  infer time=885.7 ms

promoted default, test.wav:
  infer time=5233.3 ms

odd5 opt-in, test.wav:
  infer time=5171.7 ms
```

Odd5 Nsight `test_clean.wav`:

```text
ffn12_fused256_split2_pairh32_cutile_kernel<odd5>:
  calls=96 total=162.708 ms avg=1694.872 us
  useful=48.3 TF/s roof70=69.0%
  REG=255 STACK=0 SMEM=98304 smCTA=1 HMMA=256 scalar=416 BAR=35 STG=8

time_attention1301_main1280_split_contig_input_kernel:
  calls=48 total=162.447 ms avg=3384.315 us
  useful=60.5 TF/s roof70=86.4%
```

Output difference, odd5 versus promoted default:

```text
test_clean.wav Vocals:
  max_abs=0.126771227 rms=0.00771012157 rel_rms=0.0419139681 snr=27.553 dB

test_clean.wav Instrumental:
  max_abs=0.138799384 rms=0.00807322189 rel_rms=0.0335443016 snr=29.488 dB

test.wav Vocals:
  max_abs=0.122407928 rms=0.00463276962 rel_rms=0.0245090971 snr=32.213 dB

test.wav Instrumental:
  max_abs=0.137393504 rms=0.00511542195 rel_rms=0.0266815519 snr=31.476 dB
```

Interpretation:

- FFN12 remains the best immediate CUDA Tile/A10G resource-shape target in the
  default path: it is the top kernel, far below the 70 TF/s dense-BF16
  reference, and has concrete static causes.
- Odd5 proves that GELU scalar side work is material: it reduces the FFN12 main
  group by `12.568 ms` in the current `test_clean.wav` trace and lifts the
  kernel from `44.8` to `48.3 TF/s`.
- That gain is not enough to close the FFN12 gap. The odd5 kernel still uses
  `98304B` shared memory, keeps one CTA/SM residency, and remains `REG=255`.
- Odd5 also creates much larger output drift than BF16-roundoff-only toggles
  (`rel_rms` about `2.5%-4.2%`), so it should stay an explicit experiment
  rather than the default.
- Once FFN12 is pushed to the odd5 regime, time attention becomes essentially
  tied for the largest wall-clock group. This means further progress must come
  from either a real FFN12 resource-shape change or a time-attention softmax/
  row-state change, not from repeatedly lowering the GELU polynomial degree.

Decision:

- Keep `CUDASEP_ENABLE_FFN12_FUSED_ODD5_GELU=1` opt-in only.
- Next FFN12 candidate must attack the actual resource causes:
  `98304B` shared memory, `REG=255`, stack pressure, barriers, address/control
  overhead, or global-store/layout writes, while preserving the `HMMA=256`
  source-matching dataflow.
- If no such FFN12 candidate can preserve `HMMA=256`, move the next experiment
  to time attention, where the remaining wall-clock is dominated by softmax
  scalar/MUFU work around an already strong Tensor Core body.

## 2026-06-06 - Negative Probe: FFN12 TM/Hidden/TK Shape Sweep

Goal:

- Test whether a smaller fused FFN12 Tile shape can escape the current
  `98304B` shared-memory wall on A10G without adding a hidden global-memory
  phase boundary.
- Keep the same one-launch fused W1/GELU/W2 dataflow and use only CUDA Tile
  `__tile_global__`, `ct::partition_view`, and `ct::mma`.
- Do not change source inference until a bench shape proves it can keep enough
  Tensor Core issue density.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- CUDA Tile shapes are compile-time kernel properties, so this probe adds fixed
  bench instantiations rather than runtime shape knobs.
- Reference checked before the edit:
  https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html

Change:

- Added three bench-only variants to
  `bench/bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile.cu`:
  - `fused_tm16_pairh32_lat2_outnoround`: `TM=16,TK=64,THidden=32`
  - `fused_pairh16_lat2_outnoround`: `TM=32,TK=64,THidden=16`
  - `fused_pairh16_tk32_lat2_outnoround`: `TM=32,TK=32,THidden=16`
- Source `cudasep_infer` is unchanged.

Commands:

```bash
ninja -C build-min bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile -j8

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile --describe \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile --describe \
  --variant fused_tm16_pairh32_lat2_outnoround
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile --describe \
  --variant fused_pairh16_lat2_outnoround
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile --describe \
  --variant fused_pairh16_tk32_lat2_outnoround

./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh32_lat2 --warmup 20 --iters 300
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_tm16_pairh32_lat2_outnoround --warmup 20 --iters 300
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh16_lat2_outnoround --warmup 20 --iters 300
./build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  --variant fused_pairh16_tk32_lat2_outnoround --warmup 20 --iters 300

./bench/cuobjdump_kernel_summary.sh \
  build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile \
  'ffn12_fused_pairh32_lat2_kernel<(16, 64, 32|32, 64, 16|32, 32, 16|32, 64, 32)' \
  120

./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
```

Runtime resource:

```text
baseline fused_pairh32_lat2:
  grid=(2439,1,1) attr_regs=255 static_shared=98304B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=8B

fused_tm16_pairh32_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(4878,1,1) attr_regs=228 static_shared=100352B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=0B

fused_pairh16_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) attr_regs=255 static_shared=96256B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=8B

fused_pairh16_tk32_lat2_outnoround:
  grid=(2439,1,1) attr_regs=230 static_shared=96256B
  shared_limit=1 occupancy_active_cta_per_sm=1 local=0B
```

Timing:

```text
fused_pairh32_lat2:
  best=1.7917 ms median=1.7993 ms useful=45.68 TF/s roof=65.3%

fused_tm16_pairh32_lat2_outnoround:
  best=2.8852 ms median=2.9181 ms useful=28.37 TF/s roof=40.5%

fused_pairh16_lat2_outnoround:
  best=2.2933 ms median=2.2976 ms useful=35.69 TF/s roof=51.0%

fused_pairh16_tk32_lat2_outnoround:
  best=2.8852 ms median=2.9178 ms useful=28.37 TF/s roof=40.5%
```

SASS/resource:

```text
baseline fused_pairh32_lat2<32,64,32>:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=98304
  instr=2536 HMMA=256 LDSM=128 LDGSTS=80
  BAR=35 DEPBAR=29 IMAD=194

fused_tm16_pairh32_lat2_outnoround<16,64,32>:
  REG=228 STACK=0 SHARED=100352
  instr=1624 HMMA=128 LDSM=96 LDGSTS=72
  BAR=27 DEPBAR=27 IMAD=172

fused_pairh16_lat2_outnoround<32,64,16>:
  REG=255 STACK=8 SHARED=96256
  instr=2480 HMMA=192 LDSM=120 LDGSTS=72
  BAR=32 DEPBAR=41 IMAD=273

fused_pairh16_tk32_lat2_outnoround<32,32,16>:
  REG=230 STACK=0 SHARED=96256
  instr=2680 HMMA=192 LDSM=120 LDGSTS=96
  BAR=47 DEPBAR=69 IMAD=297
```

Tile-first purity:

```text
./bench/audit_cuda_tile_first.sh build-min/bench_bf16_ffn12_two_stage_cutile
PASS: CUDA Tile-first purity gate passed
```

Interpretation:

- None of these shapes reaches the A10G two-resident-CTA shared-memory
  threshold. `pairH16` only moves `98304B -> 96256B`, still one CTA/SM, and
  `TM16` is worse at `100352B`.
- The more important loss is Tensor Core issue density. The baseline fused
  spelling emits `HMMA=256`; `pairH16` emits only `HMMA=192`, and `TM16` emits
  only `HMMA=128`.
- `pairH16_tk32` reduces registers and removes the tiny stack spill, but it
  increases barriers/control work (`BAR=47`, `DEPBAR=69`, `IMAD=297`) while
  keeping one CTA/SM and only `HMMA=192`.
- The bottom cause is therefore not "hidden tile too large" in isolation.
  Simple reductions of `TM`, `THidden`, or `TK` do not solve the 98KB shared
  wall under CUDA Tile lowering and also reduce the static HMMA body.

Decision:

- Do not source-port these shapes.
- Close simple `TM16`, `pairH16`, and `TK32+pairH16` as FFN12 A10G candidates.
- Future FFN12 work must either preserve the current `HMMA=256` two-output-half
  body while cutting shared below a real residency threshold, or shift to a
  different fusion that removes a global-memory boundary elsewhere without
  reducing Tensor Core issue density.

## 2026-06-06 - Current Recheck: Time Attention Clamped Approx Placement

Goal:

- Recheck the existing clamped approximate softmax production path under the
  current promoted QKV/FFN12 default tree.
- Do not add new kernels. Use the existing CUDA Tile production instantiations
  and the current A10G dense BF16 reference of `70 TF/s`.
- Answer whether the safer clamped approximation is now useful as a speed path,
  or only as a diagnostic/placement tool.

Official CUDA Tile context:

- The measured production kernels are `__tile_global__` CUDA Tile kernels using
  `ct::partition_view`, tile reductions, `ct::mma`, and `<<<grid, 1>>>`.
- Reference checked during the experiment:
  https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-programming-guide/02-basics/writing-tile-kernels.html

Commands:

```bash
./build-min/cudasep_infer \
  --model mel-band-roformer-deux/becruily_deux.csm \
  --input test_clean.wav \
  --output outputs/time_approx_clamp_current_baseline_clean \
  --bf16 --chunk-batch-size 1 --stem -1
```
